subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:10
Section: Cell Cycle
Summary: This section introduces the cell cycle, defining it as the coordinated sequence of events involving cell growth, DNA replication, and division. It details the two main phases—Interphase (consisting of G1, S, and G2 sub-phases) and M Phase—along with the non-dividing quiescent stage (G0).
Key terms: Cell Cycle, Interphase, M Phase, G1 Phase, S Phase, G2 Phase, Quiescent Stage, Karyokinesis, Cytokinesis
Teaches concepts: concept:cell_cycle (Cell Cycle), concept:g1_phase (G1 Phase), concept:g2_phase (G2 Phase), concept:interphase (Interphase), concept:quiescent_stage_g0 (Quiescent Stage (G0)), concept:s_phase (S Phase)
Requires concepts: concept:dna (DNA), concept:haploid_and_diploid (Haploid and Diploid)
title: Cell Cycle

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:10
Section: M Phase
Summary: The M Phase (Mitosis) represents the equational division of the cell cycle, consisting of nuclear division (karyokinesis) and cytoplasmic division (cytokinesis). Karyokinesis progresses through four sequential stages—prophase, metaphase, anaphase, and telophase—to ensure equal chromosome segregation, followed by cytokinesis which splits the cytoplasm to form two daughter cells.
Key terms: Mitosis, Karyokinesis, Cytokinesis, Prophase, Metaphase, Anaphase, Telophase, Kinetochore, Metaphase plate, Cell-plate
Teaches concepts: concept:anaphase (Anaphase), concept:cytokinesis (Cytokinesis), concept:karyokinesis (Karyokinesis), concept:metaphase (Metaphase), concept:prophase (Prophase), concept:telophase (Telophase)
Requires concepts: concept:chromosome_structure (Chromosome Structure), concept:interphase (Interphase)
title: M Phase

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:10
Section: Significance of Mitosis
Summary: This section details the biological significance of mitosis, also known as equational division. It explains how mitosis facilitates multicellular growth, cell repair, restoration of the nucleo-cytoplasmic ratio, and continuous plant development.
Key terms: mitosis, equational division, nucleo-cytoplasmic ratio, cell repair, meristematic tissues
Teaches concepts: concept:cell_repair (Cell Repair), concept:equational_division (Equational Division), concept:meristematic_mitosis (Meristematic Mitosis), concept:nucleo_cytoplasmic_ratio_restoration (Nucleo-cytoplasmic Ratio Restoration)
Requires concepts: concept:diploid_and_haploid_cells (Diploid and Haploid Cells), concept:mitosis_cell_division (Mitosis (Cell Division))
title: Significance of Mitosis

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:10
Section: Meiosis
Summary: This section explains meiosis, a specialized cell division that reduces the chromosome number by half to produce four haploid daughter cells. It details the sequential phases of Meiosis I and Meiosis II, highlighting key events such as homologous chromosome pairing, crossing over, and the separation of sister chromatids.
Key terms: Meiosis, Synapsis, Crossing over, Chiasmata, Meiosis I, Meiosis II
Teaches concepts: concept:chiasmata (Chiasmata), concept:crossing_over (Crossing Over), concept:meiosis (Meiosis), concept:meiosis_i (Meiosis I), concept:meiosis_ii (Meiosis II), concept:synapsis (Synapsis)
Requires concepts: concept:chromosomes (Chromosomes), concept:mitosis (Mitosis), concept:ploidy (Ploidy)
title: Meiosis

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:10
Section: Significance of Meiosis
Summary: This section outlines the evolutionary and biological significance of meiosis. It explains how meiosis maintains a constant chromosome number across generations in sexually reproducing species and introduces genetic variability essential for evolution.
Key terms: Meiosis, Chromosome number conservation, Genetic variability, Evolution, Sexual reproduction
Teaches concepts: concept:conservation_of_chromosome_number (Conservation of Chromosome Number), concept:generation_of_genetic_variability (Generation of Genetic Variability)
Requires concepts: concept:evolution (Evolution), concept:meiosis (Meiosis), concept:sexual_reproduction (Sexual Reproduction)
title: Significance of Meiosis

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: Photosynthesis in Higher Plants
Summary: This section introduces photosynthesis as a vital physico-chemical process where green plants convert light energy into chemical energy to synthesize organic compounds. It emphasizes its role as the primary source of food on Earth and the generator of atmospheric oxygen.
Key terms: Photosynthesis, Autotrophs, Heterotrophs, Light energy, Chemical energy, Oxygen release
Teaches concepts: concept:autotrophs (Autotrophs), concept:heterotrophs (Heterotrophs), concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis)
Requires concepts:
title: Photosynthesis in Higher Plants

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: What do we Know?
Summary: This section introduces the fundamental requirements for photosynthesis, which include chlorophyll, light, and carbon dioxide. It describes classic experiments, such as starch testing on variegated leaves and KOH-treated setups, to demonstrate the necessity of these components.
Key terms: Photosynthesis, Chlorophyll, Carbon dioxide, Starch, Variegated leaf, Potassium hydroxide
Teaches concepts: concept:koh_leaf_experiment (KOH Leaf Experiment), concept:requirements_for_photosynthesis (Requirements for Photosynthesis), concept:starch_test_for_photosynthesis (Starch Test for Photosynthesis), concept:variegated_leaf_experiment (Variegated Leaf Experiment)
Requires concepts: concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis), concept:starch (Starch)
title: What do we Know?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: Factors affecting Photosynthesis
Summary: This section explores the internal and external factors that regulate the rate of photosynthesis in plants. It highlights Blackman's Law of Limiting Factors, which states that the rate of a multi-factor biochemical process is governed by the factor closest to its minimum level.
Key terms: photosynthesis rate, internal factors, external factors, Blackman's Law of Limiting Factors, limiting factors
Teaches concepts: concept:blackmans_law_of_limiting_factors (Blackman's Law of Limiting Factors), concept:external_factors_of_photosynthesis (External Factors of Photosynthesis), concept:internal_factors_of_photosynthesis (Internal Factors of Photosynthesis)
Requires concepts: concept:chloroplast_structure (Chloroplast Structure), concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis)
title: Factors affecting Photosynthesis

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: Light
Summary: This section explores how light intensity affects the rate of photosynthesis, demonstrating a linear relationship at low intensities that plateaus as other factors become limiting. It defines the light saturation point and explains how excessive light beyond this point can cause chlorophyll breakdown, reducing photosynthesis.
Key terms: light intensity, photosynthesis rate, light saturation, limiting factor, chlorophyll breakdown, CO2 fixation
Teaches concepts: concept:chlorophyll_breakdown_under_high_light_intensity (Chlorophyll Breakdown under High Light Intensity), concept:light_intensity_and_photosynthesis_rate_relationship (Light Intensity and Photosynthesis Rate Relationship), concept:light_saturation_point (Light Saturation Point)
Requires concepts: concept:chlorophyll (Chlorophyll), concept:law_of_limiting_factors (Law of Limiting Factors), concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis)
title: Light

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: Carbon dioxide Concentration
Summary: This section explains how carbon dioxide concentration acts as a major limiting factor for photosynthesis. It highlights the differential responses and saturation points of C3 and C4 plants, and discusses the practical application of CO2 enrichment in greenhouse farming.
Key terms: Carbon dioxide concentration, Limiting factor, C3 plants, C4 plants, Saturation point, Greenhouse farming
Teaches concepts: concept:carbon_dioxide_as_a_limiting_factor (Carbon dioxide as a limiting factor), concept:co2_enrichment_in_greenhouse_farming (CO2 enrichment in greenhouse farming), concept:differential_co2_response_of_c3_and_c4_plants (Differential CO2 response of C3 and C4 plants)
Requires concepts: concept:c3_plants (C3 plants), concept:c4_plants (C4 plants), concept:law_of_limiting_factors (Law of Limiting Factors)
title: Carbon dioxide Concentration

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: Temperature
Summary: This section explains how temperature affects the rate of photosynthesis, highlighting the temperature sensitivity of dark and light reactions. It contrasts the temperature optimums of C3 and C4 plants, as well as plants adapted to tropical versus temperate habitats.
Key terms: Temperature optimum, Dark reactions, Light reactions, C3 plants, C4 plants, Enzymatic reactions
Teaches concepts: concept:habitat_dependent_temperature_optimum (Habitat-Dependent Temperature Optimum), concept:temperature_optimum_in_c3_and_c4_plants (Temperature Optimum in C3 and C4 Plants), concept:temperature_sensitivity_of_photosynthesis (Temperature Sensitivity of Photosynthesis)
Requires concepts: concept:c3_and_c4_plants (C3 and C4 Plants), concept:dark_reactions_of_photosynthesis (Dark Reactions of Photosynthesis), concept:light_reactions_of_photosynthesis (Light Reactions of Photosynthesis)
title: Temperature

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: Water
Summary: This section describes how water acts as a limiting factor in photosynthesis, primarily through indirect physiological effects. It explains how water stress leads to stomatal closure and leaf wilting, which subsequently reduce carbon dioxide availability and light absorption.
Key terms: Water stress, Limiting factor, Stomatal closure, Leaf wilting, Photosynthesis
Teaches concepts: concept:indirect_effects_of_water_stress (Indirect Effects of Water Stress), concept:leaf_wilting (Leaf Wilting), concept:water_stress_induced_stomatal_closure (Water Stress Induced Stomatal Closure)
Requires concepts: concept:light_reaction (Light Reaction), concept:limiting_factors_in_photosynthesis (Limiting Factors in Photosynthesis), concept:stomata (Stomata)
title: Water

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: Early Experiments
Summary: This section traces the historical milestones and key experiments that led to our understanding of photosynthesis. It highlights the contributions of scientists like Priestley, Ingenhousz, Sachs, Engelmann, and van Niel, culminating in the correct balanced chemical equation for photosynthesis.
Key terms: Photosynthesis, Action spectrum, Chloroplasts, Oxygen evolution, Cornelius van Niel, Joseph Priestley
Teaches concepts: concept:action_spectrum_of_photosynthesis (Action Spectrum of Photosynthesis), concept:glucose_production_and_storage (Glucose Production and Storage), concept:light_and_green_tissue_requirement (Light and Green Tissue Requirement), concept:priestleys_oxygen_discovery_experiment (Priestley's Oxygen Discovery Experiment), concept:water_as_the_oxygen_source (Water as the Oxygen Source)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_equations (Chemical Equations), concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis), concept:plant_cell_structure (Plant Cell Structure)
title: Early Experiments

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: Where does Photosynthesis take Place?
Summary: This section identifies the chloroplasts within mesophyll cells as the site of photosynthesis and details their internal structure. It explains the division of labor within the chloroplast, distinguishing between light-driven reactions in the membrane system and carbon-fixing dark reactions in the stroma.
Key terms: mesophyll cells, chloroplast, grana, stroma, light reactions, dark reactions
Teaches concepts: concept:chloroplast_structure (Chloroplast Structure), concept:dark_reactions (Dark Reactions), concept:light_reaction (Light Reaction), concept:mesophyll_cells (Mesophyll Cells)
Requires concepts: concept:atp_and_nadph (ATP and NADPH), concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis)
title: Where does Photosynthesis take Place?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: How many Types of Pigments are Involved in Photosynthesis?
Summary: This section explains how leaf pigments can be separated using paper chromatography and identifies chlorophyll a, chlorophyll b, xanthophylls, and carotenoids. It describes the relationship between light absorption and photosynthetic rate using absorption and action spectra, highlighting chlorophyll a as the primary pigment. Finally, it discusses how accessory pigments assist in light absorption and protect chlorophyll a from photo-oxidation.
Key terms: Chlorophyll a, Chlorophyll b, Xanthophylls, Carotenoids, Paper chromatography, Absorption spectrum, Action spectrum, Accessory pigments, Photo-oxidation
Teaches concepts: concept:absorption_spectrum (Absorption Spectrum), concept:accessory_pigments (Accessory Pigments), concept:action_spectrum (Action Spectrum), concept:paper_chromatography (Paper Chromatography), concept:photosynthetic_pigments (Photosynthetic Pigments)
Requires concepts: concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis), concept:visible_light_spectrum (Visible Light Spectrum)
title: How many Types of Pigments are Involved in Photosynthesis?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: What is Light Reaction?
Summary: This section introduces the light reaction (photochemical phase) of photosynthesis, detailing its primary events such as light absorption, water splitting, and the formation of ATP and NADPH. It explains the organization of photosynthetic pigments into Photosystems I and II, which consist of Light Harvesting Complexes (LHC) and specific reaction centres (P700 and P680).
Key terms: Light Reaction, Photochemical phase, Light Harvesting Complexes, Photosystem I, Photosystem II, Reaction Centre, P700, P680, Antennae
Teaches concepts: concept:light_harvesting_complexes (Light Harvesting Complexes), concept:light_reaction (Light Reaction), concept:photosystems (Photosystems), concept:reaction_centre (Reaction Centre)
Requires concepts: concept:chlorophyll_a (Chlorophyll a), concept:thylakoid_membrane (Thylakoid Membrane)
title: What is Light Reaction?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: The Electron Transport
Summary: This section explains the light-dependent electron transport process in photosynthesis, detailing how electrons flow from Photosystem II through an electron transport system to Photosystem I. It describes the reduction of NADP+ to NADPH and outlines the characteristic Z scheme of electron transfer based on redox potential.
Key terms: Photosystem II, Photosystem I, Electron Transport System, Cytochromes, Redox Potential, Z Scheme, NADP+
Teaches concepts: concept:electron_flow_in_photosystem_i (Electron Flow in Photosystem I), concept:electron_flow_in_photosystem_ii (Electron Flow in Photosystem II), concept:electron_transport_system_of_cytochromes (Electron Transport System of Cytochromes), concept:the_z_scheme (The Z Scheme)
Requires concepts: concept:chlorophyll (Chlorophyll), concept:photosystems (Photosystems), concept:redox_potential (Redox Potential)
title: The Electron Transport

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: Splitting of Water
Summary: This section explains the mechanism of water splitting associated with Photosystem II (PS II) during photosynthesis. It details how water is split to provide electrons to PS II, releasing protons and oxygen into the thylakoid lumen.
Key terms: splitting of water, Photosystem II, water splitting complex, thylakoid membrane, lumen
Teaches concepts: concept:splitting_of_water (Splitting of Water), concept:water_splitting_complex (Water Splitting Complex)
Requires concepts: concept:photosystem_ii (Photosystem II), concept:thylakoid_membrane (Thylakoid Membrane)
title: Splitting of Water

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: Cyclic and Non-cyclic Photo-phosphorylation
Summary: This section explains photo-phosphorylation, the light-driven synthesis of ATP in chloroplasts. It distinguishes between non-cyclic photo-phosphorylation, which involves both PS I and PS II to produce ATP and NADPH, and cyclic photo-phosphorylation, which involves only PS I to produce ATP.
Key terms: photo-phosphorylation, non-cyclic photo-phosphorylation, cyclic photo-phosphorylation, stroma lamellae, ATP synthesis
Teaches concepts: concept:cyclic_photo_phosphorylation (Cyclic photo-phosphorylation), concept:non_cyclic_photo_phosphorylation (Non-cyclic photo-phosphorylation), concept:photo_phosphorylation (Photo-phosphorylation), concept:stroma_lamellae (Stroma lamellae)
Requires concepts: concept:adenosine_triphosphate_atp (Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP)), concept:electron_transport_chain (Electron Transport Chain), concept:photosystem_i (Photosystem I), concept:photosystem_ii (Photosystem II)
title: Cyclic and Non-cyclic Photo-phosphorylation

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: Chemiosmotic Hypothesis
Summary: This section explains the chemiosmotic hypothesis, which describes how ATP is synthesized in chloroplasts via a proton gradient across the thylakoid membrane. It details the factors contributing to the gradient—water splitting, proton transport by plastoquinone, and NADP reductase activity—and how ATP synthase utilizes this gradient to produce ATP.
Key terms: chemiosmotic hypothesis, proton gradient, thylakoid membrane, lumen, stroma, water splitting, plastoquinone, NADP reductase, ATP synthase, CF0, CF1
Teaches concepts: concept:atp_synthase_cf0_cf1_complex (ATP Synthase (CF0-CF1 Complex)), concept:chemiosmotic_hypothesis (Chemiosmotic Hypothesis), concept:nadp_reductase (NADP Reductase), concept:plastoquinone_pq (Plastoquinone (PQ)), concept:proton_gradient_thylakoid (Proton Gradient (Thylakoid)), concept:water_splitting_photolysis (Water Splitting (Photolysis))
Requires concepts: concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis), concept:photosystem_i_and_ii (Photosystem I and II), concept:thylakoid_membrane (Thylakoid Membrane)
title: Chemiosmotic Hypothesis

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: Where are the ATP and NADPH Used?
Summary: This section describes the biosynthetic phase of photosynthesis, where ATP and NADPH generated during the light reactions are used to synthesize sugars. It details the discovery, stages, and chemical requirements of the Calvin cycle, as well as the distinction between C3 and C4 carbon fixation pathways.
Key terms: Biosynthetic phase, Calvin cycle, 3-phosphoglyceric acid (PGA), RuBisCO, Ribulose bisphosphate (RuBP), C3 pathway, C4 pathway
Teaches concepts: concept:biosynthetic_phase (Biosynthetic Phase), concept:c3_and_c4_pathways (C3 and C4 Pathways), concept:calvin_cycle (Calvin Cycle), concept:carboxylation (Carboxylation), concept:reduction_and_regeneration_stages (Reduction and Regeneration Stages), concept:rubisco (RuBisCO)
Requires concepts: concept:atp_and_nadph (ATP and NADPH), concept:light_reactions_of_photosynthesis (Light Reactions of Photosynthesis)
title: Where are the ATP and NADPH Used?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: The $C_4$ Pathway
Summary: This section explains the C4 pathway, also known as the Hatch and Slack Pathway, which is an adaptation of plants in dry tropical regions. It describes the specialized Kranz anatomy of C4 leaves and details the cyclic process of carbon dioxide fixation, transport, decarboxylation, and regeneration.
Key terms: C4 pathway, Kranz anatomy, bundle sheath cells, Hatch and Slack Pathway, phosphoenol pyruvate, PEP carboxylase, oxaloacetic acid
Teaches concepts: concept:bundle_sheath_cells (Bundle Sheath Cells), concept:c4_pathway (C4 Pathway), concept:hatch_and_slack_pathway (Hatch and Slack Pathway), concept:kranz_anatomy (Kranz Anatomy), concept:phosphoenol_pyruvate_carboxylation (Phosphoenol Pyruvate Carboxylation)
Requires concepts: concept:calvin_cycle (Calvin Cycle), concept:rubisco (RuBisCO)
title: The $C_4$ Pathway

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:11
Section: Photorespiration
Summary: This section explains photorespiration, a process where the enzyme RuBisCO binds oxygen instead of carbon dioxide in C3 plants, leading to a loss of fixed carbon and energy. It contrasts this with C4 plants, which avoid photorespiration by concentrating carbon dioxide around RuBisCO to maximize productivity.
Key terms: Photorespiration, RuBisCO, C3 plants, C4 plants, Phosphoglycolate, RuBP
Teaches concepts: concept:absence_of_photorespiration_in_c4_plants (Absence of Photorespiration in C4 Plants), concept:dual_nature_of_rubisco (Dual Nature of RuBisCO), concept:photorespiration (Photorespiration)
Requires concepts: concept:c3_pathway (C3 Pathway), concept:c4_pathway (C4 Pathway), concept:calvin_cycle (Calvin Cycle)
title: Photorespiration

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:12
Section: Introduction to Cellular Respiration
Summary: This section introduces cellular respiration as the process of breaking down food materials within cells to release and trap energy as ATP. It contrasts autotrophic and heterotrophic energy acquisition and defines respiratory substrates like carbohydrates, proteins, and fats.
Key terms: Cellular respiration, Respiratory substrates, Adenosine Triphosphate, Autotrophic, Heterotrophic
Teaches concepts: concept:adenosine_triphosphate_atp (Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP)), concept:autotrophic_nutrition (Autotrophic Nutrition), concept:cellular_respiration (Cellular Respiration), concept:heterotrophic_nutrition (Heterotrophic Nutrition), concept:respiratory_substrates (Respiratory Substrates)
Requires concepts: concept:eukaryotic_cell_organelles (Eukaryotic Cell Organelles), concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis)
title: Introduction to Cellular Respiration

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:12
Section: Do Plants Breathe?
Summary: This section explains how plants perform gaseous exchange without specialized respiratory organs, utilizing stomata, lenticels, and loose parenchyma cells. It introduces the chemical equation of respiration, where glucose is catabolized to release energy, and discusses anaerobic respiration and glycolysis as evolutionary adaptations.
Key terms: stomata, lenticels, parenchyma, cellular respiration, glycolysis, anaerobic respiration
Teaches concepts: concept:anaerobic_respiration (Anaerobic Respiration), concept:chemical_equation_of_respiration (Chemical Equation of Respiration), concept:gaseous_exchange_in_plants (Gaseous Exchange in Plants), concept:glycolysis (Glycolysis), concept:plant_respiratory_adaptations (Plant Respiratory Adaptations)
Requires concepts: concept:atp_adenosine_triphosphate (ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate)), concept:diffusion (Diffusion), concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis)
title: Do Plants Breathe?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:12
Section: Glycolysis
Summary: This section explains glycolysis, also known as the EMP pathway, which is the sequence of ten enzyme-catalyzed reactions occurring in the cytoplasm that partially oxidizes glucose into pyruvic acid. It details the steps of ATP consumption, molecular splitting, redox reactions yielding NADH, and ATP generation, concluding with the metabolic fates of pyruvate.
Key terms: Glycolysis, EMP pathway, Pyruvic acid, Hexokinase, Sucrose, Invertase, PGAL, BPGA, PEP, ATP, NADH
Teaches concepts: concept:atp_and_nadh_generation (ATP and NADH Generation), concept:glycolysis (Glycolysis), concept:hexokinase_reaction (Hexokinase Reaction), concept:pyruvic_acid_fate (Pyruvic Acid Fate), concept:triose_phosphate_splitting (Triose Phosphate Splitting)
Requires concepts: concept:atp_adenosine_triphosphate (ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate)), concept:glucose (Glucose), concept:oxidation_reduction_reactions (Oxidation-Reduction Reactions)
title: Glycolysis

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:12
Section: Fermentation
Summary: This section describes fermentation as an anaerobic pathway where glucose undergoes incomplete oxidation to produce either ethanol and carbon dioxide or lactic acid. It details the enzymatic reactions involved in alcoholic and lactic acid fermentation, along with their low energy yields and toxicity limits.
Key terms: Fermentation, Anaerobic respiration, Alcoholic fermentation, Lactic acid fermentation, Lactate dehydrogenase, Alcohol dehydrogenase, NAD+
Teaches concepts: concept:alcoholic_fermentation (Alcoholic Fermentation), concept:energy_yield_of_fermentation (Energy Yield of Fermentation), concept:lactic_acid_fermentation (Lactic Acid Fermentation), concept:reoxidation_of_nadh (Reoxidation of NADH)
Requires concepts: concept:anaerobic_respiration (Anaerobic Respiration), concept:glycolysis (Glycolysis)
title: Fermentation

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:12
Section: Aerobic Respiration
Summary: This section introduces aerobic respiration, detailing its primary stages, cellular locations, and the oxidative decarboxylation of pyruvate. It explains how pyruvate from glycolysis is converted into acetyl CoA within the mitochondrial matrix by the pyruvate dehydrogenase complex, preparing it for the tricarboxylic acid cycle.
Key terms: Aerobic respiration, Mitochondrial matrix, Oxidative decarboxylation, Pyruvate dehydrogenase, Acetyl CoA
Teaches concepts: concept:acetyl_coa (Acetyl CoA), concept:aerobic_respiration (Aerobic respiration), concept:mitochondrial_matrix (Mitochondrial Matrix), concept:oxidative_decarboxylation_of_pyruvate (Oxidative Decarboxylation of Pyruvate), concept:pyruvate_dehydrogenase (Pyruvate Dehydrogenase)
Requires concepts: concept:glycolysis (Glycolysis), concept:mitochondrion_structure (Mitochondrion Structure)
title: Aerobic Respiration

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:12
Section: Tricarboxylic Acid Cycle
Summary: This section details the Tricarboxylic Acid (TCA) cycle, also known as the Krebs' cycle, which occurs in the mitochondrial matrix. It describes the sequential steps of acetyl group condensation with oxaloacetic acid, decarboxylation, substrate-level phosphorylation, and the generation of reduced cofactors.
Key terms: Tricarboxylic Acid Cycle, Krebs' cycle, Oxaloacetic acid, Citric acid, Citrate synthase, Substrate level phosphorylation, Succinyl-CoA, NADH, FADH2
Teaches concepts: concept:citrate_synthase (Citrate Synthase), concept:regeneration_of_oxaloacetic_acid (Regeneration of Oxaloacetic Acid), concept:substrate_level_phosphorylation_in_tca_cycle (Substrate Level Phosphorylation in TCA Cycle), concept:summary_equation_of_the_tca_cycle (Summary Equation of the TCA Cycle), concept:tricarboxylic_acid_cycle (Tricarboxylic Acid Cycle)
Requires concepts: concept:acetyl_coenzyme_a (Acetyl Coenzyme A), concept:mitochondrial_matrix (Mitochondrial Matrix), concept:pyruvic_acid (Pyruvic Acid)
title: Tricarboxylic Acid Cycle

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:12
Section: Electron Transport System (ETS) and Oxidative Phosphorylation
Summary: This section explains the Electron Transport System (ETS) and oxidative phosphorylation in the inner mitochondrial membrane. It details the flow of electrons through Complexes I to IV, the role of mobile carriers like ubiquinone and cytochrome c, and how the resulting proton gradient drives ATP synthesis via Complex V (ATP synthase).
Key terms: Electron Transport System, Oxidative Phosphorylation, Cytochrome c, ATP Synthase, Ubiquinone, Complex IV
Teaches concepts: concept:atp_synthase_complex_v (ATP Synthase (Complex V)), concept:complex_iv_cytochrome_c_oxidase (Complex IV (Cytochrome c Oxidase)), concept:electron_transport_system_ets (Electron Transport System (ETS)), concept:oxidative_phosphorylation (Oxidative Phosphorylation), concept:ubiquinone_and_cytochrome_c (Ubiquinone and Cytochrome c)
Requires concepts: concept:citric_acid_cycle (Citric Acid Cycle), concept:electrochemical_proton_gradient (Electrochemical Proton Gradient)
title: Electron Transport System (ETS) and Oxidative Phosphorylation

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:12
Section: The Respiratory Balance Sheet
Summary: This section outlines the theoretical assumptions and calculations used to determine the net yield of 38 ATP molecules from the aerobic respiration of one glucose molecule. It also contrasts the efficiency, ATP yield, and NADH oxidation rates of aerobic respiration with fermentation.
Key terms: Respiratory Balance Sheet, ATP Yield, Aerobic Respiration, Fermentation, NADH Oxidation
Teaches concepts: concept:assumptions_of_the_respiratory_balance_sheet (Assumptions of the Respiratory Balance Sheet), concept:comparison_of_fermentation_and_aerobic_respiration (Comparison of Fermentation and Aerobic Respiration), concept:net_atp_gain_in_aerobic_respiration (Net ATP Gain in Aerobic Respiration)
Requires concepts: concept:electron_transport_system_ets_and_oxidative_phosphorylation (Electron Transport System (ETS) and Oxidative Phosphorylation), concept:fermentation (Fermentation), concept:glycolysis (Glycolysis), concept:tricarboxylic_acid_tca_cycle (Tricarboxylic Acid (TCA) Cycle)
title: The Respiratory Balance Sheet

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:12
Section: Amphibolic Pathway
Summary: This section explains how various respiratory substrates, including carbohydrates, fats, and proteins, enter the respiratory pathway at different stages. It defines respiration as an amphibolic pathway because it encompasses both catabolic breakdown and anabolic synthesis processes.
Key terms: Amphibolic Pathway, Catabolism, Anabolism, Acetyl CoA, PGAL, Respiratory Substrates
Teaches concepts: concept:amphibolic_pathway (Amphibolic Pathway), concept:anabolism (Anabolism), concept:catabolism (Catabolism), concept:respiratory_substrate_entry_points (Respiratory Substrate Entry Points)
Requires concepts: concept:cellular_respiration (Cellular Respiration), concept:glycolysis (Glycolysis), concept:krebs_cycle (Krebs' Cycle)
title: Amphibolic Pathway

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:12
Section: Respiratory Quotient
Summary: This section defines the Respiratory Quotient (RQ) as the ratio of carbon dioxide evolved to oxygen consumed during respiration. It explains how the RQ value varies depending on the specific respiratory substrate being oxidized, providing values for carbohydrates, fats, and proteins.
Key terms: Respiratory Quotient, Respiratory Substrate, Carbohydrates, Tripalmitin, Proteins
Teaches concepts: concept:respiratory_quotient (Respiratory Quotient), concept:respiratory_quotient_of_carbohydrates (Respiratory Quotient of Carbohydrates), concept:respiratory_quotient_of_fats (Respiratory Quotient of Fats), concept:respiratory_quotient_of_proteins (Respiratory Quotient of Proteins)
Requires concepts: concept:aerobic_respiration (Aerobic respiration), concept:respiratory_substrates (Respiratory Substrates)
title: Respiratory Quotient

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:13
Section: Growth
Summary: This section introduces plant development as the combined outcome of growth and differentiation, beginning with seed germination under favorable conditions. It formally defines growth as an irreversible, permanent increase in the size of an organ, part, or cell, which is driven by energy-consuming metabolic processes.
Key terms: Development, Growth, Differentiation, Seed Germination, Metabolism, Anabolic, Catabolic
Teaches concepts: concept:plant_development (Plant Development), concept:plant_growth (Plant Growth), concept:seed_germination (Seed Germination)
Requires concepts: concept:metabolism (Metabolism), concept:zygote (Zygote)
title: Growth

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:13
Section: Plant Growth Generally is Indeterminate
Summary: This section explains that plant growth is unique and indeterminate due to the lifelong activity of meristems, which continuously add new cells in an open form of growth. It distinguishes between primary growth, which elongates the plant axis via apical meristems, and secondary growth, which increases girth via lateral meristems.
Key terms: indeterminate growth, meristems, open form of growth, primary growth, secondary growth, apical meristem, lateral meristem
Teaches concepts: concept:indeterminate_growth (Indeterminate Growth), concept:open_form_of_growth (Open Form of Growth), concept:primary_growth (Primary Growth), concept:secondary_growth (Secondary Growth)
Requires concepts: concept:dicotyledonous_plants (Dicotyledonous Plants), concept:gymnosperms (Gymnosperms)
title: Plant Growth Generally is Indeterminate

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:13
Section: Growth is Measurable
Summary: This section explains how plant growth is measured at the cellular and organismal levels using various parameters. It highlights metrics such as protoplasm increase, cell number, cell size, length, and surface area, illustrated with specific biological examples.
Key terms: protoplasm, fresh weight, dry weight, cell number, cell size, apical meristem, pollen tube
Teaches concepts: concept:cellular_growth (Cellular Growth), concept:growth_by_cell_number (Growth by Cell Number), concept:growth_by_cell_size (Growth by Cell Size), concept:parameters_of_growth_measurement (Parameters of Growth Measurement)
Requires concepts: concept:apical_meristem (Apical Meristem), concept:plant_growth (Plant Growth)
title: Growth is Measurable

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:13
Section: Phases of Growth
Summary: This section outlines the three distinct phases of plant growth: the meristematic phase, the phase of elongation, and the phase of maturation. It describes the specific cellular characteristics, structural changes, and locations associated with each phase.
Key terms: Meristematic phase, Phase of elongation, Phase of maturation, Vacuolation, Protoplasmic modifications, Cell enlargement
Teaches concepts: concept:meristematic_phase_of_growth (Meristematic Phase of Growth), concept:phase_of_elongation (Phase of Elongation), concept:phase_of_maturation (Phase of Maturation)
Requires concepts: concept:meristematic_tissue (Meristematic Tissue), concept:plant_cell_structure (Plant Cell Structure)
title: Phases of Growth

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:13
Section: Growth Rates
Summary: This section defines growth rate and explains its mathematical representation through arithmetic and geometric growth models. It describes the characteristic sigmoid growth curve of living organisms and distinguishes between absolute and relative growth rates.
Key terms: growth rate, arithmetic growth, geometric growth, sigmoid curve, absolute growth rate, relative growth rate, efficiency index
Teaches concepts: concept:absolute_growth_rate (Absolute Growth Rate), concept:arithmetic_growth (Arithmetic Growth), concept:geometric_growth (Geometric Growth), concept:growth_rate (Growth Rate), concept:relative_growth_rate (Relative Growth Rate), concept:sigmoid_growth_curve (Sigmoid Growth Curve)
Requires concepts: concept:linear_and_exponential_functions (Linear and Exponential Functions), concept:mitosis (Mitosis)
title: Growth Rates

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:13
Section: Conditions for Growth
Summary: This section details the essential conditions and environmental factors required for plant growth. It explains the roles of water, oxygen, and nutrients, alongside environmental signals like temperature, light, and gravity.
Key terms: turgidity, cell enlargement, enzymatic activities, metabolic energy, macro and micro nutrients, optimum temperature
Teaches concepts: concept:environmental_signals_in_plant_growth (Environmental Signals in Plant Growth), concept:nutrients_in_plant_growth (Nutrients in Plant Growth), concept:optimum_temperature_for_growth (Optimum Temperature for Growth), concept:oxygen_in_plant_growth (Oxygen in Plant Growth), concept:water_in_plant_growth (Water in Plant Growth)
Requires concepts: concept:cell_turgidity (Cell Turgidity), concept:plant_growth (Plant Growth)
title: Conditions for Growth

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:13
Section: Differentiation, Dedifferentiation and Redifferentiation
Summary: This section explains the developmental processes of differentiation, dedifferentiation, and redifferentiation in plant cells. It describes how cells mature, regain division capacity, and mature again, highlighting that plant differentiation is an open process determined by cell location.
Key terms: differentiation, dedifferentiation, redifferentiation, tracheary element, open differentiation, interfascicular cambium, cork cambium
Teaches concepts: concept:dedifferentiation (Dedifferentiation), concept:differentiation (Differentiation), concept:open_differentiation (Open Differentiation), concept:redifferentiation (Redifferentiation)
Requires concepts: concept:meristems (Meristems), concept:parenchyma (Parenchyma)
title: Differentiation, Dedifferentiation and Redifferentiation

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:13
Section: Development
Summary: This section defines plant development as the comprehensive sum of growth and differentiation, outlining the cellular sequence from division to senescence. It introduces the concepts of plasticity and heterophylly as structural adaptations to environmental or life-stage changes, and identifies the intrinsic and extrinsic factors that control these processes.
Key terms: Development, Plasticity, Heterophylly, Intrinsic factors, Extrinsic factors, Senescence
Teaches concepts: concept:factors_controlling_plant_development (Factors Controlling Plant Development), concept:heterophylly (Heterophylly), concept:plant_development (Plant Development), concept:plasticity (Plasticity)
Requires concepts: concept:cell_differentiation (Cell Differentiation), concept:plant_growth (Plant Growth)
title: Development

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:13
Section: Characteristics of Plant Growth Regulators
Summary: This section defines Plant Growth Regulators (PGRs), also known as phytohormones, and describes their diverse chemical compositions. It classifies them into plant growth promoters and plant growth inhibitors based on their primary physiological functions.
Key terms: Plant Growth Regulators, Phytohormones, Plant growth promoters, Plant growth inhibitors, Ethylene
Teaches concepts: concept:plant_growth_inhibitors (Plant Growth Inhibitors), concept:plant_growth_promoters (Plant Growth Promoters), concept:plant_growth_regulators (Plant Growth Regulators)
Requires concepts: concept:plant_growth (Plant Growth)
title: Characteristics of Plant Growth Regulators

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:13
Section: The Discovery of Plant Growth Regulators
Summary: This section details the historical and accidental discoveries of the five major groups of Plant Growth Regulators (PGRs). It highlights key experiments and researchers, including the Darwins' work on phototropism leading to auxin discovery, and the identification of gibberellins, cytokinins, abscisic acid, and ethylene.
Key terms: Plant Growth Regulators, Auxin, Gibberellic acid, Kinetin, Abscisic acid, Ethylene, Phototropism
Teaches concepts: concept:abscisic_acid_discovery (Abscisic Acid Discovery), concept:auxin_discovery (Auxin Discovery), concept:cytokinin_discovery (Cytokinin Discovery), concept:ethylene_discovery (Ethylene Discovery), concept:gibberellin_discovery (Gibberellin Discovery)
Requires concepts: concept:callus (Callus), concept:phototropism (Phototropism), concept:plant_growth_regulators (Plant Growth Regulators)
title: The Discovery of Plant Growth Regulators

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:13
Section: Physiological Effects of Plant Growth Regulators
Summary: This section details the physiological effects and agricultural applications of five major classes of plant growth regulators: auxins, gibberellins, cytokinins, ethylene, and abscisic acid. It explains how these hormones individually, synergistically, or antagonistically regulate plant growth, development, and responses to environmental stimuli.
Key terms: Auxins, Gibberellins, Cytokinins, Ethylene, Abscisic Acid, Apical Dominance, Bolting, Respiratory Climactic, Ethephon
Teaches concepts: concept:abscisic_acid (Abscisic Acid), concept:auxins (Auxins), concept:cytokinins (Cytokinins), concept:ethylene (Ethylene), concept:gibberellins (Gibberellins)
Requires concepts: concept:cell_division (Cell Division), concept:plant_growth_regulators (Plant Growth Regulators), concept:senescence_and_abscission (Senescence and Abscission)
title: Physiological Effects of Plant Growth Regulators

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:14
Section: Breathing and Exchange of Gases
Summary: This section introduces the biological processes of breathing and respiration, explaining how organisms exchange atmospheric oxygen for cellular carbon dioxide. It describes how oxygen is utilized in catabolic pathways to break down nutrients and derive energy for life activities.
Key terms: breathing, respiration, catabolic process, oxygen, carbon dioxide
Teaches concepts: concept:breathing (Breathing), concept:catabolic_nutrient_breakdown (Catabolic Nutrient Breakdown)
Requires concepts: concept:glucose (Glucose)
title: Breathing and Exchange of Gases

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:14
Section: Respiratory Organs
Summary: This section introduces the diverse respiratory organs found across the animal kingdom and details the anatomy of the human respiratory system. It describes the structure of the bronchial tree, alveoli, pleural membranes, and the thoracic chamber, while outlining the functional divisions and basic steps of respiration.
Key terms: Respiration, Alveoli, Pleura, Conducting part, Exchange part, Thoracic chamber, Diaphragm
Teaches concepts: concept:alveoli (Alveoli), concept:animal_respiratory_mechanisms (Animal Respiratory Mechanisms), concept:conducting_and_exchange_parts (Conducting and Exchange Parts), concept:human_respiratory_tract (Human Respiratory Tract), concept:pleural_membrane (Pleural Membrane), concept:thoracic_chamber (Thoracic Chamber)
Requires concepts: concept:cellular_respiration (Cellular Respiration), concept:simple_diffusion (Simple Diffusion)
title: Respiratory Organs

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:14
Section: Mechanism of Breathing
Summary: This section explains the physical mechanism of breathing, which consists of inspiration and expiration driven by pressure gradients between the lungs and the atmosphere. It details how the diaphragm and intercostal muscles alter thoracic volume to facilitate air movement, and introduces spirometry for measuring breathing volumes.
Key terms: Inspiration, Expiration, Intra-pulmonary pressure, Diaphragm, Intercostal muscles, Spirometer
Teaches concepts: concept:expiration (Expiration), concept:inspiration (Inspiration), concept:intra_pulmonary_pressure (Intra-pulmonary Pressure), concept:respiratory_muscles (Respiratory Muscles), concept:spirometry (Spirometry)
Requires concepts: concept:atmospheric_pressure (Atmospheric Pressure), concept:pressure_gradient (Pressure Gradient)
title: Mechanism of Breathing

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:14
Section: Respiratory Volumes and Capacities
Summary: This section defines and quantifies the various respiratory volumes and capacities, such as Tidal Volume, Reserve Volumes, and Residual Volume. It explains how these volumes combine to form clinical capacities like Vital Capacity and Total Lung Capacity to assess pulmonary function.
Key terms: Tidal Volume, Inspiratory Reserve Volume, Expiratory Reserve Volume, Residual Volume, Vital Capacity, Total Lung Capacity, Spirometry
Teaches concepts: concept:expiratory_reserve_volume (Expiratory Reserve Volume), concept:inspiratory_reserve_volume (Inspiratory Reserve Volume), concept:pulmonary_capacities (Pulmonary Capacities), concept:residual_volume (Residual Volume), concept:tidal_volume (Tidal Volume)
Requires concepts: concept:mechanics_of_breathing (Mechanics of Breathing)
title: Respiratory Volumes and Capacities

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:14
Section: Exchange of Gases
Summary: This section explains the exchange of oxygen and carbon dioxide between alveoli, blood, and tissues via simple diffusion. It details the role of partial pressure gradients, gas solubility, and the three-layered structure of the thin diffusion membrane.
Key terms: Alveoli, Simple diffusion, Partial pressure, Solubility, Diffusion membrane, Squamous epithelium, Endothelium, Basement substance
Teaches concepts: concept:diffusion_membrane (Diffusion Membrane), concept:gas_exchange_sites_and_mechanism (Gas Exchange Sites and Mechanism), concept:gas_solubility_and_diffusion_rate (Gas Solubility and Diffusion Rate), concept:partial_pressure (Partial Pressure)
Requires concepts: concept:simple_diffusion (Simple Diffusion)
title: Exchange of Gases

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:14
Section: Transport of Gases
Summary: This section outlines the mechanisms of oxygen and carbon dioxide transport in human blood. It specifies the proportions of these gases carried by red blood cells, dissolved in plasma, and as bicarbonate.
Key terms: Oxygen transport, Carbon dioxide transport, Red blood cells, Plasma, Bicarbonate
Teaches concepts: concept:carbon_dioxide_transport (Carbon Dioxide Transport), concept:oxygen_transport (Oxygen Transport)
Requires concepts: concept:blood_plasma (Blood Plasma), concept:red_blood_cells (Red Blood Cells)
title: Transport of Gases

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:14
Section: Transport of Oxygen
Summary: This section explains the transport of oxygen in the blood through its reversible binding with haemoglobin to form oxyhaemoglobin. It details the sigmoid oxygen dissociation curve and explains how physiological factors like partial pressure, pH, and temperature regulate oxygen binding in the alveoli and its release in tissues.
Key terms: haemoglobin, oxyhaemoglobin, oxygen dissociation curve, partial pressure, oxygen delivery
Teaches concepts: concept:oxygen_delivery_to_tissues (Oxygen Delivery to Tissues), concept:oxygen_dissociation_curve (Oxygen Dissociation Curve), concept:oxyhaemoglobin_formation (Oxyhaemoglobin Formation)
Requires concepts: concept:alveoli (Alveoli), concept:partial_pressure (Partial Pressure), concept:red_blood_cells (Red Blood Cells)
title: Transport of Oxygen

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:14
Section: Transport of Carbon dioxide
Summary: This section describes the mechanisms of carbon dioxide transport in the blood, primarily through bicarbonate ions and carbamino-haemoglobin. It explains how partial pressures of carbon dioxide and oxygen regulate these processes, enabling the delivery of carbon dioxide from tissues to the alveoli.
Key terms: carbamino-haemoglobin, carbonic anhydrase, bicarbonate, partial pressure, deoxygenated blood
Teaches concepts: concept:bicarbonate_transport_of_carbon_dioxide (Bicarbonate transport of carbon dioxide), concept:carbamino_haemoglobin (Carbamino-haemoglobin), concept:carbon_dioxide_delivery_efficiency (Carbon dioxide delivery efficiency), concept:carbonic_anhydrase (Carbonic anhydrase)
Requires concepts: concept:alveoli (Alveoli), concept:haemoglobin (Haemoglobin), concept:partial_pressure_of_gases (Partial pressure of gases)
title: Transport of Carbon dioxide

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:14
Section: Regulation of Respiration
Summary: This section describes the neural and chemical mechanisms that regulate the human respiratory rhythm to meet tissue demands. It details the roles of the respiratory rhythm centre in the medulla, the pneumotaxic centre in the pons, and chemosensitive areas and arterial receptors that monitor carbon dioxide and hydrogen ion levels.
Key terms: Respiratory rhythm centre, Pneumotaxic centre, Chemosensitive area, Medulla, Pons, Aortic arch, Carotid artery
Teaches concepts: concept:arterial_chemoreceptors (Arterial Chemoreceptors), concept:chemosensitive_area_of_the_brain (Chemosensitive Area of the Brain), concept:pneumotaxic_centre (Pneumotaxic Centre), concept:respiratory_rhythm_centre (Respiratory Rhythm Centre)
Requires concepts: concept:human_brain_anatomy (Human Brain Anatomy), concept:mechanism_of_breathing (Mechanism of Breathing)
title: Regulation of Respiration

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:14
Section: Disorders of Respiratory System
Summary: This section covers major disorders of the human respiratory system, including asthma, emphysema, and occupational respiratory disorders. It explains their physiological causes, symptoms, and impacts on the respiratory system.
Key terms: Asthma, Emphysema, Occupational Respiratory Disorders, Fibrosis, Bronchi, Bronchioles, Alveoli
Teaches concepts: concept:asthma (Asthma), concept:emphysema (Emphysema), concept:fibrosis (Fibrosis), concept:occupational_respiratory_disorders (Occupational Respiratory Disorders)
Requires concepts: concept:anatomy_of_the_human_respiratory_system (Anatomy of the Human Respiratory System), concept:mechanism_of_gas_exchange (Mechanism of Gas Exchange)
title: Disorders of Respiratory System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:15
Section: Blood
Summary: This section details the composition and functions of blood, focusing on plasma and formed elements such as erythrocytes, leucocytes, and platelets. It also explains the ABO and Rh blood grouping systems, the clinical implications of Rh incompatibility, and the biochemical cascade of blood coagulation.
Key terms: Plasma, Erythrocytes, Leucocytes, Platelets, ABO Grouping, Rh Factor, Erythroblastosis Foetalis, Coagulation
Teaches concepts: concept:abo_blood_grouping (ABO Blood Grouping), concept:blood_coagulation (Blood Coagulation), concept:formed_elements (Formed Elements), concept:plasma (Plasma), concept:rh_incompatibility (Rh Incompatibility)
Requires concepts: concept:antigens_and_antibodies (Antigens and Antibodies), concept:connective_tissue (Connective Tissue)
title: Blood

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:15
Section: Lymph (Tissue Fluid)
Summary: This section describes the formation, composition, and function of tissue fluid (interstitial fluid) and its role in nutrient and gas exchange. It also introduces the lymphatic system, detailing the composition of lymph, its role in immunity, and the absorption of fats through lacteals.
Key terms: tissue fluid, interstitial fluid, lymphatic system, lymph, lymphocytes, lacteals
Teaches concepts: concept:lacteals (Lacteals), concept:lymph (Lymph), concept:lymphatic_system (Lymphatic System), concept:tissue_fluid (Tissue Fluid)
Requires concepts: concept:blood_capillaries (Blood Capillaries), concept:intestinal_villi (Intestinal Villi)
title: Lymph (Tissue Fluid)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:15
Section: Circulatory Pathways
Summary: This section introduces the two primary circulatory patterns, open and closed systems, and details the evolutionary diversity of vertebrate hearts. It explains how heart structure relates to single, incomplete double, and complete double circulation across different animal groups.
Key terms: open circulatory system, closed circulatory system, sinuses, single circulation, incomplete double circulation, double circulation
Teaches concepts: concept:closed_circulatory_system (Closed Circulatory System), concept:double_circulation (Double Circulation), concept:incomplete_double_circulation (Incomplete Double Circulation), concept:open_circulatory_system (Open Circulatory System), concept:single_circulation (Single Circulation)
Requires concepts: concept:heart (Heart), concept:vertebrates (Vertebrates)
title: Circulatory Pathways

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:15
Section: Human Circulatory System
Summary: This section details the anatomical structure, internal chambers, and valvular system of the human heart. It also explains the specialized nodal tissue, specifically the sino-atrial node, which acts as the pacemaker to regulate rhythmic cardiac contractions.
Key terms: heart, pericardium, atria, ventricles, tricuspid valve, bicuspid valve, semilunar valves, nodal tissue, sino-atrial node, pacemaker
Teaches concepts: concept:heart_chambers (Heart Chambers), concept:heart_valves (Heart Valves), concept:nodal_tissue (Nodal Tissue), concept:pericardium (Pericardium), concept:sino_atrial_node (Sino-atrial Node)
Requires concepts: concept:action_potential (Action Potential), concept:cardiac_muscle (Cardiac Muscle)
title: Human Circulatory System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:15
Section: Cardiac Cycle
Summary: This section explains the cardiac cycle, detailing the sequential events of systole and diastole that occur during a single heartbeat. It defines key physiological parameters such as stroke volume and cardiac output, and describes the origin of the clinical heart sounds, 'lub' and 'dub'.
Key terms: Cardiac Cycle, Joint Diastole, Atrial Systole, Ventricular Systole, Stroke Volume, Cardiac Output, Heart Sounds
Teaches concepts: concept:atrial_systole (Atrial Systole), concept:cardiac_cycle (Cardiac Cycle), concept:heart_sounds (Heart Sounds), concept:joint_diastole (Joint Diastole), concept:stroke_volume_and_cardiac_output (Stroke Volume and Cardiac Output), concept:ventricular_systole_and_diastole (Ventricular Systole and Diastole)
Requires concepts: concept:anatomy_of_the_human_heart (Anatomy of the Human Heart), concept:cardiac_conduction_system (Cardiac Conduction System)
title: Cardiac Cycle

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:15
Section: Electrocardiogram (ECG)
Summary: This section introduces the Electrocardiogram (ECG) as a graphical representation of the heart's electrical activity during a cardiac cycle. It explains how a standard ECG is recorded using electrical leads and details the physiological significance of the P-wave, QRS complex, and T-wave.
Key terms: Electrocardiogram, Electro-cardiograph, P-wave, QRS complex, T-wave, Depolarization, Repolarization
Teaches concepts: concept:electrocardiogram_ecg (Electrocardiogram (ECG)), concept:p_wave (P-wave), concept:qrs_complex (QRS complex), concept:t_wave (T-wave)
Requires concepts: concept:cardiac_cycle (Cardiac Cycle), concept:depolarization_and_repolarization (Depolarization and Repolarization)
title: Electrocardiogram (ECG)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:15
Section: Double Circulation
Summary: This section explains the mechanism of double circulation in humans, detailing the three-layered anatomy of blood vessels. It distinguishes between pulmonary and systemic circulation pathways and describes specialized vascular networks like the hepatic portal and coronary systems.
Key terms: Double Circulation, tunica intima, tunica media, tunica externa, pulmonary circulation, systemic circulation, hepatic portal system, coronary system
Teaches concepts: concept:anatomy_of_blood_vessels (Anatomy of Blood Vessels), concept:coronary_circulation (Coronary Circulation), concept:hepatic_portal_system (Hepatic Portal System), concept:pulmonary_circulation (Pulmonary Circulation), concept:systemic_circulation (Systemic Circulation)
Requires concepts: concept:oxygenated_and_deoxygenated_blood (Oxygenated and Deoxygenated Blood), concept:structure_of_the_human_heart (Structure of the Human Heart)
title: Double Circulation

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:15
Section: Regulation of Cardiac Activity
Summary: This section explains the regulation of cardiac activity, highlighting the intrinsic myogenic nature of the heart and its modulation by the autonomic nervous system and adrenal hormones.
Key terms: myogenic heart, medulla oblongata, autonomic nervous system, sympathetic nerves, parasympathetic neural signals, adrenal medulla
Teaches concepts: concept:hormonal_regulation_of_cardiac_output (Hormonal Regulation of Cardiac Output), concept:myogenic_regulation (Myogenic Regulation), concept:neural_regulation_of_cardiac_function (Neural Regulation of Cardiac Function), concept:parasympathetic_regulation_of_cardiac_output (Parasympathetic Regulation of Cardiac Output), concept:sympathetic_regulation_of_cardiac_output (Sympathetic Regulation of Cardiac Output)
Requires concepts: concept:autonomic_nervous_system (Autonomic Nervous System), concept:cardiac_output (Cardiac Output), concept:nodal_tissue_of_the_heart (Nodal Tissue of the Heart)
title: Regulation of Cardiac Activity

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:15
Section: Disorders of Circulatory System
Summary: This section details major disorders of the human circulatory system, including hypertension, coronary artery disease, angina, and heart failure. It outlines their physiological definitions, causes, and effects on the body.
Key terms: Hypertension, Systolic pressure, Diastolic pressure, Coronary Artery Disease, Atherosclerosis, Angina pectoris, Heart failure
Teaches concepts: concept:angina_pectoris (Angina Pectoris), concept:coronary_artery_disease (Coronary Artery Disease), concept:heart_failure (Heart Failure), concept:hypertension (Hypertension)
Requires concepts: concept:blood_pressure (Blood Pressure), concept:human_circulatory_system_anatomy (Human Circulatory System Anatomy)
title: Disorders of Circulatory System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:16
Section: Introduction to Excretion
Summary: This section introduces the major nitrogenous wastes excreted by animals—ammonia, urea, and uric acid—and classifies animals into ammonotelic, ureotelic, and uricotelic based on their mode of excretion. It also provides a comparative survey of various excretory structures, such as protonephridia, nephridia, Malpighian tubules, and antennal glands, across different animal groups.
Key terms: Ammonotelism, Ureotelism, Uricotelism, Protonephridia, Nephridia, Malpighian tubules, Antennal glands, Osmoregulation
Teaches concepts: concept:ammonotelism (Ammonotelism), concept:antennal_glands (Antennal Glands), concept:nephridia_and_malpighian_tubules (Nephridia and Malpighian Tubules), concept:protonephridia (Protonephridia), concept:ureotelism (Ureotelism), concept:uricotelism (Uricotelism)
Requires concepts: concept:metabolism (Metabolism), concept:osmosis (Osmosis)
title: Introduction to Excretion

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:16
Section: Human Excretory System
Summary: This section details the gross and internal anatomy of the human excretory system, focusing on the kidneys. It explains the structure of the nephron as the functional unit, including its vascular and tubular components, and distinguishes between cortical and juxtamedullary nephrons.
Key terms: kidney, nephron, glomerulus, Bowman's capsule, Henle's loop, cortex, medulla
Teaches concepts: concept:cortical_and_juxtamedullary_nephrons (Cortical and Juxtamedullary Nephrons), concept:human_excretory_system (Human Excretory System), concept:internal_anatomy_of_the_kidney (Internal Anatomy of the Kidney), concept:malpighian_body (Malpighian Body), concept:nephron (Nephron), concept:renal_tubule (Renal Tubule)
Requires concepts: concept:circulatory_system (Circulatory System), concept:excretion (Excretion)
title: Human Excretory System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:16
Section: Urine Formation
Summary: Urine formation is a complex process consisting of three main stages: glomerular filtration, tubular reabsorption, and tubular secretion. The kidneys regulate the glomerular filtration rate (GFR) through mechanisms like the juxtaglomerular apparatus, ensuring efficient waste excretion and fluid balance.
Key terms: glomerular filtration, ultrafiltration, glomerular filtration rate, juxtaglomerular apparatus, tubular reabsorption, tubular secretion
Teaches concepts: concept:glomerular_filtration (Glomerular Filtration), concept:glomerular_filtration_rate (Glomerular Filtration Rate), concept:juxta_glomerular_apparatus (Juxta Glomerular Apparatus), concept:tubular_reabsorption (Tubular Reabsorption), concept:tubular_secretion (Tubular Secretion)
Requires concepts: concept:active_and_passive_transport (Active and Passive Transport), concept:structure_of_the_nephron (Structure of the Nephron)
title: Urine Formation

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:16
Section: Function of the Tubules
Summary: This section details the specific physiological roles of different segments of the renal tubule in urine formation. It explains how the proximal convoluted tubule, Henle's loop, distal convoluted tubule, and collecting duct selectively reabsorb water and solutes while secreting ions to maintain homeostasis.
Key terms: Proximal Convoluted Tubule, Henle's Loop, Distal Convoluted Tubule, Collecting Duct, Reabsorption, Secretion, Osmolarity
Teaches concepts: concept:collecting_duct_function (Collecting Duct Function), concept:distal_convoluted_tubule_function (Distal Convoluted Tubule Function), concept:henles_loop_function (Henle's Loop Function), concept:proximal_convoluted_tubule_function (Proximal Convoluted Tubule Function)
Requires concepts: concept:active_and_passive_transport (Active and Passive Transport), concept:osmosis_and_osmolarity (Osmosis and Osmolarity), concept:structure_of_the_nephron (Structure of the Nephron)
title: Function of the Tubules

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:16
Section: Mechanism of Concentration of the Filtrate
Summary: This section explains the counter current mechanism in mammalian kidneys, which is facilitated by Henle's loop and vasa recta. This mechanism maintains an osmolarity gradient in the medullary interstitium, enabling the concentration of urine.
Key terms: counter current mechanism, Henle's loop, vasa recta, medullary interstitium, osmolarity gradient, urea recycling
Teaches concepts: concept:counter_current_mechanism (Counter current mechanism), concept:medullary_interstitial_gradient (Medullary interstitial gradient), concept:urea_recycling (Urea recycling)
Requires concepts: concept:osmosis_and_osmolarity (Osmosis and Osmolarity), concept:structure_of_nephron (Structure of Nephron)
title: Mechanism of Concentration of the Filtrate

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:16
Section: Regulation of Kidney Function
Summary: This section details the hormonal feedback mechanisms that monitor and regulate kidney function. It explains the regulatory roles of Antidiuretic Hormone (ADH) from the hypothalamus, the Renin-Angiotensin mechanism involving the juxtaglomerular apparatus, and Atrial Natriuretic Factor (ANF) from the heart.
Key terms: Antidiuretic Hormone, Vasopressin, Renin, Angiotensin, Aldosterone, Atrial Natriuretic Factor, Osmoreceptors
Teaches concepts: concept:aldosterone (Aldosterone), concept:antidiuretic_hormone_adh (Antidiuretic Hormone (ADH)), concept:atrial_natriuretic_factor_anf (Atrial Natriuretic Factor (ANF)), concept:renin_angiotensin_mechanism (Renin-Angiotensin Mechanism)
Requires concepts: concept:glomerular_filtration_rate_gfr (Glomerular Filtration Rate (GFR)), concept:juxtaglomerular_apparatus_jga (Juxtaglomerular Apparatus (JGA))
title: Regulation of Kidney Function

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:16
Section: Micturition
Summary: This section describes the process of micturition and its underlying neural mechanism, the micturition reflex. It also outlines the physical and chemical characteristics of normal human urine and explains how clinical urine analysis is used to diagnose metabolic disorders.
Key terms: micturition, micturition reflex, urinary bladder, urethral sphincter, urea, glycosuria, ketonuria, diabetes mellitus
Teaches concepts: concept:characteristics_of_urine (Characteristics of Urine), concept:clinical_analysis_of_urine (Clinical Analysis of Urine), concept:micturition (Micturition), concept:micturition_reflex (Micturition Reflex)
Requires concepts: concept:central_nervous_system (Central Nervous System), concept:nephron (Nephron), concept:urinary_bladder (Urinary Bladder)
title: Micturition

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:16
Section: Role of other Organs in Excretion
Summary: This section details the excretory roles of accessory organs other than the kidneys, including the lungs, liver, skin, and salivary glands. It explains how these organs eliminate specific metabolic wastes such as carbon dioxide, bile pigments, urea, and sebum.
Key terms: lungs, liver, sweat glands, sebaceous glands, sebum, bile pigments
Teaches concepts: concept:excretion_by_liver (Excretion by Liver), concept:excretion_by_lungs (Excretion by Lungs), concept:excretion_by_sebaceous_glands (Excretion by Sebaceous Glands), concept:excretion_by_sweat_glands (Excretion by Sweat Glands)
Requires concepts: concept:excretion (Excretion), concept:human_kidney_function (Human Kidney Function)
title: Role of other Organs in Excretion

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:16
Section: Disorders of the Excretory System
Summary: This section outlines various disorders of the human excretory system, including uremia, renal calculi, and glomerulonephritis. It also describes clinical interventions such as hemodialysis and kidney transplantation used to manage renal failure.
Key terms: uremia, hemodialysis, kidney transplantation, renal calculi, glomerulonephritis, artificial kidney
Teaches concepts: concept:glomerulonephritis (Glomerulonephritis), concept:hemodialysis (Hemodialysis), concept:kidney_transplantation (Kidney Transplantation), concept:renal_calculi (Renal Calculi), concept:uremia (Uremia)
Requires concepts: concept:glomerulus (Glomerulus), concept:immune_system_rejection (Immune System Rejection), concept:urea (Urea)
title: Disorders of the Excretory System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:16
Section: Summary
Summary: This section provides a comprehensive summary of excretion and osmoregulation in animals, focusing on the human excretory system. It outlines the structure of the nephron, the three-step process of urine formation, the counter-current mechanism for concentrating filtrate, and the physiological process of micturition.
Key terms: nitrogenous wastes, nephron, glomerular filtration rate, counter current mechanism, micturition, osmoregulation
Teaches concepts: concept:counter_current_mechanism (Counter current mechanism), concept:glomerular_filtration_rate_gfr (Glomerular Filtration Rate (GFR)), concept:micturition (Micturition), concept:nephron_structure (Nephron Structure), concept:nitrogenous_wastes_and_excretory_organs (Nitrogenous Wastes and Excretory Organs), concept:urine_formation (Urine Formation)
Requires concepts: concept:circulatory_system (Circulatory System), concept:osmosis_and_diffusion (Osmosis and Diffusion)
title: Summary

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:17
Section: Types of Movement
Summary: This section introduces the distinction between movement and locomotion in living organisms. It details the four primary types of movement: amoeboid, ciliary, muscular, and flagellar, providing examples of where each occurs in humans and other organisms.
Key terms: Movement, Locomotion, Amoeboid movement, Ciliary movement, Muscular movement, Flagellar movement
Teaches concepts: concept:amoeboid_movement (Amoeboid Movement), concept:ciliary_movement (Ciliary Movement), concept:flagellar_movement (Flagellar Movement), concept:locomotion (Locomotion), concept:muscular_movement (Muscular Movement)
Requires concepts: concept:cytoskeleton (Cytoskeleton), concept:epithelial_tissue (Epithelial Tissue)
title: Types of Movement

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:17
Section: Muscle
Summary: This section introduces muscle tissue, classifying it into skeletal, visceral, and cardiac types based on location, appearance, and regulation. It further details the anatomical structure of skeletal muscle, focusing on the organization of muscle fibers, myofibrils, contractile proteins, and the sarcomere.
Key terms: Skeletal muscle, Visceral muscle, Cardiac muscle, Sarcolemma, Sarcoplasmic reticulum, Myofibril, Actin, Myosin, Sarcomere
Teaches concepts: concept:actin_and_myosin_filaments (Actin and Myosin Filaments), concept:muscle_fibre (Muscle Fibre), concept:muscle_types (Muscle Types), concept:myofibril (Myofibril), concept:sarcomere (Sarcomere)
Requires concepts: concept:connective_tissue (Connective Tissue), concept:endoplasmic_reticulum (Endoplasmic Reticulum)
title: Muscle

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:17
Section: Structure of Contractile Proteins
Summary: This section details the molecular structure of the contractile proteins actin and myosin, which constitute the thin and thick filaments of muscle fibers. It explains the composition of actin filaments, including F-actin, tropomyosin, and troponin, as well as the structure of myosin filaments composed of meromyosin subunits.
Key terms: Actin filament, Myosin filament, F-actin, G-actin, Tropomyosin, Troponin, Meromyosin, Heavy meromyosin, Light meromyosin
Teaches concepts: concept:actin_filament (Actin Filament), concept:meromyosin (Meromyosin), concept:myosin_filament (Myosin Filament), concept:tropomyosin (Tropomyosin), concept:troponin (Troponin)
Requires concepts: concept:adenosine_triphosphate_atp (Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP)), concept:myofibril (Myofibril)
title: Structure of Contractile Proteins

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:17
Section: Mechanism of Muscle Contraction
Summary: This section explains the mechanism of muscle contraction through the sliding filament theory, detailing neural activation, calcium release, and the cross-bridge cycle. It also covers the processes of muscle relaxation, muscle fatigue, and the classification of red and white muscle fibers.
Key terms: Sliding filament theory, Neuromuscular junction, Cross bridge cycle, Muscle fatigue, Red muscle fibres, White muscle fibres
Teaches concepts: concept:cross_bridge_cycle (Cross Bridge Cycle), concept:muscle_fatigue (Muscle Fatigue), concept:neuromuscular_junction (Neuromuscular Junction), concept:red_and_white_muscle_fibres (Red and White Muscle Fibres), concept:sliding_filament_theory (Sliding Filament Theory)
Requires concepts: concept:action_potential (Action Potential), concept:atp_hydrolysis (ATP Hydrolysis), concept:sarcomere_structure (Sarcomere Structure)
title: Mechanism of Muscle Contraction

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:17
Section: Skeletal System
Summary: The human skeletal system consists of 206 bones and a few cartilages, organized into the axial and appendicular skeletons. It provides a protective and supportive framework that enables body movement through articulation with muscles.
Key terms: Axial skeleton, Appendicular skeleton, Vertebral column, Rib cage, Pectoral girdle, Pelvic girdle
Teaches concepts: concept:appendicular_skeleton (Appendicular Skeleton), concept:axial_skeleton (Axial Skeleton), concept:pectoral_girdle (Pectoral Girdle), concept:pelvic_girdle (Pelvic Girdle), concept:rib_cage (Rib Cage), concept:vertebral_column (Vertebral Column)
Requires concepts: concept:connective_tissue (Connective Tissue)
title: Skeletal System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:17
Section: Joints
Summary: This section introduces joints as points of contact between bones or between bones and cartilages that act as fulcrums for movement. It classifies joints into three major structural categories—fibrous, cartilaginous, and synovial—explaining their structural characteristics, degree of mobility, and anatomical examples.
Key terms: Joints, Fibrous joints, Cartilaginous joints, Synovial joints, Sutures, Synovial cavity
Teaches concepts: concept:cartilaginous_joints (Cartilaginous Joints), concept:fibrous_joints (Fibrous Joints), concept:joints (Joints), concept:synovial_joints (Synovial Joints)
Requires concepts: concept:bones (Bones), concept:cartilage (Cartilage)
title: Joints

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:17
Section: Disorders of Muscular and Skeletal System
Summary: This section outlines the common disorders affecting the human muscular and skeletal systems. It details the causes and symptoms of conditions such as myasthenia gravis, muscular dystrophy, tetany, arthritis, osteoporosis, and gout.
Key terms: Myasthenia gravis, Muscular dystrophy, Tetany, Arthritis, Osteoporosis, Gout, Neuromuscular junction, Estrogen, Uric acid
Teaches concepts: concept:arthritis (Arthritis), concept:gout (Gout), concept:muscular_dystrophy (Muscular dystrophy), concept:myasthenia_gravis (Myasthenia gravis), concept:osteoporosis (Osteoporosis), concept:tetany (Tetany)
Requires concepts: concept:calcium_ions_in_muscle_contraction (Calcium ions in muscle contraction), concept:joints (Joints), concept:neuromuscular_junction (Neuromuscular Junction), concept:skeletal_muscle (Skeletal muscle)
title: Disorders of Muscular and Skeletal System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:18
Section: Neural System
Summary: This section introduces coordination and homeostasis, explaining how the neural and endocrine systems jointly integrate organ activities. It also describes the evolutionary complexity of neural organization in animals, ranging from simple networks in Hydra to highly developed systems in vertebrates.
Key terms: Coordination, Homeostasis, Neural System, Endocrine System, Neurons, Neural Organisation
Teaches concepts: concept:coordination (Coordination), concept:endocrine_system (Endocrine System), concept:homeostasis (Homeostasis), concept:neural_organisation (Neural Organisation), concept:neural_system (Neural System), concept:neuron (Neuron)
Requires concepts: concept:organ_system (Organ System), concept:stimulus (Stimulus)
title: Neural System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:18
Section: Human Neural System
Summary: This section outlines the structural and functional divisions of the human neural system, categorizing it into the central neural system (CNS) and the peripheral neural system (PNS). It details the classification of PNS nerve fibres into afferent and efferent types, and further divides the PNS into the somatic, autonomic, and visceral nervous systems.
Key terms: Central Neural System, Peripheral Neural System, Afferent fibres, Efferent fibres, Somatic neural system, Autonomic neural system, Visceral nervous system
Teaches concepts: concept:afferent_and_efferent_nerve_fibres (Afferent and Efferent Nerve Fibres), concept:autonomic_neural_system (Autonomic Neural System), concept:central_neural_system (Central Neural System), concept:peripheral_neural_system (Peripheral Neural System), concept:somatic_neural_system (Somatic Neural System), concept:visceral_nervous_system (Visceral Nervous System)
Requires concepts: concept:involuntary_organs (Involuntary Organs), concept:neural_system (Neural System), concept:skeletal_muscles (Skeletal Muscles)
title: Human Neural System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:18
Section: Neuron as Structural and Functional Unit of Neural System
Summary: This section details the structure of a neuron, highlighting its three main components: the cell body, dendrites, and axon. It classifies neurons into multipolar, bipolar, and unipolar types, and distinguishes between myelinated and unmyelinated nerve fibres.
Key terms: neuron, dendrites, axon, Nissl's granules, synaptic knob, neurotransmitters, multipolar neuron, bipolar neuron, unipolar neuron, myelin sheath, nodes of Ranvier, Schwann cells
Teaches concepts: concept:axon (Axon), concept:dendrites (Dendrites), concept:myelinated_nerve_fibres (Myelinated Nerve Fibres), concept:neuron (Neuron), concept:types_of_neurons (Types of Neurons), concept:unmyelinated_nerve_fibres (Unmyelinated Nerve Fibres)
Requires concepts: concept:cell_structure_and_organelles (Cell Structure and Organelles), concept:neural_system (Neural System)
title: Neuron as Structural and Functional Unit of Neural System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:18
Section: Generation and Conduction of Nerve Impulse
Summary: This section explains the physiological mechanisms underlying the generation, propagation, and restoration of a nerve impulse along an axonal membrane. It details how ionic gradients, active transport, and selective permeability changes create resting and action potentials.
Key terms: polarised state, resting potential, sodium-potassium pump, depolarisation, action potential, repolarisation
Teaches concepts: concept:action_potential (Action Potential), concept:depolarisation (Depolarisation), concept:nerve_impulse_propagation (Nerve Impulse Propagation), concept:repolarisation (Repolarisation), concept:resting_potential (Resting Potential), concept:sodium_potassium_pump (Sodium-Potassium Pump)
Requires concepts: concept:active_transport (Active Transport), concept:neuron_structure (Neuron Structure)
title: Generation and Conduction of Nerve Impulse

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:18
Section: Transmission of Impulses
