Summary: This section describes how nerve impulses are transmitted between neurons at junctions called synapses. It distinguishes between electrical and chemical synapses and details the step-by-step mechanism of chemical synaptic transmission.
Key terms: synapse, pre-synaptic neuron, post-synaptic neuron, synaptic cleft, electrical synapse, chemical synapse, neurotransmitter, synaptic vesicle
Teaches concepts: concept:chemical_synapse (Chemical Synapse), concept:chemical_synaptic_transmission (Chemical Synaptic Transmission), concept:electrical_synapse (Electrical Synapse), concept:synapse (Synapse)
Requires concepts: concept:action_potential (Action Potential), concept:neuron_structure (Neuron Structure)
title: Transmission of Impulses

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:18
Section: Central Neural System
Summary: This section introduces the human brain as the central information processing and command center of the body. It describes the protective cranial meninges and outlines the three primary anatomical divisions: the forebrain, midbrain, and hindbrain.
Key terms: Central Neural System, Brain, Cranial Meninges, Dura Mater, Arachnoid, Pia Mater, Forebrain, Midbrain, Hindbrain
Teaches concepts: concept:brain (Brain), concept:cranial_meninges (Cranial Meninges), concept:forebrain (Forebrain), concept:hindbrain (Hindbrain), concept:midbrain (Midbrain)
Requires concepts: concept:neural_system (Neural System)
title: Central Neural System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:18
Section: Forebrain
Summary: This section details the anatomy and functional regions of the forebrain, which consists of the cerebrum, thalamus, and hypothalamus. It explains the roles of the cerebral cortex, corpus callosum, and the limbic system in regulating sensory, motor, emotional, and autonomic activities.
Key terms: Forebrain, Cerebrum, Cerebral Cortex, Corpus Callosum, Thalamus, Hypothalamus, Limbic System, Association Areas, Grey Matter, White Matter
Teaches concepts: concept:cerebral_cortex (Cerebral Cortex), concept:cerebrum (Cerebrum), concept:corpus_callosum (Corpus Callosum), concept:hypothalamus (Hypothalamus), concept:limbic_system (Limbic System), concept:thalamus (Thalamus)
Requires concepts: concept:hormones (Hormones), concept:neuron (Neuron)
title: Forebrain

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:18
Section: Midbrain
Summary: This section describes the anatomical location, structure, and function of the midbrain. It highlights key features such as the cerebral aqueduct and the corpora quadrigemina, as well as its role in integrating sensory inputs.
Key terms: midbrain, cerebral aqueduct, corpora quadrigemina, forebrain, hindbrain
Teaches concepts: concept:cerebral_aqueduct (Cerebral aqueduct), concept:corpora_quadrigemina (Corpora quadrigemina), concept:midbrain (Midbrain)
Requires concepts: concept:forebrain (Forebrain), concept:hindbrain (Hindbrain)
title: Midbrain

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:18
Section: Hindbrain
Summary: This section details the components and functions of the hindbrain, which consists of the pons, cerebellum, and medulla oblongata. It also defines the brain stem, explaining its composition and its role in connecting the brain to the spinal cord.
Key terms: hindbrain, pons, cerebellum, medulla oblongata, brain stem
Teaches concepts: concept:brain_stem (Brain stem), concept:cerebellum (Cerebellum), concept:hindbrain (Hindbrain), concept:medulla_oblongata (Medulla oblongata), concept:pons (Pons)
Requires concepts: concept:midbrain (Midbrain), concept:spinal_cord (Spinal cord)
title: Hindbrain

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:19
Section: Endocrine Glands and Hormones
Summary: This section introduces chemical coordination, defining endocrine glands as ductless glands and hormones as non-nutrient intercellular messengers. It highlights how the endocrine and neural systems jointly coordinate and regulate physiological functions.
Key terms: Endocrine glands, Hormones, Ductless glands, Intercellular messengers, Chemical coordination, Neural coordination
Teaches concepts: concept:chemical_coordination (Chemical Coordination), concept:endocrine_glands (Endocrine Glands), concept:hormones (Hormones)
Requires concepts: concept:neural_system (Neural System)
title: Endocrine Glands and Hormones

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:19
Section: Human Endocrine System
Summary: This section introduces the human endocrine system, focusing on the anatomical structure and regulatory roles of the hypothalamus and the pituitary gland. It details the various hormones secreted by these glands, their physiological functions, and clinical disorders arising from their imbalance, such as gigantism, acromegaly, and diabetes insipidus.
Key terms: Endocrine system, Hypothalamus, Pituitary gland, Adenohypophysis, Neurohypophysis, Growth hormone, Gonadotrophins, Oxytocin, Vasopressin, Diabetes insipidus
Teaches concepts: concept:diabetes_insipidus (Diabetes Insipidus), concept:growth_hormone_disorders (Growth Hormone Disorders), concept:human_endocrine_system (Human Endocrine System), concept:hypothalamus (Hypothalamus), concept:pituitary_gland (Pituitary Gland), concept:pituitary_hormones (Pituitary Hormones)
Requires concepts: concept:forebrain_anatomy (Forebrain Anatomy), concept:hormones (Hormones)
title: Human Endocrine System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:19
Section: Ovary
Summary: This section explains the structure and endocrine functions of the ovary, the primary female sex organ. It details the synthesis, secretion, and physiological roles of the ovarian steroid hormones, estrogen and progesterone, in regulating female reproduction and secondary sexual characteristics.
Key terms: Ovary, Estrogen, Progesterone, Corpus luteum, Ovarian follicles
Teaches concepts: concept:corpus_luteum (Corpus luteum), concept:estrogen (Estrogen), concept:ovarian_follicles (Ovarian follicles), concept:ovary (Ovary), concept:progesterone (Progesterone)
Requires concepts: concept:menstrual_cycle (Menstrual cycle), concept:ovulation (Ovulation)
title: Ovary

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:19
Section: The Pineal Gland
Summary: This section introduces the pineal gland, detailing its location on the dorsal side of the forebrain and its secretion of the hormone melatonin. It highlights melatonin's essential role in regulating the body's 24-hour diurnal rhythm, sleep-wake cycle, body temperature, and other physiological processes.
Key terms: Pineal gland, Melatonin, Diurnal rhythm, Sleep-wake cycle, Forebrain
Teaches concepts: concept:diurnal_rhythm (Diurnal Rhythm), concept:melatonin (Melatonin), concept:pineal_gland (Pineal Gland)
Requires concepts: concept:forebrain (Forebrain), concept:hormone (Hormone)
title: The Pineal Gland

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:19
Section: Thyroid Gland
Summary: This section covers the anatomy, hormones, and physiological functions of the thyroid gland, including thyroxine (T4), triiodothyronine (T3), and thyrocalcitonin (TCT). It also details the clinical disorders associated with thyroid dysfunction, such as hypothyroidism (goitre, cretinism) and hyperthyroidism (Graves' disease).
Key terms: Thyroid Gland, Thyroxine, Triiodothyronine, Hypothyroidism, Hyperthyroidism, Thyrocalcitonin, Goitre, Graves' Disease
Teaches concepts: concept:hyperthyroidism (Hyperthyroidism), concept:hypothyroidism (Hypothyroidism), concept:thyrocalcitonin_tct (Thyrocalcitonin (TCT)), concept:thyroid_gland_anatomy (Thyroid Gland Anatomy), concept:thyroid_hormones_t3_and_t4 (Thyroid Hormones (T3 and T4))
Requires concepts: concept:hormones (Hormones), concept:metabolism (Metabolism)
title: Thyroid Gland

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:19
Section: Parathyroid Gland
Summary: This section covers the anatomy and function of the parathyroid glands, which secrete parathyroid hormone (PTH). It explains how PTH acts as a hypercalcemic hormone to increase blood calcium levels and regulate calcium balance in the body.
Key terms: Parathyroid gland, Parathyroid hormone, PTH, Hypercalcemic hormone, Calcium balance, Thyrocalcitonin
Teaches concepts: concept:calcium_balance_regulation (Calcium Balance Regulation), concept:hypercalcemic_hormone (Hypercalcemic Hormone), concept:parathyroid_gland (Parathyroid Gland), concept:parathyroid_hormone (Parathyroid Hormone)
Requires concepts: concept:thyrocalcitonin (Thyrocalcitonin), concept:thyroid_gland (Thyroid Gland)
title: Parathyroid Gland

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:19
Section: Thymus
Summary: This section introduces the thymus gland, describing its anatomical location and its role in secreting thymosins. It explains how these hormones regulate cell-mediated and humoral immunity, and details how the degeneration of the thymus in older individuals leads to a weakened immune response.
Key terms: Thymus gland, Thymosins, T-lymphocytes, Cell-mediated immunity, Humoral immunity
Teaches concepts: concept:cell_mediated_immunity (Cell-mediated immunity), concept:humoral_immunity (Humoral immunity), concept:thymosins (Thymosins), concept:thymus_degeneration (Thymus degeneration), concept:thymus_gland (Thymus gland)
Requires concepts: concept:antibodies (Antibodies), concept:immune_system (Immune system), concept:t_lymphocytes (T-lymphocytes)
title: Thymus

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:19
Section: Adrenal Gland
Summary: This section explains the anatomical structure and physiological functions of the adrenal gland, which is divided into the adrenal medulla and adrenal cortex. It details the roles of catecholamines in the fight-or-flight response, and the regulation of metabolism, electrolyte balance, and development by cortical hormones.
Key terms: Adrenal gland, Adrenal medulla, Adrenal cortex, Catecholamines, Glucocorticoids, Mineralocorticoids, Addison's disease
Teaches concepts: concept:addisons_disease (Addison's Disease), concept:adrenal_androgenic_steroids (Adrenal Androgenic Steroids), concept:adrenal_gland_structure (Adrenal Gland Structure), concept:catecholamines (Catecholamines), concept:glucocorticoids (Glucocorticoids), concept:mineralocorticoids (Mineralocorticoids)
Requires concepts: concept:carbohydrate_metabolism (Carbohydrate Metabolism), concept:kidney_structure_and_function (Kidney Structure and Function)
title: Adrenal Gland

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:19
Section: Pancreas
Summary: This section covers the pancreas as a composite gland, focusing on its endocrine function mediated by the Islets of Langerhans. It details the antagonistic roles of glucagon and insulin in maintaining blood glucose homeostasis, and discusses diabetes mellitus as a consequence of prolonged hyperglycemia.
Key terms: Pancreas, Islets of Langerhans, Glucagon, Insulin, Diabetes Mellitus, Glycogenolysis, Gluconeogenesis, Glycogenesis
Teaches concepts: concept:diabetes_mellitus (Diabetes Mellitus), concept:glucagon (Glucagon), concept:insulin (Insulin), concept:islets_of_langerhans (Islets of Langerhans), concept:pancreas (Pancreas)
Requires concepts: concept:adipocytes (Adipocytes), concept:hepatocytes (Hepatocytes)
title: Pancreas

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:19
Section: Testis
Summary: This section describes the structure and dual functions of the testis as both a primary sex organ and an endocrine gland. It details the secretion of androgens, primarily testosterone, by Leydig cells and outlines their diverse physiological roles in male development, spermatogenesis, and metabolism.
Key terms: Testis, Leydig cells, Androgens, Testosterone, Spermatogenesis
Teaches concepts: concept:androgens (Androgens), concept:leydig_cells (Leydig cells), concept:spermatogenesis (Spermatogenesis), concept:testis (Testis)
Requires concepts: concept:endocrine_glands (Endocrine Glands), concept:hormone (Hormone)
title: Testis

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:19
Section: Hormones of Heart, Kidney and Gastrointestinal Tract
Summary: This section details the hormones secreted by organs and tissues other than the primary endocrine glands, including the heart, kidneys, gastrointestinal tract, and non-endocrine tissues. It explains how these hormones, such as atrial natriuretic factor, erythropoietin, gastro-intestinal hormones, and growth factors, regulate blood pressure, red blood cell production, digestion, and tissue repair.
Key terms: Atrial Natriuretic Factor, Erythropoietin, Erythropoiesis, Gastrin, Secretin, Cholecystokinin, Gastric Inhibitory Peptide, Growth Factors
Teaches concepts: concept:atrial_natriuretic_factor (Atrial Natriuretic Factor), concept:erythropoietin (Erythropoietin), concept:gastro_intestinal_hormones (Gastro-intestinal Hormones), concept:growth_factors (Growth Factors)
Requires concepts: concept:erythropoiesis (Erythropoiesis), concept:hormone (Hormone)
title: Hormones of Heart, Kidney and Gastrointestinal Tract

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:19
Section: Mechanism of Hormone Action
Summary: This section explains how hormones regulate physiological functions by binding to specific membrane-bound or intracellular receptors. It classifies hormones based on their chemical nature and details the distinct signaling pathways of water-soluble and lipid-soluble hormones, including the role of second messengers and direct gene regulation.
Key terms: hormone receptors, membrane-bound receptors, intracellular receptors, second messengers, steroid hormones, peptide hormones
Teaches concepts: concept:chemical_classification_of_hormones (Chemical Classification of Hormones), concept:hormone_receptors (Hormone Receptors), concept:intracellular_receptors (Intracellular Receptors), concept:membrane_bound_receptors (Membrane-bound Receptors), concept:second_messengers (Second Messengers)
Requires concepts: concept:cell_membrane (Cell Membrane), concept:gene_expression (Gene Expression), concept:hormones (Hormones)
title: Mechanism of Hormone Action

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:1
Section: The Living World
Summary: This section introduces the vast diversity of life and habitats in the natural world. It sets up the fundamental technical inquiry into what distinguishes living organisms from non-living matter.
Key terms: living, non-living, habitats, biological phenomena
Teaches concepts: concept:biological_diversity (Biological Diversity), concept:definition_of_living (Definition of Living)
Requires concepts:

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:1
Section: Diversity in the Living World
Summary: This section introduces the concept of biodiversity and the critical need for standardizing organism names globally. It details the rules of binomial nomenclature, the classification of organisms into taxa, and the foundational principles of taxonomy and systematics.
Key terms: Biodiversity, Nomenclature, Binomial Nomenclature, Taxa, Taxonomy, Systematics
Teaches concepts: concept:binomial_nomenclature (Binomial Nomenclature), concept:biodiversity (Biodiversity), concept:systematics (Systematics), concept:taxa (Taxa), concept:taxonomy (Taxonomy)
Requires concepts:
title: Diversity in the Living World

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:1
Section: Taxonomic Categories
Summary: This section introduces the concept of taxonomic categories and the taxonomic hierarchy as a multi-step classification process. It defines a taxon as a unit of classification representing a specific rank or category, illustrated by the example of insects.
Key terms: taxonomic categories, taxonomic hierarchy, taxon, classification
Teaches concepts: concept:taxon (Taxon), concept:taxonomic_category (Taxonomic Category), concept:taxonomic_hierarchy (Taxonomic Hierarchy)
Requires concepts: concept:biological_classification (Biological Classification)
title: Taxonomic Categories

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:1
Section: Species
Summary: This section defines a species as a group of individual organisms sharing fundamental morphological similarities. It explains how species are distinguished from each other and how they are grouped under a genus using examples like Panthera, Solanum, and Homo sapiens.
Key terms: Species, Genus, Specific epithet, Morphological similarities, Homo sapiens
Teaches concepts: concept:genus (Genus), concept:species (Species), concept:specific_epithet (Specific Epithet)
Requires concepts: concept:binomial_nomenclature (Binomial Nomenclature), concept:taxonomy (Taxonomy)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:1
Section: Genus
Summary: This section defines the taxonomic category of Genus as a group or aggregate of closely related species sharing common characteristics. It illustrates this concept using examples such as the genus Solanum for potato and brinjal, and Panthera for lions, leopards, and tigers.
Key terms: Genus, Species, Solanum, Panthera, Felis
Teaches concepts: concept:genus (Genus)
Requires concepts: concept:species (Species)
title: Genus

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:1
Section: Family
Summary: This section defines the taxonomic category 'Family' as a group of related genera with fewer similarities than genus and species. It explains how families are characterized using vegetative and reproductive features, providing examples like Solanaceae, Felidae, and Canidae.
Key terms: Family, Solanaceae, Felidae, Canidae, Genus, Taxonomic category
Teaches concepts: concept:canidae (Canidae), concept:family_taxonomy (Family (Taxonomy)), concept:felidae (Felidae), concept:solanaceae (Solanaceae)
Requires concepts: concept:genus (Genus), concept:species (Species), concept:taxonomic_category (Taxonomic Category)
title: Family

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:1
Section: Order
Summary: This section defines the taxonomic category 'Order' as a higher-level grouping that assembles families sharing a few similar characters. It illustrates this category using plant examples like Polymoniales and animal examples like Carnivora.
Key terms: Order, Taxonomic Category, Family, Polymoniales, Carnivora
Teaches concepts: concept:carnivora (Carnivora), concept:order (Order), concept:polymoniales (Polymoniales)
Requires concepts: concept:family (Family), concept:taxonomic_hierarchy (Taxonomic Hierarchy)
title: Order

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:1
Section: Class
Summary: This section defines the taxonomic category 'Class' and explains how it groups related orders. It provides the example of class Mammalia, which includes orders such as Primata and Carnivora.
Key terms: Class, Order, Mammalia, Primata, Carnivora
Teaches concepts: concept:class (Class), concept:mammalia (Mammalia)
Requires concepts: concept:order_taxonomy (Order (Taxonomy)), concept:taxonomic_category (Taxonomic Category)
title: Class

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:1
Section: Phylum
Summary: This section defines the taxonomic categories Phylum and Division, which represent the next higher level of classification above Class for animals and plants respectively. It illustrates this with the phylum Chordata, which groups classes sharing features like a notochord and a dorsal hollow neural system.
Key terms: Phylum, Division, Chordata, Class, Notochord, Dorsal hollow neural system
Teaches concepts: concept:chordata (Chordata), concept:division (Division), concept:phylum (Phylum)
Requires concepts: concept:class_taxonomy (Class (Taxonomy))
title: Phylum

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:1
Section: Kingdom
Summary: This section defines the taxonomic category of Kingdom as the highest level of classification. It introduces Kingdom Animalia for animals and Kingdom Plantae for plants as the primary examples.
Key terms: Kingdom, Kingdom Animalia, Kingdom Plantae, Phylum, Division
Teaches concepts: concept:kingdom (Kingdom), concept:kingdom_animalia (Kingdom Animalia), concept:kingdom_plantae (Kingdom Plantae)
Requires concepts: concept:division (Division), concept:phylum (Phylum), concept:taxonomic_hierarchy (Taxonomic Hierarchy)
title: Kingdom

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:1
Section: Taxonomic Hierarchy
Summary: This section explains the taxonomic hierarchy, which organizes biological categories in an ascending order from species to kingdom. It details how the number of shared characteristics decreases and classification complexity increases as one moves up the hierarchy.
Key terms: Taxonomic Hierarchy, Taxon, Species, Kingdom, Common Characteristics
Teaches concepts: concept:taxonomic_categories (Taxonomic Categories), concept:taxonomic_hierarchy (Taxonomic Hierarchy), concept:taxonomic_trends (Taxonomic Trends)
Requires concepts: concept:biological_classification (Biological Classification), concept:species (Species)
title: Taxonomic Hierarchy

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:2
Section: Biological Classification
Summary: This section traces the historical development of biological classification systems, from Aristotle's early morphological groupings and Linnaeus's Two Kingdom system to R.H. Whittaker's Five Kingdom Classification. It highlights how changing criteria—such as cell structure, body organization, mode of nutrition, and evolutionary relationships—led to more accurate taxonomic groupings.
Key terms: Two Kingdom system, Five Kingdom Classification, Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, Animalia, Phylogenetic relationships, Three-domain system
Teaches concepts: concept:five_kingdom_classification (Five Kingdom Classification), concept:kingdom_fungi (Kingdom Fungi), concept:kingdom_monera (Kingdom Monera), concept:kingdom_protista (Kingdom Protista), concept:phylogenetic_classification (Phylogenetic Classification), concept:two_kingdom_classification (Two Kingdom Classification)
Requires concepts: concept:autotrophic_and_heterotrophic_nutrition (Autotrophic and Heterotrophic Nutrition), concept:prokaryotes_and_eukaryotes (Prokaryotes and Eukaryotes), concept:unicellular_and_multicellular_organisms (Unicellular and Multicellular Organisms)
title: Biological Classification

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:2
Section: Kingdom Monera
Summary: This section introduces Kingdom Monera, which solely consists of bacteria, the most abundant microorganisms on Earth. It details their classification based on shape, metabolic diversity, and divides them into Archaebacteria, Eubacteria, and Heterotrophic bacteria, while also covering Mycoplasma and bacterial reproduction.
Key terms: Kingdom Monera, Archaebacteria, Eubacteria, Cyanobacteria, Heterotrophic bacteria, Mycoplasma, Binary fission, Heterocysts
Teaches concepts: concept:archaebacteria (Archaebacteria), concept:eubacteria (Eubacteria), concept:heterotrophic_bacteria (Heterotrophic Bacteria), concept:kingdom_monera (Kingdom Monera), concept:mycoplasma (Mycoplasma)
Requires concepts: concept:autotrophic_and_heterotrophic_nutrition (Autotrophic and Heterotrophic Nutrition), concept:prokaryotic_cell_structure (Prokaryotic Cell Structure)
title: Kingdom Monera

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:2
Section: Kingdom Protista
Summary: This section introduces Kingdom Protista, which encompasses all single-celled eukaryotic organisms. It details the classification, structural features, and ecological roles of its five major groups: Chrysophytes, Dinoflagellates, Euglenoids, Slime moulds, and Protozoans.
Key terms: Protista, Chrysophytes, Dinoflagellates, Euglenoids, Slime moulds, Protozoans, Diatomaceous earth, Pellicle, Plasmodium
Teaches concepts: concept:chrysophytes (Chrysophytes), concept:dinoflagellates (Dinoflagellates), concept:euglenoids (Euglenoids), concept:kingdom_protista (Kingdom Protista), concept:protozoans (Protozoans), concept:slime_moulds (Slime Moulds)
Requires concepts: concept:autotrophic_and_heterotrophic_nutrition (Autotrophic and Heterotrophic Nutrition), concept:eukaryotic_cells (Eukaryotic Cells)
title: Kingdom Protista

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:2
Section: Kingdom Fungi
Summary: This section covers Kingdom Fungi, detailing their heterotrophic nutrition, filamentous morphology, and cell walls composed of chitin. It explains their reproductive strategies, including the three-step sexual cycle (plasmogamy, karyogamy, and meiosis), and classifies them into Phycomycetes, Ascomycetes, Basidiomycetes, and Deuteromycetes.
Key terms: Hyphae, Mycelium, Coenocytic, Chitin, Plasmogamy, Karyogamy, Dikaryophase, Phycomycetes, Ascomycetes, Basidiomycetes, Deuteromycetes
Teaches concepts: concept:ascomycetes_and_basidiomycetes (Ascomycetes and Basidiomycetes), concept:deuteromycetes (Deuteromycetes), concept:fungal_morphology (Fungal Morphology), concept:fungal_sexual_cycle (Fungal Sexual Cycle), concept:kingdom_fungi_characteristics (Kingdom Fungi Characteristics), concept:phycomycetes (Phycomycetes)
Requires concepts: concept:heterotrophic_nutrition (Heterotrophic Nutrition), concept:meiosis_and_ploidy (Meiosis and Ploidy)
title: Kingdom Fungi

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:2
Section: Kingdom Plantae
Summary: This section introduces Kingdom Plantae, describing its members as eukaryotic, chlorophyll-containing, and primarily autotrophic organisms with cellulose cell walls. It also outlines the plant life cycle, which is characterized by the alternation of generations between diploid sporophytic and haploid gametophytic phases.
Key terms: Kingdom Plantae, chlorophyll, autotrophic, cellulose, alternation of generations, sporophytic phase, gametophytic phase
Teaches concepts: concept:alternation_of_generations (Alternation of Generations), concept:kingdom_plantae (Kingdom Plantae)
Requires concepts: concept:eukaryotic_cell_structure (Eukaryotic Cell Structure), concept:ploidy (Ploidy)
title: Kingdom Plantae

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:2
Section: Kingdom Animalia
Summary: This section outlines the defining characteristics of Kingdom Animalia, which comprises multicellular, heterotrophic, eukaryotic organisms that lack cell walls. It details their holozoic mode of nutrition, internal digestion, growth patterns, and reproductive processes.
Key terms: Kingdom Animalia, heterotrophic, eukaryotic, holozoic nutrition, glycogen, locomotion
Teaches concepts: concept:holozoic_nutrition (Holozoic nutrition), concept:kingdom_animalia (Kingdom Animalia)
Requires concepts: concept:eukaryote (Eukaryote), concept:heterotrophy (Heterotrophy), concept:multicellularity (Multicellularity)
title: Kingdom Animalia

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:2
Section: Viruses, Viroids, Prions and Lichens
Summary: This section covers acellular infectious agents—viruses, viroids, and prions—as well as symbiotic lichens, which are excluded from Whittaker's five-kingdom classification. It details their structural characteristics, historical discoveries, associated diseases, and ecological roles.
Key terms: Viruses, Viroids, Prions, Lichens, Capsid, Bacteriophages, Phycobiont, Mycobiont
Teaches concepts: concept:bacteriophages (Bacteriophages), concept:lichens (Lichens), concept:prions (Prions), concept:viroids (Viroids), concept:viruses (Viruses)
Requires concepts: concept:five_kingdom_classification (Five Kingdom Classification), concept:symbiosis (Symbiosis)
title: Viruses, Viroids, Prions and Lichens

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:3
Section: Plant Kingdom
Summary: This section outlines the evolution of the classification of Kingdom Plantae, highlighting the transition from artificial and natural systems to modern phylogenetic systems. It also introduces modern taxonomic methods, including numerical taxonomy, cytotaxonomy, and chemotaxonomy, which help resolve classification ambiguities.
Key terms: Kingdom Plantae, Artificial Classification, Natural Classification, Phylogenetic Classification, Numerical Taxonomy, Cytotaxonomy, Chemotaxonomy
Teaches concepts: concept:artificial_classification_system (Artificial Classification System), concept:cytotaxonomy (Cytotaxonomy), concept:kingdom_plantae (Kingdom Plantae), concept:natural_classification_system (Natural Classification System), concept:numerical_taxonomy (Numerical Taxonomy), concept:phylogenetic_classification_system (Phylogenetic Classification System)
Requires concepts: concept:five_kingdom_classification (Five Kingdom Classification)
title: Plant Kingdom

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:3
Section: Algae
Summary: This section introduces algae, detailing their general characteristics, habitats, and diverse morphological forms. It explains their vegetative, asexual, and sexual reproductive methods, highlights their ecological and commercial importance, and outlines their classification into three main classes.
Key terms: Algae, Thallus, Zoospores, Isogamous, Anisogamous, Oogamous, Hydrocolloids, Chlorophyceae, Phaeophyceae, Rhodophyceae
Teaches concepts: concept:algae (Algae), concept:algal_reproduction (Algal Reproduction), concept:classification_of_algae (Classification of Algae), concept:economic_importance_of_algae (Economic Importance of Algae), concept:modes_of_sexual_reproduction_in_algae (Modes of Sexual Reproduction in Algae), concept:thallus_organization_in_algae (Thallus Organization in Algae)
Requires concepts: concept:gametes (Gametes), concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis)
title: Algae

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:3
Section: Chlorophyceae
Summary: This section covers Chlorophyceae, commonly known as green algae, detailing their cellular structure, pigmentation, and storage bodies. It also explains their cell wall composition, modes of vegetative, asexual, and sexual reproduction, and provides common examples.
Key terms: Chlorophyceae, green algae, chlorophyll, pyrenoids, zoospores, cellulose, pectose
Teaches concepts: concept:chlorophyceae (Chlorophyceae), concept:chlorophyceae_cell_wall (Chlorophyceae Cell Wall), concept:chlorophyceae_reproduction (Chlorophyceae Reproduction), concept:pyrenoids (Pyrenoids)
Requires concepts: concept:algae (Algae), concept:chloroplasts (Chloroplasts), concept:isogamous_anisogamous_and_oogamous_reproduction (Isogamous, Anisogamous, and Oogamous Reproduction)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:3
Section: Phaeophyceae
Summary: This section covers Phaeophyceae, commonly known as brown algae, detailing their marine habitats, morphology, pigmentation, and structural components like the holdfast, stipe, and frond. It also explains their food storage mechanisms, cell wall composition, and methods of vegetative, asexual, and sexual reproduction.
Key terms: Phaeophyceae, Brown Algae, Fucoxanthin, Laminarin, Mannitol, Algin, Holdfast, Stipe, Frond, Zoospores
Teaches concepts: concept:algin (Algin), concept:fucoxanthin (Fucoxanthin), concept:holdfast_stipe_and_frond (Holdfast, Stipe, and Frond), concept:laminarin_and_mannitol (Laminarin and Mannitol), concept:phaeophyceae_brown_algae (Phaeophyceae (Brown Algae)), concept:reproduction_in_phaeophyceae (Reproduction in Phaeophyceae)
Requires concepts: concept:algae (Algae), concept:isogamy_anisogamy_and_oogamy (Isogamy, Anisogamy, and Oogamy)
title: Phaeophyceae

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:3
Section: Rhodophyceae
Summary: This section introduces the class Rhodophyceae, commonly known as red algae, detailing their morphology, pigmentation, and habitat. It explains their reproductive methods, including vegetative fragmentation, non-motile asexual spores, and oogamous sexual reproduction, alongside key examples.
Key terms: Rhodophyceae, red algae, r-phycoerythrin, floridean starch, oogamous, Polysiphonia, Porphyra, Gracilaria, Gelidium
Teaches concepts: concept:floridean_starch (Floridean starch), concept:oogamy_in_red_algae (Oogamy in Red Algae), concept:r_phycoerythrin (r-phycoerythrin), concept:rhodophyceae (Rhodophyceae)
Requires concepts: concept:algae (Algae), concept:amylopectin (Amylopectin), concept:glycogen (Glycogen), concept:oogamy (Oogamy)
title: Rhodophyceae

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:3
Section: Bryophytes
Summary: This section introduces Bryophytes, known as the amphibians of the plant kingdom, which include liverworts and mosses. It describes their haploid gametophyte body, multicellular reproductive organs, and the dependent diploid sporophyte phase. Additionally, it highlights their ecological significance in plant succession and soil erosion prevention.
Key terms: Bryophytes, Gametophyte, Sporophyte, Antheridium, Archegonium, Sphagnum, Rhizoids, Peat
Teaches concepts: concept:antheridium_and_archegonium (Antheridium and Archegonium), concept:bryophytes (Bryophytes), concept:ecological_and_economic_importance_of_bryophytes (Ecological and Economic Importance of Bryophytes), concept:gametophyte_of_bryophytes (Gametophyte of Bryophytes), concept:sporophyte_of_bryophytes (Sporophyte of Bryophytes)
Requires concepts: concept:haploid_and_diploid (Haploid and Diploid), concept:meiosis (Meiosis)
title: Bryophytes

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:3
Section: Liverworts
Summary: This section describes the habitat, morphology, and reproductive mechanisms of liverworts, a group of bryophytes. It details their thalloid structure, asexual reproduction via gemmae, and sexual reproduction involving a sporophyte differentiated into a foot, seta, and capsule.
Key terms: Liverworts, Thalloid, Marchantia, Gemmae, Gemma cups, Sporophyte, Capsule
Teaches concepts: concept:gemmae (Gemmae), concept:liverworts (Liverworts), concept:sporophyte_of_liverworts (Sporophyte of Liverworts), concept:thalloid_plant_body (Thalloid plant body)
Requires concepts: concept:alternation_of_generations (Alternation of Generations), concept:bryophytes (Bryophytes), concept:meiosis (Meiosis)
title: Liverworts

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:3
Section: Mosses
Summary: This section details the life cycle, structural stages, and reproductive mechanisms of mosses. It explains the two gametophytic phases—the protonema and leafy stages—as well as vegetative and sexual reproduction, including sporophyte structure and spore dispersal.
Key terms: Mosses, Protonema, Leafy stage, Rhizoids, Antheridia, Archegonia, Sporophyte, Spore dispersal
Teaches concepts: concept:leafy_stage (Leafy stage), concept:moss_sporophyte (Moss sporophyte), concept:protonema_stage (Protonema stage), concept:reproduction_in_mosses (Reproduction in mosses)
Requires concepts: concept:gametophyte (Gametophyte), concept:liverworts (Liverworts), concept:sporophyte (Sporophyte)
title: Mosses

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:3
Section: Pteridophytes
Summary: This section introduces Pteridophytes, the first terrestrial plants with vascular tissues, detailing their morphology, habitat, and life cycle. It explains the alternation of generations between the dominant sporophyte and the gametophyte (prothallus), as well as the evolutionary significance of heterospory and the seed habit.
Key terms: Pteridophytes, Vascular tissues, Prothallus, Sporophylls, Heterospory, Seed habit
Teaches concepts: concept:heterospory (Heterospory), concept:prothallus (Prothallus), concept:pteridophytes (Pteridophytes), concept:seed_habit (Seed Habit), concept:sporophyte_pteridophytes (Sporophyte (Pteridophytes))
Requires concepts: concept:alternation_of_generations (Alternation of Generations), concept:bryophytes (Bryophytes), concept:vascular_tissues (Vascular Tissues)
title: Pteridophytes

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:3
Section: Gymnosperms
Summary: This section covers gymnosperms, which are characterized by naked seeds not enclosed by an ovary wall. It details their morphological adaptations, heterosporous reproduction involving male and female strobili, and their unique gametophyte development and fertilization processes.
Key terms: Gymnosperms, naked seeds, mycorrhiza, coralloid roots, heterosporous, strobili, pollen grain, ovule, archegonia, pollen tube
Teaches concepts: concept:gymnosperm_fertilization_and_seed_development (Gymnosperm Fertilization and Seed Development), concept:gymnosperm_gametophytes (Gymnosperm Gametophytes), concept:gymnosperm_root_associations (Gymnosperm Root Associations), concept:gymnosperm_strobili (Gymnosperm Strobili), concept:gymnosperms (Gymnosperms)
Requires concepts: concept:bryophytes (Bryophytes), concept:heterospory (Heterospory), concept:pteridophytes (Pteridophytes)
title: Gymnosperms

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:3
Section: Angiosperms
Summary: This section introduces angiosperms, or flowering plants, which are characterized by having seeds enclosed within fruits and reproductive structures developed in flowers. It also outlines their classification into two major classes, dicotyledons and monocotyledons, based on cotyledon number.
Key terms: Angiosperms, flowering plants, dicotyledons, monocotyledons, cotyledon, flowers, fruits
Teaches concepts: concept:angiosperms (Angiosperms), concept:dicotyledons (Dicotyledons), concept:monocotyledons (Monocotyledons)
Requires concepts: concept:cotyledon (Cotyledon), concept:gymnosperms (Gymnosperms), concept:ovule (Ovule)
title: Angiosperms

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:4
Section: Basis of Classification
Summary: This section outlines the fundamental structural and anatomical features used to classify the animal kingdom. These key criteria include levels of cellular organization, body symmetry, embryonic germ layers, the presence or absence of a coelom, body segmentation, and the presence of a notochord.
Key terms: levels of organisation, body symmetry, diploblastic, triploblastic, coelom, metamerism, notochord
Teaches concepts: concept:body_symmetry (Body Symmetry), concept:coelom (Coelom), concept:diploblastic_and_triploblastic_organisation (Diploblastic and Triploblastic Organisation), concept:levels_of_organisation (Levels of Organisation), concept:metamerism (Metamerism), concept:notochord (Notochord)
Requires concepts: concept:embryonic_development (Embryonic Development), concept:multicellularity (Multicellularity)
title: Basis of Classification

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:4
Section: Classification of Animals
Summary: This section provides an overview of the broad classification of Kingdom Animalia based on fundamental features. It outlines how animals are categorized into different phyla using levels of organization, body symmetry, and the presence or absence of a coelom.
Key terms: Kingdom Animalia, Levels of Organisation, Symmetry, Coelom, Acoelomates, Pseudocoelomates, Coelomates
Teaches concepts: concept:animal_classification (Animal Classification), concept:classification_by_coelom (Classification by Coelom), concept:classification_by_levels_of_organisation (Classification by Levels of Organisation), concept:classification_by_symmetry (Classification by Symmetry)
Requires concepts: concept:animal_symmetry (Animal Symmetry), concept:coelom (Coelom), concept:levels_of_organisation (Levels of Organisation)
title: Classification of Animals

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:4
Section: Phylum - Porifera
Summary: This section introduces Phylum Porifera, commonly known as sponges, detailing their cellular level of organization, asymmetrical body plan, and unique water canal system. It also describes their structural support via spicules or spongin, hermaphroditic reproduction, and key representative examples like Sycon and Spongilla.
Key terms: Porifera, Sponges, Water canal system, Ostia, Spongocoel, Osculum, Choanocytes, Spicules, Spongin, Hermaphrodite
Teaches concepts: concept:choanocytes (Choanocytes), concept:phylum_porifera (Phylum Porifera), concept:porifera_reproduction (Porifera Reproduction), concept:spicules_and_spongin (Spicules and Spongin), concept:water_canal_system (Water Canal System)
Requires concepts: concept:animal_symmetry (Animal Symmetry), concept:levels_of_animal_organisation (Levels of Animal Organisation)
title: Phylum - Porifera

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:4
Section: Phylum - Hemichordata
Summary: This section introduces the phylum Hemichordata, describing its classification, anatomical structure, and physiological systems. It highlights key features such as the stomochord, a tripartite body division, and representative examples like Balanoglossus.

text:
Subject: biology

Section: The Stem
Summary: This section introduces the stem as the ascending axis of a plant, detailing its origin from the embryo's plumule. It defines key structural features such as nodes, internodes, and buds, and outlines the primary functions of the stem, including conduction, support, and storage.
Key terms: Stem, Plumule, Nodes, Internodes, Buds, Photosynthates

text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:5
Section: The Leaf
Summary: This section introduces the leaf as a lateral, flattened vegetative organ borne on the stem. It details the origin of leaves from shoot apical meristems and describes the three main parts of a typical leaf: the leaf base, petiole, and lamina.
Key terms: Leaf, Leaf Base, Petiole, Lamina, Stipules, Pulvinus, Midrib

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:5
Section: Venation
Summary: This section defines venation as the arrangement of veins and veinlets in a leaf's lamina. It distinguishes between reticulate venation, which forms a network in dicotyledonous plants, and parallel venation, where veins run parallel in monocotyledonous plants.
Key terms: venation, reticulate venation, parallel venation, lamina, veins, veinlets
Teaches concepts: concept:parallel_venation (Parallel Venation), concept:reticulate_venation (Reticulate Venation), concept:venation (Venation)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:5
Section: Types of Leaves
Summary: This section classifies leaves into simple and compound types based on the incision of the lamina. It further distinguishes compound leaves into pinnately compound and palmately compound forms, providing structural definitions and examples for each.
Key terms: simple leaf, compound leaf, pinnately compound leaf, palmately compound leaf, rachis, leaflet
Teaches concepts: concept:compound_leaf (Compound Leaf), concept:palmately_compound_leaf (Palmately Compound Leaf), concept:pinnately_compound_leaf (Pinnately Compound Leaf), concept:simple_leaf (Simple Leaf)
Requires concepts: concept:leaf_anatomy (Leaf Anatomy)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:5
Section: Phyllotaxy
Summary: This section defines phyllotaxy as the pattern of leaf arrangement on a stem or branch. It describes and provides examples for the three primary types of phyllotaxy: alternate, opposite, and whorled.
Key terms: Phyllotaxy, Alternate phyllotaxy, Opposite phyllotaxy, Whorled phyllotaxy, Node
Teaches concepts: concept:alternate_phyllotaxy (Alternate Phyllotaxy), concept:opposite_phyllotaxy (Opposite Phyllotaxy), concept:phyllotaxy (Phyllotaxy), concept:whorled_phyllotaxy (Whorled Phyllotaxy)

subject: biology
text:

Summary: This section explains the biological nature of a flower as a modified shoot originating from the floral meristem. It defines inflorescence and details its two primary classifications, racemose and cymose, based on the growth pattern of the floral axis.

text:
