Key terms: Epidermal tissue system, Ground tissue system, Vascular tissue system, Stomatal apparatus, Cambium, Vascular bundles
Teaches concepts: concept:epidermal_tissue_system (Epidermal Tissue System), concept:ground_tissue_system (Ground Tissue System), concept:open_and_closed_vascular_bundles (Open and Closed Vascular Bundles), concept:radial_and_conjoint_vascular_arrangements (Radial and Conjoint Vascular Arrangements), concept:stomatal_apparatus (Stomatal Apparatus), concept:vascular_tissue_system (Vascular Tissue System)
Requires concepts: concept:monocots_and_dicots (Monocots and Dicots), concept:plant_cells_and_tissues (Plant Cells and Tissues), concept:xylem_and_phloem (Xylem and Phloem)
title: The Tissue System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:6
Section: Anatomy of Dicotyledonous and Monocotyledonous Plants
Summary: This section introduces the comparative anatomy of dicotyledonous and monocotyledonous plants, focusing on the internal tissue organization of the dicotyledonous root. It details the structural layers from the outermost epiblema to the innermost stele, including the cortex, endodermis, pericycle, and vascular tissues.
Key terms: Epiblema, Cortex, Endodermis, Casparian strips, Pericycle, Conjunctive tissue, Stele, Dicotyledonous root
Teaches concepts: concept:conjunctive_tissue (Conjunctive Tissue), concept:endodermis (Endodermis), concept:epiblema (Epiblema), concept:pericycle (Pericycle), concept:stele (Stele)
Requires concepts: concept:dicotyledonous_plants (Dicotyledonous Plants), concept:parenchyma (Parenchyma), concept:xylem_and_phloem (Xylem and Phloem)
title: Anatomy of Dicotyledonous and Monocotyledonous Plants

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:6
Section: Monocotyledonous Root
Summary: This section details the anatomical structure of the monocotyledonous root, comparing it to the dicotyledonous root. It highlights key features such as a polyarch xylem arrangement, a large and well-developed pith, and the absence of secondary growth.
Key terms: Monocotyledonous root, Polyarch, Pith, Secondary growth, Vascular bundles
Teaches concepts: concept:monocotyledonous_root_anatomy (Monocotyledonous Root Anatomy), concept:polyarch_xylem (Polyarch Xylem)
Requires concepts: concept:dicotyledonous_root_anatomy (Dicotyledonous Root Anatomy), concept:plant_tissue_systems (Plant Tissue Systems), concept:secondary_growth (Secondary Growth)
title: Monocotyledonous Root

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:6
Section: Dicotyledonous Stem
Summary: This section details the anatomical structure of a typical young dicotyledonous stem as observed in a transverse section. It describes the arrangement of various tissue layers from the outermost epidermis down to the central pith. Key features discussed include the collenchymatous hypodermis, the starch-rich endodermis, medullary rays, and the characteristic ring arrangement of conjoint, open vascular bundles.
Key terms: Dicotyledonous stem, Hypodermis, Starch sheath, Medullary rays, Vascular bundles, Pith, Collenchyma, Sclerenchyma
Teaches concepts: concept:dicotyledonous_stem_anatomy (Dicotyledonous Stem Anatomy), concept:hypodermis (Hypodermis), concept:medullary_rays (Medullary Rays), concept:pith (Pith), concept:ring_arrangement_of_vascular_bundles (Ring Arrangement of Vascular Bundles), concept:starch_sheath (Starch Sheath)
Requires concepts: concept:collenchyma (Collenchyma), concept:epidermis (Epidermis), concept:sclerenchyma (Sclerenchyma), concept:vascular_bundle (Vascular Bundle)
title: Dicotyledonous Stem

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:6
Section: Monocotyledonous Stem
Summary: This section details the anatomical structure of the monocotyledonous stem. It highlights key features such as a sclerenchymatous hypodermis, scattered conjoint and closed vascular bundles, and the absence of phloem parenchyma.
Key terms: Monocotyledonous stem, Sclerenchymatous hypodermis, Vascular bundles, Bundle sheath, Ground tissue, Conjoint and closed, Phloem parenchyma
Teaches concepts: concept:monocot_vascular_bundles (Monocot Vascular Bundles), concept:monocotyledonous_stem_anatomy (Monocotyledonous Stem Anatomy)
Requires concepts: concept:conjoint_and_closed_vascular_bundles (Conjoint and Closed Vascular Bundles), concept:parenchyma (Parenchyma), concept:sclerenchyma (Sclerenchyma)
title: Monocotyledonous Stem

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:6
Section: Dorsiventral (Dicotyledonous) Leaf
Summary: This section details the anatomical structure of a dorsiventral (dicotyledonous) leaf, which consists of three main parts: the epidermis, mesophyll, and vascular system. It explains the differentiation of the mesophyll into palisade and spongy parenchyma and describes the arrangement of vascular bundles surrounded by bundle sheath cells.
Key terms: Dorsiventral leaf, Adaxial epidermis, Abaxial epidermis, Mesophyll, Palisade parenchyma, Spongy parenchyma, Bundle sheath cells
Teaches concepts: concept:adaxial_and_abaxial_epidermis (Adaxial and Abaxial Epidermis), concept:bundle_sheath_cells (Bundle Sheath Cells), concept:dorsiventral_leaf (Dorsiventral Leaf), concept:mesophyll (Mesophyll), concept:palisade_parenchyma (Palisade Parenchyma), concept:spongy_parenchyma (Spongy Parenchyma)
Requires concepts: concept:parenchyma (Parenchyma), concept:reticulate_venation (Reticulate Venation), concept:stomata (Stomata), concept:vascular_bundle (Vascular Bundle)
title: Dorsiventral (Dicotyledonous) Leaf

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:6
Section: Isobilateral (Monocotyledonous) Leaf
Summary: This section describes the anatomical structure of the isobilateral (monocotyledonous) leaf, highlighting its key differences from the dorsiventral leaf. It explains the uniform mesophyll, amphistomatic stomata distribution, and the function of specialized bulliform cells in water conservation.
Key terms: Isobilateral leaf, Monocotyledonous leaf, Amphistomatic, Mesophyll, Bulliform cells, Parallel venation
Teaches concepts: concept:bulliform_cells (Bulliform Cells), concept:isobilateral_leaf_anatomy (Isobilateral Leaf Anatomy), concept:monocot_leaf_venation (Monocot Leaf Venation)
Requires concepts: concept:dorsiventral_leaf_anatomy (Dorsiventral Leaf Anatomy), concept:stomata (Stomata), concept:transpiration (Transpiration)
title: Isobilateral (Monocotyledonous) Leaf

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:7
Section: Organ and Organ System
Summary: This section introduces the hierarchical organization of multicellular organisms, defining tissues, organs, and organ systems. It explains how these levels of organization exhibit a division of labor and distinguishes between the biological studies of morphology and anatomy.
Key terms: tissue, organ, organ system, division of labour, morphology, anatomy
Teaches concepts: concept:anatomy (Anatomy), concept:division_of_labour (Division of Labour), concept:morphology (Morphology), concept:organ (Organ), concept:organ_system (Organ System), concept:tissue (Tissue)
Requires concepts: concept:cell (Cell), concept:multicellular_organisms (Multicellular Organisms)
title: Organ and Organ System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:7
Section: Frogs
Summary: This section provides a comprehensive overview of the morphology, anatomy, and ecological significance of frogs, focusing on the Indian species Rana tigrina. It details their physiological adaptations for survival, such as aestivation and hibernation, and describes their major organ systems, including digestive, respiratory, vascular, excretory, nervous, and reproductive systems.
Key terms: Rana tigrina, poikilotherms, aestivation, hibernation, cutaneous respiration, portal system, Bidder's canal, tadpole, metamorphosis
Teaches concepts: concept:frog_digestive_system (Frog Digestive System), concept:frog_excretory_system (Frog Excretory System), concept:frog_morphology_and_adaptations (Frog Morphology and Adaptations), concept:frog_reproductive_system_and_development (Frog Reproductive System and Development), concept:frog_respiratory_system (Frog Respiratory System), concept:frog_vascular_system (Frog Vascular System)
Requires concepts: concept:class_amphibia (Class Amphibia), concept:phylum_chordata (Phylum Chordata)
title: Frogs

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: What is a Cell?
Summary: This section introduces the cell as the fundamental structural and functional unit of all living organisms, distinguishing between unicellular and multicellular life. It also outlines key historical milestones in cell discovery, including the first observation of a live cell and the discovery of the nucleus.
Key terms: cell, unicellular, multicellular, nucleus, microscope, Antonie Von Leeuwenhoek, Robert Brown
Teaches concepts: concept:cell (Cell), concept:cell_discovery_milestones (Cell Discovery Milestones), concept:multicellular_organisms (Multicellular Organisms), concept:nucleus (Nucleus), concept:unicellular_organisms (Unicellular Organisms)
Requires concepts:
title: What is a Cell?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Cell Theory
Summary: This section details the formulation of the Cell Theory by Matthias Schleiden and Theodore Schwann, and its subsequent modification by Rudolf Virchow. It explains the core tenets of modern cell theory, including the principle that all cells arise from pre-existing cells.
Key terms: Cell Theory, Matthias Schleiden, Theodore Schwann, Rudolf Virchow, Omnis cellula-e cellula, Plasma membrane, Cell wall
Teaches concepts: concept:cell_theory (Cell Theory), concept:omnis_cellula_e_cellula (Omnis cellula-e cellula)
Requires concepts: concept:cell (Cell)
title: Cell Theory

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: An Overview of Cell
Summary: This section provides a comprehensive overview of cell structure, distinguishing between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells based on the presence of a membrane-bound nucleus. It also details the various membrane-bound and non-membrane-bound organelles, alongside the significant variations in cell size and shape.
Key terms: Eukaryotic, Prokaryotic, Cytoplasm, Organelles, Ribosomes, Centrosome
Teaches concepts: concept:cellular_diversity (Cellular Diversity), concept:centrosome (Centrosome), concept:cytoplasm (Cytoplasm), concept:eukaryotic_cells (Eukaryotic Cells), concept:prokaryotic_cells (Prokaryotic Cells), concept:ribosomes (Ribosomes)
Requires concepts: concept:cell_theory (Cell Theory)
title: An Overview of Cell

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Prokaryotic Cells
Summary: This section introduces prokaryotic cells, detailing their representative organisms, rapid multiplication, and diverse bacterial shapes. It also describes their internal organization, highlighting the lack of a nuclear membrane, the presence of genomic DNA and plasmids, and specialized structures like mesosomes.
Key terms: Prokaryotic cells, Mycoplasma, PPLO, Bacillus, Coccus, Vibrio, Spirillum, Plasmid, Mesosome, Genomic DNA
Teaches concepts: concept:bacterial_shapes (Bacterial Shapes), concept:mesosomes (Mesosomes), concept:plasmids (Plasmids), concept:prokaryotic_cells (Prokaryotic Cells), concept:prokaryotic_genetic_material (Prokaryotic Genetic Material)
Requires concepts: concept:dna (DNA), concept:eukaryotic_cells (Eukaryotic Cells)
title: Prokaryotic Cells

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Cell Envelope and its Modifications
Summary: This section describes the structure and function of the prokaryotic cell envelope, which consists of the glycocalyx, cell wall, and plasma membrane. It explains bacterial classification via Gram staining and details various cellular modifications and surface structures, including mesosomes, chromatophores, flagella, pili, and fimbriae.
Key terms: cell envelope, glycocalyx, Gram stain, mesosome, chromatophores, flagella, pili, fimbriae
Teaches concepts: concept:bacterial_flagella (Bacterial Flagella), concept:cell_envelope (Cell Envelope), concept:chromatophores (Chromatophores), concept:gram_staining (Gram Staining), concept:mesosomes (Mesosomes), concept:pili_and_fimbriae (Pili and Fimbriae)
Requires concepts: concept:prokaryotic_cells (Prokaryotic Cells)
title: Cell Envelope and its Modifications

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Ribosomes and Inclusion Bodies
Summary: This section details the structure and function of prokaryotic ribosomes, which form 70S complexes and translate mRNA into proteins as polysomes. It also describes inclusion bodies, which are non-membrane-bound structures used for storing reserve materials in prokaryotic cytoplasm.
Key terms: 70S Ribosomes, Polysome, Inclusion Bodies, Gas Vacuoles, Protein Synthesis
Teaches concepts: concept:gas_vacuoles (Gas Vacuoles), concept:inclusion_bodies (Inclusion Bodies), concept:polysome (Polysome), concept:prokaryotic_ribosomes (Prokaryotic Ribosomes)
Requires concepts: concept:mrna (mRNA), concept:prokaryotic_cells (Prokaryotic Cells)
title: Ribosomes and Inclusion Bodies

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Eukaryotic Cells
Summary: This section introduces eukaryotic cells, highlighting their extensive cytoplasmic compartmentalisation, membrane-bound organelles, and organised nucleus. It also details the structural differences between plant and animal cells, such as the presence of cell walls and plastids in plants, and centrioles in animals.
Key terms: Eukaryotic Cells, Compartmentalisation, Organelles, Plant Cells, Animal Cells, Centrioles
Teaches concepts: concept:animal_cells (Animal Cells), concept:eukaryotic_cells (Eukaryotic Cells), concept:plant_cells (Plant Cells)
Requires concepts: concept:cell (Cell)
title: Eukaryotic Cells

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Cell Membrane
Summary: This section explains the structure, chemical composition, and function of the cell membrane, focusing on the lipid bilayer and membrane proteins. It details the Fluid Mosaic Model and describes the mechanisms of passive transport, osmosis, and active transport across the membrane.
Key terms: lipid bilayer, fluid mosaic model, integral proteins, peripheral proteins, passive transport, active transport, osmosis
Teaches concepts: concept:active_transport (Active Transport), concept:fluid_mosaic_model (Fluid Mosaic Model), concept:lipid_bilayer_structure (Lipid Bilayer Structure), concept:membrane_proteins (Membrane Proteins), concept:passive_transport_and_osmosis (Passive Transport and Osmosis)
Requires concepts: concept:atp_adenosine_triphosphate (ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate)), concept:concentration_gradient (Concentration Gradient), concept:lipids_and_proteins (Lipids and Proteins)
title: Cell Membrane

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Nucleus
Summary: This section details the structure and function of the nucleus, including the nuclear envelope, nucleoplasm, nucleolus, and chromatin. It also explains the organization of chromosomes during cell division, their morphological features like kinetochores, and their classification based on centromere position.
Key terms: Nucleus, Chromatin, Nucleolus, Nuclear envelope, Nuclear pores, Centromere, Kinetochore, Metacentric, Sub-metacentric, Acrocentric, Telocentric, Satellite
Teaches concepts: concept:centromere (Centromere), concept:chromatin (Chromatin), concept:chromosome (Chromosome), concept:nucleolus (Nucleolus), concept:nucleus (Nucleus)
Requires concepts: concept:dna (DNA), concept:endoplasmic_reticulum (Endoplasmic Reticulum), concept:eukaryotic_cells (Eukaryotic Cells)
title: Nucleus

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Microbodies
Summary: This section introduces microbodies as membrane-bound, minute vesicles containing various enzymes. These organelles are present in both plant and animal cells.
Key terms: Microbodies, Vesicles, Enzymes, Plant cells, Animal cells
Teaches concepts: concept:microbodies (Microbodies)
Requires concepts: concept:eukaryotic_cells (Eukaryotic Cells)
title: Microbodies

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Cell Wall
Summary: This section details the structure, function, and composition of the cell wall in plants, fungi, and algae. It explains the distinction between primary and secondary cell walls, and describes how the middle lamella and plasmodesmata facilitate cell adhesion and communication.
Key terms: Cell wall, Primary wall, Secondary wall, Middle lamella, Plasmodesmata, Calcium pectate, Cellulose
Teaches concepts: concept:cell_wall (Cell Wall), concept:middle_lamella (Middle Lamella), concept:plasmodesmata (Plasmodesmata), concept:primary_wall (Primary Wall), concept:secondary_wall (Secondary Wall)
Requires concepts: concept:cytoplasm (Cytoplasm), concept:plasma_membrane (Plasma Membrane)
title: Cell Wall

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Endomembrane System
Summary: This section details the endomembrane system, which consists of functionally coordinated organelles including the endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus, lysosomes, and vacuoles. It explains their structures, polarities, and specialized roles in synthesis, modification, transport, and degradation within the cell.
Key terms: Endomembrane system, Endoplasmic reticulum, Rough ER, Smooth ER, Golgi apparatus, Cisternae, Lysosomes, Vacuoles, Tonoplast
Teaches concepts: concept:endomembrane_system (Endomembrane System), concept:endoplasmic_reticulum (Endoplasmic Reticulum), concept:golgi_apparatus (Golgi Apparatus), concept:lysosomes (Lysosomes), concept:vacuoles (Vacuoles)
Requires concepts: concept:cytoplasm (Cytoplasm), concept:eukaryotic_cells (Eukaryotic Cells), concept:ribosomes (Ribosomes)
title: Endomembrane System

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Mitochondria
Summary: This section details the morphology, structure, and functions of mitochondria, which act as the primary sites for aerobic respiration. It explains their double-membrane structure, the role of cristae in increasing surface area, and their semi-autonomous nature due to having their own DNA and ribosomes.
Key terms: Mitochondria, Cristae, Matrix, Aerobic respiration, ATP, Fission, 70S ribosomes
Teaches concepts: concept:aerobic_respiration (Aerobic respiration), concept:cristae (Cristae), concept:mitochondria (Mitochondria), concept:mitochondrial_autonomy (Mitochondrial Autonomy), concept:mitochondrial_matrix (Mitochondrial Matrix)
Requires concepts: concept:adenosine_triphosphate_atp (Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP)), concept:eukaryotic_cells (Eukaryotic Cells)
title: Mitochondria

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Plastids
Summary: This section covers plastids, which are large organelles found in plant cells and euglenoids, classified into chloroplasts, chromoplasts, and leucoplasts. It details the storage functions of different leucoplasts and provides an in-depth look at the morphology and internal double-membrane structure of chloroplasts.
Key terms: Plastids, Chloroplasts, Chromoplasts, Leucoplasts, Thylakoids, Stroma, Grana
Teaches concepts: concept:chloroplasts (Chloroplasts), concept:leucoplasts (Leucoplasts), concept:plastids (Plastids), concept:stroma (Stroma), concept:thylakoids (Thylakoids)
Requires concepts: concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis), concept:plant_cells (Plant Cells), concept:ribosomes (Ribosomes)
title: Plastids

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Ribosomes
Summary: This section covers the discovery, composition, and subunit structure of ribosomes. It explains the differences between eukaryotic (80S) and prokaryotic (70S) ribosomes and defines Svedberg's Unit.
Key terms: Ribosomes, George Palade, Svedberg's Unit, Sedimentation coefficient, Eukaryotic ribosomes, Prokaryotic ribosomes
Teaches concepts: concept:eukaryotic_ribosomes (Eukaryotic Ribosomes), concept:prokaryotic_ribosomes (Prokaryotic Ribosomes), concept:ribosomes (Ribosomes), concept:svedbergs_unit (Svedberg's Unit)
Requires concepts: concept:eukaryotic_cells (Eukaryotic Cells), concept:prokaryotic_cells (Prokaryotic Cells), concept:ribonucleic_acid_rna (Ribonucleic Acid (RNA))
title: Ribosomes

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Cytoskeleton
Summary: The cytoskeleton is an extensive network of proteinaceous filaments located in the cytoplasm, comprising microtubules, microfilaments, and intermediate filaments. It functions to provide mechanical support, facilitate motility, and maintain the structural shape of the cell.
Key terms: Cytoskeleton, Microtubules, Microfilaments, Intermediate filaments, Cytoplasm
Teaches concepts: concept:cytoskeleton (Cytoskeleton), concept:intermediate_filaments (Intermediate filaments), concept:microfilaments (Microfilaments), concept:microtubules (Microtubules)
Requires concepts: concept:cytoplasm (Cytoplasm), concept:proteins (Proteins)
title: Cytoskeleton

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Cilia and Flagella
Summary: This section explains the structure and function of cilia and flagella as hair-like outgrowths of the cell membrane. It details their internal axoneme structure, characterized by the 9+2 microtubule arrangement, and notes their origin from basal bodies.
Key terms: Cilia, Flagella, Axoneme, 9+2 pattern, Basal bodies, Microtubules
Teaches concepts: concept:9_2_arrangement (9+2 Arrangement), concept:axoneme (Axoneme), concept:basal_bodies (Basal Bodies), concept:cilia (Cilia), concept:flagella (Flagella)
Requires concepts: concept:eukaryotic_cells (Eukaryotic Cells), concept:microtubules (Microtubules), concept:plasma_membrane (Plasma Membrane)
title: Cilia and Flagella

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:8
Section: Centrosome and Centrioles
Summary: This section describes the structure and function of the centrosome and its constituent centrioles. It details their cartwheel-like organization, consisting of tubulin triplets arranged in a ring around a central hub, and explains their role in forming basal bodies and spindle fibers.
Key terms: Centrosome, Centriole, Pericentriolar material, Tubulin, Hub, Radial spokes, Basal body, Spindle apparatus
Teaches concepts: concept:basal_body (Basal Body), concept:cartwheel_organization_of_centrioles (Cartwheel Organization of Centrioles), concept:centriole (Centriole), concept:centrosome (Centrosome), concept:spindle_apparatus (Spindle Apparatus)
Requires concepts: concept:cell_division (Cell Division)
title: Centrosome and Centrioles

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: How to Analyse Chemical Composition?
Summary: This section describes the analytical methods used to determine the chemical composition of living tissues, distinguishing between organic biomolecules and inorganic constituents. It introduces the structural characteristics and classifications of major small molecular weight biomolecules, including amino acids, lipids, nitrogenous bases, nucleosides, and nucleotides.
Key terms: biomolecules, acid-soluble pool, acid-insoluble fraction, zwitterion, phospholipids, nucleotides
Teaches concepts: concept:amino_acids (Amino Acids), concept:ash_method (Ash Method), concept:chemical_analysis_of_biomolecules (Chemical Analysis of Biomolecules), concept:lipids (Lipids), concept:nucleosides_and_nucleotides (Nucleosides and Nucleotides), concept:zwitterion (Zwitterion)
Requires concepts: concept:acids_bases_and_ph (Acids, Bases, and pH), concept:organic_chemistry_functional_groups (Organic Chemistry Functional Groups)
title: How to Analyse Chemical Composition?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: Primary and Secondary Metabolites
Summary: This section defines and distinguishes between primary and secondary metabolites in living organisms. Primary metabolites perform known physiological functions, whereas secondary metabolites have less understood roles in host organisms but possess significant ecological and human utility.
Key terms: Metabolites, Primary metabolites, Secondary metabolites, Alkaloids, Lectins, Toxins
Teaches concepts: concept:metabolites (Metabolites), concept:primary_metabolites (Primary Metabolites), concept:secondary_metabolites (Secondary Metabolites)
Requires concepts: concept:biomolecules (Biomolecules)
title: Primary and Secondary Metabolites

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: Biomacromolecules
Summary: This section classifies cellular chemical compounds into micromolecules and biomacromolecules based on molecular weight and acid solubility. It explains the lipid exception in the acid-insoluble fraction and outlines the average chemical composition of cells.
Key terms: Biomacromolecules, Micromolecules, Acid-soluble pool, Acid-insoluble fraction, Lipids, Cellular composition
Teaches concepts: concept:acid_insoluble_fraction (Acid-insoluble fraction), concept:acid_soluble_pool (Acid-soluble pool), concept:biomacromolecules (Biomacromolecules), concept:cellular_composition (Cellular Composition), concept:lipid_exception (Lipid Exception), concept:micromolecules (Micromolecules)
Requires concepts: concept:cell_membranes (Cell membranes), concept:molecular_weight (Molecular weight)
title: Biomacromolecules

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: Proteins
Summary: This section introduces proteins as heteropolymers composed of linear chains of amino acids linked by peptide bonds. It distinguishes between essential and non-essential amino acids and details the diverse physiological functions of proteins, highlighting key examples like collagen and RuBisCO.
Key terms: Proteins, Polypeptides, Heteropolymer, Essential Amino Acids, Collagen, RuBisCO, GLUT-4
Teaches concepts: concept:collagen (Collagen), concept:essential_amino_acids (Essential Amino Acids), concept:heteropolymer (Heteropolymer), concept:proteins (Proteins), concept:rubisco (RuBisCO)
Requires concepts: concept:amino_acids (Amino Acids)
title: Proteins

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: Polysaccharides
Summary: This section introduces polysaccharides as macromolecular chains of monosaccharides, classifying them into homopolymers and complex polysaccharides. It covers key biological examples including cellulose, starch, glycogen, inulin, and chitin, alongside their structural properties and diagnostic tests.
Key terms: Polysaccharides, Homopolymer, Starch, Glycogen, Cellulose, Chitin, Reducing end
Teaches concepts: concept:chitin (Chitin), concept:glycogen (Glycogen), concept:homopolymer (Homopolymer), concept:polysaccharides (Polysaccharides), concept:starch (Starch)
Requires concepts: concept:biomacromolecules (Biomacromolecules), concept:monosaccharides (Monosaccharides)
title: Polysaccharides

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: Nucleic Acids
Summary: This section introduces nucleic acids as polynucleotides that constitute the macromolecular fraction of living tissues. It details the chemical structure of nucleotides, which are composed of a nitrogenous base, a pentose sugar, and a phosphate group, distinguishing between DNA and RNA.
Key terms: nucleic acids, polynucleotides, nucleotide, nitrogenous bases, purines, pyrimidines, deoxyribonucleic acid, ribonucleic acid
Teaches concepts: concept:deoxyribonucleic_acid (Deoxyribonucleic Acid), concept:nitrogenous_bases (Nitrogenous Bases), concept:nucleic_acids (Nucleic Acids), concept:nucleotide (Nucleotide), concept:ribonucleic_acid (Ribonucleic Acid)
Requires concepts: concept:macromolecules (Macromolecules), concept:monosaccharides (Monosaccharides)
title: Nucleic Acids

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: Structure of Proteins
Summary: This section explains the four hierarchical levels of protein structure: primary, secondary, tertiary, and quaternary. It details how amino acid sequences fold into complex three-dimensional shapes and assemble into multi-subunit functional proteins like haemoglobin.
Key terms: primary structure, secondary structure, tertiary structure, quaternary structure, N-terminal, C-terminal, alpha-helix, beta-pleated sheet, haemoglobin
Teaches concepts: concept:haemoglobin (Haemoglobin), concept:primary_structure_of_proteins (Primary Structure of Proteins), concept:quaternary_structure_of_proteins (Quaternary Structure of Proteins), concept:secondary_structure_of_proteins (Secondary Structure of Proteins), concept:tertiary_structure_of_proteins (Tertiary Structure of Proteins)
Requires concepts: concept:amino_acids (Amino Acids), concept:polypeptide (Polypeptide)
title: Structure of Proteins

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: Enzymes
Summary: This section introduces enzymes as biological catalysts, primarily composed of proteins, and explains their structural features including active sites. It also contrasts organic enzymes with inorganic catalysts, highlighting their thermal sensitivity and the unique stability of thermophilic enzymes.
Key terms: enzymes, ribozymes, active sites, thermal sensitivity, thermophilic organisms
Teaches concepts: concept:active_sites (Active Sites), concept:enzymes (Enzymes), concept:ribozymes (Ribozymes), concept:thermal_sensitivity_of_enzymes (Thermal Sensitivity of Enzymes)
Requires concepts: concept:nucleic_acids (Nucleic Acids), concept:protein_structure (Protein Structure)
title: Enzymes

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: Chemical Reactions
Summary: This section defines physical and chemical changes, explaining how the rate of a reaction is measured and affected by temperature. It highlights the extraordinary catalytic power of enzymes using carbonic anhydrase as an example, and introduces metabolic pathways as series of linked, enzyme-catalyzed reactions.
Key terms: physical change, chemical reaction, rate of reaction, carbonic anhydrase, metabolic pathway, glycolysis
Teaches concepts: concept:catalytic_power_of_enzymes (Catalytic Power of Enzymes), concept:chemical_reaction (Chemical Reaction), concept:metabolic_pathway (Metabolic Pathway), concept:physical_change (Physical Change), concept:rate_of_reaction (Rate of Reaction)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_bond (Chemical Bond), concept:enzymes (Enzymes)
title: Chemical Reactions

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: How do Enzymes bring about such High Rates of Chemical Conversions?
Summary: This section explains how enzymes accelerate chemical reactions by forming a temporary enzyme-substrate complex and lowering the activation energy barrier. It describes the transition state of substrates and how enzymes facilitate both exothermic and endothermic reactions.
Key terms: active site, enzyme-substrate complex, transition state, activation energy, exothermic, endothermic
Teaches concepts: concept:activation_energy (Activation Energy), concept:enzymatic_catalysis_by_lowering_activation_energy (Enzymatic Catalysis by Lowering Activation Energy), concept:enzyme_substrate_complex (Enzyme-Substrate Complex), concept:transition_state (Transition State)
Requires concepts: concept:enzyme (Enzyme), concept:exothermic_and_endothermic_reactions (Exothermic and Endothermic Reactions), concept:substrate (Substrate)
title: How do Enzymes bring about such High Rates of Chemical Conversions?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: Nature of Enzyme Action
Summary: This section describes the step-by-step catalytic cycle of enzyme action, explaining how an enzyme binds to a substrate, undergoes conformational changes, facilitates chemical transformation, and releases the final product.
Key terms: catalytic cycle, enzyme-substrate complex, enzyme-product complex, active site, substrate
Teaches concepts: concept:catalytic_cycle_of_enzyme_action (Catalytic Cycle of Enzyme Action), concept:enzyme_product_complex (Enzyme-Product Complex), concept:enzyme_substrate_complex (Enzyme-Substrate Complex)
Requires concepts: concept:active_site (Active Site), concept:enzyme (Enzyme), concept:substrate (Substrate)
title: Nature of Enzyme Action

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: Factors Affecting Enzyme Activity
Summary: This section explores the environmental and chemical factors that regulate enzyme activity, including temperature, pH, substrate concentration, and inhibitors. It explains how extreme conditions denature enzymes, how substrate saturation leads to a maximum reaction velocity, and how competitive inhibitors block active sites.
Key terms: optimum temperature, optimum pH, denaturation, maximum velocity, Michaelis constant, competitive inhibition
Teaches concepts: concept:competitive_inhibition (Competitive Inhibition), concept:enzyme_denaturation (Enzyme Denaturation), concept:maximum_velocity_vmax (Maximum Velocity (Vmax)), concept:michaelis_constant_km (Michaelis Constant (Km)), concept:optimum_temperature_and_ph (Optimum Temperature and pH)
Requires concepts: concept:enzyme_active_site (Enzyme Active Site), concept:protein_tertiary_structure (Protein Tertiary Structure)
title: Factors Affecting Enzyme Activity

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: Classification and Nomenclature of Enzymes
Summary: This section outlines the classification and nomenclature of enzymes into six major classes based on the reactions they catalyze. Each class, including oxidoreductases, transferases, hydrolases, lyases, isomerases, and ligases, is defined by its specific catalytic mechanism.
Key terms: Enzyme classification, Oxidoreductases, Transferases, Hydrolases, Lyases, Isomerases, Ligases, Four-digit number system
Teaches concepts: concept:hydrolases (Hydrolases), concept:isomerases (Isomerases), concept:ligases (Ligases), concept:lyases (Lyases), concept:oxidoreductases (Oxidoreductases), concept:transferases (Transferases)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_bond (Chemical Bond), concept:enzymes (Enzymes)
title: Classification and Nomenclature of Enzymes

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:biology:11:9
Section: Co-factors
Summary: This section explains co-factors, which are non-protein components essential for the catalytic activity of certain enzymes. It defines the protein portion as the apoenzyme and details the three types of co-factors: prosthetic groups, co-enzymes, and metal ions.
Key terms: co-factors, apoenzyme, prosthetic groups, co-enzymes, metal ions, catalytic activity
Teaches concepts: concept:apoenzyme (Apoenzyme), concept:co_enzymes (Co-enzymes), concept:co_factors (Co-factors), concept:metal_ions_as_co_factors (Metal Ions as Co-factors), concept:prosthetic_groups (Prosthetic Groups)
Requires concepts: concept:active_site (Active Site), concept:enzymes (Enzymes)
title: Co-factors

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:10
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section introduces the diverse applications of biotechnology, including therapeutics, agriculture, and bioremediation, and outlines its three critical research areas: catalyst optimization, environmental engineering, and downstream processing.
Key terms: Biotechnology, Biopharmaceuticals, Genetically modified organisms, Bioremediation, Downstream processing, Catalyst
Teaches concepts: concept:biotechnology_applications (Biotechnology Applications), concept:critical_research_areas_of_biotechnology (Critical Research Areas of Biotechnology), concept:downstream_processing (Downstream Processing)
Requires concepts: concept:genetically_modified_organisms (Genetically Modified Organisms)
title: Introduction

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:10
Section: Biotechnological Applications in Agriculture
Summary: This section explores biotechnological applications in agriculture, highlighting tissue culture techniques like micro-propagation and somatic hybridization. It details the creation and benefits of Genetically Modified Organisms (GMOs), focusing on pest-resistant crops developed via Bt toxin expression and RNA interference (RNAi).
Key terms: Tissue culture, Totipotency, Somaclones, Somatic hybridisation, Genetically Modified Organisms, Bt cotton, RNA interference
Teaches concepts: concept:bt_cotton (Bt Cotton), concept:genetically_modified_organisms_gmos (Genetically Modified Organisms (GMOs)), concept:rna_interference_rnai (RNA Interference (RNAi)), concept:somatic_hybridisation (Somatic Hybridisation), concept:tissue_culture_and_totipotency (Tissue Culture and Totipotency)
Requires concepts: concept:recombinant_dna_technology (Recombinant DNA Technology), concept:transcription_and_translation (Transcription and Translation)
title: Biotechnological Applications in Agriculture

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:10
Section: Biotechnological Applications in Medicine
Summary: This section introduces the application of recombinant DNA technology in medicine, focusing on the production of recombinant therapeutics. These therapeutics provide safe, effective treatments without triggering the unwanted immunological responses associated with non-human sources.
Key terms: recombinant DNA technology, recombinant therapeutics, immunological response, healthcare
Teaches concepts: concept:recombinant_therapeutics (Recombinant Therapeutics)
Requires concepts: concept:immunological_response (Immunological Response), concept:recombinant_dna_technology (Recombinant DNA Technology)
title: Biotechnological Applications in Medicine

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:10
Section: Genetically Engineered Insulin
Summary: This section describes the structure of human insulin and its synthesis as an inactive pro-hormone that requires maturation. It details the historical challenges of animal-derived insulin and explains how recombinant DNA technology was used to produce genetically engineered human insulin.
Key terms: insulin, pro-insulin, C peptide, disulfide bonds, recombinant DNA, Eli Lilly, Escherichia coli
Teaches concepts: concept:genetically_engineered_insulin (Genetically Engineered Insulin), concept:insulin_maturation (Insulin Maturation), concept:insulin_structure (Insulin Structure), concept:pro_insulin (Pro-insulin)
Requires concepts: concept:disulfide_bonds (Disulfide Bonds), concept:recombinant_dna_technology (Recombinant DNA Technology)
title: Genetically Engineered Insulin

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:10
Section: Gene Therapy
Summary: This section introduces gene therapy as a therapeutic technique to correct genetic defects by delivering functional genes into a patient's cells or embryos. It highlights the landmark 1990 clinical trial for Adenosine Deaminase (ADA) deficiency, explaining both temporary treatments and the requirements for a permanent cure.
Key terms: gene therapy, adenosine deaminase deficiency, ADA, lymphocytes, retroviral vector, cDNA, enzyme replacement therapy
Teaches concepts: concept:adenosine_deaminase_ada_deficiency (Adenosine Deaminase (ADA) Deficiency), concept:enzyme_replacement_therapy (Enzyme Replacement Therapy), concept:gene_therapy (Gene Therapy), concept:retroviral_vector_gene_delivery (Retroviral Vector Gene Delivery)
Requires concepts: concept:complementary_dna_cdna (Complementary DNA (cDNA)), concept:lymphocytes (Lymphocytes), concept:retroviral_vectors (Retroviral Vectors)
title: Gene Therapy

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:10
Section: Molecular Diagnosis
Summary: This section explains the importance of molecular diagnosis for early detection of diseases before symptoms manifest. It details key diagnostic techniques including Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR), radioactive probes with autoradiography, and Enzyme Linked Immuno-sorbent Assay (ELISA).
Key terms: Molecular Diagnosis, Polymerase Chain Reaction, ELISA, Radioactive Probe, Autoradiography, Antigen-Antibody Interaction
Teaches concepts: concept:enzyme_linked_immuno_sorbent_assay_elisa (Enzyme Linked Immuno-sorbent Assay (ELISA)), concept:gene_detection_using_probes_and_autoradiography (Gene Detection using Probes and Autoradiography), concept:molecular_diagnosis (Molecular Diagnosis), concept:pcr_in_diagnosis (PCR in Diagnosis)
Requires concepts: concept:antigen_antibody_interaction (Antigen-Antibody Interaction), concept:nucleic_acid_hybridization (Nucleic Acid Hybridization), concept:polymerase_chain_reaction_pcr (Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR))
title: Molecular Diagnosis

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:10
Section: Transgenic Animals
Summary: This section defines transgenic animals as organisms with manipulated DNA that express foreign genes, with mice representing over 95 percent of them. It details their critical applications in studying gene regulation, modeling human diseases, producing biological products like alpha-1-antitrypsin, and testing vaccine and chemical safety.
Key terms: transgenic animals, foreign gene, alpha-1-antitrypsin, Rosie, vaccine safety, toxicity testing
Teaches concepts: concept:biological_product_synthesis (Biological Product Synthesis), concept:gene_regulation_and_physiology_study (Gene Regulation and Physiology Study), concept:transgenic_animals (Transgenic Animals), concept:transgenic_disease_models (Transgenic Disease Models), concept:vaccine_and_chemical_safety_testing (Vaccine and Chemical Safety Testing)
Requires concepts: concept:gene_expression (Gene Expression), concept:recombinant_dna_technology (Recombinant DNA Technology)
title: Transgenic Animals

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:10
Section: Ethical Issues
Summary: This section explores the ethical, regulatory, and legal challenges associated with biotechnology, focusing on the regulation of genetic modification and the exploitation of bio-resources. It highlights the role of the Genetic Engineering Approval Committee (GEAC), defines biopiracy, and discusses biopatents and legislative responses like the Indian Patents Bill.
Key terms: GEAC, Biopiracy, Biopatents, Basmati rice patent, Indian Patents Bill, Genetic modification
Teaches concepts: concept:biopatents (Biopatents), concept:biopiracy (Biopiracy), concept:genetic_engineering_approval_committee_geac (Genetic Engineering Approval Committee (GEAC)), concept:indian_patents_bill (Indian Patents Bill)
Requires concepts: concept:genetically_modified_organisms_gmos (Genetically Modified Organisms (GMOs))
title: Ethical Issues

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:11
Section: Populations
Summary: This section defines biological populations and explores their key attributes, including birth and death rates, sex ratios, age structures, and population density. It models population dynamics over time using the exponential growth model under unlimited resources and the more realistic Verhulst-Pearl logistic growth model governed by carrying capacity.
Key terms: Population, Birth rate, Death rate, Age pyramid, Population density, Exponential growth, Carrying capacity, Logistic growth
Teaches concepts: concept:biological_population (Biological Population), concept:carrying_capacity (Carrying Capacity), concept:exponential_growth_model (Exponential Growth Model), concept:population_attributes (Population Attributes), concept:population_density (Population Density), concept:verhulst_pearl_logistic_growth (Verhulst-Pearl Logistic Growth)
Requires concepts: concept:calculus (Basic Calculus), concept:natural_selection (Natural Selection)
title: Populations

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:11
Section: Life History Variation
Summary: This section explains how populations evolve to maximize their reproductive or Darwinian fitness through diverse reproductive strategies. It describes variations in breeding frequency and offspring size and number as life history traits shaped by habitat constraints.
Key terms: Reproductive fitness, Darwinian fitness, Reproductive strategy, Life history traits, Selection pressures
Teaches concepts: concept:life_history_traits (Life History Traits), concept:reproductive_fitness (Reproductive Fitness), concept:reproductive_strategies (Reproductive Strategies)
Requires concepts: concept:intrinsic_rate_of_natural_increase (Intrinsic Rate of Natural Increase), concept:natural_selection (Natural Selection)
title: Life History Variation

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:11
Section: Population Interactions
Summary: This section explores the diverse interspecific interactions that shape biological communities, categorizing them into mutualism, competition, predation, parasitism, commensalism, and amensalism. It details the ecological significance, evolutionary adaptations, and population dynamics associated with each interaction type, highlighting mechanisms like competitive exclusion, resource partitioning, and co-evolution.
Key terms: Interspecific interactions, Mutualism, Competition, Predation, Parasitism, Commensalism, Amensalism, Competitive Exclusion Principle, Resource partitioning, Co-evolution
Teaches concepts: concept:commensalism (Commensalism), concept:interspecific_competition (Interspecific Competition), concept:interspecific_interactions (Interspecific Interactions), concept:mutualism (Mutualism), concept:parasitism (Parasitism), concept:predation (Predation)
Requires concepts: concept:natural_selection (Natural Selection), concept:population (Population), concept:trophic_level (Trophic Level)
title: Population Interactions

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:12
Section: Ecosystem – Structure and Function
Summary: This section defines ecosystems and classifies them into terrestrial, aquatic, and man-made categories. It details ecosystem structure through species composition and stratification, and introduces the four functional aspects—productivity, decomposition, energy flow, and nutrient cycling—illustrated by a pond ecosystem.
Key terms: Ecosystem, Stratification, Species Composition, Productivity, Decomposition, Energy flow, Nutrient cycling, Pond ecosystem
Teaches concepts: concept:ecosystem (Ecosystem), concept:functional_aspects_of_ecosystems (Functional Aspects of Ecosystems), concept:pond_ecosystem (Pond Ecosystem), concept:species_composition (Species Composition), concept:stratification (Stratification)
Requires concepts: concept:autotrophs_consumers_and_decomposers (Autotrophs, Consumers, and Decomposers), concept:biotic_and_abiotic_components (Biotic and Abiotic Components)
title: Ecosystem – Structure and Function

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:12
Section: Productivity
Summary: This section defines and distinguishes between primary production, primary productivity, gross primary productivity (GPP), net primary productivity (NPP), and secondary productivity. It also provides global statistics on biosphere productivity, highlighting the difference between terrestrial and marine contributions.
Key terms: Primary production, Productivity, Gross Primary Productivity, Net Primary Productivity, Secondary Productivity
Teaches concepts: concept:gross_primary_productivity (Gross Primary Productivity), concept:net_primary_productivity (Net Primary Productivity), concept:primary_production (Primary Production), concept:productivity (Productivity), concept:secondary_productivity (Secondary Productivity)
Requires concepts: concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis), concept:respiration (Respiration)
title: Productivity

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:12
Section: Decomposition
Summary: This section defines decomposition as the breakdown of complex organic matter, or detritus, into inorganic substances. It details the five sequential and simultaneous steps of decomposition and explains how chemical composition and climatic factors regulate the rate of this process.
Key terms: Decomposition, Detritus, Fragmentation, Leaching, Catabolism, Humification, Mineralisation, Humus
Teaches concepts: concept:decomposition (Decomposition), concept:detritus (Detritus), concept:factors_regulating_decomposition (Factors Regulating Decomposition), concept:fragmentation_leaching_and_catabolism (Fragmentation, Leaching, and Catabolism), concept:humification (Humification), concept:mineralisation (Mineralisation)
Requires concepts: concept:decomposers (Decomposers), concept:ecosystem (Ecosystem)
title: Decomposition

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:12
Section: Energy Flow
Summary: This section explains the unidirectional flow of energy through ecosystems, starting from solar radiation captured by producers. It details the grazing and detritus food chains, food webs, trophic levels, and the 10 per cent law of energy transfer.
Key terms: Photosynthetically Active Radiation (PAR), Grazing Food Chain (GFC), Detritus Food Chain (DFC), Food Web, Trophic Level, 10 Per Cent Law, Standing Crop
Teaches concepts: concept:10_per_cent_law (10 Per Cent Law), concept:detritus_food_chain_dfc (Detritus Food Chain (DFC)), concept:food_web (Food Web), concept:grazing_food_chain_gfc (Grazing Food Chain (GFC)), concept:photosynthetically_active_radiation_par (Photosynthetically Active Radiation (PAR)), concept:trophic_level (Trophic Level)
Requires concepts: concept:first_law_of_thermodynamics (First Law of Thermodynamics), concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis), concept:second_law_of_thermodynamics (Second Law of Thermodynamics)
title: Energy Flow

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:12
Section: Ecological Pyramids
Summary: This section explains ecological pyramids, which represent the trophic relationships in an ecosystem in terms of numbers, biomass, and energy. It describes the three types of pyramids (numbers, biomass, and energy), their shapes (upright or inverted), and discusses their limitations, such as the exclusion of saprophytes and food webs.
Key terms: ecological pyramid, pyramid of numbers, pyramid of biomass, pyramid of energy, trophic level, saprophytes
Teaches concepts: concept:ecological_pyramid (Ecological Pyramid), concept:limitations_of_ecological_pyramids (Limitations of Ecological Pyramids), concept:pyramid_of_biomass (Pyramid of Biomass), concept:pyramid_of_energy (Pyramid of Energy), concept:pyramid_of_numbers (Pyramid of Numbers)
Requires concepts: concept:ecosystem (Ecosystem), concept:food_chain (Food Chain), concept:trophic_level (Trophic Level)
title: Ecological Pyramids

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:13
Section: BIODIVERSITY
Summary: This section introduces the concept of biodiversity as the combined heterogeneity across all levels of biological organization. It explains the three primary levels of biodiversity: genetic diversity, species diversity, and ecological diversity, using real-world examples.
Key terms: Biodiversity, Genetic diversity, Species diversity, Ecological diversity, Edward Wilson, Heterogeneity
Teaches concepts: concept:biodiversity (Biodiversity), concept:ecological_diversity (Ecological Diversity), concept:genetic_diversity (Genetic Diversity), concept:species_diversity (Species Diversity)
Requires concepts: concept:ecosystem (Ecosystem), concept:gene (Gene), concept:species (Species)
title: BIODIVERSITY

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:13
Section: How Many Species are there on Earth and How Many in India?
Summary: This section explores global and national species diversity, highlighting Robert May's estimate of 7 million global species. It details taxonomic patterns of biodiversity, the challenges of estimating prokaryotic diversity, and India's status as a mega-diverse nation.
Key terms: species diversity, IUCN, Robert May's estimate, taxonomic patterns, prokaryotic diversity, mega diversity country
Teaches concepts: concept:global_species_diversity_estimate (Global Species Diversity Estimate), concept:mega_diversity_country (Mega Diversity Country), concept:prokaryotic_diversity_estimation_challenges (Prokaryotic Diversity Estimation Challenges), concept:taxonomic_patterns_of_biodiversity (Taxonomic Patterns of Biodiversity)
Requires concepts: concept:prokaryotes (Prokaryotes), concept:species (Species), concept:taxonomy (Taxonomy)
title: How Many Species are there on Earth and How Many in India?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:13
Section: Patterns of Biodiversity
Summary: This section examines the uneven distribution of global biodiversity, focusing on latitudinal gradients and the species-area relationship. It details the hypotheses explaining high tropical species richness and introduces Alexander von Humboldt's mathematical model for species-area scaling.
Key terms: Latitudinal gradient, Species richness, Niche specialisation, Species-area relationship, Regression coefficient, Alexander von Humboldt
Teaches concepts: concept:hypotheses_for_greater_tropical_diversity (Hypotheses for Greater Tropical Diversity), concept:latitudinal_gradients_of_biodiversity (Latitudinal Gradients of Biodiversity), concept:regression_coefficient_z_value (Regression Coefficient (Z-value)), concept:species_area_relationship (Species-Area Relationship)
Requires concepts: concept:biodiversity (Biodiversity), concept:logarithmic_functions_and_linear_graphs (Logarithmic Functions and Linear Graphs), concept:speciation (Speciation)
title: Patterns of Biodiversity

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:13
Section: The importance of Species Diversity to the Ecosystem
Summary: This section explains how species diversity contributes to ecosystem stability, productivity, and resilience. It discusses David Tilman's long-term outdoor plot experiments and Paul Ehrlich's 'rivet popper hypothesis' to illustrate the ecological consequences of species loss.
Key terms: Species diversity, Ecosystem stability, Biomass productivity, David Tilman's experiments, Rivet popper hypothesis, Key species
Teaches concepts: concept:david_tilmans_ecosystem_experiments (David Tilman's Ecosystem Experiments), concept:ecosystem_stability (Ecosystem Stability), concept:key_species (Key Species), concept:rivet_popper_hypothesis (Rivet Popper Hypothesis)
Requires concepts: concept:ecosystem (Ecosystem), concept:species_diversity (Species Diversity)
title: The importance of Species Diversity to the Ecosystem

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:13
Section: Loss of Biodiversity
Summary: This section details the rapid decline of global biodiversity, highlighting recent extinction trends, IUCN Red List data, and the ongoing sixth mass extinction driven by human activities. It outlines the consequences of biodiversity loss and explains its four major causes, collectively known as 'The Evil Quartet'.
Key terms: IUCN Red List, Sixth Extinction, The Evil Quartet, Habitat Loss and Fragmentation, Over-exploitation, Alien Species Invasions, Co-extinctions
Teaches concepts: concept:alien_species_invasions (Alien Species Invasions), concept:co_extinctions (Co-extinctions), concept:consequences_of_biodiversity_loss (Consequences of Biodiversity Loss), concept:habitat_loss_and_fragmentation (Habitat Loss and Fragmentation), concept:iucn_red_list_and_extinction_trends (IUCN Red List and Extinction Trends), concept:sixth_mass_extinction (Sixth Mass Extinction)
Requires concepts: concept:biodiversity (Biodiversity)
title: Loss of Biodiversity

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:13
Section: Why Should We Conserve Biodiversity?
Summary: This section outlines the three primary categories of arguments for conserving biodiversity: narrowly utilitarian, broadly utilitarian, and ethical. It explains how biodiversity provides direct economic benefits, critical ecosystem services, and carries intrinsic moral value.
Key terms: Narrowly Utilitarian, Broadly Utilitarian, Bioprospecting, Ecosystem Services, Ethical Arguments, Biodiversity Conservation
Teaches concepts: concept:bioprospecting (Bioprospecting), concept:broadly_utilitarian_arguments (Broadly Utilitarian Arguments), concept:ethical_arguments_for_conservation (Ethical Arguments for Conservation), concept:narrowly_utilitarian_arguments (Narrowly Utilitarian Arguments)
Requires concepts: concept:biodiversity (Biodiversity), concept:ecosystem_services (Ecosystem Services)
title: Why Should We Conserve Biodiversity?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:13
Section: How do we conserve Biodiversity?
Summary: This section details the strategies used to conserve biodiversity, distinguishing between in situ (on-site) and ex situ (off-site) conservation. It highlights key conservation tools such as biodiversity hotspots, sacred groves, cryopreservation, and international conventions like the Earth Summit.
Key terms: In situ conservation, Ex situ conservation, Biodiversity hotspots, Sacred groves, Cryopreservation, Earth Summit
Teaches concepts: concept:biodiversity_hotspots (Biodiversity Hotspots), concept:cryopreservation (Cryopreservation), concept:ex_situ_conservation (Ex situ Conservation), concept:in_situ_conservation (In situ Conservation), concept:international_biodiversity_conventions (International Biodiversity Conventions), concept:sacred_groves (Sacred Groves)
Requires concepts: concept:biodiversity (Biodiversity), concept:threats_to_biodiversity (Threats to Biodiversity)
title: How do we conserve Biodiversity?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:1
Section: Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants
Summary: This section introduces flowers as the primary sites of sexual reproduction in plants. It describes the basic anatomy of a typical flower, detailing the male reproductive organ (androecium) and the female reproductive organ (gynoecium).
Key terms: flower, androecium, gynoecium, anther, filament, stigma, style, ovary
Teaches concepts: concept:androecium (Androecium), concept:flower_anatomy (Flower Anatomy), concept:gynoecium (Gynoecium)
Requires concepts: concept:sexual_reproduction (Sexual Reproduction)
title: Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:1
Section: Pre-fertilisation: Structures and Events
Summary: This section explains the hormonal and structural changes that trigger floral initiation and differentiation in plants. It details the development of the floral primordium into inflorescences and the differentiation of the male (androecium) and female (gynoecium) reproductive structures.
Key terms: floral primordium, inflorescence, androecium, gynoecium, stamens
Teaches concepts: concept:androecium (Androecium), concept:floral_primordium (Floral Primordium), concept:gynoecium (Gynoecium), concept:inflorescence (Inflorescence)
Requires concepts: concept:flower_anatomy (Flower Anatomy), concept:plant_hormones (Plant Hormones)
title: Pre-fertilisation: Structures and Events

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:1
Section: Stamen, Microsporangium and Pollen Grain
Summary: This section details the structure and development of the stamen, the male reproductive organ of angiosperms. It covers the anatomy of the anther and microsporangium, the process of microsporogenesis, the structural and cellular organization of mature pollen grains, and their viability and storage.
Key terms: Stamen, Anther, Microsporangium, Tapetum, Microsporogenesis, Pollen grain, Sporopollenin, Vegetative cell, Generative cell
Teaches concepts: concept:cellular_organization_of_mature_pollen (Cellular Organization of Mature Pollen), concept:microsporangium_wall_layers (Microsporangium Wall Layers), concept:microsporogenesis (Microsporogenesis), concept:pollen_grain_structure (Pollen Grain Structure), concept:pollen_viability_and_cryopreservation (Pollen Viability and Cryopreservation), concept:stamen_and_anther_anatomy (Stamen and Anther Anatomy)
Requires concepts: concept:angiosperm_flower_morphology (Angiosperm Flower Morphology), concept:meiosis (Meiosis), concept:ploidy (Ploidy)
title: Stamen, Microsporangium and Pollen Grain

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:1
Section: The Pistil, Megasporangium (ovule) and Embryo sac
Summary: This section details the structure of the gynoecium and the anatomy of the megasporangium (ovule). It explains megasporogenesis and the subsequent development of the female gametophyte (embryo sac) into a mature 8-nucleate, 7-celled structure.
Key terms: Gynoecium, Pistil, Megasporangium, Megasporogenesis, Monosporic development, Embryo sac
Teaches concepts: concept:embryo_sac_female_gametophyte (Embryo Sac (Female Gametophyte)), concept:gynoecium_and_pistil_structure (Gynoecium and Pistil Structure), concept:megasporangium_ovule (Megasporangium (Ovule)), concept:megasporogenesis (Megasporogenesis), concept:monosporic_development (Monosporic Development)
Requires concepts: concept:meiosis (Meiosis), concept:mitosis (Mitosis), concept:ploidy_haploid_and_diploid (Ploidy (Haploid and Diploid))
title: The Pistil, Megasporangium (ovule) and Embryo sac

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:1
Section: Pollination
Summary: This section defines pollination as the transfer of pollen grains from the anther to the stigma and classifies it into autogamy, geitonogamy, and xenogamy. It also details the abiotic (wind, water) and biotic (animal) agents of pollination, along with the specific floral adaptations and mutualistic rewards that facilitate this process.
Key terms: Pollination, Autogamy, Chasmogamous, Cleistogamous, Geitonogamy, Xenogamy, Abiotic agents, Biotic agents, Floral rewards
Teaches concepts: concept:autogamy (Autogamy), concept:chasmogamous_and_cleistogamous_flowers (Chasmogamous and Cleistogamous Flowers), concept:geitonogamy (Geitonogamy), concept:pollination (Pollination), concept:pollination_agents_and_adaptations (Pollination Agents and Adaptations), concept:xenogamy (Xenogamy)
Requires concepts: concept:flower_anatomy (Flower Anatomy), concept:plant_fertilisation (Plant Fertilisation)
title: Pollination

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:1
Section: Outbreeding Devices
Summary: This section describes outbreeding devices, which are evolutionary mechanisms in flowering plants that discourage self-pollination and promote cross-pollination to avoid inbreeding depression. These devices include temporal and spatial separation of reproductive organs, genetic self-incompatibility, and the production of unisexual flowers.
Key terms: outbreeding devices, inbreeding depression, self-incompatibility, autogamy, geitonogamy, monoecious, dioecious
Teaches concepts: concept:inbreeding_depression (Inbreeding Depression), concept:outbreeding_devices (Outbreeding Devices), concept:self_incompatibility (Self-incompatibility), concept:spatial_and_temporal_separation_of_organs (Spatial and Temporal Separation of Organs), concept:unisexual_flowers (Unisexual Flowers)
Requires concepts: concept:autogamy (Autogamy), concept:geitonogamy (Geitonogamy), concept:hermaphrodite_flowers (Hermaphrodite Flowers)
title: Outbreeding Devices

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:1
Section: Pollen-pistil Interaction
Summary: This section details the pollen-pistil interaction, which includes pollen recognition, germination, and pollen tube growth guided by the filiform apparatus. It also explains the practical application of these processes in plant breeding through artificial hybridization techniques like emasculation and bagging.
Key terms: Pollen-pistil interaction, Pollen recognition, Pollen tube, Filiform apparatus, Artificial hybridisation, Emasculation, Bagging
Teaches concepts: concept:artificial_hybridisation (Artificial Hybridisation), concept:emasculation_and_bagging (Emasculation and Bagging), concept:filiform_apparatus (Filiform Apparatus), concept:pollen_pistil_interaction (Pollen-pistil Interaction), concept:pollen_recognition (Pollen Recognition), concept:pollen_tube_growth (Pollen Tube Growth)
Requires concepts: concept:pistil_structure (Pistil Structure), concept:pollen_grain_structure (Pollen Grain Structure), concept:pollination (Pollination)
title: Pollen-pistil Interaction

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:1
Section: Double Fertilisation
Summary: This section explains the process of double fertilisation in flowering plants, which involves two distinct fusion events: syngamy and triple fusion. It details how these fusions lead to the formation of a diploid zygote and a triploid primary endosperm nucleus, which subsequently develop into the embryo and endosperm.
Key terms: Double Fertilisation, Syngamy, Triple Fusion, Primary Endosperm Nucleus, Zygote, Primary Endosperm Cell
Teaches concepts: concept:double_fertilisation (Double Fertilisation), concept:primary_endosperm_cell (Primary Endosperm Cell), concept:primary_endosperm_nucleus (Primary Endosperm Nucleus), concept:syngamy (Syngamy), concept:triple_fusion (Triple Fusion)
Requires concepts: concept:embryo_sac (Embryo Sac), concept:male_gametes (Male Gametes), concept:polar_nuclei (Polar Nuclei)
title: Double Fertilisation

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:1
Section: Post-fertilisation: Structures and Events
Summary: This section details the post-fertilisation events in flowering plants, including the development of endosperm and embryo, and the maturation of ovules into seeds and ovaries into fruits. It describes the structural differences between monocotyledonous and dicotyledonous embryos, seed types, and fruit classifications such as true, false, and parthenocarpic fruits.
Key terms: Endosperm, Embryogeny, Scutellum, Perisperm, Albuminous seed, Parthenocarpy, Pericarp
Teaches concepts: concept:albuminous_and_non_albuminous_seeds (Albuminous and Non-albuminous Seeds), concept:embryogeny (Embryogeny), concept:endosperm_development (Endosperm Development), concept:false_fruits (False Fruits), concept:parthenocarpy (Parthenocarpy), concept:perisperm (Perisperm)
Requires concepts: concept:double_fertilisation (Double Fertilisation), concept:structure_of_ovule_and_ovary (Structure of Ovule and Ovary)
title: Post-fertilisation: Structures and Events

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:1
Section: Apomixis and Polyembryony
Summary: This section explains apomixis, a form of asexual reproduction that produces seeds without fertilization, and parthenocarpy, which is fruit production without fertilization. It also describes polyembryony, where multiple embryos develop in a single seed, and discusses the economic significance of apomixis in maintaining hybrid crop traits.
Key terms: Apomixis, Polyembryony, Parthenocarpy, Nucellar cells, Hybrid seeds, Segregation of characters
Teaches concepts: concept:apomictic_hybrid_seeds (Apomictic Hybrid Seeds), concept:apomixis (Apomixis), concept:parthenocarpy (Parthenocarpy), concept:polyembryony (Polyembryony)
Requires concepts: concept:embryo (Embryo), concept:fertilisation (Fertilisation), concept:genetic_segregation (Genetic Segregation), concept:meiosis (Meiosis)
title: Apomixis and Polyembryony

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:2
Section: The Male Reproductive System
Summary: This section details the anatomy and function of the human male reproductive system, including the testes, scrotum, accessory ducts, glands, and external genitalia. It explains the internal structure of the testes, focusing on the roles of seminiferous tubules, Sertoli cells, and Leydig cells in sperm production and hormone secretion.
Key terms: testes, scrotum, seminiferous tubules, Sertoli cells, Leydig cells, androgens, epididymis, vas deferens, seminal plasma

Requires concepts: concept:meiosis (Meiosis), concept:sexual_reproduction (Sexual Reproduction)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:2
Section: The Female Reproductive System
Summary: This section details the anatomy and function of the female reproductive system, including the ovaries, accessory ducts, external genitalia, and mammary glands. It explains how these structures integrate to support ovulation, fertilization, pregnancy, birth, and lactation.
Key terms: Ovaries, Oviducts, Uterus, Endometrium, Myometrium, Mammary glands, Fimbriae, Cervix, Hymen
Teaches concepts: concept:female_external_genitalia (Female External Genitalia), concept:mammary_glands (Mammary Glands), concept:ovaries (Ovaries), concept:oviducts (Oviducts), concept:uterus (Uterus)
Requires concepts: concept:fertilization (Fertilization), concept:gamete (Gamete)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:2
Section: Gametogenesis
Summary: This section details gametogenesis, the biological process of gamete production in human males and females. It covers the stages, hormonal control, and cellular transformations of spermatogenesis and oogenesis, highlighting key structural differences and developmental timelines.
Key terms: Gametogenesis, Spermatogenesis, Spermiogenesis, Spermiation, Oogenesis, Ovulation, Spermatozoa, Graafian follicle, Polar body

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:2
Section: Menstrual Cycle
Summary: This section describes the menstrual cycle in female primates, detailing its four main phases: menstrual, follicular, ovulatory, and luteal. It explains the hormonal regulation of these phases by pituitary and ovarian hormones, and discusses menarche, menopause, and menstrual hygiene.
Key terms: Menstrual cycle, Menarche, Menopause, LH surge, Corpus luteum, Progesterone, Estrogen, Follicular phase, Luteal phase, Endometrium
Teaches concepts: concept:follicular_phase (Follicular Phase), concept:luteal_phase (Luteal Phase), concept:menarche (Menarche), concept:menopause (Menopause), concept:menstrual_cycle (Menstrual cycle), concept:menstrual_phase (Menstrual Phase)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:2
Section: Fertilisation and Implantation
Summary: This section covers the processes of insemination, fertilisation, and early embryonic development in humans, including sex determination. It details the progression of the zygote through cleavage, morula, and blastocyst stages, culminating in implantation within the uterine endometrium.
Key terms: Insemination, Fertilisation, Zygote, Sex Determination, Cleavage, Morula, Blastocyst, Implantation
Teaches concepts: concept:cleavage (Cleavage), concept:fertilisation (Fertilisation), concept:implantation (Implantation), concept:insemination (Insemination), concept:morula (Morula), concept:sex_determination_in_humans (Sex Determination in Humans)
Requires concepts: concept:gametogenesis (Gametogenesis), concept:meiosis (Meiosis)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:2
Section: Pregnancy and Embryonic Development
Summary: This section covers the formation, functions, and endocrine roles of the placenta during human pregnancy. It also details the differentiation of the inner cell mass into three primary germ layers, the role of stem cells, and the chronological timeline of fetal development over the nine-month gestation period.
Key terms: Placenta, Chorionic villi, Umbilical cord, hCG, Germ layers, Stem cells, Gestation period
Teaches concepts: concept:embryonic_stem_cells (Embryonic Stem Cells), concept:germ_layer_differentiation (Germ Layer Differentiation), concept:human_embryonic_development_timeline (Human Embryonic Development Timeline), concept:placenta_formation_and_structure (Placenta Formation and Structure), concept:placental_hormones_and_functions (Placental Hormones and Functions)
Requires concepts: concept:blastocyst_and_inner_cell_mass (Blastocyst and Inner Cell Mass), concept:implantation (Implantation)
title: Pregnancy and Embryonic Development

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:2
Section: Parturition and Lactation
Summary: This section details the physiological processes of parturition and lactation in humans. It explains the neuroendocrine mechanism of childbirth, the hormonal feedback loop involving oxytocin, and the immunological importance of colostrum during early lactation.
Key terms: parturition, foetal ejection reflex, oxytocin, lactation, colostrum, passive immunity
Teaches concepts: concept:colostrum (Colostrum), concept:foetal_ejection_reflex (Foetal Ejection Reflex), concept:hormonal_control_of_childbirth (Hormonal Control of Childbirth), concept:lactation (Lactation), concept:parturition (Parturition)
Requires concepts: concept:gestation (Gestation), concept:mammary_gland_anatomy (Mammary Gland Anatomy), concept:pituitary_gland_and_endocrine_system (Pituitary Gland and Endocrine System)
title: Parturition and Lactation

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:3
Section: Reproductive Health – Problems and Strategies
Summary: This section introduces the concept of reproductive health as defined by the WHO and outlines India's national initiatives, such as the Family Planning and Reproductive and Child Health Care (RCH) programmes. It highlights the role of sex education, medical infrastructure, the diagnostic technique of amniocentesis, and the development of the oral contraceptive 'Saheli'.
Key terms: Reproductive Health, Family Planning, Reproductive and Child Health Care (RCH), Amniocentesis, Saheli, Maternal Mortality Rate (MMR), Infant Mortality Rate (IMR)
Teaches concepts: concept:amniocentesis (Amniocentesis), concept:maternal_and_infant_mortality_rates (Maternal and Infant Mortality Rates), concept:reproductive_and_child_health_care_rch_programmes (Reproductive and Child Health Care (RCH) Programmes), concept:reproductive_health (Reproductive Health), concept:saheli (Saheli)
Requires concepts: concept:chromosomes_and_genetic_disorders (Chromosomes and Genetic Disorders), concept:human_reproductive_system (Human Reproductive System)
title: Reproductive Health – Problems and Strategies

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:3
Section: Population Stabilisation and Birth Control
Summary: This section explores the causes and consequences of the global and national population explosion and outlines strategies for population control. It details the characteristics of an ideal contraceptive and classifies various birth control methods, including natural, barrier, intra-uterine, oral, injectable, emergency, and surgical techniques.
Key terms: Population explosion, Contraceptive, Intra Uterine Devices, Oral contraceptives, Sterilisation, Vasectomy, Tubectomy
Teaches concepts: concept:barrier_contraceptive_methods (Barrier Contraceptive Methods), concept:intra_uterine_devices (Intra Uterine Devices), concept:natural_contraceptive_methods (Natural Contraceptive Methods), concept:oral_contraceptives (Oral Contraceptives), concept:population_explosion (Population Explosion), concept:surgical_sterilisation (Surgical Sterilisation)
Requires concepts: concept:human_fertilisation_and_implantation (Human Fertilisation and Implantation), concept:human_reproductive_anatomy (Human Reproductive Anatomy), concept:menstrual_cycle (Menstrual cycle)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:3
Section: Medical Termination of Pregnancy (MTP)
Summary: This section defines Medical Termination of Pregnancy (MTP), also known as induced abortion, and discusses its global context and safety timelines. It details the legal framework of the MTP Amendment Act in India, highlighting the conditions under which it is permitted. Additionally, it addresses the critical issues of illegal abortions, the misuse of amniocentesis for female foeticide, and the preventive measures required to protect maternal health.
Key terms: Medical Termination of Pregnancy, MTP, Induced abortion, MTP Amendment Act, Female foeticide, Amniocentesis, First trimester
Teaches concepts: concept:first_trimester_abortion_safety (First Trimester Abortion Safety), concept:medical_termination_of_pregnancy_amendment_act (Medical Termination of Pregnancy (Amendment) Act), concept:medical_termination_of_pregnancy_mtp (Medical Termination of Pregnancy (MTP)), concept:misuse_of_amniocentesis_for_female_foeticide (Misuse of Amniocentesis for Female Foeticide)
Requires concepts: concept:amniocentesis (Amniocentesis), concept:pregnancy_and_gestation_periods (Pregnancy and Gestation Periods)
title: Medical Termination of Pregnancy (MTP)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:3
Section: Sexually Transmitted Infections (STIs)
Summary: This section defines sexually transmitted infections (STIs) and outlines their common examples, modes of transmission, and curability. It also discusses the symptoms, severe complications of untreated STIs, and key preventive measures to avoid infection.
Key terms: Sexually Transmitted Infections, Venereal Diseases, Reproductive Tract Infections, HIV, Pelvic Inflammatory Diseases, Condoms
Teaches concepts: concept:complications_of_stis (Complications of STIs), concept:incurable_stis (Incurable STIs), concept:modes_of_sti_transmission (Modes of STI Transmission), concept:prevention_of_stis (Prevention of STIs), concept:sexually_transmitted_infections_stis (Sexually Transmitted Infections (STIs))
Requires concepts: concept:human_reproductive_system (Human Reproductive System)
