subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:3
Section: Infertility
Summary: This section defines infertility and explores various Assisted Reproductive Technologies (ART) used to treat it. It details procedures such as IVF, ET, ZIFT, IUT, GIFT, ICSI, and artificial insemination, while also discussing social aspects and legal adoption.
Key terms: Infertility, Assisted Reproductive Technologies, In Vitro Fertilisation, Zygote Intra Fallopian Transfer, Gamete Intra Fallopian Transfer, Artificial Insemination
Teaches concepts: concept:artificial_insemination_ai (Artificial Insemination (AI)), concept:assisted_reproductive_technologies_art (Assisted Reproductive Technologies (ART)), concept:gamete_intra_fallopian_transfer_gift (Gamete Intra Fallopian Transfer (GIFT)), concept:in_vitro_fertilisation_ivf (In Vitro Fertilisation (IVF)), concept:infertility (Infertility), concept:zygote_intra_fallopian_transfer_zift (Zygote Intra Fallopian Transfer (ZIFT))
Requires concepts: concept:female_reproductive_system (Female Reproductive System), concept:fertilisation (Fertilisation), concept:zygote (Zygote)
title: Infertility

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Principles of Inheritance and Variation
Summary: This section introduces genetics as the scientific study of inheritance and variation. It defines key terms such as inheritance and variation, and provides a historical perspective on how humans exploited natural variation through artificial selection.
Key terms: Genetics, Inheritance, Variation, Heredity, Artificial selection
Teaches concepts: concept:artificial_selection (Artificial Selection), concept:genetics (Genetics), concept:inheritance (Inheritance), concept:variation (Variation)
Requires concepts: concept:sexual_reproduction (Sexual Reproduction)
title: Principles of Inheritance and Variation

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Mendel’s Laws of Inheritance
Summary: This section introduces Gregor Mendel's pioneering experimental approach to understanding inheritance using hybridization experiments on garden peas. It details his use of statistical analysis, selection of fourteen true-breeding pea plant varieties, and the study of seven pairs of contrasting traits.
Key terms: Gregor Mendel, hybridisation experiments, laws of inheritance, true-breeding line, contrasting traits, self-pollination, cross pollination
Teaches concepts: concept:contrasting_traits (Contrasting Traits), concept:mendelian_hybridisation_experiments (Mendelian Hybridisation Experiments), concept:mendels_laws_of_inheritance (Mendel's Laws of Inheritance), concept:true_breeding_line (True-Breeding Line)
Requires concepts: concept:inheritance (Inheritance), concept:pollination (Pollination)
title: Mendel’s Laws of Inheritance

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Inheritance of One Gene
Summary: This section explains the inheritance of a single gene through Mendel's monohybrid crosses with pea plants. It introduces fundamental genetic concepts including genes, alleles, genotypes, phenotypes, Punnett squares, test crosses, and Mendel's laws of inheritance.
Key terms: Monohybrid Cross, Gene, Allele, Genotype, Phenotype, Punnett Square, Test Cross, Law of Dominance, Law of Segregation
Teaches concepts: concept:gene_and_allele (Gene and Allele), concept:genotype_and_phenotype (Genotype and Phenotype), concept:mendels_laws_of_inheritance (Mendel's Laws of Inheritance), concept:monohybrid_cross (Monohybrid Cross), concept:punnett_square (Punnett Square), concept:test_cross (Test Cross)
Requires concepts: concept:fertilization (Fertilization), concept:meiosis (Meiosis)
title: Inheritance of One Gene

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Law of Dominance
Summary: This section introduces Mendel's Law of Dominance, which explains how traits are controlled by paired discrete units called factors. It details how a dominant factor masks a recessive factor in a dissimilar pair, explaining the phenotypic outcomes of the F1 and F2 generations.
Key terms: Law of Dominance, Factors, Dominant, Recessive, F1 generation, F2 generation, 3:1 ratio
Teaches concepts: concept:dominant_and_recessive_factors (Dominant and Recessive Factors), concept:factors (Factors), concept:law_of_dominance (Law of Dominance)
Requires concepts: concept:f1_and_f2_generations (F1 and F2 Generations), concept:monohybrid_cross (Monohybrid Cross)
title: Law of Dominance

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Law of Segregation
Summary: This section explains Mendel's Law of Segregation, which states that allele pairs separate during gamete formation so that each gamete carries only one allele. It emphasizes that alleles do not blend and describes how homozygous and heterozygous parents produce gametes.
Key terms: Law of Segregation, Alleles, Gamete formation, Homozygous, Heterozygous
Teaches concepts: concept:gamete_production_in_homozygotes_and_heterozygotes (Gamete Production in Homozygotes and Heterozygotes), concept:law_of_segregation (Law of Segregation)
Requires concepts: concept:alleles (Alleles), concept:f1_and_f2_generations (F1 and F2 Generations), concept:homozygous_and_heterozygous (Homozygous and Heterozygous)
title: Law of Segregation

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Incomplete Dominance
Summary: This section explains incomplete dominance, where the F1 generation exhibits an intermediate phenotype between those of the two parents, as demonstrated by flower color inheritance in snapdragons. It also provides a molecular explanation of dominance, describing how mutations modify alleles to produce functional, non-functional, or no enzymes, thereby determining dominant and recessive traits.
Key terms: Incomplete Dominance, Snapdragon, Phenotypic Ratio, Genotypic Ratio, Dominant Allele, Recessive Allele
Teaches concepts: concept:incomplete_dominance (Incomplete Dominance), concept:incomplete_dominance_phenotypic_ratio (Incomplete Dominance Phenotypic Ratio), concept:molecular_basis_of_dominance (Molecular Basis of Dominance)
Requires concepts: concept:allele (Allele), concept:genotype_and_phenotype (Genotype and Phenotype), concept:mendelian_monohybrid_cross (Mendelian Monohybrid Cross)
title: Incomplete Dominance

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Co-dominance
Summary: This section explains co-dominance using the human ABO blood grouping system, which also serves as an example of multiple alleles. It further discusses how dominance is a relative feature rather than an autonomous property of a gene, depending on the specific phenotype under examination.
Key terms: Co-dominance, ABO blood grouping, Multiple alleles, Gene I, Dominance, Phenotype
Teaches concepts: concept:abo_blood_grouping (ABO Blood Grouping), concept:co_dominance (Co-dominance), concept:dominance_as_a_relative_feature (Dominance as a Relative Feature), concept:multiple_alleles (Multiple Alleles)
Requires concepts: concept:allele (Allele), concept:dominance (Dominance)
title: Co-dominance

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Inheritance of Two Genes
Summary: This section explains the inheritance of two genes through Mendel's dihybrid crosses in pea plants, using seed color and shape as examples. It demonstrates how traits segregate independently in the F2 generation, resulting in a characteristic 9:3:3:1 phenotypic ratio.
Key terms: Dihybrid Cross, Independent Segregation, Phenotypic Ratio, Punnett Square, F1 Generation, F2 Generation
Teaches concepts: concept:dihybrid_cross (Dihybrid Cross), concept:dihybrid_phenotypic_ratio (Dihybrid Phenotypic Ratio), concept:independent_segregation_of_traits (Independent Segregation of Traits)
Requires concepts: concept:dominant_and_recessive_alleles (Dominant and Recessive Alleles), concept:monohybrid_cross (Monohybrid Cross), concept:punnett_square (Punnett Square)
title: Inheritance of Two Genes

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Law of Independent Assortment
Summary: This section explains Mendel's Law of Independent Assortment, which states that the segregation of one pair of traits is independent of another during gamete formation. It demonstrates how the 9:3:3:1 dihybrid ratio is derived from two independent 3:1 monohybrid ratios and details the genetic mechanism of gamete production in dihybrids.
Key terms: Law of Independent Assortment, Dihybrid Ratio, Independent Segregation, Gametes, Genotype, Phenotype
Teaches concepts: concept:dihybrid_ratio_derivation (Dihybrid Ratio Derivation), concept:independent_segregation_in_gametes (Independent Segregation in Gametes), concept:law_of_independent_assortment (Law of Independent Assortment)
Requires concepts: concept:dihybrid_cross (Dihybrid Cross), concept:meiosis (Meiosis), concept:monohybrid_ratio (Monohybrid Ratio), concept:punnett_square (Punnett Square)
title: Law of Independent Assortment

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Chromosomal Theory of Inheritance
Summary: This section details the historical rediscovery of Mendel's laws and the formulation of the Chromosomal Theory of Inheritance by Sutton and Boveri. It explains how the parallel behavior of chromosomes and genes during meiosis provides the physical basis for Mendelian inheritance.
Key terms: Chromosomal Theory of Inheritance, Chromosomes, Sutton and Boveri, Parallelism, Independent Assortment
Teaches concepts: concept:chromosomal_theory_of_inheritance (Chromosomal Theory of Inheritance), concept:chromosomes (Chromosomes), concept:independent_assortment_of_chromosomes (Independent Assortment of Chromosomes), concept:parallelism_between_genes_and_chromosomes (Parallelism Between Genes and Chromosomes)
Requires concepts: concept:meiosis (Meiosis), concept:mendels_laws_of_inheritance (Mendel's Laws of Inheritance)
title: Chromosomal Theory of Inheritance

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Linkag e an d Recombinatio n
Summary: This section details T.H. Morgan's experimental verification of the chromosomal theory of inheritance using Drosophila melanogaster, which led to the discovery of linkage and recombination. It explains how linkage prevents independent assortment and how recombination frequencies are utilized to construct genetic maps.
Key terms: Linkage, Recombination, Drosophila melanogaster, Genetic mapping, Recombination frequency, Dihybrid cross
Teaches concepts: concept:drosophila_melanogaster_as_a_model_organism (Drosophila melanogaster as a Model Organism), concept:genetic_mapping (Genetic Mapping), concept:linkage (Linkage), concept:recombination (Recombination), concept:strength_of_linkage (Strength of Linkage)
Requires concepts: concept:chromosomal_theory_of_inheritance (Chromosomal Theory of Inheritance), concept:dihybrid_cross (Dihybrid Cross), concept:law_of_independent_assortment (Law of Independent Assortment)
title: Linkag e an d Recombinatio n

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Polygenic Inheritance
Summary: This section introduces polygenic inheritance, where traits are controlled by multiple genes and exhibit a continuous gradient of phenotypes rather than distinct alternate forms. It explains how the additive effect of alleles and environmental factors influence these traits, using human skin color and height as primary examples.
Key terms: Polygenic inheritance, Polygenic traits, Additive effect, Continuous variation, Environmental influence
Teaches concepts: concept:additive_allelic_effect (Additive Allelic Effect), concept:polygenic_inheritance (Polygenic Inheritance)
Requires concepts: concept:alleles (Alleles), concept:genotype_and_phenotype (Genotype and Phenotype), concept:mendelian_inheritance (Mendelian Inheritance)
title: Polygenic Inheritance

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Pleiotropy
Summary: This section introduces pleiotropy, a genetic phenomenon where a single gene influences multiple phenotypic traits. It explains the underlying metabolic mechanism and illustrates this concept using the human genetic disorder phenylketonuria.
Key terms: pleiotropy, pleiotropic gene, phenylketonuria, phenylalanine hydroxylase, metabolic pathways, phenotype
Teaches concepts: concept:phenylketonuria (Phenylketonuria), concept:pleiotropy (Pleiotropy)
Requires concepts: concept:gene (Gene), concept:mutation (Mutation), concept:phenotype (Phenotype)
title: Pleiotropy

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Sex Determination
Summary: This section explains the genetic and chromosomal basis of sex determination, tracing its discovery from Henking's identification of the X body. It details different mechanisms of sex determination, including XO, XY, and ZW systems, and distinguishes between male and female heterogamety.
Key terms: Sex Determination, X-chromosome, XO type, XY type, Male Heterogamety, Female Heterogamety, ZW system
Teaches concepts: concept:female_heterogamety (Female Heterogamety), concept:male_heterogamety (Male Heterogamety), concept:x_chromosome (X-chromosome), concept:xo_sex_determination (XO Sex Determination), concept:xy_sex_determination (XY Sex Determination)
Requires concepts: concept:chromosomes (Chromosomes), concept:gametes (Gametes)
title: Sex Determination

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Sex Determination in Humans
Summary: This section explains the XY mechanism of sex determination in humans, where females have XX sex chromosomes and males have XY. It details how the genetic makeup of the sperm determines the sex of the offspring with equal probability.
Key terms: XY sex determination, autosomes, sex chromosomes, spermatogenesis, fertilization
Teaches concepts: concept:autosomes (Autosomes), concept:male_heterogamety_in_humans (Male Heterogamety in Humans), concept:sex_chromosomes (Sex Chromosomes), concept:xy_sex_determination_in_humans (XY sex determination in humans)
Requires concepts: concept:chromosomes (Chromosomes), concept:spermatogenesis (Spermatogenesis)
title: Sex Determination in Humans

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Sex Determination in Honey Bee
Summary: This section explains the haplodiploid sex-determination system in honey bees, where sex is determined by the number of chromosome sets. Females are diploid and develop from fertilized eggs, while males are haploid and develop from unfertilized eggs via parthenogenesis.
Key terms: haplodiploid system, parthenogenesis, diploid, haploid, drones, mitosis
Teaches concepts: concept:haplodiploid_sex_determination_system (Haplodiploid Sex-Determination System), concept:parthenogenesis_in_honey_bees (Parthenogenesis in Honey Bees)
Requires concepts: concept:fertilization (Fertilization), concept:haploid_and_diploid (Haploid and Diploid), concept:mitosis_and_meiosis (Mitosis and Meiosis)
title: Sex Determination in Honey Bee

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Mutation
Summary: This section introduces mutation as a key driver of genetic variation, defining it as an alteration in DNA sequences that impacts genotype and phenotype. It details different types of mutations, including chromosomal aberrations, point mutations, and frame-shift mutations, and identifies physical and chemical mutagens that induce these changes.
Key terms: Mutation, Chromosomal Aberrations, Point Mutation, Frame-shift Mutation, Mutagens
Teaches concepts: concept:chromosomal_aberrations (Chromosomal Aberrations), concept:frame_shift_mutation (Frame-shift Mutation), concept:mutagens (Mutagens), concept:mutation (Mutation), concept:point_mutation (Point Mutation)
Requires concepts: concept:chromosomes_and_genes (Chromosomes and Genes), concept:dna_structure (DNA Structure), concept:genotype_and_phenotype (Genotype and Phenotype)
title: Mutation

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Genetic Disorders
Summary: This section introduces genetic disorders in humans, classifying them into Mendelian and chromosomal disorders. It explains pedigree analysis as a tool to trace inheritance patterns and details the genetic and molecular mechanisms of major Mendelian disorders, including colour blindness, haemophilia, sickle-cell anaemia, phenylketonuria, and thalassemia.
Key terms: Pedigree Analysis, Mendelian Disorders, Colour Blindness, Haemophilia, Sickle-cell anaemia, Phenylketonuria, Thalassemia
Teaches concepts: concept:colour_blindness (Colour Blindness), concept:haemophilia (Haemophilia), concept:mendelian_disorders (Mendelian Disorders), concept:pedigree_analysis (Pedigree Analysis), concept:sickle_cell_anaemia (Sickle-cell Anaemia), concept:thalassemia (Thalassemia)
Requires concepts: concept:genetic_code_and_protein_synthesis (Genetic Code and Protein Synthesis), concept:mendelian_inheritance (Mendelian Inheritance), concept:sex_chromosomes_and_autosomes (Sex Chromosomes and Autosomes)
title: Genetic Disorders

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:4
Section: Chromosomal Disorders
Summary: This section explains chromosomal disorders arising from abnormal chromosome numbers or arrangements. It defines aneuploidy and polyploidy, and describes the genetic basis and physical characteristics of Down's, Klinefelter's, and Turner's syndromes.
Key terms: Chromosomal disorders, Aneuploidy, Polyploidy, Trisomy, Monosomy, Karyotype
Teaches concepts: concept:aneuploidy (Aneuploidy), concept:chromosomal_disorders (Chromosomal Disorders), concept:downs_syndrome (Down's Syndrome), concept:klinefelters_syndrome (Klinefelter's Syndrome), concept:polyploidy (Polyploidy), concept:turners_syndrome (Turner's Syndrome)
Requires concepts: concept:cell_division (Cell Division), concept:chromosomes (Chromosomes)
title: Chromosomal Disorders

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: The DNA
Summary: This section introduces DNA and RNA as the two primary nucleic acids, explaining their roles as genetic material and functional molecules. It also defines DNA length in terms of nucleotide or base pair count, providing characteristic examples from various organisms.
Key terms: DNA, RNA, Nucleic acids, Deoxyribonucleotides, Base pairs, Genomics
Teaches concepts: concept:dna_deoxyribonucleic_acid (DNA (Deoxyribonucleic acid)), concept:dna_length (DNA Length), concept:rna_ribonucleic_acid (RNA (Ribonucleic acid))
Requires concepts: concept:haploid (Haploid), concept:nucleotides (Nucleotides)
title: The DNA

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Structure of Polynucleotide Chain
Summary: This section explains the chemical structure of polynucleotide chains, detailing the components of nucleotides and their linkages. It describes the structural features of the DNA double helix, including complementary base pairing, antiparallel polarity, and the Central Dogma of molecular biology.
Key terms: Nucleotide, Phosphodiester linkage, Double Helix, Chargaff's Rule, Complementary base pairing, Central Dogma
Teaches concepts: concept:chargaffs_rule (Chargaff's Rule), concept:complementary_base_pairing (Complementary Base Pairing), concept:dna_double_helix_model (DNA Double Helix Model), concept:nucleotide_structure_and_linkages (Nucleotide Structure and Linkages), concept:polynucleotide_chain_polarity (Polynucleotide Chain Polarity), concept:the_central_dogma (The Central Dogma)
Requires concepts: concept:biomolecules (Biomolecules), concept:chemical_bonding (Chemical Bonding)
title: Structure of Polynucleotide Chain

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Packaging of DNA Helix
Summary: This section explains the packaging mechanisms of the long DNA double helix in both prokaryotes and eukaryotes. It details the formation of nucleosomes from histones, the organization of chromatin into 'beads-on-string' structures, and the distinction between transcriptionally active euchromatin and inactive heterochromatin.
Key terms: nucleoid, histones, histone octamer, nucleosome, chromatin, euchromatin, heterochromatin, Non-histone Chromosomal proteins
Teaches concepts: concept:chromatin (Chromatin), concept:euchromatin (Euchromatin), concept:heterochromatin (Heterochromatin), concept:histones (Histones), concept:nucleoid (Nucleoid), concept:nucleosome (Nucleosome)
Requires concepts: concept:dna_double_helix_structure (DNA Double Helix Structure), concept:prokaryotic_and_eukaryotic_cell_differences (Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic Cell Differences), concept:transcription (Transcription)
title: Packaging of DNA Helix

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: DNA Fingerprinting
Summary: This section introduces DNA fingerprinting, a rapid technique used to compare DNA sequences among individuals by identifying variations in repetitive DNA. It details the role of DNA polymorphism, satellite DNA, and Variable Number of Tandem Repeats (VNTRs), alongside the experimental methodology and forensic applications of this technology.
Key terms: DNA Fingerprinting, Repetitive DNA, Satellite DNA, DNA Polymorphism, Variable Number of Tandem Repeats, VNTR, Southern Blotting
Teaches concepts: concept:dna_fingerprinting (DNA Fingerprinting), concept:dna_polymorphism (DNA Polymorphism), concept:methodology_of_dna_fingerprinting (Methodology of DNA Fingerprinting), concept:repetitive_and_satellite_dna (Repetitive and Satellite DNA), concept:variable_number_of_tandem_repeats (Variable Number of Tandem Repeats)
Requires concepts: concept:gel_electrophoresis (Gel Electrophoresis), concept:mutation (Mutation)
title: DNA Fingerprinting

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: The Search for Genetic Material
Summary: This section details the historical search for the genetic material, focusing on Frederick Griffith's transformation experiments with Streptococcus pneumoniae. It also covers the subsequent biochemical characterization by Avery, MacLeod, and McCarty, which experimentally demonstrated that DNA is the transforming principle.
Key terms: Transforming Principle, Streptococcus pneumoniae, S strain, R strain, Avery, MacLeod, and McCarty, DNase
Teaches concepts: concept:biochemical_characterisation_of_transforming_principle (Biochemical Characterisation of Transforming Principle), concept:griffiths_transforming_principle (Griffith's Transforming Principle), concept:s_strain_and_r_strain_of_streptococcus_pneumoniae (S strain and R strain of Streptococcus pneumoniae)
Requires concepts: concept:chromosomes (Chromosomes), concept:dna_deoxyribonucleic_acid (DNA (Deoxyribonucleic acid))
title: The Search for Genetic Material

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: The Genetic Material is DNA
Summary: This section describes the Hershey-Chase experiment (1952), which provided the definitive proof that DNA is the genetic material. By using radioactive isotopes of phosphorus and sulfur to label bacteriophages, they demonstrated that only viral DNA enters E. coli cells during infection.
Key terms: Hershey-Chase experiment, bacteriophage, radioactive phosphorus, radioactive sulfur, E. coli, blending, centrifugation
Teaches concepts: concept:bacteriophage (Bacteriophage), concept:hershey_chase_experiment (Hershey-Chase Experiment), concept:infection_blending_and_centrifugation (Infection, Blending, and Centrifugation), concept:radioactive_labeling_of_biomolecules (Radioactive Labeling of Biomolecules)
Requires concepts: concept:bacteria (Bacteria), concept:dna (DNA), concept:proteins (Proteins)
title: The Genetic Material is DNA

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Properties of Genetic Material (DNA versus RNA)
Summary: This section establishes the criteria for a molecule to act as genetic material and compares DNA and RNA. It explains that while DNA is preferred for the stable, long-term storage of genetic information, RNA is better suited for its transmission and direct protein synthesis.
Key terms: genetic material, replication, chemical stability, mutation, thymine, uracil, 2'-OH group, protein synthesis
Teaches concepts: concept:criteria_for_genetic_material (Criteria for Genetic Material), concept:dna_and_rna_stability_comparison (DNA and RNA Stability Comparison), concept:rna_mutability_and_evolution (RNA Mutability and Evolution), concept:storage_versus_transmission_of_genetic_information (Storage versus Transmission of Genetic Information)
Requires concepts: concept:griffiths_transforming_principle (Griffith's Transforming Principle), concept:structure_of_nucleic_acids (Structure of Nucleic Acids)
title: Properties of Genetic Material (DNA versus RNA)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: RNA World
Summary: This section introduces the RNA World hypothesis, highlighting RNA as the first genetic material and a biological catalyst. It explains how DNA evolved from RNA through chemical modifications that provided greater stability and repair mechanisms.
Key terms: RNA World, ribozymes, genetic material, DNA evolution, chemical stability
Teaches concepts: concept:evolution_of_dna (Evolution of DNA), concept:ribozymes (Ribozymes), concept:rna_as_the_first_genetic_material (RNA as the First Genetic Material)
Requires concepts: concept:catalysis_and_enzymes (Catalysis and Enzymes), concept:genetic_material (Concept of Genetic Material), concept:structure_of_dna (Structure of DNA)
title: RNA World

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Replication
Summary: This section introduces the semiconservative model of DNA replication proposed by Watson and Crick. It explains how the two strands of the DNA double helix separate to act as templates for synthesizing new complementary strands.
Key terms: semiconservative DNA replication, DNA replication, template strand, complementary strand, Watson and Crick
Teaches concepts: concept:semiconservative_dna_replication (Semiconservative DNA Replication)
Requires concepts: concept:complementary_base_pairing (Complementary Base Pairing), concept:dna_double_helix_structure (DNA Double Helix Structure)
title: Replication

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: The Experimental Proof
Summary: This section describes the experimental validation of semiconservative DNA replication. It details Meselson and Stahl's density-gradient centrifugation experiment using E. coli and Taylor's chromosome replication experiment using radioactive thymidine.
Key terms: Semiconservative replication, Meselson and Stahl's experiment, Cesium chloride density gradient centrifugation, Radioactive thymidine, Vicia faba
Teaches concepts: concept:cesium_chloride_density_gradient_centrifugation (Cesium Chloride Density Gradient Centrifugation), concept:meselson_and_stahls_experiment (Meselson and Stahl's Experiment), concept:semiconservative_dna_replication (Semiconservative DNA Replication), concept:taylors_experiment (Taylor's Experiment)
Requires concepts: concept:dna_double_helix_structure (DNA Double Helix Structure), concept:isotopes (Isotopes)
title: The Experimental Proof

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: The Machinery and the Enzymes
Summary: This section details the enzymatic machinery and energetics of DNA replication, focusing on the primary role of DNA-dependent DNA polymerase. It explains the formation of the replication fork, the mechanisms of continuous and discontinuous synthesis, the necessity of an origin of replication, and the coordination of replication with the eukaryotic cell cycle.
Key terms: DNA-dependent DNA polymerase, Deoxyribonucleoside triphosphates, Replication fork, DNA ligase, Origin of replication, Polyploidy
Teaches concepts: concept:deoxyribonucleoside_triphosphates_dntps (Deoxyribonucleoside Triphosphates (dNTPs)), concept:dna_dependent_dna_polymerase (DNA-dependent DNA Polymerase), concept:dna_ligase (DNA Ligase), concept:origin_of_replication (Origin of Replication), concept:polyploidy (Polyploidy), concept:replication_fork (Replication Fork)
Requires concepts: concept:cell_cycle (Cell Cycle), concept:dna_double_helix_structure (DNA Double Helix Structure)
title: The Machinery and the Enzymes

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Transcription
Summary: This section defines transcription as the selective copying of genetic information from a single DNA strand into a complementary RNA strand. It also explains the evolutionary and functional reasons why only one of the two DNA strands is transcribed.
Key terms: Transcription, Principle of complementarity, Uracil, Double-stranded RNA, Translation
Teaches concepts: concept:principle_of_complementarity_in_transcription (Principle of Complementarity in Transcription), concept:single_strand_transcription_selectivity (Single-Strand Transcription Selectivity), concept:transcription (Transcription)
Requires concepts: concept:dna_replication (DNA Replication), concept:translation (Translation)
title: Transcription

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Transcription Unit
Summary: This section defines the structure and components of a transcription unit in DNA, which includes the promoter, structural gene, and terminator. It explains the functional distinction between the template and coding strands based on their polarity and the direction of transcription.
Key terms: Transcription Unit, Promoter, Structural gene, Terminator, Template strand, Coding strand, RNA polymerase
Teaches concepts: concept:coding_strand (Coding Strand), concept:promoter (Promoter), concept:template_strand (Template Strand), concept:terminator (Terminator), concept:transcription_unit (Transcription Unit)
Requires concepts: concept:dna_polarity (DNA Polarity), concept:rna_polymerase (RNA Polymerase), concept:transcription (Transcription)
title: Transcription Unit

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Transcription Unit and the Gene
Summary: This section defines the gene as the functional unit of inheritance and introduces the concept of a cistron, distinguishing between monocistronic and polycistronic transcription units. It also explains the split-gene arrangement in eukaryotes, highlighting the differences between coding exons and non-coding introns.
Key terms: gene, cistron, monocistronic, polycistronic, split-gene arrangement, exons, introns
Teaches concepts: concept:cistron (Cistron), concept:exons_and_introns (Exons and Introns), concept:gene (Gene), concept:monocistronic_and_polycistronic_transcription_units (Monocistronic and Polycistronic Transcription Units), concept:split_gene_arrangement (Split-gene Arrangement)
Requires concepts: concept:dna_structure (DNA Structure), concept:eukaryotes_and_prokaryotes (Eukaryotes and Prokaryotes), concept:transcription (Transcription)
title: Transcription Unit and the Gene

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Types of RNA and the process of Transcription
Summary: This section details the three major types of RNA (mRNA, tRNA, and rRNA) and explains the mechanism of transcription in both prokaryotes and eukaryotes. It describes the stages of initiation, elongation, and termination, highlighting the roles of sigma and rho factors in bacteria, and post-transcriptional modifications like splicing, capping, and tailing in eukaryotes.
Key terms: mRNA, tRNA, rRNA, RNA polymerase, initiation factor, termination factor, hnRNA, splicing, capping, tailing
Teaches concepts: concept:bacterial_transcription (Bacterial Transcription), concept:eukaryotic_rna_polymerases (Eukaryotic RNA Polymerases), concept:initiation_and_termination_factors (Initiation and Termination Factors), concept:post_transcriptional_processing (Post-transcriptional Processing), concept:types_of_rna (Types of RNA)
Requires concepts: concept:complementarity_rule (Complementarity Rule), concept:transcription (Transcription), concept:translation (Translation)
title: Types of RNA and the process of Transcription

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Genetic Code
Summary: This section explains the genetic code, detailing how triplet codons translate nucleotide sequences into amino acids. It covers the experimental deciphering, key features of the code (such as degeneracy and universality), the impact of point and frameshift mutations, and the role of tRNA as an adapter molecule.
Key terms: Genetic Code, Triplet Codon, Degeneracy, Point Mutation, Frameshift Mutation, tRNA, Anticodon
Teaches concepts: concept:degeneracy_of_the_genetic_code (Degeneracy of the Genetic Code), concept:frameshift_mutation (Frameshift Mutation), concept:point_mutation (Point Mutation), concept:triplet_codon (Triplet Codon), concept:trna_transfer_rna (tRNA (Transfer RNA))
Requires concepts: concept:nucleic_acids_and_proteins (Nucleic Acids and Proteins), concept:transcription (Transcription), concept:translation (Translation)
title: Genetic Code

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Translation
Summary: This section explains translation, the process of synthesizing polypeptide chains from mRNA templates. It details the activation of amino acids, the catalytic role of ribosomes, and the three stages of translation: initiation, elongation, and termination.
Key terms: Translation, Aminoacylation of tRNA, Ribosome, Untranslated Regions, Initiation, Elongation, Termination
Teaches concepts: concept:aminoacylation_of_trna (Aminoacylation of tRNA), concept:ribosome (Ribosome), concept:stages_of_translation (Stages of Translation), concept:translation (Translation), concept:untranslated_regions_utrs (Untranslated Regions (UTRs))
Requires concepts: concept:genetic_code (Genetic Code), concept:mrna (mRNA), concept:trna (tRNA)
title: Translation

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Regulation of Gene Expression
Summary: This section explains the multi-level regulation of gene expression in eukaryotes and prokaryotes, driven by metabolic, physiological, and environmental conditions. It introduces the mechanisms of prokaryotic transcriptional control involving promoters, operators, activators, and repressors within operons.
Key terms: Gene expression, Splicing, Beta-galactosidase, Operon, Operator, Repressor, Activator
Teaches concepts: concept:activators_and_repressors (Activators and Repressors), concept:environmental_and_metabolic_gene_regulation (Environmental and Metabolic Gene Regulation), concept:eukaryotic_gene_regulation_levels (Eukaryotic Gene Regulation Levels), concept:operators_and_operons (Operators and Operons), concept:prokaryotic_transcriptional_regulation (Prokaryotic Transcriptional Regulation)
Requires concepts: concept:prokaryotic_and_eukaryotic_cell_structure (Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic Cell Structure), concept:transcription (Transcription), concept:translation (Translation)
title: Regulation of Gene Expression

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: The Lac operon
Summary: This section explains the structure, function, and regulation of the lac operon in bacteria. It details how the regulatory i gene and structural genes (z, y, a) coordinate lactose metabolism through negative regulation by a repressor and activation by an inducer.
Key terms: lac operon, repressor, inducer, beta-galactosidase, negative regulation
Teaches concepts: concept:inducer (Inducer), concept:lac_operon (Lac operon), concept:negative_regulation (Negative regulation), concept:repressor_protein (Repressor protein), concept:structural_genes_of_the_lac_operon (Structural genes of the lac operon)
Requires concepts: concept:promoter (Promoter), concept:transcription (Transcription)
title: The Lac operon

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Human Genome Project
Summary: The Human Genome Project (HGP) was a massive international scientific initiative launched in 1990 to sequence the entire human genome, completed in 2003. It utilized methodologies like Expressed Sequence Tags (ESTs) and Sequence Annotation, relying on cloning vectors (BACs and YACs) and automated Sanger sequencing. The project catalyzed the field of bioinformatics to manage and analyze the vast amount of genomic data generated.
Key terms: Human Genome Project, Bioinformatics, Expressed Sequence Tags, Sequence Annotation, Bacterial Artificial Chromosomes, Yeast Artificial Chromosomes, Sanger sequencing
Teaches concepts: concept:bac_and_yac_vectors (BAC and YAC Vectors), concept:bioinformatics (Bioinformatics), concept:expressed_sequence_tags (Expressed Sequence Tags), concept:human_genome_project (Human Genome Project), concept:sanger_sequencing_method (Sanger Sequencing Method), concept:sequence_annotation (Sequence Annotation)
Requires concepts: concept:cloning_vectors_and_recombinant_dna (Cloning Vectors and Recombinant DNA), concept:dna_structure_and_base_pairing (DNA Structure and Base Pairing)
title: Human Genome Project

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Salient Features of Human Genome
Summary: This section outlines the key observations and salient features of the human genome discovered through the Human Genome Project. It details statistics on genome size, gene count, protein-coding regions, repetitive DNA sequences, and single nucleotide polymorphisms.
Key terms: Human Genome, Dystrophin, Repetitive sequences, Single nucleotide polymorphism, SNPs, Chromosome 1
Teaches concepts: concept:repetitive_sequences (Repetitive Sequences), concept:salient_features_of_the_human_genome (Salient Features of the Human Genome), concept:single_nucleotide_polymorphism (Single Nucleotide Polymorphism)
Requires concepts: concept:gene (Gene), concept:human_genome_project (Human Genome Project), concept:nucleotide (Nucleotide)
title: Salient Features of Human Genome

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:5
Section: Applications and Future Challenges
Summary: This section outlines the transformative impact of the Human Genome sequence on biological research, shifting the focus from studying individual genes to analyzing entire genomes systematically. It highlights how high-throughput technologies enable the study of interconnected gene and protein networks to understand biological systems.
Key terms: Human Genome sequence, high-throughput technologies, systematic biological research, genome analysis, transcripts
Teaches concepts: concept:systematic_biological_research (Systematic Biological Research), concept:whole_genome_sequencing_applications (Whole-Genome Sequencing Applications)
Requires concepts: concept:gene_expression_and_transcription (Gene Expression and Transcription), concept:genome (Genome), concept:human_genome_project (Human Genome Project)
title: Applications and Future Challenges

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:6
Section: Origin of Life
Summary: This section covers the origin of the universe and Earth, detailing early atmospheric conditions and theories on how life began. It contrasts historical ideas like spontaneous generation and panspermia with Louis Pasteur's biogenesis, and explains chemical evolution supported by the Oparin-Haldane hypothesis and Miller's experiment.
Key terms: Evolutionary Biology, Big Bang Theory, Panspermia, Spontaneous Generation, Biogenesis, Chemical Evolution, Miller's Experiment
Teaches concepts: concept:big_bang_theory (Big Bang Theory), concept:biogenesis (Biogenesis), concept:chemical_evolution (Chemical Evolution), concept:millers_experiment (Miller's Experiment), concept:spontaneous_generation (Spontaneous Generation)
Requires concepts: concept:gravitational_force (Gravitational Force), concept:organic_compounds (Organic compounds)
title: Origin of Life

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:6
Section: Evolution of Life Forms - A Theory
Summary: This section contrasts the traditional Theory of Special Creation with the scientific theory of gradual evolution proposed by Charles Darwin and Alfred Wallace. It explains how natural selection, driven by reproductive fitness and variation within populations, leads to the evolution of life forms sharing common ancestry over billions of years.
Key terms: Theory of Special Creation, Gradual Evolution, Reproductive Fitness, Natural Selection, Common Ancestors
Teaches concepts: concept:common_ancestry (Common Ancestry), concept:gradual_evolution (Gradual Evolution), concept:natural_selection (Natural Selection), concept:reproductive_fitness (Reproductive Fitness), concept:theory_of_special_creation (Theory of Special Creation)
Requires concepts: concept:population (Population)
title: Evolution of Life Forms - A Theory

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:6
Section: What are the Evidences for Evolution?
Summary: This section explores the diverse lines of evidence supporting the theory of evolution, including paleontological, embryological, comparative anatomical (homology and analogy), biochemical, and anthropogenic evidence. It highlights key concepts such as divergent and convergent evolution, and illustrates natural selection in action through the classic case of industrial melanism in moths.
Key terms: Fossils, Paleontological evidence, Homology, Divergent evolution, Analogy, Convergent evolution, Industrial melanism, Anthropogenic evolution
Teaches concepts: concept:analogy_and_convergent_evolution (Analogy and Convergent Evolution), concept:anthropogenic_evolution (Anthropogenic Evolution), concept:embryological_support_for_evolution (Embryological Support for Evolution), concept:homology_and_divergent_evolution (Homology and Divergent Evolution), concept:industrial_melanism (Industrial Melanism), concept:paleontological_evidence (Paleontological Evidence)
Requires concepts: concept:natural_selection (Natural Selection), concept:theory_of_evolution (Theory of Evolution)
title: What are the Evidences for Evolution?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:6
Section: What is Adaptive Radiation?
Summary: This section defines adaptive radiation as the evolutionary process where multiple species radiate from a common ancestor to adapt to diverse habitats. It illustrates this concept using Darwin's finches and Australian marsupials, and explains how multiple adaptive radiations in an isolated area lead to convergent evolution.
Key terms: Adaptive radiation, Darwin's finches, Australian marsupials, Convergent evolution, Placental mammals
Teaches concepts: concept:adaptive_radiation (Adaptive Radiation), concept:adaptive_radiation_of_australian_marsupials (Adaptive Radiation of Australian Marsupials), concept:convergent_evolution_via_adaptive_radiation (Convergent Evolution via Adaptive Radiation), concept:darwins_finches (Darwin's Finches)
Requires concepts: concept:natural_selection (Natural Selection), concept:speciation (Speciation)
title: What is Adaptive Radiation?

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:6
Section: Biological Evolution
Summary: This section explains the origin and mechanisms of biological evolution, focusing on Darwinian theory and its key concepts of branching descent and natural selection. It also discusses the genetic basis of fitness, Lamarck's theory of use and disuse, and the influence of Malthusian population principles on Darwin's work.
Key terms: natural selection, branching descent, Lamarckism, fitness, Malthusian theory
Teaches concepts: concept:biological_evolution (Biological Evolution), concept:darwinian_theory_of_evolution (Darwinian Theory of Evolution), concept:genetic_basis_of_fitness (Genetic Basis of Fitness), concept:lamarckism (Lamarckism), concept:malthusian_influence_on_natural_selection (Malthusian Influence on Natural Selection)
Requires concepts: concept:mendelian_genetics (Mendelian Genetics)
title: Biological Evolution

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:6
Section: Mechanism of Evolution
Summary: This section explains the mechanism of evolution through Hugo de Vries' Mutation Theory, contrasting it with Darwinian evolution. It introduces mutations as random, directionless, single-step large variations (saltation) that drive speciation.
Key terms: Mutation Theory, Hugo de Vries, saltation, speciation, Darwinian variation
Teaches concepts: concept:darwinian_variation (Darwinian Variation), concept:mutation_theory_of_evolution (Mutation Theory of Evolution), concept:saltation (Saltation)
Requires concepts: concept:darwins_theory_of_evolution (Darwin's Theory of Evolution), concept:speciation (Speciation)
title: Mechanism of Evolution

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:6
Section: Hardy-Weinberg Principle
Summary: This section introduces the Hardy-Weinberg principle, which states that allele frequencies in a population remain constant across generations under genetic equilibrium. It presents the mathematical representation of this equilibrium and details the evolutionary factors, including modes of natural selection, that disrupt it.
Key terms: Hardy-Weinberg principle, genetic equilibrium, gene pool, genetic drift, founder effect, natural selection
Teaches concepts: concept:genetic_drift_and_founder_effect (Genetic Drift and Founder Effect), concept:hardy_weinberg_equation (Hardy-Weinberg Equation), concept:hardy_weinberg_principle (Hardy-Weinberg Principle), concept:modes_of_natural_selection (Modes of Natural Selection)
Requires concepts: concept:alleles_and_genes (Alleles and Genes), concept:diploid_genotypes (Diploid Genotypes)
title: Hardy-Weinberg Principle

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:6
Section: A Brief Account of Evolution
Summary: This section outlines the major milestones in the evolutionary history of life on Earth, spanning from the origin of the first cellular forms and early plants to the diversification of vertebrates, reptiles, dinosaurs, and mammals. It highlights key transitions, such as the invasion of land by plants and animals, and the impact of geological events like continental drift on mammalian distribution.
Key terms: Coelacanth, Lobefins, Dinosaurs, Tyrannosaurus rex, Viviparous, Continental drift, Ichthyosaurs
Teaches concepts: concept:evolution_of_amphibians (Evolution of Amphibians), concept:evolution_of_land_plants (Evolution of Land Plants), concept:evolution_of_mammals (Evolution of Mammals), concept:evolution_of_reptiles_and_dinosaurs (Evolution of Reptiles and Dinosaurs), concept:origin_of_cellular_life (Origin of Cellular Life)
Requires concepts: concept:continental_drift (Continental Drift), concept:geological_time_scale (Geological Time Scale), concept:photosynthesis (Photosynthesis)
title: A Brief Account of Evolution

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:6
Section: Origin and Evolution of Man
Summary: This section traces the evolutionary lineage of humans from early primates like Dryopithecus and Ramapithecus to modern Homo sapiens, highlighting key hominid stages and their physical and behavioral traits. It also summarizes the fundamental principles of organic evolution, including natural selection and speciation.
Key terms: Dryopithecus, Ramapithecus, Australopithecines, Homo habilis, Homo erectus, Neanderthal man, Homo sapiens, brain capacity
Teaches concepts: concept:australopithecines (Australopithecines), concept:dryopithecus_and_ramapithecus (Dryopithecus and Ramapithecus), concept:homo_erectus (Homo erectus), concept:homo_habilis (Homo habilis), concept:homo_sapiens (Homo sapiens), concept:neanderthal_man (Neanderthal Man)
Requires concepts: concept:fossil_evidence (Fossil Evidence), concept:natural_selection (Natural Selection)
title: Origin and Evolution of Man

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:7
Section: Introduction to Human Health and Disease
Summary: This section introduces the concept of human health, tracing its transition from historical humor-based hypotheses to modern biological definitions. It outlines the factors affecting health, methods for maintaining wellness, and the classification of diseases into infectious and non-infectious categories.
Key terms: good humor hypothesis, health, antibody molecule, infectious diseases, non-infectious diseases
Teaches concepts: concept:antibody_molecule (Antibody Molecule), concept:good_humor_hypothesis (Good Humor Hypothesis), concept:infectious_diseases (Infectious Diseases), concept:modern_concept_of_health (Modern Concept of Health), concept:non_infectious_diseases (Non-infectious Diseases)
Requires concepts: concept:circulatory_system (Circulatory System), concept:immune_system (Immune system)
title: Introduction to Human Health and Disease

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:7
Section: Common Diseases in Humans
Summary: This section introduces common human diseases caused by various pathogens, including bacteria, viruses, fungi, protozoans, and helminths. It details their symptoms, modes of transmission, and life cycles, while outlining essential strategies for prevention, vector control, and immunization.
Key terms: Pathogens, Typhoid fever, Pneumonia, Common cold, Malaria, Plasmodium, Amoebiasis, Filariasis, Ringworms, Vector control
Teaches concepts: concept:bacterial_and_viral_human_diseases (Bacterial and Viral Human Diseases), concept:helminthic_and_fungal_diseases (Helminthic and Fungal Diseases), concept:pathogens_and_host_adaptation (Pathogens and Host Adaptation), concept:prevention_and_control_of_infectious_diseases (Prevention and Control of Infectious Diseases), concept:protozoan_diseases_and_plasmodium_life_cycle (Protozoan Diseases and Plasmodium Life Cycle)
Requires concepts: concept:classification_of_microorganisms (Classification of Microorganisms), concept:human_anatomy_and_physiology (Human Anatomy and Physiology)
title: Common Diseases in Humans

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:7
Section: Immunity
Summary: This section covers the human immune system, distinguishing between active and passive immunity, and explaining vaccination, allergies, and autoimmune diseases. It also details the roles of primary and secondary lymphoid organs, including MALT, in lymphocyte maturation and activation.
Key terms: Immunity, Active immunity, Passive immunity, Vaccination, Allergies, Auto Immunity, Lymphoid Organs, MALT
Teaches concepts: concept:active_and_passive_immunity (Active and Passive Immunity), concept:allergies_and_autoimmunity (Allergies and Autoimmunity), concept:immunity (Immunity), concept:lymphoid_organs (Lymphoid Organs), concept:vaccination_and_immunisation (Vaccination and Immunisation)
Requires concepts: concept:antibodies_and_antigens (Antibodies and Antigens), concept:lymphocytes (Lymphocytes), concept:pathogen (Pathogen)
title: Immunity

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:7
Section: Innate Immunity
Summary: This section introduces innate immunity as a non-specific defense system present at birth. It details the four types of barriers that prevent the entry and growth of foreign agents: physical, physiological, cellular, and cytokine barriers.
Key terms: Innate immunity, Physical barriers, Physiological barriers, Cellular barriers, Cytokine barriers, Interferons, Phagocytosis
Teaches concepts: concept:cellular_barriers (Cellular Barriers), concept:cytokine_barriers (Cytokine Barriers), concept:innate_immunity (Innate Immunity), concept:physical_barriers (Physical Barriers), concept:physiological_barriers (Physiological Barriers)
Requires concepts: concept:leukocytes (Leukocytes), concept:phagocytosis (Phagocytosis)
title: Innate Immunity

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:7
Section: Acquired Immunity
Summary: This section introduces acquired immunity, which is pathogen-specific and characterized by immunological memory. It details the roles of B-lymphocytes and T-lymphocytes, the structural composition of antibodies, and the distinction between humoral and cell-mediated immune responses.
Key terms: Acquired immunity, Primary response, Secondary response, Anamnestic response, B-lymphocytes, T-lymphocytes, Antibodies, Humoral immune response, Cell-mediated immunity
Teaches concepts: concept:acquired_immunity (Acquired Immunity), concept:antibody (Antibody), concept:cell_mediated_immunity (Cell-mediated immunity), concept:humoral_immune_response (Humoral Immune Response), concept:primary_and_secondary_immune_responses (Primary and Secondary Immune Responses)
Requires concepts: concept:lymphocytes (Lymphocytes), concept:pathogen (Pathogen)
title: Acquired Immunity

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:7
Section: AIDS
Summary: This section covers Acquired Immuno Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS), detailing its transmission routes, the replication mechanism of HIV within host cells, and its impact on helper T-lymphocytes. It also discusses the clinical manifestations, diagnosis via ELISA, and the critical role of prevention and social support in managing the disease.
Key terms: AIDS, HIV, Retrovirus, Reverse transcriptase, Helper T-lymphocytes, ELISA, NACO
Teaches concepts: concept:acquired_immuno_deficiency_syndrome_aids (Acquired Immuno Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS)), concept:elisa_enzyme_linked_immunosorbent_assay (ELISA (Enzyme-Linked Immunosorbent Assay)), concept:helper_t_lymphocyte_depletion (Helper T-lymphocyte Depletion), concept:hiv_replication_mechanism (HIV Replication Mechanism), concept:human_immunodeficiency_virus_hiv (Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV))
Requires concepts: concept:immune_system_and_lymphocytes (Immune System and Lymphocytes), concept:retroviruses_and_reverse_transcription (Retroviruses and Reverse Transcription)
title: AIDS

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:7
Section: Cancer
Summary: This section covers the biological basis of cancer, explaining how the breakdown of cell growth regulation and loss of contact inhibition lead to tumor formation. It distinguishes between benign and malignant tumors, identifies various physical, chemical, and biological carcinogens, and outlines modern methods for cancer detection, diagnosis, and treatment.
Key terms: Cancer, Contact inhibition, Benign tumor, Malignant tumor, Metastasis, Carcinogens, Oncogenes, Proto-oncogenes, Biopsy, Radiotherapy, Chemotherapy, Immunotherapy
Teaches concepts: concept:cancer_detection_and_diagnosis (Cancer Detection and Diagnosis), concept:cancer_treatment (Cancer Treatment), concept:carcinogens (Carcinogens), concept:contact_inhibition (Contact Inhibition), concept:metastasis (Metastasis), concept:oncogenes_and_proto_oncogenes (Oncogenes and Proto-oncogenes)
Requires concepts: concept:cell_cycle_and_cell_division (Cell Cycle and Cell Division), concept:dna_structure_and_mutation (DNA Structure and Mutation), concept:immune_system_and_antibodies (Immune System and Antibodies)
title: Cancer

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:7
Section: Drugs and Alcohol Abuse
Summary: This section covers the classification, sources, and physiological effects of commonly abused substances, including opioids, cannabinoids, cocaine, hallucinogens, and tobacco. It explains how these drugs interact with specific receptors and neurotransmitters in the human body, leading to physical and psychological health impairments.
Key terms: Opioids, Cannabinoids, Cocaine, Hallucinogens, Nicotine, Drug Abuse
Teaches concepts: concept:cannabinoids (Cannabinoids), concept:cocaine (Cocaine), concept:drug_abuse (Drug Abuse), concept:hallucinogens (Hallucinogens), concept:opioids (Opioids), concept:tobacco_and_nicotine (Tobacco and Nicotine)
Requires concepts: concept:cardiovascular_system (Cardiovascular System), concept:central_nervous_system (Central Nervous System), concept:neurotransmitters (Neurotransmitters)
title: Drugs and Alcohol Abuse

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:7
Section: Adolescence and Drug/Alcohol Abuse
Summary: This section defines adolescence as a critical transitional phase between childhood and adulthood marked by significant biological and behavioral changes. It also examines the primary psychological, social, and environmental factors that motivate adolescents to experiment with drugs and alcohol.
Key terms: Adolescence, Substance Abuse, Peer Pressure, Vulnerability, Academic Pressure
Teaches concepts: concept:adolescence (Adolescence), concept:adolescent_substance_abuse (Adolescent Substance Abuse)
Requires concepts:
title: Adolescence and Drug/Alcohol Abuse

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:7
Section: Addiction and Dependence
Summary: This section explains the psychological and physiological mechanisms of substance abuse, focusing on addiction, tolerance, dependence, and withdrawal syndrome. It describes how repeated drug or alcohol use leads to receptor tolerance and physical dependence, resulting in severe withdrawal symptoms upon discontinuation.
Key terms: Addiction, Tolerance, Dependence, Withdrawal Syndrome, Receptors
Teaches concepts: concept:addiction (Addiction), concept:dependence (Dependence), concept:tolerance (Tolerance), concept:withdrawal_syndrome (Withdrawal Syndrome)
Requires concepts: concept:drugs_and_alcohol (Drugs and Alcohol), concept:receptors (Receptors)
title: Addiction and Dependence

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:7
Section: Effects of Drug/Alcohol Abuse
Summary: This section details the immediate and long-term physical, psychological, and social consequences of drug and alcohol abuse. It highlights warning signs in youth, the severe risks of intravenous drug use such as AIDS and Hepatitis B transmission, and the damaging side effects of anabolic steroid misuse in sports.
Key terms: drug abuse, alcohol abuse, cirrhosis, intravenous drug use, anabolic steroids, masculinisation
Teaches concepts: concept:anabolic_steroid_misuse (Anabolic Steroid Misuse), concept:cirrhosis (Cirrhosis), concept:immediate_health_consequences_of_substance_abuse (Immediate Health Consequences of Substance Abuse), concept:intravenous_drug_use_risks (Intravenous Drug Use Risks), concept:warning_signs_of_substance_abuse (Warning Signs of Substance Abuse)
Requires concepts: concept:aids (AIDS), concept:hepatitis_b (Hepatitis B)
title: Effects of Drug/Alcohol Abuse

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:7
Section: Prevention and Control
Summary: This section outlines strategies for the prevention and control of drug and alcohol abuse among adolescents. It highlights the critical roles of parents, teachers, and peers, and details specific measures such as counseling, recognizing danger signs, and seeking professional medical assistance.
Key terms: prevention, adolescence, peer pressure, counselling, danger signs, rehabilitation
Teaches concepts: concept:parental_and_teacher_responsibility_in_prevention (Parental and Teacher Responsibility in Prevention), concept:peer_pressure_management (Peer Pressure Management), concept:prevention_and_control_of_substance_abuse (Prevention and Control of Substance Abuse), concept:professional_and_medical_help_for_addiction (Professional and Medical Help for Addiction)
Requires concepts: concept:addiction_and_dependence (Addiction and Dependence), concept:adolescence_and_drug_alcohol_abuse (Adolescence and Drug/Alcohol Abuse)
title: Prevention and Control

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:8
Section: Introduction to Microbes
Summary: This section introduces microbes as omnipresent and highly diverse biological entities capable of surviving in extreme environments. It outlines the major groups of microbes, including bacteria, fungi, viruses, viroids, and prions, and explains how they can be cultured on nutritive media for scientific study.
Key terms: microbes, extreme environments, prions, microbial colonies, nutritive media, bacteriophage
Teaches concepts: concept:extreme_habitats_of_microbes (Extreme Habitats of Microbes), concept:microbes (Microbes), concept:microbial_colonies (Microbial Colonies), concept:prions (Prions)
Requires concepts: concept:biological_classification (Biological Classification), concept:viruses (Viruses)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:8
Section: Microbes in Household Products
Summary: This section explores the essential role of microorganisms in preparing everyday household food products. It details how bacteria and fungi are utilized in the production of curd, fermented foods like bread and dosa, traditional drinks like toddy, and various types of cheese.
Key terms: Lactic Acid Bacteria, Lactobacillus, Inoculum, Baker's yeast, Saccharomyces cerevisiae, Toddy, Swiss cheese, Roquefort cheese
Teaches concepts: concept:cheese_ripening_and_production (Cheese Ripening and Production), concept:fermented_dough_products (Fermented Dough Products), concept:lactic_acid_bacteria_lab (Lactic Acid Bacteria (LAB)), concept:toddy (Toddy)
Requires concepts: concept:fermentation (Fermentation), concept:microorganisms (Microorganisms)
title: Microbes in Household Products

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:8
Section: Microbes in Industrial Products
Summary: This section details the industrial applications of microbes in synthesizing beverages, antibiotics, enzymes, and bioactive molecules. It explains the role of large-scale fermentors and highlights specific microbial species used for commercial and medical purposes.
Key terms: Fermentors, Saccharomyces cerevisiae, Antibiotics, Penicillin, Streptokinase, Cyclosporin A, Statins
Teaches concepts: concept:antibiotics (Antibiotics), concept:bioactive_molecules (Bioactive Molecules), concept:brewers_yeast (Brewer's Yeast), concept:fermentors (Fermentors), concept:microbial_enzymes (Microbial Enzymes)
Requires concepts: concept:fermentation (Fermentation), concept:microorganisms (Microorganisms)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:8
Section: Microbes in Sewage Treatment
Summary: This section explains how municipal wastewater (sewage) is treated in Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) using natural heterotrophic microbes. It details the physical processes of primary treatment and the biological processes of secondary treatment, including the roles of aerobic flocs, Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) reduction, anaerobic sludge digestion, and biogas production.
Key terms: Sewage, Primary Treatment, Secondary Treatment, Flocs, Biochemical Oxygen Demand, Activated Sludge, Anaerobic Sludge Digesters, Biogas
Teaches concepts: concept:activated_sludge (Activated Sludge), concept:anaerobic_sludge_digestion (Anaerobic Sludge Digestion), concept:biochemical_oxygen_demand_bod (Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD)), concept:biogas (Biogas), concept:primary_treatment (Primary Treatment), concept:secondary_treatment (Secondary Treatment)
Requires concepts: concept:aerobic_and_anaerobic_microorganisms (Aerobic and Anaerobic Microorganisms), concept:pathogen (Pathogen)
title: Microbes in Sewage Treatment

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:8
Section: Microbes in Production of Biogas
Summary: This section explains the microbial production of biogas, a methane-rich fuel gas mixture generated by anaerobic bacteria known as methanogens. It describes the structure and operation of a typical biogas plant utilizing cattle dung slurry, alongside the institutional history of biogas technology development in India.
Key terms: Biogas, Methanogens, Methanobacterium, Biogas plant, Cattle dung, IARI, KVIC
Teaches concepts: concept:biogas (Biogas), concept:biogas_plant (Biogas Plant), concept:methanogens (Methanogens)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:8
Section: Microbes as Biocontrol Agents
Summary: This section introduces biocontrol as a sustainable alternative to chemical pesticides, highlighting organic farming's reliance on natural predation. It details specific biological control agents, including Bacillus thuringiensis, Trichoderma, and Baculoviruses, which target pests without harming non-target organisms.
Key terms: Biocontrol, Organic farming, Bacillus thuringiensis, Bt-cotton, Trichoderma, Baculoviruses, Integrated Pest Management
Teaches concepts: concept:bacillus_thuringiensis (Bacillus thuringiensis), concept:baculoviruses (Baculoviruses), concept:biocontrol (Biocontrol), concept:organic_farming (Organic Farming), concept:trichoderma (Trichoderma)
Requires concepts: concept:genetic_engineering (Genetic engineering)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:8
Section: Microbes as Biofertilisers
Summary: This section introduces biofertilisers as eco-friendly alternatives to chemical fertilisers in organic farming. It highlights how specific bacteria, fungi (mycorrhiza), and cyanobacteria enrich soil nutrients through nitrogen fixation and mineral absorption.
Key terms: Biofertilisers, Organic farming, Rhizobium, Mycorrhiza, Glomus, Cyanobacteria, Nitrogen fixation
Teaches concepts: concept:biofertilisers (Biofertilisers), concept:cyanobacteria_as_biofertilisers (Cyanobacteria as Biofertilisers), concept:mycorrhiza (Mycorrhiza), concept:nitrogen_fixing_bacteria (Nitrogen-fixing Bacteria), concept:organic_farming (Organic Farming)
Requires concepts: concept:nitrogen_cycle (Nitrogen Cycle), concept:symbiosis (Symbiosis)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:9

Summary: This section introduces biotechnology by tracing its historical foundation through the pioneering work of Herbert Boyer and Stanley Cohen on recombinant DNA. It defines biotechnology in both traditional and modern contexts, including the official definition provided by the European Federation of Biotechnology (EFB).

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:9
Section: Principles of Biotechnology
Summary: This section introduces the two core techniques of modern biotechnology: genetic engineering and bioprocess engineering. It explains the conceptual advantages of genetic engineering over traditional hybridization and details the historical construction of the first artificial recombinant DNA molecule.
Key terms: Genetic engineering, Bioprocess engineering, Recombinant DNA, Origin of replication, Plasmid, Cloning, Vector
Teaches concepts: concept:bioprocess_engineering (Bioprocess Engineering), concept:gene_cloning (Gene Cloning), concept:genetic_engineering (Genetic engineering), concept:origin_of_replication (Origin of Replication), concept:plasmid_vector (Plasmid Vector), concept:recombinant_dna (Recombinant DNA)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:9
Section: Tools of Recombinant DNA Technology
Summary: This section introduces the essential tools required to perform recombinant DNA technology. These key tools include restriction enzymes, polymerase enzymes, ligases, vectors, and the host organism.
Key terms: recombinant DNA technology, restriction enzymes, polymerase enzymes, ligases, vectors, host organism

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:9
Section: Restriction Enzymes
Summary: This section explains the discovery, naming, and mechanism of restriction enzymes, which cut DNA at specific palindromic sequences to produce sticky ends. It also describes how the resulting DNA fragments are separated using agarose gel electrophoresis, visualized, and purified via elution for recombinant DNA construction.
Key terms: Restriction endonuclease, Palindromic sequence, Sticky ends, DNA ligase, Gel electrophoresis, Agarose, Ethidium bromide, Elution
Teaches concepts: concept:elution (Elution), concept:gel_electrophoresis (Gel Electrophoresis), concept:palindromic_sequence (Palindromic Sequence), concept:recombinant_dna_formation (Recombinant DNA Formation), concept:restriction_endonucleases (Restriction Endonucleases), concept:sticky_ends (Sticky Ends)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:9
Section: Cloning Vectors
Summary: This section details the essential characteristics of cloning vectors, including the origin of replication, selectable markers, and cloning sites. It explains how recombinants are selected using antibiotic resistance or chromogenic substrates, and describes specialized vectors like the Ti plasmid and retroviruses for plants and animals.
Key terms: cloning vector, origin of replication, selectable marker, transformation, insertional inactivation, pBR322, Ti plasmid

text:

Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:9

Summary: This section outlines the techniques used to introduce recombinant DNA into host cells. It details chemical treatment and heat shock for bacterial transformation, alongside alternative methods such as micro-injection, biolistics (gene gun), and disarmed pathogen vectors.

Teaches concepts: concept:biolistics (Biolistics), concept:competent_cells (Competent Cells), concept:disarmed_pathogen_vectors (Disarmed Pathogen Vectors), concept:heat_shock_method (Heat Shock Method), concept:micro_injection (Micro-injection)

text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:9
Section: Processes of Recombinant DNA Technology
Summary: This section outlines the sequential steps involved in recombinant DNA technology. It covers the entire workflow from the initial isolation and fragmentation of DNA to the final extraction of the desired product.

Teaches concepts: concept:processes_of_recombinant_dna_technology (Processes of Recombinant DNA Technology)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:9
Section: Isolation of the Genetic Material (DNA)
Summary: This section describes the process of isolating and purifying DNA from various cell types. It details the enzymatic breakdown of cell walls and other macromolecules, followed by the precipitation and collection of pure DNA using chilled ethanol and spooling.
Key terms: DNA isolation, cell lysis, lysozyme, cellulase, chitinase, ribonuclease, protease, chilled ethanol, spooling

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:9
Section: Cutting of DNA at Specific Locations
Summary: This section explains the process of cutting DNA at specific locations using restriction enzymes and monitoring the digestion progress via agarose gel electrophoresis. It also details how the resulting gene of interest and cut vector DNA are joined using DNA ligase to form recombinant DNA.
Key terms: Restriction digestion, Agarose gel electrophoresis, DNA ligase, Recombinant DNA, Vector DNA
Teaches concepts: concept:ligation_of_dna (Ligation of DNA), concept:monitoring_dna_digestion (Monitoring DNA Digestion), concept:restriction_digestion (Restriction Digestion)
Requires concepts: concept:agarose_gel_electrophoresis (Agarose Gel Electrophoresis), concept:dna_ligase (DNA Ligase), concept:restriction_enzymes (Restriction Enzymes), concept:vector_dna (Vector DNA)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:9
Section: Amplification of Gene of Interest using PCR
Summary: This section explains the Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) process used to amplify a gene of interest in vitro. It details the three main steps of PCR—denaturation, annealing, and extension—and highlights the role of the thermostable Taq polymerase in achieving exponential DNA amplification.
Key terms: Polymerase Chain Reaction, PCR, Denaturation, Annealing, Extension, Taq polymerase, Primers, Thermus aquaticus
Teaches concepts: concept:annealing (Annealing), concept:denaturation (Denaturation), concept:extension (Extension), concept:polymerase_chain_reaction (Polymerase Chain Reaction), concept:taq_polymerase (Taq polymerase)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:9
Section: Insertion of Recombinant DNA into the Host Cell/Organism
Summary: This section explains how recombinant DNA is introduced into competent host cells and details the selection process of transformants using selectable markers, such as antibiotic resistance genes.
Key terms: recombinant DNA, competent cells, transformation, selectable marker, ampicillin resistance
Teaches concepts: concept:selectable_marker (Selectable Marker), concept:transformation (Transformation)
Requires concepts: concept:competent_host_cells (Competent Host Cells), concept:recombinant_dna (Recombinant DNA)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:9
Section: Obtaining the Foreign Gene Product
Summary: This section describes the methods used to obtain foreign gene products on both small and large scales. It explains the expression of recombinant proteins, the use of continuous culture systems to maintain exponential growth, and the design and operation of bioreactors, particularly stirred-tank bioreactors, for mass production.
Key terms: recombinant protein, heterologous host, continuous culture, bioreactor, stirred-tank bioreactor, sparged bioreactor
Teaches concepts: concept:bioreactor (Bioreactor), concept:continuous_culture_system (Continuous Culture System), concept:recombinant_protein (Recombinant Protein), concept:stirred_tank_bioreactor (Stirred-tank Bioreactor)

subject: biology
text:
Subject: biology
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:biology:12:9
Section: Downstream Processing
Summary: This section explains downstream processing, which encompasses the post-biosynthetic stages of separation, purification, and formulation of a product. It also highlights the necessity of clinical trials for drug formulations and strict quality control testing before marketing.
Key terms: downstream processing, separation, purification, preservatives, clinical trials, quality control testing
Teaches concepts: concept:downstream_processing (Downstream Processing), concept:quality_control_testing (Quality Control Testing)
Requires concepts: concept:biosynthetic_stage (Biosynthetic Stage)

text:

Section: Summary
Summary: This section provides a comprehensive summary of biotechnology, highlighting the role of genetic engineering in altering DNA chemistry. It outlines the key tools and sequential steps involved in recombinant DNA technology, concluding with downstream processing and the use of bioreactors for product purification.
Key terms: Biotechnology, Genetic engineering, Restriction endonucleases, DNA ligase, Vectors, Downstream processing, Bioreactors

Summary: This section traces the historical evolution of chemistry from ancient alchemical traditions to modern science, highlighting significant contributions from ancient Indian scholars, literature, and archaeological findings. It defines chemistry as the study of the composition, structure, properties, and transformations of matter, and introduces early concepts of atomic theory and metallurgy.
Key terms: Chemistry, Alchemy, Iatrochemistry, Rasayan Shastra, Paramānu, Bhasmas

Summary: This section introduces the central role of chemistry in connecting various scientific disciplines and its applications in industry, healthcare, and environmental protection. It highlights the synthesis of life-saving drugs, advanced materials, and eco-friendly alternatives to hazardous substances.

Teaches concepts: concept:advanced_materials_synthesis (Advanced Materials Synthesis), concept:environmental_chemistry_and_green_alternatives (Environmental Chemistry and Green Alternatives), concept:industrial_chemistry_applications (Industrial Chemistry Applications), concept:pharmaceutical_chemistry (Pharmaceutical Chemistry)

Section: STOICHIOMETRY AND STOICHIOMETRIC CALCULATIONS
Summary: This section introduces stoichiometry and the quantitative relationships derived from balanced chemical equations. It explains how to balance chemical equations based on the law of conservation of mass and perform stoichiometric calculations to determine the amounts of reactants and products involved in a reaction.

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:1
Section: Limiting Reagent
Summary: This section introduces the concept of a limiting reagent, which is the reactant that is completely consumed first in a chemical reaction and limits the amount of product formed. It also provides a worked example demonstrating how to identify the limiting reagent and calculate product yields using stoichiometric ratios.
Key terms: limiting reagent, stoichiometric calculations, balanced chemical reaction, moles
Teaches concepts: concept:limiting_reagent (Limiting Reagent)

Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:1

Summary: This section explains how to express the concentration of a substance in a solution using various quantitative methods. It defines and provides formulas for mass percent, mole fraction, molarity, and molality, while discussing their temperature dependence.

Summary: This section defines matter and explores its physical states (solid, liquid, and gas) along with their interconversion. It also classifies matter at the macroscopic level into mixtures (homogeneous and heterogeneous) and pure substances (elements and compounds).

Summary: This section introduces the classification of matter's properties into physical and chemical categories and details the International System of Units (SI) used for quantitative measurements. It defines the seven fundamental base units, SI prefixes, and explains key physical quantities including mass, weight, volume, density, and temperature scales.
Key terms: Physical Properties, Chemical Properties, International System of Units, SI Base Units, SI Prefixes, Mass and Weight, Density, Temperature Scales

Summary: This section addresses the handling of experimental data in chemistry, focusing on scientific notation, precision, accuracy, significant figures, and dimensional analysis to manage uncertainty in measurements.

Summary: This section introduces scientific notation as a method to represent extremely large or small numbers in chemistry using the exponential form $N \times 10^n$. It also details how to perform basic arithmetic operations, including multiplication, division, addition, and subtraction, on numbers written in this notation.

text:
