Section: Significant Figures
Summary: This section defines significant figures, precision, and accuracy in experimental measurements. It outlines the rules for determining significant figures, handling them in arithmetic operations, and rounding off numerical results.

Section: Dimensional Analysis
Summary: This section introduces dimensional analysis, also known as the factor label or unit factor method, which is used to convert units between different systems. It explains the concept of unit factors and demonstrates their application through length, volume, and time conversion examples.

Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:1
Section: LAWS OF CHEMICAL COMBINATIONS
Summary: This section introduces the laws of chemical combinations, which govern how elements combine to form compounds. It establishes that there are five basic laws underlying these chemical reactions.

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:1
Section: Law of Conservation of Mass
Summary: This section introduces the Law of Conservation of Mass, formulated by Antoine Lavoisier in 1789. It states that matter can neither be created nor destroyed, meaning there is no net change in mass during physical and chemical changes.
Key terms: Law of Conservation of Mass, Antoine Lavoisier, Combustion reactions, Conservation of mass, Matter
Teaches concepts: concept:law_of_conservation_of_mass (Law of Conservation of Mass)
Requires concepts: concept:mass (Mass), concept:physical_and_chemical_changes (Physical and Chemical Changes)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:1
Section: Law of Definite Proportions
Summary: This section introduces the Law of Definite Proportions, formulated by Joseph Proust, which states that a chemical compound always contains the same proportion of elements by weight regardless of its source. This principle is demonstrated using Proust's experimental analysis of natural and synthetic cupric carbonate.
Key terms: Law of Definite Proportions, Joseph Proust, cupric carbonate, Law of Definite Composition, proportion by weight
Teaches concepts: concept:law_of_definite_proportions (Law of Definite Proportions)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_compound (Chemical Compound), concept:chemical_element (Chemical Element), concept:mass (Mass)
title: Law of Definite Proportions

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:1
Section: Law of Multiple Proportions
Summary: This section introduces the Law of Multiple Proportions, proposed by John Dalton in 1803. It states that when two elements combine to form more than one compound, the masses of one element that combine with a fixed mass of the other are in a ratio of small whole numbers, as demonstrated by the formation of water and hydrogen peroxide.
Key terms: Law of Multiple Proportions, Dalton, Mass ratio, Water, Hydrogen peroxide
Teaches concepts: concept:law_of_multiple_proportions (Law of Multiple Proportions)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_compound (Chemical Compound), concept:chemical_element (Chemical Element), concept:mass (Mass)
title: Law of Multiple Proportions

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:1
Section: Gay Lussac’s Law of Gaseous Volumes
Summary: This section introduces Gay Lussac's Law of Gaseous Volumes, which states that reacting gases combine or are produced in simple integer ratios by volume under constant temperature and pressure. It illustrates this law using the reaction of hydrogen and oxygen to form water vapour, linking it to the law of definite proportions.
Key terms: Gay Lussac's Law of Gaseous Volumes, simple ratio by volume, law of definite proportions by volume
Teaches concepts: concept:gay_lussacs_law_of_gaseous_volumes (Gay Lussac's Law of Gaseous Volumes), concept:law_of_definite_proportions_by_volume (Law of Definite Proportions by Volume)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_reaction (Chemical Reaction), concept:law_of_definite_proportions (Law of Definite Proportions)
title: Gay Lussac’s Law of Gaseous Volumes

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:1
Section: Avogadro’s Law
Summary: Avogadro's Law proposes that equal volumes of all gases at the same temperature and pressure contain an equal number of molecules. This principle distinguished between atoms and molecules, successfully explaining gas reaction volumes by introducing the concept of polyatomic molecules.
Key terms: Avogadro's Law, atoms, molecules, polyatomic molecules, diatomic molecules
Teaches concepts: concept:avogadros_law (Avogadro's Law), concept:distinction_between_atoms_and_molecules (Distinction between Atoms and Molecules), concept:polyatomic_molecules (Polyatomic Molecules)
Requires concepts: concept:daltons_atomic_theory (Dalton's Atomic Theory), concept:gay_lussacs_law_of_gaseous_volumes (Gay Lussac's Law of Gaseous Volumes)
title: Avogadro’s Law

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:1
Section: DALTON’S ATOMIC THEORY
Summary: This section introduces Dalton's Atomic Theory, detailing its core postulates regarding the indivisible nature of atoms, their properties, and their role in chemical reactions. It also highlights the theory's success in explaining the laws of chemical combination, as well as its limitations regarding gaseous volumes.
Key terms: Dalton's Atomic Theory, Atom, Postulates, Chemical Combination, Gaseous Volumes
Teaches concepts: concept:daltons_atomic_theory (Dalton's Atomic Theory), concept:limitations_of_daltons_atomic_theory (Limitations of Dalton's Atomic Theory), concept:postulates_of_daltons_atomic_theory (Postulates of Dalton's Atomic Theory)
Requires concepts: concept:laws_of_chemical_combination (Laws of Chemical Combination)
title: DALTON’S ATOMIC THEORY

text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:1
Section: ATOMIC AND MOLECULAR MASSES
Summary: This section introduces the concepts of atomic, molecular, and formula masses. It explains how atomic masses are determined relative to the Carbon-12 standard, how average atomic mass accounts for isotopic abundance, and how molecular and formula masses are calculated for covalent and ionic substances.
Key terms: atomic mass, unified mass, average atomic mass, molecular mass, formula mass, carbon-12, isotope
Teaches concepts: concept:atomic_mass (Atomic Mass), concept:average_atomic_mass (Average Atomic Mass), concept:formula_mass (Formula Mass), concept:molecular_mass (Molecular Mass)
Requires concepts: concept:atoms_and_molecules (Atoms and Molecules), concept:ionic_compound (Ionic Compound), concept:isotopes (Isotopes)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:1
Section: MOLE CONCEPT AND MOLAR MASSES
Summary: This section introduces the mole as the SI base unit for measuring the amount of substance. It defines the Avogadro constant, explains its derivation using carbon-12, and defines molar mass as the mass of one mole of a substance in grams.
Key terms: mole, Avogadro constant, Avogadro number, molar mass, amount of substance
Teaches concepts: concept:avogadro_constant (Avogadro Constant), concept:molar_mass (Molar Mass), concept:mole (Mole)

text:

Grade: 11

Summary: This section explains how to calculate the percentage composition of elements in a compound using their mass. It provides the formula for mass percentage and demonstrates calculations using water and ethanol.

text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:1
Section: Empirical Formula for Molecular Formula
Summary: This section defines empirical and molecular formulas and explains how to determine them from elemental mass percentages and molar mass. A detailed worked example illustrates the step-by-step calculation process, including converting mass percent to moles, finding the simplest ratio, and using empirical formula mass.

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Structure of Atom
Summary: This section traces the evolution of atomic theory from early philosophical concepts to Dalton's scientific atomic theory, highlighting its limitations in explaining static electricity. It introduces the subsequent discovery of sub-atomic particles, establishing that atoms are divisible into electrons, protons, and neutrons.
Key terms: Dalton's atomic theory, sub-atomic particles, electrons, protons, neutrons, laws of chemical combination
Teaches concepts: concept:daltons_atomic_theory (Dalton's Atomic Theory), concept:sub_atomic_particles (Sub-atomic Particles)

text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Discovery of Sub-atomic Particles
Summary: This section details the experimental discovery and characterization of the three primary sub-atomic particles: electrons, protons, and neutrons. It explains key historical experiments, including cathode ray tube experiments, J.J. Thomson's charge-to-mass ratio determination, Millikan's oil drop experiment, and Chadwick's neutron discovery.

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Atomic Models
Summary: This section introduces early atomic models, focusing on Thomson's plum pudding model and Rutherford's nuclear model derived from the alpha-particle scattering experiment. It also defines fundamental atomic properties including atomic number, mass number, isotopes, and isobars, while discussing the limitations of Rutherford's model.

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: DEVELOPMENTS LEADING TO THE BOHR’S MODEL OF ATOM
Summary: This section introduces the key developments leading to Bohr's model of the atom, focusing on the wave nature of electromagnetic radiation. It defines the fundamental properties of electromagnetic waves, including wavelength, frequency, and wavenumber, and describes the regions of the electromagnetic spectrum.
Key terms: electromagnetic radiation, electromagnetic spectrum, wavelength, frequency, wavenumber, speed of light
Teaches concepts: concept:electromagnetic_spectrum (Electromagnetic Spectrum), concept:frequency_of_electromagnetic_radiation (Frequency of Electromagnetic Radiation), concept:wave_nature_of_electromagnetic_radiation (Wave Nature of Electromagnetic Radiation), concept:wavelength_of_electromagnetic_radiation (Wavelength of Electromagnetic Radiation), concept:wavenumber (Wavenumber)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Particle Nature of Electromagnetic Radiation: Planck’s Quantum Theory
Summary: This section introduces the particle nature of electromagnetic radiation by highlighting the limitations of classical wave theory in explaining phenomena like black-body radiation. It presents Planck's Quantum Theory, defining the quantum as the discrete packet of energy and explaining energy quantization.
Key terms: Black-body radiation, Quantum, Planck's constant, Quantisation, Electromagnetic radiation, Classical wave theory
Teaches concepts: concept:black_body_radiation (Black-body Radiation), concept:plancks_equation (Planck's Equation), concept:quantisation_of_energy (Quantisation of Energy), concept:quantum (Quantum)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Photoelectric Effect
Summary: This section introduces the photoelectric effect, where electrons are ejected from metal surfaces upon exposure to light of a minimum threshold frequency. Einstein's explanation using Planck's quantum theory establishes the photon concept and leads to the mathematical formulation of the work function and kinetic energy of photoelectrons. This phenomenon ultimately demonstrates the dual wave-particle behavior of electromagnetic radiation.
Key terms: Photoelectric effect, Threshold frequency, Photon, Work function, Einstein's photoelectric equation, Dual behaviour of electromagnetic radiation
Teaches concepts: concept:dual_behaviour_of_electromagnetic_radiation (Dual Behaviour of Electromagnetic Radiation), concept:einsteins_photoelectric_equation (Einstein's Photoelectric Equation), concept:photoelectric_effect (Photoelectric Effect), concept:photon (Photon), concept:threshold_frequency (Threshold Frequency), concept:work_function (Work Function)
Requires concepts: concept:electromagnetic_radiation_wave_nature (Electromagnetic Radiation Wave Nature), concept:plancks_quantum_theory (Planck's Quantum Theory)
title: Photoelectric Effect

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Evidence for the Quantized Electronic Energy Levels: Atomic Spectra
Summary: This section explains atomic spectra as key evidence for quantized electronic energy levels, distinguishing between continuous, emission, and absorption spectra. It introduces spectroscopy and details the line spectrum of hydrogen, characterized by the Balmer series and the generalized Rydberg formula.
Key terms: continuous spectrum, emission spectrum, absorption spectrum, quantization, spectroscopy, line spectrum, Balmer series, Rydberg formula
Teaches concepts: concept:absorption_spectrum (Absorption Spectrum), concept:atomic_line_spectrum (Atomic Line Spectrum), concept:continuous_spectrum (Continuous Spectrum), concept:emission_spectrum (Emission Spectrum), concept:quantization (Quantization), concept:rydberg_formula (Rydberg Formula)
Requires concepts: concept:electromagnetic_radiation (Electromagnetic Radiation), concept:wavelength_and_frequency (Wavelength and Frequency)
title: Evidence for the Quantized Electronic Energy Levels: Atomic Spectra

text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: BOHR’S MODEL FOR HYDROGEN ATOM
Summary: This section introduces Bohr's model for the hydrogen atom, detailing its key postulates including stationary orbits, energy transitions, and the quantization of angular momentum. It provides mathematical expressions for calculating the radii and energies of stationary states for hydrogen and hydrogen-like single-electron species.
Key terms: Bohr's model, stationary states, Bohr's frequency rule, quantization of angular momentum, Bohr radius, Rydberg constant, hydrogen-like species
Teaches concepts: concept:bohr_orbit_radius (Bohr Orbit Radius), concept:bohrs_frequency_rule (Bohr's Frequency Rule), concept:bohrs_postulates_of_hydrogen_atom (Bohr's Postulates of Hydrogen Atom), concept:energy_of_stationary_states (Energy of Stationary States), concept:hydrogen_like_species (Hydrogen-like Species), concept:quantization_of_angular_momentum (Quantization of Angular Momentum)
Requires concepts: concept:angular_momentum (Angular Momentum), concept:plancks_quantum_theory (Planck's Quantum Theory)
title: BOHR’S MODEL FOR HYDROGEN ATOM

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Explanation of Line Spectrum of Hydrogen
Summary: This section quantitatively explains the line spectrum of hydrogen using Bohr's model. It derives expressions for the energy change, frequency, and wavenumber of photons during electronic transitions, and distinguishes between absorption and emission spectra.
Key terms: Bohr's model, line spectrum of hydrogen, Rydberg constant, wavenumber, absorption spectra, emission spectra, hydrogen-like ions
Teaches concepts: concept:absorption_vs_emission_spectra_in_bohrs_model (Absorption vs Emission Spectra in Bohr's Model), concept:energy_and_radius_of_hydrogen_like_ions (Energy and Radius of Hydrogen-like Ions), concept:energy_change_during_electronic_transitions (Energy Change during Electronic Transitions), concept:frequency_and_wavenumber_of_spectral_lines (Frequency and Wavenumber of Spectral Lines)
Requires concepts: concept:bohrs_model_of_hydrogen_atom (Bohr's Model of Hydrogen Atom), concept:plancks_quantum_theory (Planck's Quantum Theory)
title: Explanation of Line Spectrum of Hydrogen

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Limitations of Bohr’s Model
Summary: This section outlines the key limitations of Bohr's atomic model. It highlights its inability to explain multi-electron spectra, the splitting of spectral lines in magnetic and electric fields, and the formation of chemical bonds.
Key terms: Bohr's model, Zeeman effect, Stark effect, Multi-electron atoms, Chemical bonding
Teaches concepts: concept:limitations_of_bohrs_model (Limitations of Bohr's Model), concept:stark_effect (Stark Effect), concept:zeeman_effect (Zeeman Effect)
Requires concepts: concept:bohrs_model_of_hydrogen_atom (Bohr's Model of Hydrogen Atom), concept:hydrogen_spectrum (Hydrogen Spectrum)
title: Limitations of Bohr’s Model

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: TOWARDS QUANTUM MECHANICAL MODEL OF THE ATOM
Summary: This section introduces the transition from Bohr's model to the quantum mechanical model of the atom. It highlights the two key developments that necessitated this new model: the dual behavior of matter and the Heisenberg uncertainty principle.
Key terms: quantum mechanical model, Bohr's model, dual behaviour of matter, Heisenberg uncertainty principle
Teaches concepts: concept:quantum_mechanical_model_of_the_atom (Quantum Mechanical Model of the Atom)
Requires concepts: concept:bohrs_model_of_the_atom (Bohr's Model of the Atom), concept:dual_behaviour_of_matter (Dual Behaviour of Matter), concept:heisenbergs_uncertainty_principle (Heisenberg's Uncertainty Principle)
title: TOWARDS QUANTUM MECHANICAL MODEL OF THE ATOM

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Dual Behaviour of Matter
Summary: This section introduces de Broglie's hypothesis that matter exhibits dual wave-particle behavior. It presents the de Broglie relation linking wavelength to momentum and explains why wave properties are detectable only for microscopic particles, with practical applications like the electron microscope.
Key terms: de Broglie's hypothesis, dual behaviour of matter, de Broglie wavelength, electron microscope, diffraction, momentum
Teaches concepts: concept:de_broglie_relation (de Broglie Relation), concept:dual_behaviour_of_matter (Dual Behaviour of Matter), concept:electron_microscope (Electron Microscope), concept:wave_nature_of_macroscopic_vs_microscopic_objects (Wave Nature of Macroscopic vs Microscopic Objects)
Requires concepts: concept:kinetic_energy (Kinetic Energy), concept:momentum (Momentum), concept:plancks_constant (Planck's Constant), concept:wave_particle_duality_of_light (Wave-Particle Duality of Light)
title: Dual Behaviour of Matter

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Heisenberg's Uncertainty Principle
Summary: This section introduces Heisenberg's Uncertainty Principle, which states that the exact position and momentum of a sub-atomic particle cannot be determined simultaneously. It explores the mathematical formulation, physical interpretation, and explains why this principle is significant only for microscopic objects, thereby ruling out definite electron trajectories.
Key terms: Heisenberg's Uncertainty Principle, uncertainty in position, uncertainty in momentum, trajectory, microscopic objects, macroscopic objects
Teaches concepts: concept:electron_trajectory (Electron Trajectory), concept:heisenbergs_uncertainty_principle (Heisenberg's Uncertainty Principle), concept:scale_significance_of_uncertainty (Scale Significance of Uncertainty)
Requires concepts: concept:momentum (Momentum), concept:photon_momentum_and_wavelength (Photon Momentum and Wavelength), concept:plancks_constant (Planck's Constant)
title: Heisenberg's Uncertainty Principle

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Reasons for the Failure of the Bohr Model
Summary: This section explains the failure of the Bohr model of the atom due to its inability to account for the wave-particle duality of matter and the Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle. It highlights how these limitations necessitated the transition to a quantum mechanical model of the atom.
Key terms: Bohr model, wave-particle duality, Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle, quantum mechanics, probability
Teaches concepts: concept:limitations_of_the_bohr_model (Limitations of the Bohr Model), concept:transition_to_quantum_mechanics (Transition to Quantum Mechanics)
Requires concepts: concept:bohrs_model_of_hydrogen_atom (Bohr's Model of Hydrogen Atom), concept:dual_behaviour_of_matter (Dual Behaviour of Matter), concept:heisenbergs_uncertainty_principle (Heisenberg's Uncertainty Principle)
title: Reasons for the Failure of the Bohr Model

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: QUANTUM MECHANICAL MODEL OF ATOM
Summary: This section introduces the quantum mechanical model of the atom, which addresses the limitations of classical mechanics for microscopic systems. It explains the Schrödinger equation, the physical significance of the wave function and probability density, and outlines the key features of atomic orbitals.
Key terms: Quantum mechanics, Schrödinger equation, Hamiltonian operator, Wave function, Atomic orbital, Probability density
Teaches concepts: concept:atomic_orbital (Atomic Orbital), concept:probability_density (Probability Density), concept:quantum_mechanics (Quantum Mechanics), concept:schr_dinger_equation (Schrödinger Equation), concept:wave_function (Wave Function)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Orbitals and Quantum Numbers
Summary: This section introduces the quantum mechanical description of atomic orbitals using four quantum numbers: principal, azimuthal, magnetic, and electron spin. It explains how these numbers define the size, shape, orientation, and spin of electrons, while contrasting the quantum mechanical orbital with Bohr's classical orbit.
Key terms: Principal quantum number, Azimuthal quantum number, Magnetic orbital quantum number, Electron spin quantum number, Atomic orbital, Orbit, Probability density, Subshell

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Shapes of Atomic Orbitals
Summary: This section explains the shapes, sizes, and spatial orientations of s, p, and d atomic orbitals using boundary surface and probability density diagrams. It also defines radial and angular nodes, providing formulas to calculate them based on quantum numbers.
Key terms: probability density, boundary surface diagram, s-orbital, p-orbital, d-orbital, radial node, angular node, degenerate orbitals
Teaches concepts: concept:d_orbital_shape_and_orientation (d-orbital Shape and Orientation), concept:p_orbital_shape_and_orientation (p-orbital Shape and Orientation), concept:probability_density (Probability Density), concept:radial_and_angular_nodes (Radial and Angular Nodes), concept:s_orbital_shape_and_boundary_surface (s-orbital Shape and Boundary Surface)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Energies of Orbitals
Summary: This section explains the factors determining the energies of orbitals in both hydrogen and multi-electron atoms. It introduces the concepts of degenerate orbitals, shielding effects, effective nuclear charge, and the (n+l) rule for determining relative orbital energies.
Key terms: degenerate orbitals, ground state, excited state, shielding effect, effective nuclear charge, (n+l) rule
Teaches concepts: concept:degenerate_orbitals (Degenerate Orbitals), concept:effective_nuclear_charge (Effective Nuclear Charge), concept:ground_state_and_excited_state (Ground State and Excited State), concept:shielding_effect (Shielding Effect), concept:the_n_l_rule (The (n+l) Rule)
Requires concepts: concept:atomic_orbital (Atomic Orbital), concept:azimuthal_quantum_number (Azimuthal Quantum Number), concept:principal_quantum_number (Principal Quantum Number)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Filling of Orbitals in Atom
Summary: This section outlines the three fundamental rules governing the distribution of electrons in atomic orbitals: the Aufbau principle, the Pauli exclusion principle, and Hund's rule. Together, these principles dictate how orbitals are filled to minimize the total energy of an atom in its ground state.
Key terms: Aufbau principle, Pauli exclusion principle, Hund's rule of maximum multiplicity, degenerate orbitals, electron configuration
Teaches concepts: concept:aufbau_principle (Aufbau Principle), concept:degenerate_orbitals (Degenerate Orbitals), concept:hunds_rule_of_maximum_multiplicity (Hund's Rule of Maximum Multiplicity), concept:pauli_exclusion_principle (Pauli Exclusion Principle)
Requires concepts: concept:atomic_orbital (Atomic Orbital), concept:quantum_numbers (Quantum Numbers)
title: Filling of Orbitals in Atom

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Electronic Configuration of Atoms
Summary: This section defines electronic configuration and explains its representation using notation and orbital diagrams. It details the filling of orbitals across elements, distinguishes between core and valence electrons, and explains anomalous configurations in transition metals due to the stability of half-filled and fully filled subshells.
Key terms: Electronic configuration, Orbital diagram, Core electrons, Valence electrons, Noble gas notation, Anomalous configuration
Teaches concepts: concept:anomalous_electronic_configurations (Anomalous Electronic Configurations), concept:core_electrons (Core Electrons), concept:electronic_configuration (Electronic Configuration), concept:orbital_diagram_notation (Orbital Diagram Notation), concept:valence_electrons (Valence Electrons)
Requires concepts: concept:aufbau_principle (Aufbau Principle), concept:pauli_exclusion_principle (Pauli Exclusion Principle), concept:quantum_numbers (Quantum Numbers)
title: Electronic Configuration of Atoms

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:2
Section: Stability of Completely Filled and Half Filled Subshells
Summary: This section explains the extra stability of half-filled and completely filled subshells, which is driven by symmetrical electron distribution and maximum exchange energy. It also details how these stabilizing factors lead to exceptional electronic configurations in elements like chromium and copper.
Key terms: Symmetrical distribution, Exchange energy, Anomalous electronic configuration, Degenerate orbitals
Teaches concepts: concept:anomalous_electronic_configurations (Anomalous Electronic Configurations), concept:exchange_energy (Exchange Energy), concept:symmetrical_electron_distribution (Symmetrical Electron Distribution)
Requires concepts: concept:aufbau_principle (Aufbau Principle), concept:degenerate_orbitals (Degenerate Orbitals), concept:hunds_rule_of_maximum_multiplicity (Hund's Rule of Maximum Multiplicity)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:3
Section: WHY DO WE NEED TO CLASSIFY ELEMENTS ?
Summary: This section explains the historical necessity of classifying elements due to the rapid increase in their discovery, which made individual study of elements and their compounds impractical. Classification provides a systematic way to organize chemical knowledge and predict the properties of undiscovered elements.
Key terms: classification of elements, elements, chemical facts, systematic organization
Teaches concepts: concept:classification_of_elements (Classification of Elements)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_element (Chemical Element)
title: WHY DO WE NEED TO CLASSIFY ELEMENTS ?

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:3
Section: GENESIS OF PERIODIC CLASSIFICATION
Summary: This section outlines the historical development of the periodic table, tracing early classification attempts from Dobereiner's Triads and Newlands' Law of Octaves to Lothar Meyer's physical property plots. It focuses on Mendeleev's Periodic Law, his tabular organization of elements, and his successful predictions of undiscovered elements.
Key terms: Dobereiner's Triads, Law of Triads, Law of Octaves, Lothar Meyer's plots, Mendeleev's Periodic Law, Eka-Aluminium, Eka-Silicon, Groups, Series
Teaches concepts: concept:dobereiners_triads (Dobereiner's Triads), concept:law_of_octaves (Law of Octaves), concept:lothar_meyers_periodic_classification (Lothar Meyer's Periodic Classification), concept:mendeleevs_periodic_law (Mendeleev's Periodic Law), concept:mendeleevs_periodic_table (Mendeleev's Periodic Table)
Requires concepts: concept:atomic_weight (Atomic Weight), concept:chemical_and_physical_properties_of_elements (Chemical and Physical Properties of Elements)
title: GENESIS OF PERIODIC CLASSIFICATION

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:3
Section: MODERN PERIODIC LAW AND THE PRESENT FORM OF THE PERIODIC TABLE
Summary: This section introduces the Modern Periodic Law, which states that the properties of elements are periodic functions of their atomic numbers. It explains the basis of periodicity through electronic configurations and describes the structure of the long form of the periodic table, including periods, groups, and the placement of lanthanoids and actinoids.
Key terms: Modern Periodic Law, Atomic Number, Periodicity, Long Form of the Periodic Table, Lanthanoids, Actinoids
Teaches concepts: concept:atomic_number_as_fundamental_property (Atomic Number as Fundamental Property), concept:basis_of_periodicity (Basis of Periodicity), concept:lanthanoids_and_actinoids (Lanthanoids and Actinoids), concept:long_form_of_the_periodic_table (Long Form of the Periodic Table), concept:modern_periodic_law (Modern Periodic Law), concept:periods_and_quantum_numbers (Periods and Quantum Numbers)
Requires concepts: concept:electronic_configuration (Electronic Configuration), concept:principal_quantum_number (Principal Quantum Number)
title: MODERN PERIODIC LAW AND THE PRESENT FORM OF THE PERIODIC TABLE

text:

Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:3
Section: NOMENCLATURE OF ELEMENTS WITH ATOMIC NUMBERS > 100
Summary: This section introduces the systematic IUPAC nomenclature for naming superheavy elements with atomic numbers greater than 100. It explains how to derive temporary names and three-letter symbols using numerical roots for digits 0-9, and lists the officially ratified names up to element 118.
Key terms: IUPAC nomenclature, superheavy elements, atomic number, numerical roots, systematic naming
Teaches concepts: concept:iupac_systematic_nomenclature_for_elements_100 (IUPAC Systematic Nomenclature for Elements > 100), concept:numerical_roots_for_element_naming (Numerical Roots for Element Naming), concept:superheavy_elements (Superheavy Elements)
Requires concepts: concept:atomic_number (Atomic Number), concept:chemical_symbol (Chemical Symbol)

text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:3
Section: ELECTRONIC CONFIGURATIONS OF ELEMENTS AND THE PERIODIC TABLE
Summary: This section explains the fundamental relationship between the electronic configuration of elements and their positions in the Periodic Table. It details how period numbers correspond to the highest principal quantum number being filled and how elements in the same group share similar valence shell configurations.
Key terms: Electronic configuration, Principal quantum number, Valence shell, Period, Group, Orbitals

Grade: 11

Section: ELECTRONIC CONFIGURATIONS AND TYPES OF ELEMENTS: S-, P-, D-, F- BLOCKS
Summary: This section classifies elements into s-, p-, d-, and f-blocks based on their electronic configurations and orbital filling. It details the characteristic properties of each block, including transition and inner-transition elements, and outlines the periodic trends of metals, non-metals, and metalloids.
Key terms: s-block, p-block, d-block, f-block, transition elements, representative elements, metalloids, metallic character

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:3
Section: PERIODIC TRENDS IN PROPERTIES OF ELEMENTS
Summary: This section explores the periodic trends in physical and chemical properties of elements across periods and down groups. It details key properties including atomic and ionic radii, ionization enthalpy, electron gain enthalpy, electronegativity, valence states, and the anomalous behavior of second-period elements.
Key terms: Atomic Radius, Ionic Radius, Ionization Enthalpy, Electron Gain Enthalpy, Electronegativity, Valence, Oxidation State, Diagonal Relationship, Shielding Effect
Teaches concepts: concept:anomalous_properties_of_second_period_elements (Anomalous Properties of Second Period Elements), concept:atomic_and_ionic_radii (Atomic and Ionic Radii), concept:electron_gain_enthalpy (Electron Gain Enthalpy), concept:electronegativity (Electronegativity), concept:ionization_enthalpy (Ionization Enthalpy), concept:valence_and_oxidation_states (Valence and Oxidation States)

text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: KÖSSEL-LEWIS APPROACH TO CHEMICAL BONDING
Summary: This section introduces the Kössel-Lewis approach to chemical bonding, explaining how atoms achieve stability by attaining a stable octet configuration. It details Lewis symbols, valence electrons, and Kössel's postulates on ionic bonding, illustrating the formation of electrovalent bonds through electron transfer.
Key terms: chemical bond, octet rule, valence electrons, Lewis symbols, electrovalent bond, electrovalence
Teaches concepts: concept:chemical_bond (Chemical Bond), concept:electrovalence (Electrovalence), concept:electrovalent_bond (Electrovalent Bond), concept:octet_rule (Octet Rule), concept:valence_electrons_and_lewis_symbols (Valence Electrons and Lewis Symbols)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: Octet Rule
Summary: This section introduces the electronic theory of chemical bonding and the octet rule. It explains that atoms combine by transferring or sharing valence electrons to achieve a stable octet of eight electrons in their valence shells.
Key terms: electronic theory of chemical bonding, octet rule, valence electrons, electron transfer, electron sharing
Teaches concepts: concept:electronic_theory_of_chemical_bonding (Electronic Theory of Chemical Bonding), concept:octet_rule (Octet Rule)

Grade: 11

Section: Covalent Bond
Summary: This section introduces the covalent bond through the Lewis-Langmuir theory, explaining how atoms share electron pairs to achieve stable configurations. It details the construction of Lewis dot structures and distinguishes between single, double, and triple covalent bonds.
Key terms: Covalent bond, Lewis dot structure, Single bond, Double bond, Triple bond, Octet rule
Teaches concepts: concept:covalent_bond (Covalent Bond), concept:double_bond (Double Bond), concept:lewis_dot_structure (Lewis Dot Structure), concept:single_covalent_bond (Single Covalent Bond), concept:triple_bond (Triple Bond)

Summary: This section explains the systematic process of writing Lewis dot structures for molecules and polyatomic ions. It details how to calculate total valence electrons, determine skeletal structures, select central atoms, and distribute electrons to satisfy the octet rule.
Key terms: Lewis dot structures, valence electrons, skeletal structure, central atom, octet rule, lone pairs, duplet
Teaches concepts: concept:electron_distribution_and_octet_rule_application (Electron Distribution and Octet Rule Application), concept:lewis_dot_structures (Lewis dot structures), concept:skeletal_structure_and_central_atom_selection (Skeletal Structure and Central Atom Selection), concept:total_valence_electrons_calculation (Total Valence Electrons Calculation)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: Formal Charge
Summary: This section defines formal charge as a method to track valence electrons in Lewis structures and provides its mathematical formula. It demonstrates the calculation of formal charges using the ozone molecule as an example and explains how formal charges help identify the most stable molecular structure.

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: Limitations of the Octet Rule
Summary: This section details the limitations and exceptions of the octet rule, classifying them into incomplete octets, odd-electron molecules, and expanded octets. It also highlights other drawbacks of the theory, including its inability to explain noble gas reactivity, molecular shapes, and relative stability.
Key terms: incomplete octet, odd-electron molecules, expanded octet, octet rule limitations, noble gas compounds
Teaches concepts: concept:expanded_octet (Expanded Octet), concept:incomplete_octet (Incomplete Octet), concept:limitations_of_the_octet_theory (Limitations of the Octet Theory), concept:odd_electron_molecules (Odd-electron Molecules)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: IONIC OR ELECTROVALENT BOND
Summary: This section explains the formation and stability of ionic or electrovalent bonds, focusing on the energy considerations of ionization, electron gain, and lattice formation. It also describes the three-dimensional crystalline structure of ionic solids, using sodium chloride as a primary example.
Key terms: Ionic Bond, Electrovalent Bond, Ionization Enthalpy, Electron Gain Enthalpy, Lattice Enthalpy, Rock Salt Structure

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: Lattice Enthalpy
Summary: This section defines lattice enthalpy as the energy required to completely separate one mole of an ionic solid into its constituent gaseous ions. It highlights the role of lattice enthalpy in determining the stability of ionic crystals, using sodium chloride as a representative example.
Key terms: Lattice Enthalpy, ionic solid, gaseous constituent ions, ionic crystals, stability
Teaches concepts: concept:lattice_enthalpy (Lattice Enthalpy), concept:stability_of_ionic_crystals (Stability of Ionic Crystals)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: BOND PARAMETERS
Summary: This section introduces key molecular bond parameters including bond length, bond angle, bond enthalpy, and bond order. It also explains the concepts of resonance, bond polarity, dipole moments, and Fajans' rules for determining the covalent character of ionic bonds.
Key terms: Bond length, Covalent radius, Van der Waals radius, Bond angle, Bond enthalpy, Bond order, Resonance hybrid, Dipole moment, Fajans' rules
Teaches concepts: concept:bond_enthalpy (Bond Enthalpy), concept:bond_length (Bond Length), concept:bond_order (Bond Order), concept:dipole_moment (Dipole Moment), concept:fajans_rules (Fajans' Rules), concept:resonance (Resonance)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: THE VALENCE SHELL ELECTRON PAIR REPULSION (VSEPR) THEORY
Summary: The Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion (VSEPR) theory predicts the three-dimensional shapes of covalent molecules by minimizing electrostatic repulsions between valence shell electron pairs. It explains how the presence of lone pairs on a central atom distorts ideal molecular geometries and reduces bond angles.
Key terms: VSEPR theory, lone pair, bond pair, molecular geometry, repulsive interaction, bond angle distortion
Teaches concepts: concept:bond_angle_distortion (Bond Angle Distortion), concept:electron_pair_repulsion_order (Electron Pair Repulsion Order), concept:geometry_of_molecules_with_lone_pairs (Geometry of Molecules with Lone Pairs), concept:geometry_of_molecules_with_no_lone_pairs (Geometry of Molecules with No Lone Pairs), concept:vsepr_theory_postulates (VSEPR Theory Postulates)
Requires concepts: concept:covalent_bond (Covalent Bond), concept:lewis_structure (Lewis Structure), concept:valence_electrons (Valence Electrons)

text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: VALENCE BOND THEORY
Summary: Valence Bond (VB) Theory explains covalent bond formation through the quantum mechanical concept of atomic orbital overlap. It describes how the directional overlap of orbitals determines bond strength and geometry, distinguishing between axial (sigma) and lateral (pi) bonds.
Key terms: Valence Bond Theory, orbital overlap, sigma bond, pi bond, bond enthalpy, potential energy curve
Teaches concepts: concept:orbital_overlap_concept (Orbital Overlap Concept), concept:orbital_overlap_phase_and_orientation (Orbital Overlap Phase and Orientation), concept:pi_bond (Pi Bond), concept:sigma_bond (Sigma Bond), concept:valence_bond_theory (Valence Bond Theory)

title: VALENCE BOND THEORY

text:

Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: HYBRIDISATION
Summary: This section introduces the concept of hybridisation, which explains the characteristic geometries of polyatomic molecules. It defines hybridisation as the intermixing of atomic orbitals to form equivalent hybrid orbitals, and details its key features and conditions.
Key terms: Hybridisation, Hybrid orbitals, Atomic orbitals, Valence shell, Molecular geometry
Teaches concepts: concept:hybrid_orbitals (Hybrid Orbitals), concept:hybridisation (Hybridisation)
Requires concepts: concept:atomic_orbital (Atomic Orbital), concept:molecular_geometry (Molecular Geometry), concept:valence_shell (Valence Shell)
title: HYBRIDISATION

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: Types of Hybridisation
Summary: This section explains the different types of orbital hybridisation, specifically sp, sp2, and sp3 hybridisation, and how they determine molecular geometry. It illustrates these concepts using molecules like BeCl2, BCl3, and CH4, and details how lone pair repulsions distort ideal geometries in NH3 and H2O.
Key terms: sp hybridisation, sp2 hybridisation, sp3 hybridisation, linear geometry, trigonal planar, tetrahedral, lone pair repulsion
Teaches concepts: concept:lone_pair_induced_geometric_distortion (Lone pair-induced geometric distortion), concept:sp2_hybridisation (sp2 hybridisation), concept:sp3_hybridisation (sp3 hybridisation), concept:sp_hybridisation (sp hybridisation)
Requires concepts: concept:atomic_orbital (Atomic Orbital), concept:orbital_hybridisation (Orbital Hybridisation), concept:valence_shell_electron_pair_repulsion_vsepr_theory (Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion (VSEPR) Theory)
title: Types of Hybridisation

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: Other Examples of sp3, sp2 and sp Hybridisation
Summary: This section illustrates the application of sp3, sp2, and sp hybridisation states in organic molecules using ethane, ethene, and ethyne as examples. It explains how orbital overlaps form sigma and pi bonds, determining the molecular geometry, bond lengths, and bond angles of these hydrocarbons.
Key terms: sp3 hybridisation, sp2 hybridisation, sp hybridisation, ethane, ethene, ethyne, sigma bond, pi bond
Teaches concepts: concept:sp2_hybridisation_in_ethene (sp2 Hybridisation in Ethene), concept:sp3_hybridisation_in_ethane (sp3 Hybridisation in Ethane), concept:sp_hybridisation_in_ethyne (sp Hybridisation in Ethyne)
Requires concepts: concept:sigma_and_pi_bonds (Sigma and Pi Bonds), concept:sp2_hybridisation (sp2 hybridisation), concept:sp3_hybridisation (sp3 hybridisation), concept:sp_hybridisation (sp hybridisation)
title: Other Examples of sp3, sp2 and sp Hybridisation

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: Hybridisation of Elements involving d Orbitals
Summary: This section explains the hybridization of elements involving d orbitals, which is common in third-period elements due to comparable orbital energies. It details the specific geometric and bonding configurations of phosphorus pentachloride via sp3d hybridization and sulfur hexafluoride via sp3d2 hybridization.
Key terms: d-orbital hybridisation, sp3d hybridisation, sp3d2 hybridisation, axial bonds, equatorial bonds, trigonal bipyramidal, octahedral
Teaches concepts: concept:axial_and_equatorial_bonds (Axial and Equatorial Bonds), concept:d_orbital_hybridisation (d-orbital Hybridisation), concept:sp3d2_hybridisation (sp3d2 Hybridisation), concept:sp3d_hybridisation (sp3d Hybridisation)
Requires concepts: concept:electronic_configuration (Electronic Configuration), concept:orbital_hybridisation (Orbital Hybridisation), concept:sigma_bond (Sigma Bond)
title: Hybridisation of Elements involving d Orbitals

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: MOLECULAR ORBITAL THEORY
Summary: This section introduces Molecular Orbital (MO) Theory, outlining its key features developed by Hund and Mulliken. It explains how atomic orbitals combine to form bonding and antibonding molecular orbitals, which are polycentric and filled according to standard quantum mechanical principles.
Key terms: Molecular Orbital Theory, bonding molecular orbital, antibonding molecular orbital, polycentric, atomic orbitals
Teaches concepts: concept:antibonding_molecular_orbital (Antibonding Molecular Orbital), concept:bonding_molecular_orbital (Bonding Molecular Orbital), concept:molecular_orbital_theory (Molecular Orbital Theory), concept:polycentric_orbital (Polycentric Orbital)
Requires concepts: concept:atomic_orbital (Atomic Orbital), concept:aufbau_principle (Aufbau Principle), concept:hunds_rule (Hund's Rule), concept:pauli_exclusion_principle (Pauli Exclusion Principle)
title: MOLECULAR ORBITAL THEORY

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: Formation of Molecular Orbitals - Linear Combination of Atomic Orbitals (LCAO)
Summary: This section introduces the Linear Combination of Atomic Orbitals (LCAO) method as an approximation for forming molecular orbitals. It explains the formation of bonding and antibonding molecular orbitals through the constructive and destructive interference of atomic wave functions, detailing their respective energy levels and electron density distributions.
Key terms: Linear Combination of Atomic Orbitals, LCAO, bonding molecular orbital, antibonding molecular orbital, wave function, constructive interference, destructive interference, nodal plane
Teaches concepts: concept:antibonding_molecular_orbital (Antibonding Molecular Orbital), concept:bonding_molecular_orbital (Bonding Molecular Orbital), concept:linear_combination_of_atomic_orbitals_lcao (Linear Combination of Atomic Orbitals (LCAO)), concept:nodal_plane (Nodal Plane)
Requires concepts: concept:atomic_orbital (Atomic Orbital), concept:wave_function (Wave Function), concept:wave_interference (Wave Interference)
title: Formation of Molecular Orbitals - Linear Combination of Atomic Orbitals (LCAO)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: Conditions for the Combination of Atomic Orbitals
Summary: This section details the three essential conditions required for the linear combination of atomic orbitals (LCAO) to form molecular orbitals. These conditions specify that combining orbitals must have comparable energy, identical symmetry about the molecular axis, and maximum spatial overlap.
Key terms: Linear Combination of Atomic Orbitals, LCAO, Molecular Orbitals, Symmetry, Molecular Axis, Orbital Overlap
Teaches concepts: concept:conditions_for_linear_combination_of_atomic_orbitals (Conditions for Linear Combination of Atomic Orbitals), concept:energy_compatibility_in_lcao (Energy Compatibility in LCAO), concept:orbital_overlap_extent (Orbital Overlap Extent), concept:symmetry_compatibility_in_lcao (Symmetry Compatibility in LCAO)
Requires concepts: concept:atomic_orbital (Atomic Orbital), concept:linear_combination_of_atomic_orbitals (Linear Combination of Atomic Orbitals), concept:molecular_orbitals (Molecular Orbitals)
title: Conditions for the Combination of Atomic Orbitals

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: Types of Molecular Orbitals
Summary: This section explains the classification of molecular orbitals in diatomic molecules into sigma and pi types based on their symmetry around the internuclear axis. It details how linear combinations of 1s and 2p atomic orbitals form bonding and antibonding sigma and pi molecular orbitals.
Key terms: sigma molecular orbital, pi molecular orbital, symmetrical around the bond-axis, bonding MO, antibonding MO, internuclear axis, node
Teaches concepts: concept:pi_bonding_and_antibonding_orbitals (Pi Bonding and Antibonding Orbitals), concept:pi_molecular_orbitals (Pi Molecular Orbitals), concept:sigma_bonding_and_antibonding_orbitals (Sigma Bonding and Antibonding Orbitals), concept:sigma_molecular_orbitals (Sigma Molecular Orbitals)
Requires concepts: concept:atomic_orbital (Atomic Orbital), concept:bonding_and_antibonding_molecular_orbitals (Bonding and Antibonding Molecular Orbitals), concept:linear_combination_of_atomic_orbitals (Linear Combination of Atomic Orbitals)
title: Types of Molecular Orbitals

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: Energy Level Diagram for Molecular Orbitals
Summary: This section explains how 2s and 2p atomic orbitals combine to form eight molecular orbitals, including both bonding and antibonding types. It details the experimental energy level sequences for homonuclear diatomic molecules, highlighting the key difference in energy ordering between heavier molecules like O2 and F2 and lighter molecules like B2, C2, and N2.
Key terms: Molecular Orbitals, Bonding Orbitals, Antibonding Orbitals, Energy Level Diagram, Homonuclear Diatomic Molecules
Teaches concepts: concept:molecular_orbital_energy_order_for_lighter_diatomic_molecules (Molecular Orbital Energy Order for Lighter Diatomic Molecules), concept:molecular_orbital_energy_order_for_o2_and_f2 (Molecular Orbital Energy Order for O2 and F2), concept:molecular_orbitals_from_2s_and_2p_orbitals (Molecular Orbitals from 2s and 2p Orbitals)
Requires concepts: concept:atomic_orbital (Atomic Orbital), concept:bonding_and_antibonding_molecular_orbitals (Bonding and Antibonding Molecular Orbitals), concept:molecular_orbital_theory (Molecular Orbital Theory)
title: Energy Level Diagram for Molecular Orbitals

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: Electronic Configuration and Molecular Behaviour
Summary: This section explains how the electronic configuration of a molecule determines its stability, bond order, bond length, and magnetic properties. It defines bond order and relates it to bond strength and length, while also explaining how electron occupancy in molecular orbitals dictates magnetic behavior.
Key terms: electronic configuration, molecular stability, bond order, bond length, diamagnetic, paramagnetic
Teaches concepts: concept:bond_length_and_bond_order_relationship (Bond Length and Bond Order Relationship), concept:bond_order (Bond Order), concept:electronic_configuration_of_molecules (Electronic Configuration of Molecules), concept:magnetic_nature_of_molecules (Magnetic Nature of Molecules), concept:molecular_stability (Molecular Stability)
Requires concepts: concept:molecular_orbital_theory (Molecular Orbital Theory)
title: Electronic Configuration and Molecular Behaviour

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: BONDING IN SOME HOMONUCLEAR DIATOMIC MOLECULES
Summary: This section applies molecular orbital theory to analyze the bonding, electronic configurations, and magnetic properties of homonuclear diatomic molecules from H2 to Ne2. It demonstrates how bond order calculations predict molecular stability and explains the physical basis of phenomena like the paramagnetism of oxygen and 2s-2p orbital mixing.
Key terms: homonuclear diatomic molecules, bond order, electronic configuration, paramagnetic, diamagnetic, 2s-2p mixing
Teaches concepts: concept:2s_2p_mixing_in_diatomic_molecules (2s-2p Mixing in Diatomic Molecules), concept:bonding_in_carbon_molecule_c2 (Bonding in Carbon Molecule (C2)), concept:bonding_in_hydrogen_molecule_h2 (Bonding in Hydrogen Molecule (H2)), concept:non_existence_of_helium_he2_and_beryllium_be2_molecules (Non-existence of Helium (He2) and Beryllium (Be2) Molecules), concept:paramagnetism_of_oxygen_molecule_o2 (Paramagnetism of Oxygen Molecule (O2))
Requires concepts: concept:bond_order (Bond Order), concept:molecular_orbital_theory (Molecular Orbital Theory), concept:paramagnetism_and_diamagnetism (Paramagnetism and Diamagnetism)
title: BONDING IN SOME HOMONUCLEAR DIATOMIC MOLECULES

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:4
Section: HYDROGEN BONDING
Summary: This section introduces hydrogen bonding, explaining its definition, cause of formation, and classification. It details how highly electronegative atoms bonded to hydrogen create polar molecules that engage in intermolecular or intramolecular hydrogen bonding.
Key terms: Hydrogen bond, Electronegativity, Polar molecule, Intermolecular hydrogen bond, Intramolecular hydrogen bond
Teaches concepts: concept:cause_of_hydrogen_bond_formation (Cause of Hydrogen Bond Formation), concept:hydrogen_bond (Hydrogen Bond), concept:intermolecular_hydrogen_bond (Intermolecular Hydrogen Bond), concept:intramolecular_hydrogen_bond (Intramolecular Hydrogen Bond)
Requires concepts: concept:covalent_bond (Covalent Bond), concept:electronegativity (Electronegativity), concept:polar_molecule (Polar Molecule)
title: HYDROGEN BONDING

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:5
Section: Thermodynamic Terms
Summary: This section introduces fundamental thermodynamic terms, defining the system, surroundings, and boundaries, and classifying systems as open, closed, or isolated. It explains state functions like temperature, volume, and internal energy, and establishes the IUPAC sign conventions for heat and work. Finally, it presents the First Law of Thermodynamics, expressing the conservation of energy mathematically as the change in internal energy equal to heat plus work.
Key terms: Thermodynamics, System, Surroundings, State function, Internal energy, Adiabatic process, First Law of Thermodynamics
Teaches concepts: concept:adiabatic_process (Adiabatic Process), concept:first_law_of_thermodynamics (First Law of Thermodynamics), concept:internal_energy (Internal Energy), concept:state_functions (State Functions), concept:thermodynamic_system_and_surroundings (Thermodynamic System and Surroundings), concept:types_of_thermodynamic_systems (Types of Thermodynamic Systems)
Requires concepts: concept:conservation_of_energy (Conservation of Energy), concept:macroscopic_properties (Macroscopic Properties)
title: Thermodynamic Terms

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:5
Section: Applications
Summary: This section explains and derives pressure-volume (PV) work under various thermodynamic conditions, including constant external pressure, multi-step finite changes, and reversible processes. It provides mathematical formulations for isothermal reversible expansion of an ideal gas and analyzes free expansion into a vacuum using the first law of thermodynamics.
Key terms: Pressure-volume work, Reversible process, Irreversible process, Free expansion, Isothermal expansion
Teaches concepts: concept:free_expansion (Free Expansion), concept:isothermal_irreversible_change (Isothermal Irreversible Change), concept:pressure_volume_work (Pressure-Volume Work), concept:reversible_isothermal_work_of_an_ideal_gas (Reversible Isothermal Work of an Ideal Gas), concept:reversible_process (Reversible Process)
Requires concepts: concept:first_law_of_thermodynamics (First Law of Thermodynamics), concept:ideal_gas_law (Ideal Gas Law), concept:integral_calculus (Integral Calculus)
title: Applications

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:5
Section: Enthalpy, H
Summary: This section introduces enthalpy as a thermodynamic state function defined by the equation H = U + pV, representing heat flow at constant pressure. It derives the relationship between enthalpy change and internal energy change for general processes and gaseous reactions, and distinguishes between exothermic and endothermic reactions.
Key terms: Enthalpy, State Function, Exothermic Reaction, Endothermic Reaction, Gaseous Reactions, Enthalpy of Vaporization
Teaches concepts: concept:endothermic_reaction (Endothermic Reaction), concept:enthalpy (Enthalpy), concept:enthalpy_change_at_constant_pressure (Enthalpy Change at Constant Pressure), concept:enthalpy_change_for_gaseous_reactions (Enthalpy Change for Gaseous Reactions), concept:exothermic_reaction (Exothermic Reaction)
Requires concepts: concept:first_law_of_thermodynamics (First Law of Thermodynamics), concept:ideal_gas_law (Ideal Gas Law), concept:internal_energy (Internal Energy), concept:state_functions (State Functions)
title: Enthalpy, H

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:5
Section: Extensive and Intensive Properties
Summary: This section defines and distinguishes between extensive and intensive thermodynamic properties based on their dependence on the quantity of matter. It also explains how molar properties are intensive and illustrates these concepts using a partitioned gas system.
Key terms: extensive property, intensive property, molar property, molar volume, molar heat capacity
Teaches concepts: concept:extensive_property (Extensive Property), concept:intensive_property (Intensive Property), concept:molar_property (Molar Property)
Requires concepts: concept:mole_concept (Mole Concept), concept:thermodynamic_system (Thermodynamic System)
title: Extensive and Intensive Properties

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:5
Section: Heat Capacity
Summary: This section defines heat capacity and distinguishes between molar and specific heat capacities. It establishes the mathematical relationships for heat transfer under constant volume and constant pressure conditions, and derives the relationship $C_p - C_v = R$ for an ideal gas.
Key terms: Heat capacity, Molar heat capacity, Specific heat capacity, Constant volume heat capacity, Constant pressure heat capacity, Ideal gas constant
Teaches concepts: concept:heat_capacity (Heat Capacity), concept:heat_capacity_at_constant_pressure (Heat Capacity at Constant Pressure), concept:heat_capacity_at_constant_volume (Heat Capacity at Constant Volume), concept:molar_heat_capacity (Molar Heat Capacity), concept:relation_between_cp_and_cv_for_an_ideal_gas (Relation between Cp and Cv for an Ideal Gas), concept:specific_heat_capacity (Specific Heat Capacity)
Requires concepts: concept:enthalpy (Enthalpy), concept:ideal_gas_law (Ideal Gas Law), concept:internal_energy (Internal Energy)
title: Heat Capacity

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:5
Section: MEASUREMENT OF $\Delta U$ AND $\Delta H$: CALORIMETRY
Summary: This section introduces calorimetry as an experimental technique to measure heat changes in chemical and physical processes. It details how internal energy change is measured at constant volume using a bomb calorimeter, and how enthalpy change is measured at constant pressure.
Key terms: Calorimetry, Calorimeter, Bomb calorimeter, Heat capacity, Constant volume, Constant pressure
Teaches concepts: concept:bomb_calorimeter (Bomb Calorimeter), concept:calorimetry (Calorimetry), concept:constant_pressure_calorimetry (Constant Pressure Calorimetry), concept:constant_volume_calorimetry (Constant Volume Calorimetry)
Requires concepts: concept:enthalpy_change (Enthalpy Change), concept:heat_capacity (Heat Capacity), concept:internal_energy_change (Internal Energy Change)
title: MEASUREMENT OF $\Delta U$ AND $\Delta H$: CALORIMETRY

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:5
Section: ENTHALPY CHANGE, $\Delta_r H$ OF A REACTION – REACTION ENTHALPY
Summary: This section defines reaction enthalpy and standard enthalpy, exploring enthalpy changes during phase transformations such as fusion, vaporization, and sublimation. It also introduces thermochemical equations, standard enthalpy of formation, and Hess's Law of Constant Heat Summation for calculating overall reaction enthalpies.
Key terms: reaction enthalpy, standard enthalpy of reaction, standard enthalpy of fusion, standard enthalpy of vaporization, standard enthalpy of sublimation, standard enthalpy of formation, thermochemical equation, Hess's Law
Teaches concepts: concept:enthalpy_of_phase_transformation (Enthalpy of Phase Transformation), concept:hesss_law_of_constant_heat_summation (Hess's Law of Constant Heat Summation), concept:reaction_enthalpy (Reaction Enthalpy), concept:standard_enthalpy_of_formation (Standard Enthalpy of Formation), concept:standard_enthalpy_of_reaction (Standard Enthalpy of Reaction), concept:thermochemical_equation (Thermochemical Equation)
Requires concepts: concept:enthalpy (Enthalpy), concept:internal_energy (Internal Energy), concept:stoichiometry (Stoichiometry)
title: ENTHALPY CHANGE, $\Delta_r H$ OF A REACTION – REACTION ENTHALPY

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:5
Section: ENTHALPIES FOR DIFFERENT TYPES OF REACTIONS
Summary: This section defines and explains various types of reaction enthalpies, including standard enthalpy of combustion, enthalpy of atomization, bond enthalpy, lattice enthalpy, and enthalpy of solution. It also introduces the Born-Haber cycle as an indirect method to calculate lattice enthalpy using Hess's Law.
Key terms: Standard enthalpy of combustion, Enthalpy of atomization, Bond dissociation enthalpy, Mean bond enthalpy, Lattice enthalpy, Born-Haber cycle, Enthalpy of solution, Enthalpy of dilution
Teaches concepts: concept:bond_enthalpy (Bond Enthalpy), concept:born_haber_cycle (Born-Haber Cycle), concept:enthalpy_of_atomization (Enthalpy of Atomization), concept:enthalpy_of_solution (Enthalpy of Solution), concept:lattice_enthalpy (Lattice Enthalpy), concept:standard_enthalpy_of_combustion (Standard Enthalpy of Combustion)
Requires concepts: concept:hesss_law (Hess's Law), concept:standard_enthalpy_of_formation (Standard Enthalpy of Formation)
title: ENTHALPIES FOR DIFFERENT TYPES OF REACTIONS

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:5
Section: SPONTANEITY
Summary: This section introduces the concept of spontaneity in chemical and physical processes, demonstrating that neither enthalpy decrease nor entropy increase alone can fully predict spontaneous change. It defines entropy quantitatively, introduces the Second and Third Laws of Thermodynamics, and establishes Gibbs energy change as the definitive criterion for spontaneity at constant temperature and pressure.
Key terms: Spontaneous process, Entropy, Second law of thermodynamics, Gibbs energy, Gibbs equation, Third law of thermodynamics
Teaches concepts: concept:entropy (Entropy), concept:gibbs_energy_and_the_gibbs_equation (Gibbs Energy and the Gibbs Equation), concept:second_law_of_thermodynamics (Second Law of Thermodynamics), concept:spontaneous_process (Spontaneous Process), concept:third_law_of_thermodynamics (Third Law of Thermodynamics)
Requires concepts: concept:enthalpy (Enthalpy), concept:first_law_of_thermodynamics (First Law of Thermodynamics), concept:state_functions (State Functions)
title: SPONTANEITY

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:5
Section: GIBBS ENERGY CHANGE AND EQUILIBRIUM
Summary: This section explains the relationship between standard Gibbs energy change and the chemical equilibrium constant, demonstrating how to predict reaction spontaneity and calculate thermodynamic values. It also details how temperature, enthalpy, and entropy changes collectively determine whether a reaction is spontaneous.
Key terms: Gibbs energy change, equilibrium constant, spontaneity, reversible reaction, enthalpy change, entropy change
Teaches concepts: concept:gibbs_energy_in_reversible_reactions (Gibbs Energy in Reversible Reactions), concept:relation_between_standard_gibbs_energy_and_equilibrium_constant (Relation between Standard Gibbs Energy and Equilibrium Constant), concept:temperature_dependence_of_spontaneity (Temperature Dependence of Spontaneity)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_equilibrium_constant (Chemical Equilibrium Constant), concept:enthalpy_and_entropy_changes (Enthalpy and Entropy Changes), concept:gibbs_free_energy (Gibbs Free Energy)
title: GIBBS ENERGY CHANGE AND EQUILIBRIUM

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Equilibrium
Summary: This section introduces the concept of equilibrium in physical and chemical processes, highlighting its dynamic nature where forward and reverse reaction rates are equal. It classifies chemical equilibria based on reaction extent and introduces ionic equilibrium involving ions in aqueous solutions.
Key terms: dynamic equilibrium, equilibrium mixture, chemical equilibrium, ionic equilibrium, reversible reaction
Teaches concepts: concept:chemical_equilibrium (Chemical Equilibrium), concept:dynamic_equilibrium (Dynamic Equilibrium), concept:equilibrium_mixture (Equilibrium Mixture), concept:ionic_equilibrium (Ionic Equilibrium)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_reaction (Chemical Reaction), concept:concentration (Concentration)
title: Equilibrium

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: EQUILIBRIUM IN PHYSICAL PROCESSES
Summary: This section introduces the concept of equilibrium in physical processes, focusing on phase transformations and dissolution processes. It details the dynamic nature of solid-liquid, liquid-vapour, and solid-vapour equilibria, as well as the dissolution of solids and gases in liquids.
Key terms: Dynamic equilibrium, Phase transformation, Equilibrium vapour pressure, Normal boiling point, Sublimation, Henry's law, Closed system
Teaches concepts: concept:characteristics_of_physical_equilibrium (Characteristics of Physical Equilibrium), concept:dissolution_equilibrium_of_solids_in_liquids (Dissolution Equilibrium of Solids in Liquids), concept:henrys_law (Henry's Law), concept:liquid_vapour_equilibrium (Liquid-Vapour Equilibrium), concept:solid_liquid_equilibrium (Solid-Liquid Equilibrium), concept:solid_vapour_equilibrium (Solid-Vapour Equilibrium)
Requires concepts: concept:saturated_solution (Saturated Solution), concept:states_of_matter (States of Matter)
title: EQUILIBRIUM IN PHYSICAL PROCESSES

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: ACIDS, BASES AND SALTS
Summary: This section covers the fundamental properties of acids, bases, and salts, explaining how water's high dielectric constant facilitates the dissolution of ionic solids. It distinguishes between dissociation and ionization, and introduces the Lewis definition of acids and bases based on electron-pair transfer.
Key terms: Acids, Bases, Salts, Dielectric constant, Dissociation, Ionization, Lewis acid, Lewis base
Teaches concepts: concept:dissociation (Dissociation), concept:dissolution_of_ionic_solids (Dissolution of Ionic Solids), concept:ionization (Ionization), concept:lewis_acid (Lewis Acid), concept:lewis_base (Lewis Base)
Requires concepts: concept:electrostatic_forces (Electrostatic Forces), concept:lone_pair_of_electrons (Lone Pair of Electrons), concept:polar_molecules (Polar Molecules)
title: ACIDS, BASES AND SALTS

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Arrhenius Concept of Acids and Bases
Summary: This section introduces the Arrhenius theory of acids and bases, defining them by their dissociation behavior in water. It explains the formation of hydrated ions, such as the hydronium ion, and outlines the limitations of the theory in non-aqueous media and for compounds lacking hydroxyl groups.
Key terms: Arrhenius theory, Arrhenius acid, Arrhenius base, hydronium ion, hydrated ions, aqueous solution
Teaches concepts: concept:arrhenius_acid (Arrhenius Acid), concept:arrhenius_base (Arrhenius Base), concept:hydronium_ion (Hydronium Ion), concept:limitations_of_arrhenius_theory (Limitations of Arrhenius Theory)
Requires concepts: concept:aqueous_solution (Aqueous Solution), concept:chemical_bonding (Chemical Bonding)
title: Arrhenius Concept of Acids and Bases

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: The Brönsted-Lowry Acids and Bases
Summary: This section introduces the Brönsted-Lowry theory of acids and bases, defining them in terms of proton transfer. It explains the concept of conjugate acid-base pairs and discusses amphoteric substances, such as water, which can act as both acids and bases.
Key terms: Brönsted-Lowry acid, Brönsted-Lowry base, proton donor, proton acceptor, conjugate acid-base pair, amphoteric
Teaches concepts: concept:amphoteric_species (Amphoteric Species), concept:br_nsted_lowry_acid (Brönsted-Lowry Acid), concept:br_nsted_lowry_base (Brönsted-Lowry Base), concept:conjugate_acid_base_pair (Conjugate Acid-Base Pair)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_ions (Chemical Ions), concept:proton (Proton)
title: The Brönsted-Lowry Acids and Bases

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: IONIZATION OF ACIDS AND BASES
Summary: This section covers the ionization of acids and bases in aqueous media, explaining the behavior of strong and weak electrolytes and conjugate acid-base pairs. It introduces the autoionization of water, the ionic product constant ($K_w$), and the logarithmic pH scale used to measure acidity and basicity.
Key terms: Arrhenius concept, Brönsted-Lowry concept, conjugate acid-base pairs, ionic product of water, pH scale, acid-base indicators
Teaches concepts: concept:acid_base_indicators (Acid-Base Indicators), concept:conjugate_acid_base_pairs_and_equilibrium_direction (Conjugate Acid-Base Pairs and Equilibrium Direction), concept:ionic_product_of_water (Ionic Product of Water), concept:ionization_of_acids_and_bases_in_aqueous_medium (Ionization of Acids and Bases in Aqueous Medium), concept:relationship_between_ph_poh_and_pkw (Relationship between pH, pOH, and pKw), concept:the_ph_scale (The pH Scale)
Requires concepts: concept:arrhenius_and_br_and_ouml_nsted_lowry_acid_base_theories (Arrhenius and Brönsted-Lowry Acid-Base Theories), concept:chemical_equilibrium (Chemical Equilibrium), concept:logarithms (Logarithms)
title: IONIZATION OF ACIDS AND BASES

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Ionization Constants of Weak Acids
Summary: This section introduces the acid ionization constant ($K_a$) and the $pK_a$ scale to quantitatively measure the strength of weak acids. It provides a systematic step-wise methodology to calculate the degree of ionization, equilibrium concentrations, percent dissociation, and pH of weak acid solutions.
Key terms: Acid ionization constant, Degree of ionization, pKa scale, Weak acid, Percent dissociation, Monobasic acid
Teaches concepts: concept:acid_ionization_constant_ka (Acid Ionization Constant (Ka)), concept:degree_of_ionization (Degree of Ionization), concept:percent_dissociation (Percent Dissociation), concept:pka_scale (pKa Scale), concept:step_wise_approach_to_evaluate_ph_of_weak_electrolytes (Step-wise Approach to Evaluate pH of Weak Electrolytes)
Requires concepts: concept:br_nsted_lowry_acid_base_theory (Brönsted-Lowry Acid-Base Theory), concept:chemical_equilibrium_constant (Chemical Equilibrium Constant), concept:ph_scale (pH Scale)
title: Ionization Constants of Weak Acids

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Ionization of Weak Bases
Summary: This section covers the ionization of weak bases in aqueous solutions, defining the base ionization constant ($K_b$) and its logarithmic representation ($pK_b$). It details how to calculate these constants, the degree of ionization, and the pH of weak base solutions, including mixtures with their conjugate acids.
Key terms: weak base, base ionization constant, degree of ionization, pK_b, amines
Teaches concepts: concept:amines_as_weak_bases (Amines as Weak Bases), concept:base_ionization_constant (Base Ionization Constant), concept:degree_of_ionization_of_a_weak_base (Degree of Ionization of a Weak Base), concept:pk_b (pK_b)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_equilibrium_constant (Chemical Equilibrium Constant), concept:ionic_product_of_water (Ionic Product of Water), concept:ph_scale (pH Scale)
title: Ionization of Weak Bases

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Relation between Ka and Kb
Summary: This section establishes the mathematical relationship between the ionization constants of a conjugate acid-base pair, showing that their product equals the auto-ionization constant of water. It also generalizes the rule for equilibrium constants of net reactions and derives the logarithmic relationship between pKa and pKb.
Key terms: conjugate acid-base pair, ionization constant, auto-ionization of water, equilibrium constant, pKa, pKb, pKw
Teaches concepts: concept:equilibrium_constant_of_net_reactions (Equilibrium Constant of Net Reactions), concept:logarithmic_relationship_between_pka_and_pkb (Logarithmic Relationship between pKa and pKb), concept:relationship_between_ka_and_kb_for_conjugate_pairs (Relationship between Ka and Kb for Conjugate Pairs)
Requires concepts: concept:acid_and_base_ionization_constants_ka_and_kb (Acid and Base Ionization Constants (Ka and Kb)), concept:conjugate_acid_base_pair (Conjugate Acid-Base Pair), concept:ionic_product_of_water_kw (Ionic Product of Water (Kw)), concept:logarithmic_p_scale (Logarithmic p-Scale)
title: Relation between Ka and Kb

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Di- and Polybasic Acids and Di- and Polyacidic Bases
Summary: This section introduces polybasic (or polyprotic) acids and polyacidic bases, describing their stepwise ionization process. It explains how successive ionization constants decrease in value due to electrostatic forces.
Key terms: polybasic acids, polyprotic acids, stepwise ionization, ionization constants, electrostatic forces
Teaches concepts: concept:polyprotic_acids (polyprotic_acids), concept:stepwise_ionization (stepwise_ionization), concept:stepwise_ionization_constants (stepwise_ionization_constants), concept:successive_ionization_constant_comparison (successive_ionization_constant_comparison)
Requires concepts: concept:acid_ionization_constant (acid_ionization_constant), concept:chemical_equilibrium (Chemical Equilibrium), concept:electrostatic_force (electrostatic_force)
title: Di- and Polybasic Acids and Di- and Polyacidic Bases

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Factors Affecting Acid Strength
Summary: This section explains how bond strength and bond polarity determine the extent of acid dissociation. It describes periodic trends, showing that acid strength increases down a group due to decreasing bond strength and increases across a row due to increasing bond polarity.
Key terms: Acid strength, Bond strength, Bond polarity, Electronegativity, Bond enthalpy, Periodic trends
Teaches concepts: concept:acid_strength_trend_in_groups (Acid Strength Trend in Groups), concept:acid_strength_trend_in_rows (Acid Strength Trend in Rows), concept:factors_affecting_acid_strength (Factors Affecting Acid Strength)
Requires concepts: concept:acid_dissociation (Acid Dissociation), concept:bond_enthalpy (Bond Enthalpy), concept:electronegativity (Electronegativity)
title: Factors Affecting Acid Strength

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Common Ion Effect in the Ionization of Acids and Bases
Summary: This section defines the common ion effect as the suppression of the ionization of a weak electrolyte by the addition of an ion already present in the equilibrium. It explains this phenomenon using Le Chatelier's principle and demonstrates how to calculate the pH of acidic and basic solutions in the presence of a common ion.
Key terms: common ion effect, dissociation equilibrium, Le Chatelier's principle, degree of ionization, pH calculation
Teaches concepts: concept:common_ion_effect (Common Ion Effect), concept:ph_calculation_with_common_ion_effect (pH Calculation with Common Ion Effect)

title: Common Ion Effect in the Ionization of Acids and Bases

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Hydrolysis of Salts and the pH of their Solutions
Summary: This section explains salt hydrolysis, where cations or anions of a salt interact with water to affect the solution's pH. It details the hydrolysis behavior and pH determination for salts derived from various combinations of strong and weak acids and bases.
Key terms: Salt hydrolysis, pH of salt solutions, Weak acid, Strong base, Weak base, Strong acid, pKa and pKb
Teaches concepts: concept:hydrolysis_of_salts_of_strong_acid_and_weak_base (Hydrolysis of Salts of Strong Acid and Weak Base), concept:hydrolysis_of_salts_of_weak_acid_and_strong_base (Hydrolysis of Salts of Weak Acid and Strong Base), concept:hydrolysis_of_salts_of_weak_acid_and_weak_base (Hydrolysis of Salts of Weak Acid and Weak Base), concept:salt_hydrolysis (Salt Hydrolysis)
Requires concepts: concept:acid_and_base_strength (Acid and Base Strength), concept:ph_scale (pH Scale)
title: Hydrolysis of Salts and the pH of their Solutions

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: BUFFER SOLUTIONS
Summary: This section defines buffer solutions as mixtures that resist changes in pH upon dilution or the addition of small amounts of acid or alkali. It derives the Henderson-Hasselbalch equation for calculating the pH of acidic buffers and establishes the corresponding equations for basic buffers.
Key terms: Buffer Solutions, Acidic Buffer, Basic Buffer, Henderson-Hasselbalch Equation, pH resistance, Ionization Constant
Teaches concepts: concept:acidic_buffer (Acidic Buffer), concept:buffer (Basic Buffer), concept:buffer_solutions (Buffer Solutions), concept:henderson_hasselbalch_equation (Henderson-Hasselbalch Equation)
Requires concepts: concept:acid_base_ionization_constants (Acid-Base Ionization Constants), concept:ph_and_poh (pH and pOH), concept:weak_acids_and_weak_bases (Weak Acids and Weak Bases)
