subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: SOLUBILITY EQUILIBRIA OF SPARINGLY SOLUBLE SALTS
Summary: This section covers the solubility equilibria of sparingly soluble salts, introducing the solubility product constant (Ksp) and its relation to molar solubility. It also discusses factors affecting solubility, including lattice and solvation enthalpies, the common ion effect, and pH variations.
Key terms: Solubility, Lattice Enthalpy, Solvation Enthalpy, Sparingly Soluble Salt, Solubility Product Constant, Common Ion Effect, Molar Solubility, pH Effect
Teaches concepts: concept:classification_of_salts_by_solubility (Classification of Salts by Solubility), concept:common_ion_effect_on_solubility (Common Ion Effect on Solubility), concept:lattice_and_solvation_enthalpy_in_solubility (Lattice and Solvation Enthalpy in Solubility), concept:ph_effect_on_solubility_of_weak_acid_salts (pH Effect on Solubility of Weak Acid Salts), concept:solubility_product_constant (Solubility Product Constant)
Requires concepts: concept:acid_base_equilibria_and_ph (Acid-Base Equilibria and pH), concept:chemical_equilibrium (Chemical Equilibrium), concept:lattice_enthalpy (Lattice Enthalpy), concept:le_chateliers_principle (Le Chatelier's Principle)
title: SOLUBILITY EQUILIBRIA OF SPARINGLY SOLUBLE SALTS

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Summary
Summary: This section provides a comprehensive summary of chemical and physical equilibrium, detailing the equilibrium constant and Le Chatelier's principle. It also covers ionic equilibrium, including acid-base theories, the pH scale, buffer solutions, and the solubility product of sparingly soluble salts.
Key terms: Equilibrium Constant, Le Chatelier's Principle, Acid-Base Theories, pH Scale, Buffer Solutions, Solubility Product
Teaches concepts: concept:acid_base_theories (Acid-Base Theories), concept:buffer_solutions (Buffer Solutions), concept:equilibrium_constant (Equilibrium Constant), concept:le_chateliers_principle (Le Chatelier's Principle), concept:ph_scale_and_ionization_of_water (pH Scale and Ionization of Water), concept:solubility_product_constant (Solubility Product Constant)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: EQUILIBRIUM IN CHEMICAL PROCESSES – DYNAMIC EQUILIBRIUM
Summary: This section introduces chemical equilibrium as a dynamic state where the rates of forward and reverse reactions are equal, resulting in constant concentrations of reactants and products. The dynamic nature of this state is demonstrated experimentally through isotope scrambling in the Haber process and modeled using a water transfer analogy. Additionally, it shows that equilibrium can be reached starting from either the reactant or product side.
Key terms: chemical equilibrium, dynamic equilibrium, reversible reaction, Haber's process, isotope scrambling, deuterium
Teaches concepts: concept:attainment_of_equilibrium_from_either_direction (Attainment of Equilibrium from Either Direction), concept:chemical_equilibrium (Chemical Equilibrium), concept:dynamic_equilibrium (Dynamic Equilibrium), concept:isotope_scrambling (Isotope Scrambling), concept:reversible_reaction (Reversible Reaction)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_reaction (Chemical Reaction), concept:concentration (Concentration), concept:isotopes (Isotopes)
title: EQUILIBRIUM IN CHEMICAL PROCESSES – DYNAMIC EQUILIBRIUM

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: LAW OF CHEMICAL EQUILIBRIUM AND EQUILIBRIUM CONSTANT
Summary: This section introduces the Law of Chemical Equilibrium and defines the equilibrium constant ($K_c$) for reversible reactions. It explains how to formulate the equilibrium constant expression using stoichiometric coefficients and details how the constant changes when a reaction is reversed or multiplied by a factor.
Key terms: Equilibrium constant, Law of Mass Action, Equilibrium Law, Stoichiometric coefficients, Reversible reaction
Teaches concepts: concept:effect_of_stoichiometric_coefficients_on_equilibrium_constant (Effect of Stoichiometric Coefficients on Equilibrium Constant), concept:equilibrium_constant (Equilibrium Constant), concept:law_of_chemical_equilibrium (Law of Chemical Equilibrium), concept:law_of_mass_action (Law of Mass Action), concept:relationship_between_forward_and_reverse_equilibrium_constants (Relationship between Forward and Reverse Equilibrium Constants)
Requires concepts: concept:molar_concentration (Molar Concentration), concept:reversible_reaction (Reversible Reaction), concept:stoichiometric_coefficients (Stoichiometric Coefficients)
title: LAW OF CHEMICAL EQUILIBRIUM AND EQUILIBRIUM CONSTANT

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: HOMOGENEOUS EQUILIBRIA
Summary: This section defines homogeneous equilibria, where all reactants and products exist in the same phase. It introduces the gaseous equilibrium constant ($K_p$) and establishes its mathematical relationship with $K_c$ using the ideal gas equation.
Key terms: homogeneous equilibria, equilibrium constant, partial pressure, Kp, Kc, change in gaseous moles
Teaches concepts: concept:change_in_gaseous_moles_delta_n (Change in Gaseous Moles (Delta n)), concept:equilibrium_constant_in_gaseous_systems_kp (Equilibrium Constant in Gaseous Systems (Kp)), concept:homogeneous_equilibria (Homogeneous Equilibria), concept:relationship_between_kp_and_kc (Relationship between Kp and Kc)
Requires concepts: concept:equilibrium_constant_kc (Equilibrium Constant (Kc)), concept:ideal_gas_equation (Ideal Gas Equation), concept:partial_pressure (Partial Pressure)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: HETEROGENEOUS EQUILIBRIA
Summary: This section defines heterogeneous equilibrium as a system containing more than one phase and explains why pure solids and liquids are omitted from equilibrium constant expressions. It demonstrates these principles using examples like the thermal dissociation of calcium carbonate and the purification of nickel, while also discussing the units of equilibrium constants.
Key terms: heterogeneous equilibrium, pure solids, pure liquids, equilibrium constant, thermal dissociation, standard state
Teaches concepts: concept:concentration_of_pure_solids_and_liquids_in_equilibrium (Concentration of Pure Solids and Liquids in Equilibrium), concept:heterogeneous_equilibrium (Heterogeneous Equilibrium), concept:thermal_dissociation_of_calcium_carbonate (Thermal Dissociation of Calcium Carbonate), concept:units_of_equilibrium_constant (Units of Equilibrium Constant)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_equilibrium (Chemical Equilibrium), concept:molar_concentration (Molar Concentration), concept:partial_pressure (Partial Pressure)
title: HETEROGENEOUS EQUILIBRIA

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: APPLICATIONS OF EQUILIBRIUM CONSTANTS
Summary: This section details the practical applications of the equilibrium constant, focusing on predicting the extent and direction of a chemical reaction. It introduces the reaction quotient for determining reaction direction and provides a systematic methodology for calculating equilibrium concentrations from initial conditions.
Key terms: Equilibrium Constant, Reaction Quotient, Extent of Reaction, Reaction Direction, Equilibrium Concentration, ICE Table
Teaches concepts: concept:calculating_equilibrium_concentrations (Calculating Equilibrium Concentrations), concept:features_of_equilibrium_constants (Features of Equilibrium Constants), concept:predicting_the_direction_of_a_reaction (Predicting the Direction of a Reaction), concept:predicting_the_extent_of_a_reaction (Predicting the Extent of a Reaction), concept:reaction_quotient (Reaction Quotient)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_stoichiometry (Chemical Stoichiometry), concept:equilibrium_constant_expression (Equilibrium Constant Expression), concept:ideal_gas_law (Ideal Gas Law)
title: APPLICATIONS OF EQUILIBRIUM CONSTANTS

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN EQUILIBRIUM CONSTANT K, REACTION QUOTIENT Q AND GIBBS ENERGY G
Summary: This section establishes the thermodynamic relationship between Gibbs energy change, the reaction quotient, and the equilibrium constant. It derives the fundamental equation relating standard Gibbs energy change to the equilibrium constant and explains how the sign of standard Gibbs energy determines the extent of a reaction.
Key terms: Gibbs energy, Equilibrium constant, Reaction quotient, Standard Gibbs energy change, Spontaneity
Teaches concepts: concept:gibbs_energy_and_reaction_quotient_relationship (Gibbs Energy and Reaction Quotient Relationship), concept:spontaneity_and_gibbs_energy_change (Spontaneity and Gibbs Energy Change), concept:standard_gibbs_energy_and_equilibrium_constant_relationship (Standard Gibbs Energy and Equilibrium Constant Relationship), concept:thermodynamic_interpretation_of_standard_gibbs_energy_change (Thermodynamic Interpretation of Standard Gibbs Energy Change)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: FACTORS AFFECTING EQUILIBRIA
Summary: This section introduces Le Chatelier's principle and examines how changing concentration conditions disturbs chemical equilibria. It explains the theoretical basis of concentration stress, demonstrates its industrial applications in maximizing product yield, and provides an experimental verification using the iron(III)-thiocyanate system.
Key terms: Le Chatelier's principle, equilibrium mixture, concentration stress, reaction quotient, equilibrium constant, thiocyanate complex
Teaches concepts: concept:effect_of_concentration_change_on_equilibrium (Effect of Concentration Change on Equilibrium), concept:experimental_demonstration_of_concentration_effect (Experimental Demonstration of Concentration Effect), concept:industrial_applications_of_equilibrium_shift (Industrial Applications of Equilibrium Shift), concept:le_chateliers_principle (Le Chatelier's Principle)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_equilibrium (Chemical Equilibrium), concept:equilibrium_constant (Equilibrium Constant), concept:reaction_quotient (Reaction Quotient)
title: FACTORS AFFECTING EQUILIBRIA

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Effect of Pressure Change
Summary: This section explains how changes in pressure, typically achieved by altering volume, affect chemical equilibrium in gaseous and heterogeneous systems. Applying Le Chatelier's principle, it demonstrates that increasing pressure shifts the equilibrium toward the side with fewer moles of gas, supported by both mathematical analysis of the reaction quotient and qualitative examples.
Key terms: Pressure change, Le Chatelier's principle, Gaseous reactions, Reaction quotient, Heterogeneous equilibrium, Volume change
Teaches concepts: concept:effect_of_pressure_change_on_gaseous_equilibrium (Effect of Pressure Change on Gaseous Equilibrium), concept:le_chateliers_principle_for_pressure (Le Chatelier's Principle for Pressure), concept:mathematical_analysis_of_pressure_change_via_reaction_quotient (Mathematical Analysis of Pressure Change via Reaction Quotient), concept:pressure_effect_on_heterogeneous_equilibrium (Pressure Effect on Heterogeneous Equilibrium)
Requires concepts: concept:heterogeneous_equilibrium (Heterogeneous Equilibrium), concept:le_chateliers_principle (Le Chatelier's Principle), concept:reaction_quotient (Reaction Quotient)
title: Effect of Pressure Change

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Effect of Inert Gas Addition
Summary: This section explains the impact of adding an inert gas to a chemical system at equilibrium. It specifically demonstrates that adding an inert gas at constant volume has no effect on the equilibrium state because the partial pressures and molar concentrations of the reactants and products remain unchanged.
Key terms: inert gas addition, constant volume, chemical equilibrium, reaction quotient, partial pressure, molar concentration
Teaches concepts: concept:effect_of_inert_gas_addition_at_constant_volume (Effect of Inert Gas Addition at Constant Volume)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_equilibrium (Chemical Equilibrium), concept:molar_concentration (Molar Concentration), concept:partial_pressure (Partial Pressure), concept:reaction_quotient (Reaction Quotient)
title: Effect of Inert Gas Addition

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Effect of Temperature Change
Summary: This section explains how temperature changes affect the equilibrium constant and composition of a reaction depending on whether it is exothermic or endothermic. These principles are demonstrated through the industrial synthesis of ammonia and experimental observations of nitrogen dioxide and cobalt complex equilibria.
Key terms: Equilibrium constant, Temperature dependence, Exothermic reaction, Endothermic reaction, Le Chatelier's principle, Ammonia synthesis
Teaches concepts: concept:temperature_dependence_of_equilibrium_constant (Temperature Dependence of Equilibrium Constant), concept:temperature_effect_on_endothermic_reactions (Temperature Effect on Endothermic Reactions), concept:temperature_effect_on_exothermic_reactions (Temperature Effect on Exothermic Reactions), concept:temperature_optimization_in_ammonia_synthesis (Temperature Optimization in Ammonia Synthesis)
Requires concepts: concept:enthalpy_change (Enthalpy Change), concept:equilibrium_constant (Equilibrium Constant), concept:le_chateliers_principle (Le Chatelier's Principle)
title: Effect of Temperature Change

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: Effect of a Catalyst
Summary: This section explains the role of a catalyst in chemical equilibrium, demonstrating that it accelerates both forward and reverse reactions equally without altering the equilibrium composition or constant. It also illustrates these principles using key industrial applications, specifically the Haber and Contact processes.
Key terms: Catalyst, Activation energy, Equilibrium constant, Haber process, Contact process
Teaches concepts: concept:contact_process_catalyst (Contact Process Catalyst), concept:haber_process_catalyst (Haber Process Catalyst), concept:role_of_catalyst_in_equilibrium (Role of Catalyst in Equilibrium)
Requires concepts: concept:activation_energy (Activation Energy), concept:chemical_equilibrium (Chemical Equilibrium), concept:equilibrium_constant (Equilibrium Constant)
title: Effect of a Catalyst

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:6
Section: IONIC EQUILIBRIUM IN SOLUTION
Summary: This section introduces ionic equilibrium, which occurs in aqueous solutions of weak electrolytes. It classifies substances into electrolytes and non-electrolytes, distinguishes between strong and weak electrolytes, and introduces acids, bases, and salts as fundamental electrolytes.
Key terms: electrolytes, non-electrolytes, strong electrolytes, weak electrolytes, ionic equilibrium, acids, bases, salts
Teaches concepts: concept:acids_bases_and_salts (Acids, Bases, and Salts), concept:electrolytes_and_non_electrolytes (Electrolytes and Non-electrolytes), concept:ionic_equilibrium (Ionic Equilibrium), concept:strong_electrolytes (Strong Electrolytes), concept:weak_electrolytes (Weak Electrolytes)
Requires concepts: concept:aqueous_solution (Aqueous Solution), concept:chemical_equilibrium (Chemical Equilibrium)
title: IONIC EQUILIBRIUM IN SOLUTION

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:7
Section: CLASSICAL IDEA OF REDOX REACTIONS – OXIDATION AND REDUCTION REACTIONS
Summary: This section introduces the classical definitions of oxidation and reduction based on the transfer of oxygen, hydrogen, and electronegative or electropositive elements. It highlights that these two processes always occur simultaneously, forming the basis of redox reactions.
Key terms: oxidation, reduction, redox reactions, electronegative element, electropositive element
Teaches concepts: concept:classical_oxidation (Classical Oxidation), concept:classical_reduction (Classical Reduction), concept:redox_reactions (Redox Reactions)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:7
Section: REDOX REACTIONS IN TERMS OF ELECTRON TRANSFER REACTIONS
Summary: This section defines redox reactions in terms of electron transfer, introducing oxidation as electron loss and reduction as electron gain. It explains how to split redox reactions into oxidation and reduction half-reactions and demonstrates competitive electron transfer among metals to establish the metal activity series.
Key terms: oxidation, reduction, half-reaction, oxidising agent, reducing agent, metal activity series, electron transfer
Teaches concepts: concept:half_reaction (Half-Reaction), concept:metal_activity_series (Metal Activity Series), concept:oxidation_electronic_definition (Oxidation (electronic definition)), concept:oxidising_agent_electron_acceptor (Oxidising Agent (electron acceptor)), concept:reducing_agent_electron_donor (Reducing Agent (electron donor)), concept:reduction_electronic_definition (Reduction (electronic definition))
Requires concepts: concept:ionic_compound (Ionic Compound)
title: REDOX REACTIONS IN TERMS OF ELECTRON TRANSFER REACTIONS

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:7
Section: OXIDATION NUMBER
Summary: This section introduces the concept of oxidation number (or oxidation state) as a bookkeeping tool for electron shifts in compounds. It details the rules for assigning oxidation numbers, explains periodic trends in oxidation states, introduces Stock notation, and redefines redox processes in terms of oxidation number changes.
Key terms: Oxidation number, Oxidation state, Stock notation, Peroxide, Superoxide, Oxidant, Reductant
Teaches concepts: concept:oxidation_number (Oxidation Number), concept:periodic_trends_in_oxidation_states (Periodic Trends in Oxidation States), concept:redox_definitions_in_terms_of_oxidation_number (Redox Definitions in Terms of Oxidation Number), concept:rules_for_assigning_oxidation_numbers (Rules for Assigning Oxidation Numbers), concept:stock_notation (Stock Notation)
Requires concepts: concept:covalent_bond (Covalent Bond), concept:electronegativity (Electronegativity), concept:periodic_table_groups (Periodic Table Groups)
title: OXIDATION NUMBER

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:7
Section: Types of Redox Reactions
Summary: This section classifies redox reactions into combination, decomposition, displacement, and disproportionation reactions, detailing their mechanisms and conditions. It also addresses the paradox of fractional oxidation numbers by demonstrating how structural analysis reveals distinct whole-number oxidation states in molecules and mixed oxides.
Key terms: Combination reaction, Decomposition reaction, Displacement reaction, Disproportionation reaction, Fractional oxidation number, Mixed oxides
Teaches concepts: concept:combination_redox_reaction (Combination Redox Reaction), concept:decomposition_redox_reaction (Decomposition Redox Reaction), concept:displacement_redox_reaction (Displacement Redox Reaction), concept:disproportionation_reaction (Disproportionation Reaction), concept:fractional_oxidation_number_paradox (Fractional Oxidation Number Paradox)
Requires concepts: concept:electronegativity (Electronegativity), concept:oxidation_number (Oxidation Number), concept:redox_reactions (Redox Reactions)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:7
Section: Balancing of Redox Reactions
Summary: This section explains the two primary methods for balancing redox reactions: the Oxidation Number Method and the Half-Reaction (Ion-Electron) Method. It provides step-by-step procedures and worked examples for balancing equations in both acidic and basic media.
Key terms: Oxidation Number Method, Half-Reaction Method, Ion-Electron Method, Acidic Medium, Basic Medium
Teaches concepts: concept:balancing_in_acidic_medium (Balancing in Acidic Medium), concept:balancing_in_basic_medium (Balancing in Basic Medium), concept:half_reaction_method (Half-Reaction Method), concept:oxidation_number_method (Oxidation Number Method)
Requires concepts: concept:oxidation_number (Oxidation Number), concept:redox_reactions (Redox Reactions)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:7
Section: Redox Reactions as the Basis for Titrations
Summary: This section explains the principles of redox titrations, which are used to determine the concentration of reductants or oxidants. It details three primary methods for detecting the titration end-point: self-indicators, external indicators, and specific indicators like starch.
Key terms: Redox titration, Self-indicator, External indicator, Specific indicator, End-point, Permanganate ion, Starch indicator
Teaches concepts: concept:external_indicator (External Indicator), concept:redox_titration (Redox Titration), concept:self_indicator (Self-indicator), concept:specific_indicator (Specific Indicator)

title: Redox Reactions as the Basis for Titrations

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:7
Section: Limitations of Concept of Oxidation Number
Summary: This section outlines the limitations of using formal oxidation numbers and explains the modern evolution of redox concepts. It defines oxidation and reduction in terms of changes in electron density around atoms.
Key terms: Oxidation number, Redox processes, Electron density, Oxidation, Reduction
Teaches concepts: concept:modern_concept_of_oxidation (Modern Concept of Oxidation), concept:modern_concept_of_reduction (Modern Concept of Reduction)
Requires concepts: concept:oxidation_number (Oxidation Number), concept:redox_reactions (Redox Reactions)
title: Limitations of Concept of Oxidation Number

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:7
Section: REDOX REACTIONS AND ELECTRODE PROCESSES
Summary: This section introduces electrode processes by separating oxidation and reduction half-reactions into distinct compartments to form electrochemical cells like the Daniell cell. It defines redox couples, explains the role of a salt bridge, and introduces electrode potential and standard electrode potential as measures of the relative tendencies of species to undergo reduction or oxidation.
Key terms: Redox couple, Daniell cell, Salt bridge, Electrode potential, Standard Electrode Potential
Teaches concepts: concept:daniell_cell (Daniell Cell), concept:electrode_potential (Electrode Potential), concept:redox_couple (Redox Couple), concept:salt_bridge (Salt Bridge), concept:standard_electrode_potential (Standard Electrode Potential)

Grade: 11

Section: GENERAL INTRODUCTION
Summary: This section introduces organic chemistry, highlighting carbon's unique ability to undergo catenation and form covalent bonds. It explores the biological and industrial importance of organic compounds and traces the historical shift from the Vital Force Theory to laboratory synthesis, initiated by F. Wohler's synthesis of urea.

text:

Summary: This section covers quantitative analysis methods used to determine the mass percentage of elements in organic compounds. It details classical chemical techniques for estimating carbon, hydrogen, nitrogen, halogens, sulphur, phosphorus, and oxygen, alongside modern automated elemental analysis.

subject: chemistry
text:

It also describes the formation, geometry, restricted rotation, and high reactivity of pi bonds in carbon compounds.

Summary: This section explains the classification of organic compounds, focusing on heterocyclic aromatic compounds, functional groups, and homologous series. It defines these key classifications and describes how they determine the chemical properties and structural relationships of organic molecules.

Grade: 11

Section: Nomenclature of Substituted Benzene Compounds
Summary: This section explains the IUPAC and common nomenclature systems for monosubstituted, disubstituted, and polysubstituted benzene derivatives. It details the use of locant numbering, ortho/meta/para prefixes, and the designation of benzene as a phenyl substituent when attached to an alkane chain with a functional group.

Summary: This section introduces the concept of isomerism, where compounds share the same molecular formula but exhibit different physical or chemical properties. It outlines the classification of isomerism into structural isomerism and stereoisomerism, along with their respective sub-types.
Key terms: Isomerism, Isomers, Structural Isomerism, Stereoisomerism, Chain Isomerism, Position Isomerism, Functional Group Isomerism, Metamerism

text:

Grade: 11

Section: Structural Isomerism
Summary: This section defines structural isomerism and explains its various types. It covers chain isomerism, position isomerism, functional group isomerism, and metamerism, providing specific chemical examples for each type.
Key terms: Structural Isomerism, Chain Isomers, Position Isomers, Functional Isomers, Metamerism
Teaches concepts: concept:chain_isomerism (Chain Isomerism), concept:functional_group_isomerism (Functional Group Isomerism), concept:metamerism (Metamerism), concept:position_isomerism (Position Isomerism), concept:structural_isomerism (Structural Isomerism)

subject: chemistry
text:

Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:8
Section: Stereoisomerism
Summary: This section defines stereoisomerism, where compounds share the same chemical constitution and covalent bond sequence but differ in the spatial arrangement of their atoms or groups. It also introduces the classification of stereoisomerism into geometrical and optical isomerism.
Key terms: stereoisomerism, stereoisomers, geometrical isomerism, optical isomerism
Teaches concepts: concept:stereoisomerism (Stereoisomerism)

Summary: This section introduces the fundamental framework of organic reactions, detailing the general reaction scheme involving substrates, attacking reagents, intermediates, and products. It defines a reaction mechanism as the step-by-step description of electron movement, energetics, and kinetics during bond cleavage and formation.

Summary: This section explains the two primary modes of covalent bond fission: heterolytic and homolytic cleavage. It details the formation, structure, and stability of the resulting reactive intermediates, namely carbocations, carbanions, and free radicals.
Key terms: heterolytic cleavage, homolytic cleavage, carbocation, carbanion, free radical, bond fission

Summary: This section introduces the classification of organic reactions into four major categories: substitution, addition, elimination, and rearrangement reactions.

Teaches concepts: concept:addition_reactions (Addition reactions), concept:classification_of_organic_reactions (Classification of Organic Reactions), concept:elimination_reactions (Elimination reactions), concept:rearrangement_reactions (Rearrangement reactions), concept:substitution_reactions (Substitution reactions)

Summary: This section defines substrates and reagents in organic chemical reactions. It classifies reagents into nucleophiles and electrophiles based on their electron-donating or electron-accepting properties, and explains how to identify electrophilic centers in polar molecules.
Key terms: Substrate, Reagent, Nucleophile, Electrophile, Electrophilic center
Teaches concepts: concept:electrophile (Electrophile), concept:electrophilic_center (Electrophilic Center), concept:nucleophile (Nucleophile), concept:reagent (Reagent), concept:substrate (Substrate)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:8
Section: Electron Movement in Organic Reactions
Summary: This section explains how electron movement in organic reactions is represented using curved-arrow notation. It covers the depiction of electron pair shifts using full-headed curved arrows and single electron shifts using half-headed fish-hook arrows.
Key terms: Curved-arrow notation, Electron pair shift, Fish-hook arrow, Single electron movement

text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:8
Section: Electron Displacement Effects in Covalent Bonds
Summary: This section introduces the concept of electron displacement in covalent bonds of organic molecules. It classifies these displacements into permanent effects, like inductive and resonance effects, and temporary effects, such as the electromeric effect.
Key terms: electron displacement, permanent polarisation, inductive effect, resonance effect, electromeric effect, polarisability effect
Teaches concepts: concept:permanent_electron_displacement_effect (Permanent Electron Displacement Effect), concept:temporary_electron_displacement_effect (Temporary Electron Displacement Effect)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:8
Section: Inductive Effect
Summary: This section introduces the inductive effect, which is the polarization of a sigma-bond caused by an adjacent polar bond. It explains how this effect decreases with distance and classifies substituents into electron-withdrawing (-I) and electron-donating (+I) groups.
Key terms: inductive effect, polar covalent bond, electron-withdrawing group, electron-donating group, -I effect, +I effect
Teaches concepts: concept:electron_donating_group_i_effect (Electron-Donating Group (+I effect)), concept:electron_withdrawing_group_i_effect (Electron-Withdrawing Group (-I effect)), concept:inductive_effect (Inductive Effect), concept:polar_covalent_bond (Polar Covalent Bond)
Requires concepts: concept:covalent_bond (Covalent Bond), concept:electronegativity (Electronegativity)
title: Inductive Effect

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:8
Section: Resonance Structure
Summary: This section introduces the concept of resonance in organic molecules, explaining how some molecules are best represented as a hybrid of multiple canonical structures. It defines resonance stabilization energy and outlines the rules for drawing and determining the relative stability of resonance structures.
Key terms: Resonance, Canonical structures, Resonance hybrid, Resonance stabilization energy, Lewis structure
Teaches concepts: concept:canonical_structures (Canonical Structures), concept:resonance_hybrid (Resonance Hybrid), concept:resonance_stability_rules (Resonance Stability Rules), concept:resonance_stabilization_energy (Resonance Stabilization Energy)
Requires concepts: concept:electronegativity (Electronegativity), concept:lewis_structure (Lewis Structure), concept:octet_rule (Octet Rule)
title: Resonance Structure

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:8
Section: Resonance Effect
Summary: This section defines and explains the resonance (or mesomeric) effect, which describes polarity produced in a molecule through the interaction of pi-bonds or pi-bonds and lone pairs. It distinguishes between the positive resonance effect (+R) and negative resonance effect (-R), illustrating them with examples like aniline and nitrobenzene, and defines conjugated systems.
Key terms: Resonance Effect, Mesomeric Effect, Positive Resonance Effect, Negative Resonance Effect, Conjugated System, Delocalisation
Teaches concepts: concept:conjugated_system (Conjugated System), concept:negative_resonance_effect_r_effect (Negative Resonance Effect (-R effect)), concept:positive_resonance_effect_r_effect (Positive Resonance Effect (+R effect)), concept:resonance_effect (Resonance Effect)
Requires concepts: concept:lone_pair_of_electrons (Lone Pair of Electrons), concept:pi_bond (Pi Bond), concept:polarity (Polarity)
title: Resonance Effect

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:8
Section: Electromeric Effect (E effect)
Summary: This section defines and explains the electromeric effect (E effect), a temporary electronic effect involving the complete transfer of a shared pair of pi-electrons in the presence of an attacking reagent. It distinguishes between the positive (+E) and negative (-E) electromeric effects and describes how the electromeric effect predominates when in competition with the inductive effect.
Key terms: Electromeric Effect, Temporary effect, pi-electrons, Positive Electromeric Effect, Negative Electromeric Effect, Inductive Effect
Teaches concepts: concept:competition_between_inductive_and_electromeric_effects (Competition between Inductive and Electromeric Effects), concept:electromeric_effect (Electromeric Effect), concept:negative_electromeric_effect_e_effect (Negative Electromeric Effect (-E effect)), concept:positive_electromeric_effect_e_effect (Positive Electromeric Effect (+E effect))
Requires concepts: concept:inductive_effect (Inductive Effect), concept:pi_electrons (Pi electrons)
title: Electromeric Effect (E effect)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:8
Section: Hyperconjugation
Summary: This section introduces hyperconjugation as a permanent stabilizing interaction involving the delocalization of C-H sigma electrons. It explains the mechanism of hyperconjugation in carbocations and alkenes, and details how it determines the relative stability of carbocations.
Key terms: Hyperconjugation, No-bond resonance, Carbocation stability, Sigma-pi overlap, Delocalisation
Teaches concepts: concept:carbocation_stability_order (Carbocation Stability Order), concept:hyperconjugation (Hyperconjugation), concept:hyperconjugation_in_alkenes (Hyperconjugation in Alkenes), concept:hyperconjugation_in_carbocations (Hyperconjugation in Carbocations), concept:no_bond_resonance (No-Bond Resonance)
Requires concepts: concept:carbocations (Carbocations), concept:resonance (Resonance), concept:sigma_and_pi_bonds (Sigma and Pi Bonds)
title: Hyperconjugation

Summary: This section introduces various physical methods used to purify organic compounds based on their specific chemical and physical properties. It covers key techniques including sublimation, crystallisation, and several types of distillation such as simple, fractional, vacuum, and steam distillation.

Summary: This section describes the principle and process of differential extraction, which separates organic compounds from aqueous solutions using immiscible organic solvents. It also introduces continuous extraction as an alternative technique when the compound has low solubility in the organic solvent.

Summary: This section introduces chromatography as a versatile technique for separating, purifying, and testing the purity of compounds. It details the principles and applications of adsorption chromatography (including column and thin-layer chromatography) and partition chromatography.

Summary: This section introduces alkanes as saturated open-chain hydrocarbons, defining their general formula, homologous series, and historical designation as paraffins. Summary: This section covers the nomenclature and structural isomerism of alkanes, including the classification of carbon atoms as primary, secondary, tertiary, or quaternary. It details the systematic IUPAC rules for naming branched alkanes, drawing structures from IUPAC names, and correcting erroneous names.

Summary: This section covers the physical and chemical properties of alkanes. It explains physical trends such as boiling point and solubility, and details chemical reactions including halogenation, combustion, controlled oxidation, isomerisation, aromatization, and pyrolysis.
Key terms: Alkanes, van der Waals forces, Halogenation, Free radical mechanism, Combustion, Isomerisation, Aromatization, Pyrolysis
Teaches concepts: concept:combustion_of_alkanes (Combustion of Alkanes), concept:controlled_oxidation_of_alkanes (Controlled Oxidation of Alkanes), concept:halogenation_of_alkanes (Halogenation of Alkanes), concept:isomerisation_and_aromatization_of_alkanes (Isomerisation and Aromatization of Alkanes), concept:physical_properties_of_alkanes (Physical Properties of Alkanes), concept:pyrolysis_of_alkanes (Pyrolysis of Alkanes)
Requires concepts: concept:alkane_structure_and_nomenclature (Alkane Structure and Nomenclature), concept:covalent_bonding_and_polarity (Covalent Bonding and Polarity), concept:homolytic_cleavage_and_free_radicals (Homolytic Cleavage and Free Radicals)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: Conformations
Summary: This section introduces the concept of conformations arising from the rotation around carbon-carbon single bonds, using ethane as a primary example. It explains eclipsed, staggered, and skew conformations, their representation via Sawhorse and Newman projections, and their relative stability based on torsional strain.
Key terms: Conformations, Conformers, Rotamers, Torsional strain, Eclipsed conformation, Staggered conformation, Sawhorse projection, Newman projection, Dihedral angle
Teaches concepts: concept:conformations (Conformations), concept:dihedral_angle (Dihedral Angle), concept:eclipsed_and_staggered_conformations (Eclipsed and Staggered Conformations), concept:newman_projections (Newman Projections), concept:sawhorse_projections (Sawhorse Projections), concept:torsional_strain (Torsional Strain)
Requires concepts: concept:alkanes (Alkanes), concept:sigma_bond (Sigma Bond)
title: Conformations

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: ALKENES
Summary: This section covers alkenes, which are unsaturated hydrocarbons characterized by a carbon-carbon double bond. It details their structure, nomenclature, structural and geometrical isomerism, methods of preparation, and key chemical reactions including electrophilic additions, oxidation, and ozonolysis.
Key terms: Alkenes, Geometrical Isomerism, Dehydrohalogenation, Markovnikov Rule, Peroxide Effect, Ozonolysis
Teaches concepts: concept:dehydrohalogenation (Dehydrohalogenation), concept:geometrical_isomerism (Geometrical isomerism), concept:markovnikovs_rule (Markovnikov's Rule), concept:ozonolysis (Ozonolysis), concept:peroxide_effect (Peroxide Effect), concept:structure_of_the_carbon_carbon_double_bond (Structure of the Carbon-Carbon Double Bond)
Requires concepts: concept:electrophiles_and_nucleophiles (Electrophiles and Nucleophiles), concept:isomerism (Isomerism), concept:orbital_hybridization (Orbital Hybridization)
title: ALKENES

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: ALKYNES
Summary: This section introduces alkynes as unsaturated hydrocarbons characterized by at least one carbon-carbon triple bond. It covers their general molecular formula, the simplest member ethyne (acetylene), and their general utility.
Key terms: alkynes, unsaturated hydrocarbons, triple bond, ethyne, acetylene
Teaches concepts: concept:alkynes (Alkynes), concept:ethyne (Ethyne)
Requires concepts: concept:hydrocarbons (Hydrocarbons), concept:unsaturated_hydrocarbons (Unsaturated hydrocarbons)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: Nomenclature and Isomerism
Summary: This section explains the nomenclature and structural isomerism of alkynes. It details both the common and IUPAC naming systems and describes how alkynes exhibit position and chain isomerism.
Key terms: Alkynes, IUPAC nomenclature, Common nomenclature, Position isomerism, Chain isomerism, Acetylene
Teaches concepts: concept:chain_isomerism_in_alkynes (Chain Isomerism in Alkynes), concept:nomenclature_of_alkynes (Nomenclature of Alkynes), concept:position_isomerism_in_alkynes (Position Isomerism in Alkynes)
Requires concepts: concept:alkynes (Alkynes), concept:iupac_nomenclature_of_alkanes (IUPAC Nomenclature of Alkanes), concept:structural_isomerism (Structural Isomerism)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: Structure of Triple Bond
Summary: This section explains the electronic structure and geometry of a carbon-carbon triple bond using ethyne as the primary example. It details the sp hybridization of carbon, the formation of one sigma and two pi bonds, and the resulting linear geometry and bond parameters.
Key terms: sp hybridization, triple bond, sigma bond, pi bond, ethyne, bond enthalpy, bond length
Teaches concepts: concept:ethyne_geometry (Ethyne Geometry), concept:sp_hybridization_in_ethyne (sp Hybridization in Ethyne), concept:triple_bond_parameters (Triple Bond Parameters), concept:triple_bond_structure (Triple Bond Structure)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: Preparation
Summary: This section outlines the preparation methods for alkynes, specifically focusing on the industrial synthesis of ethyne from calcium carbide and the preparation of alkynes from vicinal dihalides via dehydrohalogenation.
Key terms: Ethyne, Calcium carbide, Vicinal dihalides, Dehydrohalogenation, Sodamide
Teaches concepts: concept:preparation_of_alkynes_from_vicinal_dihalides (Preparation of Alkynes from Vicinal Dihalides), concept:preparation_of_ethyne_from_calcium_carbide (Preparation of Ethyne from Calcium Carbide)
Requires concepts: concept:alkynes (Alkynes), concept:dehydrohalogenation (Dehydrohalogenation), concept:vicinal_dihalides (Vicinal Dihalides)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: Properties
Summary: This section covers the physical and chemical properties of alkynes, highlighting their physical trends, acidic character due to sp hybridization, and various addition reactions including hydrogenation, halogenation, hydration, and polymerisation.
Key terms: alkynes, acidic character, sp hybridisation, electrophilic addition, vinylic cation, Markovnikov's rule, hydration, polymerisation
Teaches concepts: concept:acidic_character_of_alkynes (Acidic Character of Alkynes), concept:electrophilic_addition_of_alkynes (Electrophilic Addition of Alkynes), concept:hydration_of_alkynes (Hydration of Alkynes), concept:physical_properties_of_alkynes (Physical Properties of Alkynes), concept:polymerisation_of_alkynes (Polymerisation of Alkynes)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: AROMATIC HYDROCARBON
Summary: This section introduces aromatic hydrocarbons, also known as arenes, which are characterized by their unique stability and the presence of a benzene ring. It distinguishes between benzenoid and non-benzenoid aromatic compounds and provides common examples such as benzene, toluene, naphthalene, and biphenyl.
Key terms: aromatic hydrocarbons, arenes, benzene ring, benzenoids, non-benzenoids
Teaches concepts: concept:aromatic_hydrocarbons (Aromatic hydrocarbons), concept:benzenoid_compounds (Benzenoid Compounds), concept:non_benzenoid_compounds (Non-benzenoid Compounds)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: Nomenclature and Isomerism
Summary: This section explains the nomenclature and position isomerism in benzene derivatives, focusing on monosubstituted and disubstituted products. It details the ortho, meta, and para positions using dimethylbenzene (xylene) isomers as primary examples.
Key terms: benzene derivatives, monosubstituted benzene, disubstituted benzene, position isomers, ortho, meta, para, dimethylbenzene, xylene
Teaches concepts: concept:dimethylbenzene_isomers (Dimethylbenzene Isomers), concept:disubstituted_benzene_isomerism (Disubstituted Benzene Isomerism), concept:monosubstituted_benzene_derivatives (Monosubstituted Benzene Derivatives), concept:ortho_meta_and_para_positions (Ortho, Meta, and Para Positions)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: Structure of Benzene
Summary: This section details the structural elucidation of benzene, starting from Kekulé's cyclic proposal to modern resonance and orbital descriptions. It explains benzene's stability, planar geometry, and uniform bond lengths through sp2 hybridization and pi-electron delocalization.
Key terms: Benzene, Kekulé structure, Resonance hybrid, sp2 hybridization, Delocalization, Bond length
Teaches concepts: concept:kekul_structure_of_benzene (Kekulé Structure of Benzene), concept:orbital_hybridization_in_benzene (Orbital Hybridization in Benzene), concept:pi_electron_delocalization_in_benzene (Pi Electron Delocalization in Benzene), concept:resonance_hybrid_of_benzene (Resonance Hybrid of Benzene)
Requires concepts: concept:resonance (Resonance), concept:sigma_and_pi_bonds (Sigma and Pi Bonds), concept:sp2_hybridization (sp2 Hybridization)
title: Structure of Benzene

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: Aromaticity
Summary: This section outlines the criteria required for a cyclic compound to be classified as aromatic, focusing on planarity, complete pi-electron delocalisation, and the Hückel Rule. It also provides key examples of aromatic systems, including benzene, cyclopentadienyl anion, and polycyclic hydrocarbons like naphthalene.
Key terms: Aromaticity, Hückel Rule, Planarity, Delocalisation, Pi electrons, Benzene
Teaches concepts: concept:aromatic_compounds (Aromatic Compounds), concept:aromaticity (Aromaticity), concept:h_ckel_rule (Hückel Rule)
Requires concepts: concept:delocalisation (Delocalisation), concept:pi_electrons (Pi electrons), concept:planarity (Planarity)
title: Aromaticity

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: Preparation of Benzene
Summary: This section details the laboratory and commercial methods used to prepare benzene. It covers three primary chemical pathways: the cyclic polymerisation of ethyne, the decarboxylation of sodium benzoate, and the reduction of phenol using zinc dust.
Key terms: Benzene, Cyclic polymerisation, Ethyne, Decarboxylation, Sodium benzoate, Sodalime, Phenol, Zinc dust
Teaches concepts: concept:cyclic_polymerisation_of_ethyne (Cyclic polymerisation of ethyne), concept:decarboxylation_of_sodium_benzoate (Decarboxylation of sodium benzoate), concept:reduction_of_phenol (Reduction of phenol)
Requires concepts: concept:benzene (Benzene), concept:decarboxylation (Decarboxylation), concept:ethyne (Ethyne), concept:phenol (Phenol)
title: Preparation of Benzene

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: Properties
Summary: This section details the physical and chemical properties of aromatic hydrocarbons, focusing on electrophilic aromatic substitution reactions such as nitration, halogenation, sulphonation, and Friedel-Crafts reactions. It explains the step-by-step mechanism of these substitutions via a resonance-stabilized arenium ion intermediate, alongside addition and combustion reactions of benzene.
Key terms: Aromatic hydrocarbons, Electrophilic substitution, Nitration, Halogenation, Sulphonation, Friedel-Crafts alkylation, Friedel-Crafts acylation, Arenium ion, Sigma complex, Benzene hexachloride
Teaches concepts: concept:addition_reactions_of_benzene (Addition Reactions of Benzene), concept:arenium_ion_sigma_complex (Arenium Ion (Sigma Complex)), concept:electrophilic_aromatic_substitution (Electrophilic aromatic substitution), concept:friedel_crafts_reactions (Friedel-Crafts Reactions), concept:mechanism_of_electrophilic_substitution (Mechanism of Electrophilic Substitution)
Requires concepts: concept:aromaticity (Aromaticity), concept:carbocation_rearrangement (Carbocation Rearrangement), concept:electrophiles_and_nucleophiles (Electrophiles and Nucleophiles), concept:lewis_acids_and_bases (Lewis Acids and Bases)
title: Properties

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: Directive influence of a functional group in monosubstituted benzene
Summary: This section explains how substituents on a monosubstituted benzene ring direct incoming electrophiles to specific positions (ortho, para, or meta) and alter the ring's reactivity. It details the mechanisms of activating and deactivating groups using resonance and inductive effects.
Key terms: ortho and para directing groups, meta directing groups, activating groups, deactivating groups, resonance effect, inductive effect
Teaches concepts: concept:activating_groups (Activating Groups), concept:deactivating_groups (Deactivating Groups), concept:directive_influence_of_halogens (Directive Influence of Halogens), concept:meta_directing_groups (Meta Directing Groups), concept:ortho_and_para_directing_groups (Ortho and Para Directing Groups)
Requires concepts: concept:electrophilic_aromatic_substitution (Electrophilic aromatic substitution), concept:inductive_effect (Inductive Effect), concept:resonance_effect (Resonance Effect)
title: Directive influence of a functional group in monosubstituted benzene

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:11:9
Section: CARCINOGENICITY AND TOXICITY
Summary: This section explains the toxicity and carcinogenic properties of benzene and polynuclear hydrocarbons with more than two fused benzene rings. It describes their formation via incomplete combustion of organic matter and their biochemical impact on DNA.
Key terms: carcinogenicity, polynuclear hydrocarbons, incomplete combustion, DNA damage, benzene
Teaches concepts: concept:carcinogenic_polynuclear_hydrocarbons (Carcinogenic Polynuclear Hydrocarbons)
Requires concepts: concept:benzene (Benzene), concept:incomplete_combustion (Incomplete Combustion)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:10
Section: Carbohydrates
Summary: This section introduces carbohydrates, defining them as optically active polyhydroxy aldehydes or ketones and classifying them into monosaccharides, oligosaccharides, and polysaccharides. It details the chemical preparation, open-chain structural elucidation, and D/L configuration of glucose, while addressing its limitations to establish its cyclic hemiacetal (pyranose) structure and anomeric forms.
Key terms: Carbohydrates, Monosaccharides, Oligosaccharides, Polysaccharides, Reducing sugars, Glucose, D and L configuration, Hemiacetal, Anomers, Pyranose
Teaches concepts: concept:anomers (Anomers), concept:carbohydrates (Carbohydrates), concept:cyclic_structure_of_glucose (Cyclic Structure of Glucose), concept:d_and_l_configuration (D and L Configuration), concept:reducing_and_non_reducing_sugars (Reducing and Non-reducing Sugars), concept:structural_elucidation_of_glucose (Structural Elucidation of Glucose)
Requires concepts: concept:aldehydes_and_ketones (Aldehydes and Ketones), concept:hemiacetal_formation (Hemiacetal Formation), concept:optical_activity_and_stereoisomerism (Optical Activity and Stereoisomerism)
title: Carbohydrates

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:10
Section: Fructose
Summary: This section introduces fructose as an important ketohexose monosaccharide, detailing its open-chain structure as a laevorotatory D-sugar. It also explains its cyclic furanose structure and the corresponding alpha and beta Haworth projections.
Key terms: Fructose, Ketohexose, Furanose, Anomers, Haworth projection, D-(-)-fructose
Teaches concepts: concept:anomers_of_fructose (Anomers of Fructose), concept:fructose (Fructose), concept:furanose_structure_of_fructose (Furanose Structure of Fructose)
Requires concepts: concept:anomeric_carbon (Anomeric Carbon), concept:monosaccharide (Monosaccharide)
title: Fructose

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:10
Section: Disaccharides
Summary: This section defines disaccharides and explains the formation of glycosidic linkages between monosaccharide units. It classifies disaccharides into reducing and non-reducing sugars, and details the structures and properties of sucrose, maltose, and lactose.
Key terms: Disaccharides, Glycosidic linkage, Reducing sugar, Non-reducing sugar, Sucrose, Invert sugar, Maltose, Lactose
Teaches concepts: concept:disaccharides (Disaccharides), concept:glycosidic_linkage (Glycosidic Linkage), concept:lactose (Lactose), concept:maltose (Maltose), concept:sucrose (Sucrose)
Requires concepts: concept:monosaccharides (Monosaccharides), concept:optical_rotation (Optical Rotation)
title: Disaccharides

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:10
Section: Polysaccharides
Summary: This section introduces polysaccharides as complex carbohydrates composed of numerous monosaccharide units joined by glycosidic linkages. It details the structures, properties, and biological roles of key polysaccharides: starch (comprising amylose and amylopectin), cellulose, and glycogen.
Key terms: Polysaccharides, Starch, Amylose, Amylopectin, Cellulose, Glycogen, Glycosidic linkage
Teaches concepts: concept:amylopectin (Amylopectin), concept:amylose (Amylose), concept:cellulose (Cellulose), concept:glycogen (Glycogen), concept:polysaccharides (Polysaccharides), concept:starch (Starch)
Requires concepts: concept:glycosidic_linkage (Glycosidic Linkage), concept:monosaccharides (Monosaccharides)
title: Polysaccharides

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:10
Section: Importance of Carbohydrates
Summary: This section outlines the biological and industrial significance of carbohydrates. It highlights their roles as energy sources, storage molecules like starch and glycogen, structural components like cellulose, and essential constituents of nucleic acids.
Key terms: Carbohydrates, Starch, Glycogen, Cellulose, D-ribose, 2-deoxy-D-ribose, Nucleic acids
Teaches concepts: concept:aldopentoses_in_nucleic_acids (Aldopentoses in Nucleic Acids), concept:biological_significance_of_carbohydrates (Biological Significance of Carbohydrates), concept:industrial_applications_of_cellulose (Industrial Applications of Cellulose), concept:structural_carbohydrates (Structural Carbohydrates)
Requires concepts: concept:classification_of_carbohydrates (Classification of Carbohydrates), concept:nucleic_acids (Nucleic Acids)
title: Importance of Carbohydrates

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:10
Section: Proteins
Summary: This section introduces proteins as essential biomolecules and polymers of alpha-amino acids. It details their classification into globular proteins and explains the four levels of protein structure: primary, secondary, tertiary, and quaternary.
Key terms: proteins, alpha-amino acids, globular proteins, primary structure, secondary structure, tertiary structure, quaternary structure
Teaches concepts: concept:globular_proteins (Globular Proteins), concept:primary_structure_of_proteins (Primary Structure of Proteins), concept:proteins (Proteins), concept:quaternary_structure_of_proteins (Quaternary Structure of Proteins), concept:secondary_structure_of_proteins (Secondary Structure of Proteins), concept:tertiary_structure_of_proteins (Tertiary Structure of Proteins)
Requires concepts: concept:amino_acids (Amino Acids), concept:hydrogen_bonding (Hydrogen Bonding), concept:polymers (Polymers)
title: Proteins

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:10
Section: Amino Acids
Summary: This section introduces amino acids as organic compounds containing both amino and carboxyl functional groups, focusing on the structure and nomenclature of alpha-amino acids which make up proteins. It details their classification, representation using three-letter and one-letter codes, and lists the twenty natural amino acids with their specific side chains.
Key terms: amino acids, alpha-amino acids, carboxyl group, amino group, side chain, proteins
Teaches concepts: concept:alpha_amino_acids (Alpha-Amino Acids), concept:amino_acid_side_chain (Amino Acid Side Chain), concept:amino_acids (Amino Acids)
Requires concepts: concept:amino_group (Amino Group), concept:carboxyl_group (Carboxyl Group)
title: Amino Acids

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:10
Section: Denaturation of Proteins
Summary: This section defines native proteins and explains the process of denaturation, which occurs when physical or chemical changes disrupt a protein's hydrogen bonds. It describes how denaturation destroys secondary and tertiary structures while leaving the primary structure intact, using examples like egg white coagulation and milk curdling.
Key terms: native protein, denaturation, hydrogen bonds, coagulation of egg white, curdling of milk
Teaches concepts: concept:denaturation_of_proteins (Denaturation of Proteins), concept:native_protein (Native Protein)
Requires concepts: concept:hydrogen_bonding (Hydrogen Bonding), concept:structure_of_proteins (Structure of Proteins)
title: Denaturation of Proteins

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:10
Section: Enzymes
Summary: This section introduces enzymes as highly specific biocatalysts that are almost always globular proteins. It explains their nomenclature based on substrates or reaction types, typically ending with the suffix '-ase'.
Key terms: Enzymes, Biocatalysts, Globular proteins, Nomenclature, Maltase
Teaches concepts: concept:enzyme_nomenclature (Enzyme Nomenclature), concept:enzymes (Enzymes)
Requires concepts: concept:catalysis (Catalysis), concept:globular_proteins (Globular Proteins)
title: Enzymes

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:10
Section: Mechanism of Enzyme Action
Summary: This section explains the mechanism of enzyme action, focusing on how enzymes increase reaction efficiency by lowering the activation energy. It illustrates this concept using the comparative activation energies of sucrose hydrolysis with and without the sucrase enzyme.
Key terms: Enzymes, Activation energy, Enzyme efficiency, Sucrase, Acid hydrolysis
Teaches concepts: concept:activation_energy_reduction_by_enzymes (Activation Energy Reduction by Enzymes), concept:enzyme_action_mechanism (Enzyme Action Mechanism)
Requires concepts: concept:activation_energy (Activation Energy), concept:catalysis (Catalysis), concept:enzymes (Enzymes)
title: Mechanism of Enzyme Action

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:10
Section: Vitamins
Summary: This section defines vitamins as essential organic compounds required in small amounts for growth and health. It classifies them into fat-soluble and water-soluble categories and details their dietary sources and deficiency diseases.
Key terms: Vitamins, Fat-soluble vitamins, Water-soluble vitamins, Deficiency diseases
Teaches concepts: concept:fat_soluble_vitamins (Fat-soluble vitamins), concept:vitamin_deficiency_diseases (Vitamin deficiency diseases), concept:vitamins (Vitamins), concept:water_soluble_vitamins (Water-soluble vitamins)
Requires concepts: concept:organic_compounds (Organic compounds), concept:solubility (Solubility)
title: Vitamins

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:10
Section: Nucleic Acids
Summary: This section introduces nucleic acids, focusing on their chemical composition, structures, and biological functions. It details the formation of nucleosides and nucleotides, the phosphodiester linkages that form polynucleotides, the double helix structure of DNA, and applications like DNA fingerprinting.
Key terms: Nucleic acids, DNA, RNA, Nucleotide, Nucleoside, Phosphodiester linkage, Double helix, DNA fingerprinting
Teaches concepts: concept:biological_functions_of_nucleic_acids (Biological Functions of Nucleic Acids), concept:dna_double_helix_structure (DNA Double Helix Structure), concept:dna_fingerprinting (DNA Fingerprinting), concept:nucleic_acids (Nucleic Acids), concept:nucleosides_and_nucleotides (Nucleosides and Nucleotides), concept:phosphodiester_linkage (Phosphodiester Linkage)
Requires concepts: concept:carbohydrates (Carbohydrates), concept:hydrogen_bonding (Hydrogen Bonding), concept:polymers (Polymers)
title: Nucleic Acids

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:10
Section: Hormones
Summary: This section introduces hormones as intercellular messengers secreted by endocrine glands and classifies them chemically into steroids, polypeptides, and amino acid derivatives. It details their physiological roles, focusing on glucose homeostasis, thyroid hormones like thyroxine, and adrenal and gonadal steroid hormones.
Key terms: Hormones, Endocrine glands, Insulin, Glucagon, Thyroxine, Steroid hormones, Glucocorticoids, Mineralocorticoids, Gonadal hormones
Teaches concepts: concept:adrenal_cortex_hormones (Adrenal Cortex Hormones), concept:chemical_classification_of_hormones (Chemical Classification of Hormones), concept:glucose_homeostasis (Glucose Homeostasis), concept:gonadal_hormones (Gonadal Hormones), concept:hormones (Hormones), concept:thyroxine (Thyroxine)
Requires concepts: concept:amino_acids (Amino Acids), concept:proteins_and_polypeptides (Proteins and Polypeptides)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:1
Section: Types of Solutions
Summary: This section defines solutions as homogeneous mixtures and identifies their components as solutes and solvents. It also classifies solutions into gaseous, liquid, and solid types based on the physical state of the solvent, providing examples for each of the nine possible combinations.
Key terms: homogeneous mixture, solvent, solute, binary solution, gaseous solution, liquid solution, solid solution
Teaches concepts: concept:binary_solution (Binary Solution), concept:classification_of_solutions (Classification of Solutions), concept:solute (Solute), concept:solution (Solution), concept:solvent (Solvent)
Requires concepts: concept:homogeneous_mixture (Homogeneous Mixture), concept:states_of_matter (States of Matter)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:1
Section: Expressing Concentration of Solutions
Summary: This section covers various quantitative methods for expressing the concentration of solutions, including mass percentage, volume percentage, parts per million, mole fraction, molarity, and molality. It also explains how temperature affects these concentration units depending on whether they are based on mass or volume.
Key terms: Mass percentage, Volume percentage, Parts per million, Mole fraction, Molarity, Molality
Teaches concepts: concept:mass_percentage (Mass Percentage), concept:molality (Molality), concept:molarity (Molarity), concept:mole_fraction (Mole Fraction), concept:parts_per_million (Parts Per Million), concept:volume_percentage (Volume Percentage)
Requires concepts: concept:molar_mass (Molar Mass), concept:mole_unit (Mole (Unit)), concept:solution_solute_and_solvent (Solution, Solute, and Solvent)
