subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:1
Section: Solubility
Summary: This section defines solubility and examines the factors that influence the solubility of solids and gases in liquid solvents, including temperature, pressure, and molecular interactions. It details the dynamic equilibrium of dissolution and crystallisation, and introduces Henry's Law to quantitatively describe gas solubility.
Key terms: Solubility, Dissolution, Crystallisation, Saturated solution, Henry's law, Henry's law constant
Teaches concepts: concept:dissolution_and_crystallisation_equilibrium (Dissolution and Crystallisation Equilibrium), concept:henrys_law (Henry's Law), concept:henrys_law_constant (Henry's Law Constant), concept:saturated_solution (Saturated Solution), concept:solubility (Solubility)
Requires concepts: concept:le_chateliers_principle (Le Chatelier's Principle), concept:mole_fraction (Mole Fraction), concept:partial_pressure (Partial Pressure), concept:polarity (Polarity)
title: Solubility

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:1
Section: Vapour Pressure of Liquid Solutions
Summary: This section explains the vapour pressure of binary liquid solutions, focusing on liquid-liquid and solid-liquid mixtures. It introduces Raoult's law to relate partial vapour pressure to mole fraction, derives total vapour pressure using Dalton's law, and explains the mechanism of vapour pressure lowering by non-volatile solutes.
Key terms: Vapour Pressure, Raoult's Law, Dalton's Law, Mole Fraction, Volatile, Non-volatile Solute, Vapour Pressure Lowering
Teaches concepts: concept:lowering_of_vapour_pressure (Lowering of Vapour Pressure), concept:raoults_law_as_a_special_case_of_henrys_law (Raoult's Law as a Special Case of Henry's Law), concept:raoults_law_for_volatile_solutes (Raoult's Law for Volatile Solutes), concept:total_vapour_pressure_of_binary_solutions (Total Vapour Pressure of Binary Solutions), concept:vapour_phase_composition (Vapour Phase Composition)
Requires concepts: concept:daltons_law_of_partial_pressures (Dalton's Law of Partial Pressures), concept:henrys_law (Henry's Law), concept:mole_fraction (Mole Fraction)
title: Vapour Pressure of Liquid Solutions

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:1
Section: Ideal and Non-ideal Solutions
Summary: This section classifies liquid-liquid solutions into ideal and non-ideal solutions based on their adherence to Raoult's law. It explains the molecular basis of ideal behavior and deviations (positive and negative) from Raoult's law, and introduces azeotropes as constant-boiling binary mixtures.
Key terms: Ideal solution, Non-ideal solution, Raoult's law, Positive deviation, Negative deviation, Azeotrope
Teaches concepts: concept:azeotropes (Azeotropes), concept:ideal_solution (Ideal Solution), concept:negative_deviation_from_raoults_law (Negative Deviation from Raoult's Law), concept:non_ideal_solution (Non-ideal Solution), concept:positive_deviation_from_raoults_law (Positive Deviation from Raoult's Law)
Requires concepts: concept:intermolecular_forces (Intermolecular Forces), concept:raoults_law (Raoult's Law), concept:vapour_pressure (Vapour Pressure)
title: Ideal and Non-ideal Solutions

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:1
Section: Colligative Properties and Determination of Molar Mass
Summary: This section introduces colligative properties as solution properties depending solely on the number of solute particles. It details the physical basis, mathematical derivations, and molar mass determination for three key properties: relative lowering of vapour pressure, elevation of boiling point, and depression of freezing point.
Key terms: colligative properties, relative lowering of vapour pressure, elevation of boiling point, depression of freezing point, ebullioscopic constant, cryoscopic constant, molar mass determination
Teaches concepts: concept:colligative_properties (Colligative Properties), concept:depression_of_freezing_point (Depression of Freezing Point), concept:elevation_of_boiling_point (Elevation of Boiling Point), concept:molal_elevation_and_depression_constants (Molal Elevation and Depression Constants), concept:relative_lowering_of_vapour_pressure (Relative Lowering of Vapour Pressure)
Requires concepts: concept:molality (Molality), concept:mole_fraction (Mole Fraction), concept:raoults_law (Raoult's Law), concept:vapour_pressure (Vapour Pressure)
title: Colligative Properties and Determination of Molar Mass

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:1
Section: Osmosis and Osmotic Pressure
Summary: This section introduces osmosis and semipermeable membranes, defining osmotic pressure as the excess pressure required to halt solvent flow. It establishes osmotic pressure as a colligative property, derives the equation to calculate solute molar mass, and classifies solutions as isotonic, hypertonic, or hypotonic.
Key terms: semipermeable membrane, osmosis, osmotic pressure, colligative property, isotonic, hypertonic, hypotonic
Teaches concepts: concept:isotonic_hypertonic_and_hypotonic_solutions (Isotonic, Hypertonic, and Hypotonic Solutions), concept:molar_mass_determination_via_osmotic_pressure (Molar Mass Determination via Osmotic Pressure), concept:osmosis (Osmosis), concept:osmotic_pressure (Osmotic Pressure), concept:semipermeable_membrane (Semipermeable Membrane)
Requires concepts: concept:colligative_properties (Colligative Properties), concept:gas_constant_r (Gas Constant (R)), concept:molarity (Molarity)
title: Osmosis and Osmotic Pressure

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:1
Section: Reverse Osmosis and Water Purification
Summary: This section explains the mechanism of reverse osmosis, which occurs when a pressure greater than the osmotic pressure is applied to a solution, reversing the natural flow of solvent. It highlights its practical application in the desalination of sea water using specialized polymer membranes like cellulose acetate.
Key terms: reverse osmosis, osmotic pressure, desalination, semipermeable membrane, cellulose acetate
Teaches concepts: concept:cellulose_acetate_membrane (Cellulose Acetate Membrane), concept:desalination_of_sea_water (Desalination of Sea Water), concept:reverse_osmosis (Reverse Osmosis)
Requires concepts: concept:osmosis (Osmosis), concept:osmotic_pressure (Osmotic Pressure), concept:semipermeable_membrane (Semipermeable Membrane)
title: Reverse Osmosis and Water Purification

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:1
Section: Abnormal Molar Masses
Summary: This section explains how the association or dissociation of solute particles in a solution leads to abnormal molar masses. It introduces the van't Hoff factor to quantify this behavior and presents modified equations for colligative properties.
Key terms: Abnormal molar mass, Dissociation, Association, van't Hoff factor, Colligative properties, Dimerisation
Teaches concepts: concept:abnormal_molar_masses (Abnormal Molar Masses), concept:association_of_solutes (Association of Solutes), concept:dissociation_of_solutes (Dissociation of Solutes), concept:modified_colligative_property_equations (Modified Colligative Property Equations), concept:vant_hoff_factor (van't Hoff Factor)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_equilibrium (Chemical Equilibrium), concept:colligative_properties (Colligative Properties), concept:molar_mass (Molar Mass)
title: Abnormal Molar Masses

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Electrochemical Cells
Summary: This section introduces electrochemistry and details the operation of the Daniell cell as a representative galvanic cell. It also explains how applying an external opposite potential can alter the cell's behavior, transitioning it from a galvanic to an electrolytic cell.
Key terms: Electrochemistry, Daniell cell, Galvanic cell, Electrolytic cell, Redox reaction, External potential
Teaches concepts: concept:daniell_cell (Daniell Cell), concept:electrochemistry (Electrochemistry), concept:electrolytic_cell (Electrolytic Cell), concept:galvanic_cell (Galvanic Cell)
Requires concepts: concept:redox_reactions (Redox Reactions)
title: Electrochemical Cells

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Galvanic Cells
Summary: This section introduces galvanic cells, explaining how they convert chemical energy from spontaneous redox reactions into electrical energy. It covers cell construction, electrode potentials, anode/cathode identification, and standard cell representation and emf calculation.
Key terms: Galvanic Cell, Redox Couple, Electrode Potential, Anode, Cathode, Cell Potential, Electromotive Force
Teaches concepts: concept:anode_and_cathode (Anode and Cathode), concept:cell_potential (Cell Potential), concept:cell_representation_convention (Cell Representation Convention), concept:electrode_potential (Electrode Potential), concept:galvanic_cell (Galvanic Cell), concept:redox_couple (Redox Couple)
Requires concepts: concept:gibbs_energy (Gibbs Energy), concept:redox_reactions (Redox Reactions)
title: Galvanic Cells

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Measurement of Electrode Potential
Summary: This section explains how to measure individual electrode potentials using the Standard Hydrogen Electrode (SHE) as a reference. It details the calculation of standard cell potentials, the role of inert electrodes, and the significance of standard electrode potentials in determining the strength of oxidizing and reducing agents.
Key terms: Standard Hydrogen Electrode, Standard Electrode Potential, Inert Electrode, Daniell Cell, Oxidising Agent, Reducing Agent
Teaches concepts: concept:inert_electrode (Inert Electrode), concept:standard_electrode_potential (Standard Electrode Potential), concept:standard_hydrogen_electrode_she (Standard Hydrogen Electrode (SHE))
Requires concepts: concept:electrode_potential (Electrode Potential), concept:redox_reactions (Redox Reactions)
title: Measurement of Electrode Potential

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Nernst Equation
Summary: This section introduces the Nernst equation, which describes how electrode and cell potentials depend on the concentrations of the reacting chemical species. It provides standard electrode potentials for various redox couples and demonstrates how to calculate cell potentials under non-standard conditions using the Daniell cell and other examples.
Key terms: Nernst equation, electrode potential, standard electrode potential, Daniell cell, Faraday constant, redox couple
Teaches concepts: concept:general_nernst_equation_for_cell_potential (General Nernst Equation for Cell Potential), concept:nernst_equation_for_electrode_potential (Nernst Equation for Electrode Potential), concept:nernst_equation_for_the_daniell_cell (Nernst Equation for the Daniell Cell), concept:simplified_nernst_equation_at_298_k (Simplified Nernst Equation at 298 K), concept:standard_electrode_potential (Standard Electrode Potential)
Requires concepts: concept:daniell_cell (Daniell Cell), concept:redox_reactions (Redox Reactions), concept:standard_hydrogen_electrode (Standard Hydrogen Electrode)
title: Nernst Equation

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Equilibrium Constant from Nernst Equation
Summary: This section derives the relationship between the standard cell potential and the chemical equilibrium constant using the Nernst equation. It explains that at equilibrium, the cell potential becomes zero, allowing the calculation of the equilibrium constant from standard cell potentials.
Key terms: Equilibrium Constant, Nernst Equation, Standard Cell Potential, Cell Potential at Equilibrium
Teaches concepts: concept:cell_potential_at_equilibrium (Cell Potential at Equilibrium), concept:relation_between_standard_cell_potential_and_equilibrium_constant (Relation between Standard Cell Potential and Equilibrium Constant)
Requires concepts: concept:equilibrium_constant (Equilibrium Constant), concept:nernst_equation (Nernst Equation), concept:standard_cell_potential (Standard Cell Potential)
title: Equilibrium Constant from Nernst Equation

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Electrochemical Cell and Gibbs Energy of the Reaction
Summary: This section explains the relationship between the electrical work of a galvanic cell and its Gibbs energy change. It distinguishes between the intensive nature of cell potential and the extensive nature of Gibbs energy, and relates standard cell potential to standard Gibbs energy and the equilibrium constant.
Key terms: Gibbs energy, cell potential, Faraday constant, intensive property, extensive property, equilibrium constant
Teaches concepts: concept:equilibrium_constant_and_standard_gibbs_energy_relation (Equilibrium Constant and Standard Gibbs Energy Relation), concept:gibbs_energy_of_cell_reaction (Gibbs Energy of Cell Reaction), concept:intensive_and_extensive_properties_of_cells (Intensive and Extensive Properties of Cells), concept:standard_gibbs_energy_of_cell_reaction (Standard Gibbs Energy of Cell Reaction)
Requires concepts: concept:cell_potential (Cell Potential), concept:faraday_constant (Faraday Constant), concept:gibbs_free_energy (Gibbs Free Energy)
title: Electrochemical Cell and Gibbs Energy of the Reaction

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Conductance of Electrolytic Solutions
Summary: This section introduces the fundamental concepts of electrical resistance, resistivity, conductance, and conductivity. It classifies materials based on their conductivity and distinguishes between electronic (metallic) conductance and ionic (electrolytic) conductance, detailing the factors that influence each.
Key terms: Electrical resistance, Resistivity, Conductance, Conductivity, Electronic conductance, Ionic conductance, Superconductors
Teaches concepts: concept:conductance_and_conductivity (Conductance and Conductivity), concept:electrical_resistance_and_resistivity (Electrical Resistance and Resistivity), concept:electronic_conductance (Electronic Conductance), concept:ionic_conductance (Ionic Conductance), concept:superconductors (Superconductors)
Requires concepts: concept:electrolytes (Electrolytes), concept:ohms_law (Ohm's Law)
title: Conductance of Electrolytic Solutions

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Measurement of the Conductivity of Ionic Solutions
Summary: This section explains the experimental methodology for measuring the electrical conductivity of ionic solutions using a conductivity cell and a Wheatstone bridge with an alternating current (AC) source. It introduces key parameters such as the cell constant and molar conductivity, detailing their mathematical formulations, units, and practical calculations.
Key terms: conductivity cell, cell constant, Wheatstone bridge, molar conductivity, alternating current, ionic resistance
Teaches concepts: concept:cell_constant (Cell Constant), concept:conductivity_cell (Conductivity Cell), concept:molar_conductivity (Molar Conductivity), concept:wheatstone_bridge_for_ionic_resistance (Wheatstone Bridge for Ionic Resistance)
Requires concepts: concept:electrical_conductivity (Electrical Conductivity), concept:electrical_resistance_and_resistivity (Electrical Resistance and Resistivity), concept:wheatstone_bridge_principle (Wheatstone Bridge Principle)
title: Measurement of the Conductivity of Ionic Solutions

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Variation of Conductivity and Molar Conductivity with Concentration
Summary: This section explains how conductivity and molar conductivity vary with concentration for both strong and weak electrolytes. It introduces limiting molar conductivity and Kohlrausch's law of independent migration of ions, demonstrating how to calculate the degree of dissociation and dissociation constant for weak electrolytes.
Key terms: Conductivity, Molar conductivity, Limiting molar conductivity, Kohlrausch law, Degree of dissociation, Dissociation constant
Teaches concepts: concept:degree_of_dissociation_from_molar_conductivity (Degree of Dissociation from Molar Conductivity), concept:dissociation_constant_of_weak_electrolytes (Dissociation Constant of Weak Electrolytes), concept:kohlrausch_law_of_independent_migration_of_ions (Kohlrausch Law of Independent Migration of Ions), concept:limiting_molar_conductivity (Limiting Molar Conductivity), concept:variation_of_conductivity_with_concentration (Variation of Conductivity with Concentration), concept:variation_of_molar_conductivity_with_dilution (Variation of Molar Conductivity with Dilution)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_equilibrium_and_dissociation_constant (Chemical Equilibrium and Dissociation Constant), concept:conductivity_and_molar_conductivity (Conductivity and Molar Conductivity), concept:electrolytes (Electrolytes)
title: Variation of Conductivity and Molar Conductivity with Concentration

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Electrolytic Cells and Electrolysis
Summary: This section explains the principles and applications of electrolytic cells, where an external voltage drives non-spontaneous chemical reactions. It covers the electrolysis of copper sulphate, industrial applications, Faraday's laws of electrolysis, and the quantitative stoichiometry of electrode reactions using the Faraday constant.
Key terms: Electrolytic cell, Electrolysis, Faraday's First Law, Faraday's Second Law, Faraday constant, Equivalent weight
Teaches concepts: concept:electrolytic_cell (Electrolytic Cell), concept:faraday_constant (Faraday Constant), concept:faradays_first_law_of_electrolysis (Faraday's First Law of Electrolysis), concept:faradays_second_law_of_electrolysis (Faraday's Second Law of Electrolysis), concept:stoichiometry_of_electrode_reactions (Stoichiometry of Electrode Reactions)
Requires concepts: concept:galvanic_cell (Galvanic Cell), concept:redox_reactions (Redox Reactions), concept:stoichiometry (Stoichiometry)
title: Electrolytic Cells and Electrolysis

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Products of Electrolysis
Summary: This section describes the factors that determine the products of electrolysis, such as standard electrode potentials, electrode types, and overpotential. It illustrates these principles using the electrolysis of molten and aqueous sodium chloride, as well as dilute and concentrated sulfuric acid.
Key terms: electrolysis, overpotential, inert electrodes, reactive electrodes, aqueous sodium chloride, sulfuric acid electrolysis
Teaches concepts: concept:electrolysis_of_aqueous_sodium_chloride (Electrolysis of Aqueous Sodium Chloride), concept:electrolysis_of_molten_sodium_chloride (Electrolysis of Molten Sodium Chloride), concept:electrolysis_of_sulfuric_acid (Electrolysis of Sulfuric Acid), concept:factors_influencing_electrolysis_products (Factors Influencing Electrolysis Products), concept:overpotential (Overpotential)
Requires concepts: concept:electrolysis (Electrolysis), concept:standard_electrode_potential (Standard Electrode Potential)
title: Products of Electrolysis

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Batteries
Summary: This section defines batteries as assemblies of electrochemical cells connected in series that convert chemical energy from redox reactions into electrical energy. It outlines the practical requirements of batteries and introduces their classification into primary and secondary types.
Key terms: battery, electrochemical cell, redox reaction, primary battery, secondary battery
Teaches concepts: concept:battery (Battery), concept:primary_and_secondary_batteries (Primary and Secondary Batteries)
Requires concepts: concept:electrochemical_cell (Electrochemical Cell), concept:redox_reactions (Redox Reactions)
title: Batteries

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Primary Batteries
Summary: This section introduces primary batteries, which are non-rechargeable electrochemical cells where the reaction occurs only once. It details the construction, electrode reactions, and characteristics of two common types: the dry cell (Leclanche cell) and the mercury cell.
Key terms: Primary batteries, Dry cell, Leclanche cell, Mercury cell, Cell potential, Anode, Cathode
Teaches concepts: concept:dry_cell_leclanche_cell (Dry Cell (Leclanche Cell)), concept:mercury_cell (Mercury Cell), concept:primary_batteries (Primary Batteries)
Requires concepts: concept:anode_and_cathode (Anode and Cathode), concept:cell_potential (Cell Potential), concept:redox_reactions (Redox Reactions)
title: Primary Batteries

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Secondary Batteries
Summary: This section introduces secondary batteries, which are rechargeable electrochemical cells that can undergo multiple charging and discharging cycles. It details the construction and chemical reactions of the lead storage battery and describes the nickel-cadmium cell as another important secondary cell.
Key terms: secondary cell, lead storage battery, nickel-cadmium cell, discharging, charging, electrolyte
Teaches concepts: concept:lead_storage_battery (Lead Storage Battery), concept:lead_storage_battery_reactions (Lead Storage Battery Reactions), concept:nickel_cadmium_cell (Nickel-Cadmium Cell), concept:secondary_cell (Secondary Cell)
Requires concepts: concept:anode (Anode), concept:cathode (Cathode), concept:electrolyte (Electrolyte)
title: Secondary Batteries

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Fuel Cells
Summary: This section introduces fuel cells as highly efficient, pollution-free galvanic cells that convert the chemical energy of combustion directly into electrical energy. It specifically details the operation, electrode reactions, and applications of the hydrogen-oxygen fuel cell.
Key terms: Fuel cells, Galvanic cells, Hydrogen-oxygen fuel cell, Efficiency, Electrode reactions
Teaches concepts: concept:fuel_cell_efficiency (Fuel Cell Efficiency), concept:fuel_cells (Fuel Cells), concept:hydrogen_oxygen_fuel_cell (Hydrogen-Oxygen Fuel Cell)
Requires concepts: concept:galvanic_cell (Galvanic Cell), concept:redox_reactions (Redox Reactions)
title: Fuel Cells

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:2
Section: Corrosion
Summary: This section explains corrosion as an electrochemical process, focusing on the mechanism of iron rusting and methods for its prevention. It also introduces the concept of the hydrogen economy as a sustainable, electrochemically-driven energy alternative to fossil fuels.
Key terms: Corrosion, Rusting, Electrochemical cell, Anode, Cathode, Sacrificial protection, Hydrogen economy
Teaches concepts: concept:corrosion (Corrosion), concept:corrosion_prevention (Corrosion Prevention), concept:electrochemical_mechanism_of_rusting (Electrochemical Mechanism of Rusting), concept:hydrogen_economy (Hydrogen Economy), concept:sacrificial_protection (Sacrificial Protection)
Requires concepts: concept:electrochemical_cell (Electrochemical Cell), concept:redox_reactions (Redox Reactions), concept:standard_electrode_potential (Standard Electrode Potential)
title: Corrosion

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:3
Section: Chemical Kinetics
Summary: This section introduces chemical kinetics as the study of chemical reaction rates and their mechanisms. It contrasts kinetics with thermodynamics and chemical equilibrium, highlighting how kinetic studies explore the speed of reactions and the factors that influence them.
Key terms: chemical kinetics, reaction rate, reaction mechanism, thermodynamics, chemical equilibrium
Teaches concepts: concept:chemical_kinetics (Chemical Kinetics), concept:reaction_mechanism (Reaction Mechanism), concept:reaction_rate (Reaction Rate)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_equilibrium (Chemical Equilibrium), concept:thermodynamics (Thermodynamics)
title: Chemical Kinetics

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:3
Section: Rate of a Chemical Reaction
Summary: This section defines the rate of a chemical reaction as the change in concentration of reactants or products per unit time. It distinguishes between average and instantaneous rates of reaction, explaining how to determine them graphically and mathematically. Additionally, it details how stoichiometric coefficients are used to formulate consistent rate expressions.
Key terms: Rate of reaction, Average rate, Instantaneous rate, Stoichiometric coefficient, Concentration-time curve
Teaches concepts: concept:average_rate_of_reaction (Average Rate of Reaction), concept:instantaneous_rate_of_reaction (Instantaneous Rate of Reaction), concept:rate_of_a_chemical_reaction (Rate of a Chemical Reaction), concept:stoichiometric_rate_expression (Stoichiometric Rate Expression), concept:units_of_reaction_rate (Units of Reaction Rate)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_concentration (Chemical Concentration), concept:derivative_calculus (Derivative (Calculus)), concept:stoichiometry (Stoichiometry)
title: Rate of a Chemical Reaction

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:3
Section: Factors Influencing Rate of a Reaction
Summary: This section introduces the factors that influence the rate of a chemical reaction, focusing on reactant concentration. It defines the rate law, the rate constant, and the differential rate equation, emphasizing that rate laws must be determined experimentally.
Key terms: rate law, rate constant, differential rate equation, concentration dependence, experimental rate law
Teaches concepts: concept:differential_rate_equation (Differential Rate Equation), concept:experimental_determination_of_rate_law (Experimental Determination of Rate Law), concept:rate_constant (Rate Constant), concept:rate_law (Rate Law)
Requires concepts: concept:molar_concentration (Molar Concentration), concept:rate_of_reaction (Rate of Reaction), concept:stoichiometric_coefficients (Stoichiometric Coefficients)
title: Factors Influencing Rate of a Reaction

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:3
Section: Order of a Reaction
Summary: This section defines the overall order of a chemical reaction as the sum of exponents in the rate equation. It distinguishes between elementary and complex reactions and derives the units of the rate constant for various reaction orders.
Key terms: order of a reaction, rate equation, elementary reaction, complex reaction, rate constant units
Teaches concepts: concept:complex_reaction (Complex Reaction), concept:elementary_reaction (Elementary Reaction), concept:order_of_a_reaction (Order of a Reaction), concept:units_of_the_rate_constant (Units of the Rate Constant)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_concentration (Chemical Concentration), concept:rate_law (Rate Law)
title: Order of a Reaction

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:3
Section: Molecularity of a Reaction
Summary: This section defines molecularity as the number of reacting species colliding simultaneously in an elementary reaction and classifies reactions as unimolecular, bimolecular, or trimolecular. It explains how complex reactions proceed via multi-step mechanisms where the slowest step acts as the rate-determining step, and concludes by contrasting molecularity with reaction order.
Key terms: Molecularity, Elementary reaction, Complex reaction, Rate determining step, Reaction intermediate, Order of reaction
Teaches concepts: concept:complex_reaction (Complex Reaction), concept:elementary_reaction (Elementary Reaction), concept:molecularity_of_a_reaction (Molecularity of a Reaction), concept:rate_determining_step (Rate Determining Step), concept:reaction_intermediate (Reaction Intermediate)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_stoichiometry (Chemical Stoichiometry), concept:order_of_a_reaction (Order of a Reaction)
title: Molecularity of a Reaction

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:3
Section: Integrated Rate Equations
Summary: This section introduces integrated rate equations as a practical alternative to differential rate equations. It explains that integrating differential rate equations allows for a direct relationship between experimentally measured concentrations over time and the rate constant.
Key terms: differential rate equation, integrated rate equation, instantaneous rate, rate constant, reaction order
Teaches concepts: concept:integrated_rate_equation (Integrated Rate Equation)
Requires concepts: concept:differential_rate_equation (Differential Rate Equation), concept:instantaneous_rate (Instantaneous Rate), concept:order_of_a_reaction (Order of a Reaction)
title: Integrated Rate Equations

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:3
Section: Zero Order Reactions
Summary: This section defines zero order reactions and derives their integrated rate equation. It explains how to graphically represent these reactions to determine the rate constant and provides real-world examples such as surface-catalyzed decompositions.
Key terms: Zero order reaction, Integrated rate equation, Rate constant, Initial concentration, Slope, Surface saturation
Teaches concepts: concept:graphical_representation_of_zero_order_reaction (Graphical Representation of Zero Order Reaction), concept:integrated_rate_equation_for_zero_order_reaction (Integrated Rate Equation for Zero Order Reaction), concept:rate_constant_for_zero_order_reaction (Rate Constant for Zero Order Reaction), concept:surface_catalyzed_zero_order_reactions (Surface-Catalyzed Zero Order Reactions), concept:zero_order_reaction (Zero Order Reaction)
Requires concepts: concept:integration_calculus (Integration (Calculus)), concept:order_of_a_reaction (Order of a Reaction), concept:rate_of_reaction (Rate of Reaction)
title: Zero Order Reactions

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:3
Section: First Order Reactions
Summary: This section covers first-order reactions, deriving their integrated rate equations in both concentration and gas-phase pressure terms. It explains their exponential, logarithmic, and graphical representations, and highlights radioactive decay as a key physical example.
Key terms: First-order reaction, Integrated rate equation, Rate constant, Radioactive decay, Gas-phase reaction, Partial pressure
Teaches concepts: concept:first_order_gas_phase_reactions (First-Order Gas Phase Reactions), concept:first_order_reaction_kinetics (First-Order Reaction Kinetics), concept:graphical_representation_of_first_order_reactions (Graphical Representation of First-Order Reactions), concept:integrated_rate_equation_for_first_order_reactions (Integrated Rate Equation for First-Order Reactions), concept:radioactive_decay_kinetics (Radioactive Decay Kinetics)
Requires concepts: concept:integral_calculus (Integral Calculus), concept:logarithmic_functions (Logarithmic Functions), concept:partial_pressure (Partial Pressure), concept:rate_law_and_reaction_order (Rate Law and Reaction Order)
title: First Order Reactions

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:3
Section: Half-Life of a Reaction
Summary: This section defines the half-life of a chemical reaction and derives its mathematical expressions for both zero-order and first-order reactions. It also introduces pseudo-first-order reactions, where higher-order reactions behave as first-order due to one reactant being in large excess.
Key terms: Half-life, Zero-order reaction, First-order reaction, Rate constant, Pseudo-first-order reaction, Acid hydrolysis of ethyl acetate
Teaches concepts: concept:half_life_of_a_first_order_reaction (Half-Life of a First-Order Reaction), concept:half_life_of_a_reaction (Half-Life of a Reaction), concept:half_life_of_a_zero_order_reaction (Half-Life of a Zero-Order Reaction), concept:pseudo_first_order_reaction (Pseudo First-Order Reaction)
Requires concepts: concept:integrated_rate_law_for_first_order_reactions (Integrated Rate Law for First-Order Reactions), concept:integrated_rate_law_for_zero_order_reactions (Integrated Rate Law for Zero-Order Reactions), concept:order_of_a_reaction (Order of a Reaction)
title: Half-Life of a Reaction

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:3
Section: Temperature Dependence of the Rate of a Reaction
Summary: This section explains how temperature affects chemical reaction rates using the Arrhenius equation and the concept of activation energy. It describes the formation of an activated complex and uses Maxwell-Boltzmann distribution curves to show how temperature increases the fraction of molecules with sufficient kinetic energy to react.
Key terms: Arrhenius equation, activation energy, activated complex, Maxwell-Boltzmann distribution, pre-exponential factor, rate constant
Teaches concepts: concept:activated_complex (Activated Complex), concept:activation_energy (Activation Energy), concept:arrhenius_equation (Arrhenius Equation), concept:maxwell_boltzmann_distribution_of_kinetic_energy (Maxwell-Boltzmann Distribution of Kinetic Energy), concept:temperature_dependence_of_reaction_rate (Temperature Dependence of Reaction Rate)
Requires concepts: concept:kinetic_energy (Kinetic Energy), concept:rate_constant (Rate Constant)
title: Temperature Dependence of the Rate of a Reaction

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:3
Section: Effect of Catalyst
Summary: This section explains how a catalyst increases the rate of a chemical reaction by lowering its activation energy through an alternative pathway. It details the intermediate complex theory and discusses the thermodynamic and equilibrium properties of catalysis.
Key terms: Catalyst, Intermediate complex theory, Activation energy, Gibbs energy, Equilibrium constant, Inhibitor
Teaches concepts: concept:catalyst (Catalyst), concept:catalytic_activation_energy_reduction (Catalytic Activation Energy Reduction), concept:equilibrium_in_catalysis (Equilibrium in Catalysis), concept:intermediate_complex_theory (Intermediate Complex Theory), concept:thermodynamics_of_catalysis (Thermodynamics of Catalysis)
Requires concepts: concept:activation_energy (Activation Energy), concept:arrhenius_equation (Arrhenius Equation), concept:chemical_equilibrium (Chemical Equilibrium), concept:gibbs_energy (Gibbs Energy)
title: Effect of Catalyst

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:3
Section: Collision Theory of Chemical Reactions
Summary: This section introduces the collision theory of chemical reactions, which models reactant molecules as hard spheres and explains reaction rates based on collision frequency. It defines the criteria for effective collisions, including threshold energy and the steric factor representing proper molecular orientation.
Key terms: collision theory, collision frequency, effective collision, threshold energy, steric factor
Teaches concepts: concept:collision_frequency (Collision Frequency), concept:collision_theory (Collision Theory), concept:effective_collision (Effective Collision), concept:steric_factor (Steric Factor), concept:threshold_energy (Threshold Energy)
Requires concepts: concept:activation_energy (Activation Energy), concept:arrhenius_equation (Arrhenius Equation), concept:kinetic_theory_of_gases (Kinetic Theory of Gases)
title: Collision Theory of Chemical Reactions

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:4
Section: The d- and f-Block Elements
Summary: This section introduces the d-block and f-block elements, commonly referred to as transition and inner transition metals. It details the IUPAC definition of transition metals based on incomplete d subshells and explains the classification of group 12 elements.
Key terms: d-block, f-block, transition metals, inner transition metals, lanthanoids, actinoids, group 12 elements
Teaches concepts: concept:d_block_elements (d-Block Elements), concept:f_block_elements (f-Block Elements), concept:group_12_elements (Group 12 elements), concept:inner_transition_metals (Inner transition metals), concept:transition_metals (Transition metals)
Requires concepts: concept:electronic_configuration (Electronic Configuration), concept:periodic_table_groups_and_periods (Periodic table groups and periods)
title: The d- and f-Block Elements

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:4
Section: Position in the Periodic Table
Summary: This section describes the position of the d-block elements in the periodic table, located between the s- and p-blocks. It explains that these elements are characterized by the filling of d-orbitals in their penultimate energy level, forming four series of transition metals.
Key terms: d-block, periodic table, transition metals, penultimate energy level, d-orbitals
Teaches concepts: concept:d_block_elements (d-Block Elements), concept:transition_metals (Transition metals)
Requires concepts: concept:d_orbitals (d-orbitals), concept:penultimate_energy_level (penultimate energy level), concept:periodic_table (periodic table)
title: Position in the Periodic Table

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:4
Section: Electronic Configurations of the d-Block Elements
Summary: This section explains the general electronic configuration of d-block elements, highlighting exceptions like Chromium and Copper due to the stability of half-filled and fully filled d-orbitals. It also defines transition elements based on their incompletely filled d-orbitals and outlines their general characteristics, such as variable oxidation states and complex formation.
Key terms: electronic configuration, d-block elements, transition elements, half-filled orbitals, completely filled orbitals, oxidation states
Teaches concepts: concept:definition_of_transition_elements (Definition of Transition Elements), concept:general_characteristics_of_transition_elements (General Characteristics of Transition Elements), concept:general_electronic_configuration_of_d_block_elements (General Electronic Configuration of d-Block Elements), concept:stability_of_half_filled_and_filled_orbitals_in_d_block (Stability of Half-filled and Filled Orbitals in d-Block)
Requires concepts: concept:d_orbitals (d-orbitals), concept:electronic_configuration (Electronic Configuration), concept:oxidation_states (Oxidation States)
title: Electronic Configurations of the d-Block Elements

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:4
Section: General Properties of the Transition Elements (d-Block)
Summary: This section explores the physical and chemical properties of d-block transition elements, detailing trends in atomic size, ionization enthalpy, and oxidation states. It explains key phenomena such as lanthanoid contraction, magnetic properties using the spin-only formula, color formation via d-d transitions, and the formation of interstitial compounds and alloys.
Key terms: Lanthanoid contraction, Oxidation states, Spin-only formula, d-d transition, Interstitial compounds, Alloy formation
Teaches concepts: concept:d_d_transition (d-d Transition), concept:interstitial_compounds (Interstitial Compounds), concept:lanthanoid_contraction (Lanthanoid Contraction), concept:spin_only_formula (Spin-Only Formula), concept:variable_oxidation_states_of_transition_metals (Variable Oxidation States of Transition Metals)
Requires concepts: concept:electronic_configuration_of_d_block_elements (Electronic Configuration of d-Block Elements), concept:hunds_rule (Hund's Rule), concept:shielding_effect (Shielding Effect)
title: General Properties of the Transition Elements (d-Block)

Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:4
Section: Some Important Compounds of Transition Elements
Summary: This section details the preparation, properties, structures, and redox chemistry of key transition metal compounds, focusing on potassium dichromate and potassium permanganate.
Key terms: Potassium dichromate, Potassium permanganate, Chromate, Dichromate, Manganate, Oxidizing agent

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:4
Section: Oxides and Oxoanions of Metals
Summary: This section covers the preparation, structures, and chemical properties of transition metal oxides and oxoanions, focusing primarily on potassium dichromate and potassium permanganate. It details their pH-dependent equilibria, structural characteristics, and powerful oxidizing behaviors in various media.
Key terms: Disproportionation, Chromite ore, Potassium dichromate, Chromate-dichromate equilibrium, Potassium permanganate, Manganate, Permanganate

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:4
Section: The Lanthanoids
Summary: This section introduces the lanthanoids, a series of fourteen f-block elements following lanthanum, highlighting their electronic configurations, stable +3 oxidation state, and physical properties. It explains the phenomenon of lanthanoid contraction, its impact on atomic and ionic radii, and details their chemical reactivity, general characteristics, and industrial uses such as in mischmetall alloys.
Key terms: Lanthanoids, Lanthanoid contraction, Mischmetall, f-block elements, Oxidation states, Shielding effect
Teaches concepts: concept:general_characteristics_of_lanthanoids (General Characteristics of Lanthanoids), concept:lanthanoid_contraction (Lanthanoid Contraction), concept:lanthanoids (Lanthanoids), concept:mischmetall (Mischmetall), concept:oxidation_states_of_lanthanoids (Oxidation States of Lanthanoids)
Requires concepts: concept:electronic_configuration (Electronic Configuration), concept:shielding_effect (Shielding Effect), concept:standard_electrode_potential (Standard Electrode Potential)
title: The Lanthanoids

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:4
Section: The Actinoids
Summary: This section introduces the actinoids, a series of radioactive elements from Thorium to Lawrencium, detailing their electronic configurations, variable oxidation states, and physical properties. It explains the phenomenon of actinoid contraction, comparing its magnitude and underlying causes with lanthanoid contraction, alongside differences in ionization enthalpies and magnetic properties.
Key terms: Actinoids, Actinoid contraction, Electronic configuration, Oxidation states, Shielding effect, 5f orbitals
Teaches concepts: concept:actinoid_contraction (Actinoid Contraction), concept:actinoids (Actinoids), concept:electronic_configuration_of_actinoids (Electronic Configuration of Actinoids), concept:ionisation_enthalpies_of_actinoids (Ionisation Enthalpies of Actinoids), concept:oxidation_states_of_actinoids (Oxidation States of Actinoids)
Requires concepts: concept:lanthanoid_contraction (Lanthanoid Contraction), concept:lanthanoids (Lanthanoids), concept:shielding_effect (Shielding Effect)
title: The Actinoids

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:4
Section: Some Applications of d- and f-Block Elements
Summary: This section details the diverse industrial, construction, and catalytic applications of d- and f-block elements and their compounds. It highlights their roles in manufacturing steel, batteries, pigments, and acting as crucial catalysts in major industrial chemical processes.
Key terms: Transition metals, Ziegler-Natta catalyst, Haber process, Wacker process, Coinage metals
Teaches concepts: concept:catalytic_applications_of_transition_metals (Catalytic Applications of Transition Metals), concept:industrial_and_construction_applications_of_d_block_elements (Industrial and Construction Applications of d-Block Elements), concept:wacker_process (Wacker Process), concept:ziegler_natta_catalyst (Ziegler-Natta Catalyst)

Summary: This section provides explanations and answers to key intext questions regarding the d- and f-block elements. It covers topics such as the transition character of silver, metallic bonding in zinc, stability of electronic configurations, disproportionation of copper(I), and actinoid contraction.

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:5
Section: Werner's Theory of Coordination Compounds
Summary: This section introduces coordination compounds and explains their structure using Werner's theory, which distinguishes between ionisable primary valences and non-ionisable secondary valences. It also details the difference between double salts and complex ions, and introduces modern formulations like coordination polyhedra and counter ions.
Key terms: coordination compounds, primary valence, secondary valence, coordination polyhedra, coordination entity, counter ions, double salt
Teaches concepts: concept:coordination_compounds (Coordination Compounds), concept:coordination_polyhedra (Coordination Polyhedra), concept:double_salt (Double Salt), concept:primary_valence (Primary Valence), concept:secondary_valence (Secondary Valence)
Requires concepts: concept:chemical_bonding (Chemical Bonding), concept:ionic_dissociation (Ionic Dissociation), concept:stoichiometry (Stoichiometry)
title: Werner's Theory of Coordination Compounds

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:5
Section: Definitions of Some Important Terms Pertaining to Coordination Compounds
Summary: This section defines and explains the fundamental terminology used in coordination chemistry. It covers key concepts including coordination entities, central atoms, ligands, coordination numbers, coordination spheres, polyhedra, and the classification of complexes.
Key terms: Coordination Entity, Central Atom, Ligand, Denticity, Chelate Ligand, Ambidentate Ligand, Coordination Number, Coordination Sphere, Coordination Polyhedron, Homoleptic Complex, Heteroleptic Complex

subject: chemistry

Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:5
Section: Nomenclature of Coordination Compounds
Summary: This section outlines the IUPAC rules for writing formulas and naming mononuclear coordination compounds. It details the alphabetical ordering of ligands, the use of numerical prefixes, and the determination of metal oxidation states, supported by illustrative examples and practice problems.
Key terms: IUPAC nomenclature, mononuclear coordination entities, ligands, oxidation state, anionic ligands, polydentate ligands
Teaches concepts: concept:formulas_of_mononuclear_coordination_entities (Formulas of Mononuclear Coordination Entities), concept:iupac_naming_of_coordination_compounds (IUPAC Naming of Coordination Compounds), concept:naming_neutral_coordination_complexes (Naming Neutral Coordination Complexes), concept:naming_of_anionic_complexes (Naming of Anionic Complexes)

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:5
Section: Isomerism in Coordination Compounds
Summary: This section explains isomerism in coordination compounds, classifying it into stereoisomerism and structural isomerism. It details geometric and optical isomerism in square planar and octahedral complexes, alongside structural variations including linkage, coordination, ionisation, and solvate isomerism.
Key terms: Isomerism, Stereoisomerism, Structural isomerism, Geometric isomerism, Optical isomerism, Facial isomer, Meridional isomer, Linkage isomerism, Coordination isomerism, Ionisation isomerism, Solvate isomerism

title: Isomerism in Coordination Compounds

text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:5
Section: Bonding in Coordination Compounds
Summary: This section explains the bonding, geometry, magnetic properties, and color of coordination compounds. It primarily focuses on the applications and limitations of Valence Bond Theory (VBT) and Crystal Field Theory (CFT).

Section: Bonding in Metal Carbonyls
Summary: This section describes the structures and bonding of homoleptic metal carbonyls. It explains how the metal-carbon bond exhibits both sigma and pi character, leading to a stabilizing synergic effect.

Summary: This section explores the diverse practical applications of coordination compounds across various fields. It highlights their significance in analytical chemistry, metallurgy, biological systems, industrial processes, and medicinal chemistry.

Section: Answers to Intext Questions
Summary: This section provides answers and explanations for intext questions regarding coordination compounds. It covers IUPAC nomenclature, isomerism, chemical identification of isomers, and the relationship between bonding, hybridisation, and magnetic properties.

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:9
Section: Amines
Summary: This section introduces amines as an important class of organic compounds derived from ammonia by replacing one or more hydrogen atoms with alkyl or aryl groups. It covers their natural occurrence in proteins, vitamins, alkaloids, and hormones, their biological and medical significance (such as adrenaline, ephedrine, Novocain, and Benadryl), and provides a formal definition with examples including methylamine, aniline, dimethylamine, and trimethylamine.
Key terms: Amines, Ammonia, Alkyl groups, Aryl groups, Methylamine, Aniline, Dimethylamine, Trimethylamine, Secondary amino groups, Tertiary amino group, Quaternary ammonium salts, Diazonium salts
Teaches concepts: concept:amines (Amines)
Requires concepts: concept:alkyl_groups (Alkyl groups), concept:ammonia (Ammonia), concept:aryl_group (Aryl Group)
title: Amines

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:9
Section: Structure of Amines
Summary: This section describes the structure, hybridization, and geometry of amines. The nitrogen atom in amines is trivalent, carries an unshared pair of electrons, and is sp3 hybridized, which results in a pyramidal geometry. Due to the lone pair, the bond angle is slightly less than the ideal tetrahedral angle of 109.5 degrees, as exemplified by the 108-degree bond angle in trimethylamine.
Key terms: amines, sp3 hybridization, pyramidal geometry, lone pair, bond angle, trimethylamine
Teaches concepts: concept:structure_and_hybridization_of_amines (Structure and Hybridization of Amines)
Requires concepts: concept:sp3_hybridization (sp3 Hybridization), concept:structure_of_ammonia (Structure of Ammonia), concept:vsepr_theory (VSEPR Theory)
title: Structure of Amines

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:9
Section: Importance of Diazonium Salts in Synthesis of Aromatic Compounds
Summary: This section describes the synthetic utility of diazonium salts as versatile intermediates in organic synthesis. It explains how they facilitate the introduction of various substituents (such as halogens, cyano, hydroxyl, and nitro groups) into aromatic rings, enabling the preparation of substituted aromatic compounds that cannot be synthesized via direct substitution. Practical applications are demonstrated through multi-step synthesis examples, including the preparation of 2-bromobenzoic acid, 3-nitrotoluene, and 1,3,5-tribromobenzene.
Key terms: Diazonium salts, Synthetic utility, Aromatic synthesis, Deamination, Diazotization, Multi-step synthesis
Teaches concepts: concept:deamination_of_diazonium_salts (Deamination of Diazonium Salts), concept:synthetic_utility_of_diazonium_salts (Synthetic Utility of Diazonium Salts)
Requires concepts: concept:diazotization (Diazotization), concept:electrophilic_aromatic_substitution (Electrophilic aromatic substitution), concept:oxidation_of_alkylbenzenes (Oxidation of Alkylbenzenes), concept:reduction_of_nitro_compounds (Reduction of Nitro Compounds)
title: Importance of Diazonium Salts in Synthesis of Aromatic Compounds

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:9
Section: Classification
Summary: This section explains the classification of amines into primary (1°), secondary (2°), and tertiary (3°) categories based on the number of hydrogen atoms replaced in an ammonia molecule by alkyl or aryl groups. It also distinguishes between simple amines (with identical alkyl/aryl groups) and mixed amines (with different groups).
Key terms: primary amine, secondary amine, tertiary amine, simple amine, mixed amine, ammonia, alkyl group, aryl group
Teaches concepts: concept:mixed_amine (Mixed Amine), concept:primary_amine (Primary Amine), concept:secondary_amine (Secondary Amine), concept:simple_amine (Simple Amine), concept:tertiary_amine (Tertiary Amine)
Requires concepts: concept:alkyl_groups (Alkyl groups), concept:ammonia (Ammonia), concept:aryl_group (Aryl Group)
title: Classification

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:9
Section: Nomenclature
Summary: This section covers the nomenclature of aliphatic and aromatic amines using both common and IUPAC systems. It explains the rules for naming primary, secondary, and tertiary amines, including the use of the locant 'N' for substituents on nitrogen, naming diamines, and naming arylamines like aniline.
Key terms: aliphatic amines, IUPAC nomenclature, common nomenclature, alkanamines, locant N, arylamines, benzenamine, aniline, diamines
Teaches concepts: concept:nomenclature_of_aliphatic_amines (Nomenclature of Aliphatic Amines), concept:nomenclature_of_arylamines (Nomenclature of Arylamines), concept:nomenclature_of_secondary_and_tertiary_amines (Nomenclature of Secondary and Tertiary Amines)
Requires concepts: concept:aromatic_compounds_and_benzene_derivatives (Aromatic Compounds and Benzene Derivatives), concept:classification_of_amines (Classification of Amines), concept:iupac_nomenclature_of_alkanes (IUPAC Nomenclature of Alkanes)
title: Nomenclature

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:9
Section: Preparation of Amines
Summary: This section outlines the primary chemical methods used to prepare amines. It covers the reduction of nitro compounds, nitriles, and amides; the ammonolysis of alkyl halides; the Gabriel phthalimide synthesis for primary aliphatic amines; and the Hoffmann bromamide degradation reaction which yields amines with one less carbon atom than the starting amide.
Key terms: Ammonolysis, Gabriel phthalimide synthesis, Hoffmann bromamide degradation, Reduction, Nucleophilic substitution, Quaternary ammonium salt, Nitriles, Amides
Teaches concepts: concept:ammonolysis_of_alkyl_halides (Ammonolysis of Alkyl Halides), concept:gabriel_phthalimide_synthesis (Gabriel Phthalimide Synthesis), concept:hoffmann_bromamide_degradation_reaction (Hoffmann Bromamide Degradation Reaction), concept:reduction_of_amides (Reduction of Amides), concept:reduction_of_nitriles (Reduction of Nitriles), concept:reduction_of_nitro_compounds (Reduction of Nitro Compounds)
Requires concepts: concept:alkyl_halides (Alkyl Halides), concept:amides (Amides), concept:chemical_reduction (Chemical Reduction), concept:nucleophilic_substitution (Nucleophilic Substitution)
title: Preparation of Amines

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:9
Section: Physical Properties
Summary: This section covers the physical properties of amines, including their physical state, odour, solubility, and boiling points. It explains how intermolecular hydrogen bonding influences these properties, resulting in a boiling point order of Primary > Secondary > Tertiary amines, and compares their solubility and boiling points with those of alcohols and alkanes.
Key terms: aliphatic amines, arylamines, solubility, hydrophobic alkyl part, intermolecular hydrogen bonding, boiling points of amines, isomeric amines
Teaches concepts: concept:boiling_points_of_amines (Boiling Points of Amines), concept:intermolecular_hydrogen_bonding_in_amines (Intermolecular Hydrogen Bonding in Amines), concept:physical_state_and_odour_of_amines (Physical State and Odour of Amines), concept:solubility_of_amines (Solubility of Amines)
Requires concepts: concept:classification_of_amines (Classification of Amines), concept:hydrogen_bonding (Hydrogen Bonding), concept:hydrophobic_effect (Hydrophobic Effect)
title: Physical Properties

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:9
Section: Chemical Reactions
Summary: This section covers the chemical reactions of amines, focusing on their basic character, nucleophilic reactions, and electrophilic substitution reactions. It explains the basicity of aliphatic and aromatic amines in terms of pKb values, influenced by the inductive effect, solvation effect, and steric hindrance. Key chemical reactions discussed include alkylation, acylation, benzoylation, the carbylamine reaction (isocyanide test), reaction with nitrous acid, and the Hinsberg test (using arylsulphonyl chloride) to distinguish primary, secondary, and tertiary amines. Finally, it details electrophilic aromatic substitution of aniline (bromination, nitration, sulphonation), the necessity of protecting the amino group via acetylation to control reactivity, and why aniline does not undergo Friedel-Crafts reactions.
Key terms: Amines, Basicity, pKb, Inductive Effect, Solvation Effect, Steric Hindrance, Acylation, Carbylamine Reaction, Hinsberg's Reagent, Diazotisation, Electrophilic Aromatic Substitution, Zwitter Ion, Amino Group Protection
Teaches concepts: concept:acylation_of_amines (Acylation of Amines), concept:basicity_of_arylamines (Basicity of Arylamines), concept:carbylamine_reaction (Carbylamine Reaction), concept:character_of_amines (Basic Character of Amines), concept:electrophilic_substitution_of_aniline_and_amino_group_protection (Electrophilic Substitution of Aniline and Amino Group Protection), concept:factors_affecting_amine_basicity (Factors Affecting Amine Basicity), concept:hinsbergs_test (Hinsberg's Test), concept:zwitter_ion_of_sulphanilic_acid (Zwitter Ion of Sulphanilic Acid)
Requires concepts: concept:electrophilic_aromatic_substitution (Electrophilic aromatic substitution), concept:hydrogen_bonding (Hydrogen Bonding), concept:inductive_effect (Inductive Effect), concept:lewis_acids_and_bases (Lewis Acids and Bases), concept:resonance_effect (Resonance Effect)
title: Chemical Reactions

subject: chemistry
text:
Subject: chemistry
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:9
Section: Method of Preparation of Diazonium Salts
Summary: This section covers the general formula, nomenclature, stability, and preparation of diazonium salts. It introduces the general formula of diazonium salts as R-N2+ X- and explains their nomenclature. It discusses the stability of arenediazonium salts at low temperatures (273-278 K) due to resonance stabilization, contrasting them with highly unstable alkyldiazonium salts. Finally, it describes the process of diazotisation, where primary aromatic amines (like aniline) are converted into diazonium salts using nitrous acid generated in situ.
Key terms: diazonium salts, diazotisation, arenediazonium ion, benzenediazonium chloride, resonance stabilization, nitrous acid, primary aromatic amines
Teaches concepts: concept:diazonium_salts (Diazonium Salts), concept:diazotisation (Diazotisation), concept:stability_of_arenediazonium_salts (Stability of Arenediazonium Salts)
Requires concepts: concept:in_situ_reagent_generation (In Situ Reagent Generation), concept:primary_aromatic_amines (Primary Aromatic Amines), concept:resonance (Resonance)
title: Method of Preparation of Diazonium Salts

text:

Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:chemistry:12:9
Section: Physical Properties
Summary: This section outlines the physical properties of benzenediazonium chloride and benzenediazonium fluoroborate, detailing their appearance, solubility, thermal stability, and behavior in dry states.

Teaches concepts: concept:physical_properties_of_benzenediazonium_chloride (Physical properties of benzenediazonium chloride), concept:physical_properties_of_benzenediazonium_fluoroborate (Physical properties of benzenediazonium fluoroborate)
Requires concepts: concept:diazonium_salts (Diazonium Salts)
title: Physical Properties

Summary: This section details the chemical reactions of diazonium salts, which are categorized into reactions involving the displacement of nitrogen (such as the Sandmeyer and Gatterman reactions, and replacements by iodide, fluoride, hydrogen, hydroxyl, and nitro groups) and coupling reactions where the diazo group is retained to form azo dyes.
Key terms: Diazonium salts, Sandmeyer reaction, Gatterman reaction, Coupling reactions, Azo dyes, Electrophilic substitution, Arenediazonium fluoroborate
Teaches concepts: concept:coupling_reactions_of_diazonium_salts (Coupling Reactions of Diazonium Salts), concept:displacement_of_nitrogen_in_diazonium_salts (Displacement of Nitrogen in Diazonium Salts), concept:gatterman_reaction (Gatterman Reaction), concept:sandmeyer_reaction (Sandmeyer Reaction)

subject: physics
text:

Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:10
Section: Introduction
Summary: This introductory section provides an overview of the concepts of heat and temperature, distinguishing between common-sense notions and precise physical definitions. It previews the chapter's focus on heat measurement, heat transfer processes, and practical thermal phenomena.

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:10
Section: NEWTON'S LAW OF COOLING
Summary: This section introduces Newton's Law of Cooling, which states that the rate of heat loss from a body is directly proportional to the temperature difference between the body and its surroundings. It covers the mathematical derivation of the exponential cooling equation, its experimental verification, and practical calculations using the average temperature approximation.
Key terms: Newton's Law of Cooling, rate of cooling, calorimeter, specific heat capacity, exponential decay, average temperature approximation
Teaches concepts: concept:average_temperature_approximation_for_cooling (Average Temperature Approximation for Cooling), concept:experimental_verification_of_newtons_law_of_cooling (Experimental Verification of Newton's Law of Cooling), concept:exponential_law_of_cooling (Exponential Law of Cooling), concept:newtons_law_of_cooling (Newton's Law of Cooling)
Requires concepts: concept:integration_and_logarithmic_functions (Integration and Logarithmic Functions), concept:specific_heat_capacity (Specific Heat Capacity)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:10
Section: Points to Ponder
Summary: This section provides key conceptual clarifications on temperature scales, phase equilibrium, heat transfer conditions, and the mechanism of convection.
Key terms: Kelvin temperature, Celsius temperature, triple point of water, phase equilibrium, heat transfer, convection
Teaches concepts: concept:condition_for_heat_transfer (Condition for Heat Transfer), concept:mechanism_of_convection (Mechanism of Convection), concept:phase_equilibrium_properties (Phase Equilibrium Properties), concept:triple_point_of_water_as_a_standard_fixed_point (Triple Point of Water as a Standard Fixed Point)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:10
Section: Temperature and Heat
Summary: This section defines temperature and heat, establishing their respective SI units of Kelvin and Joule. It also explains how temperature differences drive heat transfer between systems and their surroundings until thermal equilibrium is achieved.
Key terms: Temperature, Heat, Kelvin, Joule, Thermal Equilibrium, Heat Transfer
Teaches concepts: concept:heat (Heat), concept:heat_transfer (Heat Transfer), concept:temperature (Temperature), concept:thermal_equilibrium (Thermal Equilibrium)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:10
Section: Measurement of Temperature
Summary: This section explains the principles of thermometry, focusing on how thermometers are calibrated using fixed reference points like the ice and steam points of water. It also introduces the Celsius and Fahrenheit temperature scales, detailing their intervals and the linear relationship used to convert between them.
Key terms: thermometer, calibration, ice point, steam point, Celsius scale, Fahrenheit scale, temperature conversion
Teaches concepts: concept:celsius_scale (Celsius Scale), concept:fahrenheit_scale (Fahrenheit Scale), concept:temperature_scale_conversion (Temperature Scale Conversion), concept:thermometer_calibration (Thermometer Calibration), concept:thermometry (Thermometry)
Requires concepts: concept:linear_equations (Linear Equations), concept:temperature (Temperature)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:10
Section: Ideal-Gas Equation and Absolute Temperature
Summary: This section derives the ideal-gas equation by combining Boyle's and Charles' laws for low-density gases. It explains the operation of constant-volume gas thermometers and defines absolute zero and the Kelvin temperature scale based on the extrapolation of pressure-temperature relationships.
Key terms: Boyle's Law, Charles' Law, Ideal-Gas Equation, Universal Gas Constant, Constant-Volume Gas Thermometer, Absolute Zero, Kelvin Scale
Teaches concepts: concept:absolute_zero (Absolute Zero), concept:boyles_law (Boyle's Law), concept:charles_law (Charles' Law), concept:constant_volume_gas_thermometer (Constant-Volume Gas Thermometer), concept:ideal_gas_equation (Ideal Gas Equation), concept:kelvin_temperature_scale (Kelvin Temperature Scale)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:10
Section: Thermal Expansion
Summary: This section introduces thermal expansion, defining its linear, area, and volume forms along with their respective coefficients. It explores the anomalous expansion of water, derives the expansion coefficient for ideal gases, and explains the generation of thermal stress when expansion is constrained.
Key terms: Thermal expansion, Coefficient of linear expansion, Coefficient of volume expansion, Anomalous expansion of water, Thermal stress
Teaches concepts: concept:anomalous_expansion_of_water (Anomalous Expansion of Water), concept:coefficient_of_linear_expansion (Coefficient of Linear Expansion), concept:coefficient_of_volume_expansion (Coefficient of Volume Expansion), concept:thermal_expansion (Thermal Expansion), concept:thermal_stress (Thermal Stress)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:10
Section: SPECIFIC HEAT CAPACITY
Summary: This section introduces heat capacity, specific heat capacity, and molar specific heat capacity as measures of how substances absorb or reject heat. It also discusses the factors affecting heat absorption and the practical applications of water's high specific heat capacity.
Key terms: Heat capacity, Specific heat capacity, Molar specific heat capacity, Constant pressure, Constant volume
Teaches concepts: concept:heat_capacity (Heat Capacity), concept:molar_specific_heat_capacity (Molar Specific Heat Capacity), concept:molar_specific_heat_capacity_at_constant_pressure (Molar Specific Heat Capacity at Constant Pressure), concept:molar_specific_heat_capacity_at_constant_volume (Molar Specific Heat Capacity at Constant Volume), concept:specific_heat_capacity (Specific Heat Capacity)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:10
Section: CALORIMETRY
Summary: This section defines calorimetry as the measurement of heat and explains the principle of calorimetry, where heat lost equals heat gained in an isolated system. It describes the structure of a calorimeter and demonstrates its application in determining specific heat capacity.
Key terms: Calorimetry, Principle of Calorimetry, Calorimeter, Heat transfer, Conservation of energy
Teaches concepts: concept:calorimeter (Calorimeter), concept:calorimetry (Calorimetry), concept:principle_of_calorimetry (Principle of Calorimetry)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:10
Section: CHANGE OF STATE
Summary: This section covers the transitions of matter between solid, liquid, and gas phases, explaining processes such as melting, vaporization, sublimation, and regelation. It introduces phase diagrams, the triple point, and the concept of latent heat, demonstrating how to calculate total heat exchange during phase changes using calorimetry.
Key terms: change of state, regelation, triple point, phase diagram, sublimation, latent heat
Teaches concepts: concept:change_of_state (Change of State), concept:latent_heat (Latent Heat), concept:regelation (Regelation), concept:sublimation (Sublimation), concept:triple_point (Triple Point)

Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:10
Section: HEAT TRANSFER
Summary: This section covers the three fundamental modes of heat transfer: conduction, convection, and radiation. It provides quantitative formulations for conduction through thermal conductivity, describes fluid-based convection, and details radiation laws including Wien's displacement law and the Stefan-Boltzmann law.
Key terms: Conduction, Convection, Radiation, Thermal Conductivity, Blackbody Radiation, Wien's Displacement Law, Stefan-Boltzmann Law
Teaches concepts: concept:convection (Convection), concept:stefan_boltzmann_law (Stefan-Boltzmann Law), concept:thermal_conduction (Thermal Conduction), concept:thermal_conductivity (Thermal Conductivity), concept:thermal_radiation (Thermal Radiation), concept:wiens_displacement_law (Wien's Displacement Law)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:11
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section introduces thermodynamics as a macroscopic science that studies heat, temperature, and their inter-conversion with work. It distinguishes thermodynamic descriptions, which rely on bulk macroscopic variables, from microscopic descriptions and classical mechanics.
Key terms: Thermodynamics, Heat, Work, Macroscopic variables, Microscopic description, Internal disordered motion
Teaches concepts: concept:heat_as_a_form_of_energy (Heat as a Form of Energy), concept:internal_disordered_motion (Internal Disordered Motion), concept:macroscopic_variables (Macroscopic Variables), concept:microscopic_description (Microscopic Description), concept:thermodynamics (Thermodynamics)
