subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:11
Section: Reversible and Irreversible Processes
Summary: This section defines and distinguishes between reversible and irreversible thermodynamic processes. It explains that while natural processes are generally irreversible due to dissipative forces and non-equilibrium states, reversible processes represent an idealized, quasi-static, and non-dissipative limit.
Key terms: reversible process, irreversible process, dissipative forces, quasi-static process, non-equilibrium states
Teaches concepts: concept:causes_of_irreversibility (Causes of Irreversibility), concept:conditions_for_reversibility (Conditions for Reversibility), concept:irreversible_process (Irreversible Process), concept:reversible_process (Reversible Process)
Requires concepts: concept:dissipative_forces (Dissipative Forces), concept:quasi_static_process (Quasi-static Process), concept:thermodynamic_equilibrium (Thermodynamic Equilibrium), concept:thermodynamic_process (Thermodynamic Process)
title: Reversible and Irreversible Processes

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:11
Section: Carnot Engine
Summary: This section introduces the Carnot engine, an idealized reversible heat engine that achieves maximum efficiency. It details the four steps of the Carnot cycle, derives its efficiency formula, and presents Carnot's theorem along with the thermodynamic temperature scale.
Key terms: Carnot engine, Carnot cycle, isothermal expansion, adiabatic expansion, Carnot's theorem, thermodynamic temperature scale
Teaches concepts: concept:carnot_cycle (Carnot Cycle), concept:carnot_engine (Carnot Engine), concept:carnots_theorem (Carnot's Theorem), concept:efficiency_of_the_carnot_engine (Efficiency of the Carnot Engine), concept:thermodynamic_temperature_scale (Thermodynamic Temperature Scale)
Requires concepts: concept:adiabatic_process (Adiabatic Process), concept:isothermal_process (Isothermal Process), concept:reversible_process (Reversible Process), concept:second_law_of_thermodynamics (Second Law of Thermodynamics)
title: Carnot Engine

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:11
Section: Thermal Equilibrium
Summary: This section defines thermodynamic equilibrium as a state where a system's macroscopic variables remain constant over time. It introduces adiabatic and diathermic walls, explaining how their thermal conductivity properties govern the flow of heat. Finally, it describes how systems separated by a diathermic wall spontaneously exchange heat to achieve thermal equilibrium.
Key terms: Thermodynamic Equilibrium, Adiabatic Wall, Diathermic Wall, Thermal Equilibrium, Macroscopic Variables
Teaches concepts: concept:adiabatic_wall (Adiabatic Wall), concept:diathermic_wall (Diathermic Wall), concept:thermal_equilibrium (Thermal Equilibrium), concept:thermodynamic_equilibrium (Thermodynamic Equilibrium)
Requires concepts: concept:macroscopic_variables (Macroscopic Variables), concept:mechanical_equilibrium (Mechanical Equilibrium)
title: Thermal Equilibrium

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:11
Section: Zeroth Law of Thermodynamics
Summary: This section introduces the Zeroth Law of Thermodynamics, which states that two systems in thermal equilibrium with a third system are in thermal equilibrium with each other. It explains how this law provides the physical basis for defining temperature and constructing temperature scales.
Key terms: Zeroth Law of Thermodynamics, thermal equilibrium, temperature, adiabatic wall, diathermic wall, thermometry
Teaches concepts: concept:temperature (Temperature), concept:thermometry (Thermometry), concept:zeroth_law_of_thermodynamics (Zeroth Law of Thermodynamics)
Requires concepts: concept:adiabatic_and_diathermic_walls (Adiabatic and Diathermic Walls), concept:thermal_equilibrium (Thermal Equilibrium)
title: Zeroth Law of Thermodynamics

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:11
Section: Heat, Internal Energy and Work
Summary: This section defines heat, temperature, and internal energy, explaining how heat and work act as modes of energy transfer that change a system's internal energy. It distinguishes internal energy as a state variable from heat and work, which are path-dependent energy transfers.
Key terms: Heat, Temperature, Internal Energy, State Variable, Work, Thermal Equilibrium
Teaches concepts: concept:heat (Heat), concept:internal_energy (Internal Energy), concept:modes_of_changing_internal_energy (Modes of Changing Internal Energy), concept:state_variable (State Variable)
Requires concepts: concept:kinetic_energy (Kinetic Energy), concept:potential_energy (Potential Energy), concept:zeroth_law_of_thermodynamics (Zeroth Law of Thermodynamics)
title: Heat, Internal Energy and Work

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:11
Section: First Law of Thermodynamics
Summary: This section introduces the First Law of Thermodynamics as an application of the conservation of energy to thermodynamic systems. It defines the mathematical relationship between heat, work, and internal energy, demonstrating that internal energy is a state variable, and applies this law to gas expansion and phase transitions.
Key terms: First Law of Thermodynamics, Internal Energy, State Variable, Work Done by a Gas, Phase Transition
Teaches concepts: concept:first_law_of_thermodynamics (First Law of Thermodynamics), concept:internal_energy_change_in_phase_transition (Internal Energy Change in Phase Transition), concept:path_independence_of_internal_energy (Path Independence of Internal Energy), concept:work_done_by_an_expanding_gas (Work Done by an Expanding Gas)
Requires concepts: concept:conservation_of_energy (Conservation of Energy), concept:latent_heat (Latent Heat), concept:thermodynamic_system (Thermodynamic System)
title: First Law of Thermodynamics

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:11
Section: Specific Heat Capacity
Summary: This section defines heat capacity, specific heat capacity, and molar specific heat capacity, explaining their dependence on substance nature and temperature. It also derives Mayer's relation ($C_p - C_v = R$) for ideal gases using the First Law of Thermodynamics.
Key terms: Heat capacity, Specific heat capacity, Molar specific heat capacity, Calorie, Mayer's relation, Universal gas constant
Teaches concepts: concept:calorie (Calorie), concept:heat_capacity (Heat Capacity), concept:mayers_relation (Mayer's Relation), concept:molar_specific_heat_capacity (Molar Specific Heat Capacity), concept:specific_heat_capacity (Specific Heat Capacity)
Requires concepts: concept:first_law_of_thermodynamics (First Law of Thermodynamics), concept:ideal_gas_law (Ideal Gas Law), concept:law_of_equipartition_of_energy (Law of Equipartition of Energy)
title: Specific Heat Capacity

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:11
Section: Thermodynamic State Variables and Equation of State
Summary: This section introduces thermodynamic state variables and the equation of state, which describes the relationship between these variables for a system in equilibrium. It also classifies state variables into extensive and intensive categories based on their dependence on system size.
Key terms: state variables, equation of state, equilibrium state, isotherm, extensive variables, intensive variables
Teaches concepts: concept:equation_of_state (Equation of State), concept:extensive_variables (Extensive Variables), concept:intensive_variables (Intensive Variables), concept:isotherm (Isotherm), concept:thermodynamic_state_variables (Thermodynamic State Variables)
Requires concepts: concept:ideal_gas_law (Ideal Gas Law), concept:thermal_equilibrium (Thermal Equilibrium)
title: Thermodynamic State Variables and Equation of State

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:11
Section: Thermodynamic Processes
Summary: This section defines and analyzes various thermodynamic processes, including quasi-static, isothermal, adiabatic, isobaric, isochoric, and cyclic processes. It derives the work done in these processes and applies the First Law of Thermodynamics to determine the relationships between heat, work, and internal energy.
Key terms: Quasi-static process, Isothermal process, Adiabatic process, Isochoric process, Isobaric process, Cyclic process, Work done
Teaches concepts: concept:adiabatic_process (Adiabatic Process), concept:cyclic_process (Cyclic Process), concept:isobaric_process (Isobaric Process), concept:isochoric_process (Isochoric Process), concept:isothermal_process (Isothermal Process), concept:quasi_static_process (Quasi-static Process)
Requires concepts: concept:first_law_of_thermodynamics (First Law of Thermodynamics), concept:ideal_gas_law (Ideal Gas Law), concept:internal_energy (Internal Energy)
title: Thermodynamic Processes

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:11
Section: Second Law of Thermodynamics
Summary: This section introduces the Second Law of Thermodynamics, which explains why certain energy-conserving processes do not occur spontaneously in nature. It details two equivalent formulations of the law: the Kelvin-Planck statement regarding heat engines and the Clausius statement regarding heat transfer.
Key terms: Second Law of Thermodynamics, Kelvin-Planck statement, Clausius statement, Heat engine efficiency, Coefficient of performance
Teaches concepts: concept:clausius_statement (Clausius Statement), concept:kelvin_planck_statement (Kelvin-Planck Statement), concept:second_law_of_thermodynamics (Second Law of Thermodynamics)
Requires concepts: concept:first_law_of_thermodynamics (First Law of Thermodynamics), concept:heat_engine (Heat Engine), concept:refrigerator (Refrigerator)
title: Second Law of Thermodynamics

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:12
Section: INTRODUCTION
Summary: This section introduces the kinetic theory of gases, which explains macroscopic gas behavior by modeling gases as collections of rapidly moving atoms or molecules. It highlights how the theory provides a molecular basis for pressure, temperature, and various gas laws.
Key terms: kinetic theory, inter-atomic forces, molecular interpretation, gas laws, Avogadro's hypothesis
Teaches concepts: concept:kinetic_theory_of_gases (Kinetic Theory of Gases), concept:molecular_interpretation_of_gas_properties (Molecular Interpretation of Gas Properties)
Requires concepts: concept:avogadros_hypothesis (Avogadro's Hypothesis), concept:boyles_law (Boyle's Law), concept:inter_atomic_forces (Inter-atomic Forces)
title: INTRODUCTION

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:12
Section: MOLECULAR NATURE OF MATTER
Summary: This section introduces the molecular nature of matter, tracing the history of the atomic hypothesis from ancient philosophy to modern atomic theory. It explains fundamental chemical laws, such as Dalton's atomic theory and Avogadro's law, and describes molecular scales, interatomic forces, and the concept of mean free path.
Key terms: Atomic Hypothesis, Dalton's Atomic Theory, Avogadro's Law, Mean Free Path, Interatomic Forces
Teaches concepts: concept:atomic_hypothesis (Atomic Hypothesis), concept:avogadros_law (Avogadro's Law), concept:daltons_atomic_theory (Dalton's Atomic Theory), concept:interatomic_forces (Interatomic Forces), concept:mean_free_path (Mean Free Path)
Requires concepts: concept:states_of_matter (States of Matter)
title: MOLECULAR NATURE OF MATTER

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:12
Section: BEHAVIOUR OF GASES
Summary: This section introduces the behavior of gases, establishing the ideal gas equation and its various forms using the Boltzmann and universal gas constants. It defines ideal and real gas behaviors, details Boyle's and Charles' laws, and explains Dalton's law of partial pressures for gas mixtures.
Key terms: Ideal gas equation, Boltzmann constant, Avogadro's number, Mole, Boyle's law, Charles' law, Dalton's law of partial pressures
Teaches concepts: concept:avogadros_number_and_the_mole (Avogadro's Number and the Mole), concept:boltzmann_constant (Boltzmann Constant), concept:boyles_law_and_charles_law (Boyle's Law and Charles' Law), concept:daltons_law_of_partial_pressures (Dalton's Law of Partial Pressures), concept:ideal_gas_equation (Ideal Gas Equation), concept:real_gas_behavior (Real Gas Behavior)
Requires concepts: concept:density (Density), concept:molecular_mass (Molecular Mass), concept:thermodynamic_state_variables (Thermodynamic State Variables)
title: BEHAVIOUR OF GASES

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:12
Section: KINETIC THEORY OF AN IDEAL GAS
Summary: This section introduces the kinetic theory of gases, deriving the microscopic formula for the pressure of an ideal gas based on molecular collisions. It establishes the kinetic interpretation of temperature, showing that molecular kinetic energy is directly proportional to absolute temperature, and applies this to Dalton's law and root mean square speed.
Key terms: kinetic theory of gases, ideal gas pressure, average kinetic energy, Boltzmann constant, Dalton's law of partial pressures, root mean square speed
Teaches concepts: concept:kinetic_derivation_of_daltons_law (Kinetic Derivation of Dalton's Law), concept:kinetic_interpretation_of_temperature (Kinetic Interpretation of Temperature), concept:kinetic_theory_of_gases_assumptions (Kinetic Theory of Gases Assumptions), concept:pressure_of_an_ideal_gas (Pressure of an Ideal Gas), concept:root_mean_square_speed (Root Mean Square Speed)

title: KINETIC THEORY OF AN IDEAL GAS

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:12
Section: LAW OF EQUIPARTITION OF ENERGY
Summary: This section introduces the law of equipartition of energy, which states that in thermal equilibrium, the total energy of a system is distributed equally among all its degrees of freedom. It defines translational, rotational, and vibrational degrees of freedom for different molecular structures and explains how each contributes to the average energy of a molecule.
Key terms: Law of equipartition of energy, Degrees of freedom, Translational energy, Rotational energy, Vibrational energy, Thermal equilibrium
Teaches concepts: concept:degrees_of_freedom (Degrees of Freedom), concept:law_of_equipartition_of_energy (Law of Equipartition of Energy), concept:rotational_degrees_of_freedom (Rotational Degrees of Freedom), concept:translational_degrees_of_freedom (Translational Degrees of Freedom), concept:vibrational_degrees_of_freedom (Vibrational Degrees of Freedom)
Requires concepts: concept:boltzmann_constant (Boltzmann Constant), concept:kinetic_energy (Kinetic Energy), concept:thermal_equilibrium (Thermal Equilibrium)
title: LAW OF EQUIPARTITION OF ENERGY

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:12
Section: SPECIFIC HEAT CAPACITY
Summary: This section derives the molar specific heat capacities and their ratio for monatomic, diatomic, and polyatomic gases using the law of equipartition of energy. It also applies this law to predict the specific heat capacity of solids as 3R.
Key terms: Molar specific heat capacity, Monatomic gas, Diatomic gas, Polyatomic gas, Degrees of freedom, Law of equipartition of energy, Specific heat of solids
Teaches concepts: concept:specific_heat_capacity_of_diatomic_gases (Specific Heat Capacity of Diatomic Gases), concept:specific_heat_capacity_of_monatomic_gases (Specific Heat Capacity of Monatomic Gases), concept:specific_heat_capacity_of_polyatomic_gases (Specific Heat Capacity of Polyatomic Gases), concept:specific_heat_capacity_of_solids (Specific Heat Capacity of Solids)
Requires concepts: concept:degrees_of_freedom (Degrees of Freedom), concept:law_of_equipartition_of_energy (Law of Equipartition of Energy), concept:relation_between_cp_and_cv (Relation between Cp and Cv)

Section: MEAN FREE PATH
Summary: This section introduces and derives the concept of the mean free path, which is the average distance a gas molecule travels between successive collisions. It explains how the mean free path depends on molecular size and number density, and provides a refined formula that accounts for the relative motion of all molecules.
Key terms: mean free path, collision rate, number density, molecular diameter, relative velocity

text:

Section: SUMMARY AND POINTS TO PONDER
Summary: This section provides a comprehensive summary of the kinetic theory of gases, detailing the ideal gas equation, the kinetic interpretation of temperature, and the law of equipartition of energy. It also defines the mean free path of gas molecules and explains how molecular degrees of freedom determine the molar specific heats of gases.
Key terms: Ideal gas equation, Kinetic theory, Root mean square speed, Law of equipartition of energy, Molar specific heat, Mean free path

It explains how periodic motion is mathematically represented using sine and cosine functions, and introduces Fourier's theorem for representing complex periodic functions.

Summary: This section defines Simple Harmonic Motion (SHM) as a specific type of periodic motion where displacement is a sinusoidal function of time. Section: SIMPLE HARMONIC MOTION AND UNIFORM CIRCULAR MOTION
Summary: This section establishes the geometric relationship between simple harmonic motion (SHM) and uniform circular motion, demonstrating that SHM is the projection of uniform circular motion along a diameter. Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:13
Section: ENERGY IN SIMPLE HARMONIC MOTION
Summary: This section derives and analyzes the kinetic, potential, and total mechanical energy of a particle executing simple harmonic motion (SHM). It demonstrates that while kinetic and potential energies vary periodically with time and displacement, their sum remains constant, illustrating the conservation of mechanical energy.

Summary: This section introduces the concept of waves as propagating disturbances that transport energy and information without bulk matter transfer. It classifies waves into mechanical, electromagnetic, and matter waves, and explains their propagation mechanisms in different media using spring-like restoring forces.

Summary: This section classifies mechanical waves into transverse and longitudinal waves based on the direction of particle oscillation relative to wave propagation. It explains how the elastic properties of a medium, specifically its ability to sustain shearing or compressive strain, determine which wave types can propagate through it.

Summary: This section introduces the mathematical description of a sinusoidal travelling wave using a displacement function of both position and time. It defines key wave parameters such as amplitude, phase, wavelength, angular wave number, period, and frequency, and explains how the wave equation represents propagation in positive or negative directions.

Section: THE SPEED OF A TRAVELLING WAVE
Summary: This section defines the speed of a travelling wave as the rate of propagation of a point of constant phase. It derives the wave speed formula in terms of angular frequency and wave number, and relates it to wavelength and frequency. Additionally, it notes that mechanical wave speed depends on the medium's elastic and inertial properties.
Key terms: wave speed, phase point, angular frequency, wave number, wavelength, medium properties

Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:14

Section: Speed of a Longitudinal Wave (Speed of Sound)

Chapter: ncert:physics:11:14
Section: THE PRINCIPLE OF SUPERPOSITION OF WAVES
Summary: This section introduces the principle of superposition, which states that the net displacement of overlapping waves is the algebraic sum of their individual displacements. It derives the resultant wave function for two overlapping harmonic waves and defines constructive and destructive interference based on their phase difference.

text:

Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:14
Section: REFLECTION OF WAVES
Summary: This section explains the reflection of waves at rigid and open boundaries. It describes how waves undergo a phase change of pi at a rigid boundary, whereas they reflect with no phase change at an open boundary, and provides their mathematical representations.
Key terms: Reflection of waves, Rigid boundary, Open boundary, Phase change, Incident wave, Reflected wave
Teaches concepts: concept:mathematical_representation_of_reflected_waves (Mathematical Representation of Reflected Waves), concept:reflection_at_a_rigid_boundary (Reflection at a Rigid Boundary), concept:reflection_at_an_open_boundary (Reflection at an Open Boundary)

Grade: 11

Summary: This section explains the formation, mathematical representation, and characteristics of standing waves. It defines nodes and antinodes, and derives the normal modes and harmonics for stretched strings and air columns in open and closed pipes.

text:

Section: BEATS
Summary: This section explains the phenomenon of beats, which arises from the interference of two harmonic sound waves of close but unequal frequencies. It provides a mathematical derivation of the beat frequency as the difference between the two wave frequencies and explores practical applications like tuning musical instruments.
Key terms: Beats, Beat frequency, Superposition, Waxing and waning, Interference, Tuning

Summary: This section provides a comprehensive summary of wave mechanics, covering the mathematical description of sinusoidal waves, wave speed in various media, and the principle of superposition. subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:1
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section introduces the concept of measurement and units in physics. It defines a unit as an internationally accepted reference standard for measuring physical quantities. It distinguishes between fundamental (or base) units, which represent base quantities, and derived units, which are combinations of base units. Together, base and derived units form a complete system of units.
Key terms: unit, physical quantity, fundamental units, base units, derived units, system of units
Teaches concepts: concept:derived_units (Derived Units), concept:fundamental_units (Fundamental Units), concept:system_of_units (System of Units), concept:unit (Unit)
Requires concepts: concept:physical_quantity (Physical Quantity)
title: Introduction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:1
Section: The International System of Units
Summary: This section introduces the International System of Units (SI), starting with a historical overview of earlier systems of units (CGS, FPS, MKS). It details the seven SI base quantities and their modern definitions based on fundamental physical constants. Additionally, it defines the supplementary dimensionless units for plane and solid angles (radian and steradian), explains how derived units are formed, and lists common non-SI units that remain in general use.
Key terms: CGS system, FPS system, MKS system, SI system, Base units, Derived units, Plane angle, Solid angle, Radian, Steradian, Supplementary units
Teaches concepts: concept:derived_units (Derived Units), concept:historical_systems_of_units (Historical Systems of Units), concept:international_system_of_units_si (International System of Units (SI)), concept:si_base_quantities_and_units (SI Base Quantities and Units), concept:supplementary_units (Supplementary Units)
Requires concepts: concept:geometry (Basic Geometry), concept:physical_quantities (Physical Quantities)
title: The International System of Units

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:1
Section: Significant Figures
Summary: This section introduces the concept of significant figures as a representation of measurement precision, combining reliable digits with the first uncertain digit. It outlines the rules for identifying significant figures, explains how scientific notation resolves ambiguities, defines the order of magnitude, discusses exact numbers with infinite significant figures, and provides guidelines for avoiding rounding errors in multi-step calculations.
Key terms: Significant figures, Significant digits, Scientific notation, Order of magnitude, Exact numbers, Rounding errors, Precision of measurement, Least count
Teaches concepts: concept:exact_numbers (Exact Numbers), concept:order_of_magnitude (Order of Magnitude), concept:rules_for_significant_figures (Rules for Significant Figures), concept:scientific_notation (Scientific Notation), concept:significant_figures (Significant Figures)
Requires concepts: concept:exponents_and_powers_of_ten (Exponents and Powers of Ten), concept:measurement_precision (Measurement Precision), concept:unit_conversion (Unit Conversion)
title: Significant Figures

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:1
Section: Rules for Arithmetic Operations with Significant Figures
Summary: This section defines the rules for determining the correct number of significant figures or decimal places in the results of arithmetic operations. It covers two main rules: the multiplication and division rule, which limits the final significant figures to those of the least precise term, and the addition and subtraction rule, which limits the final decimal places to those of the term with the fewest decimal places.
Key terms: Significant Figures, Arithmetic Operations, Multiplication and Division Rule, Addition and Subtraction Rule, Decimal Places
Teaches concepts: concept:addition_and_subtraction_rule_for_significant_figures (Addition and Subtraction Rule for Significant Figures), concept:multiplication_and_division_rule_for_significant_figures (Multiplication and Division Rule for Significant Figures)
Requires concepts: concept:rounding_off_numbers (Rounding Off Numbers), concept:significant_figures (Significant Figures)
title: Rules for Arithmetic Operations with Significant Figures

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:1
Section: Rounding off the Uncertain Digits
Summary: This section explains the rules for rounding off uncertain digits in numerical computations to ensure the correct number of significant figures. It covers general rounding rules (when the digit to be dropped is greater than or less than 5), specific rules for when the digit to be dropped is exactly 5 (depending on whether the preceding digit is even or odd), and guidelines for rounding in multi-step calculations to avoid cumulative rounding errors.
Key terms: rounding off, significant figures, uncertain digits, insignificant digit, cumulative rounding errors
Teaches concepts: concept:general_rounding_rules (General Rounding Rules), concept:rounding_in_multi_step_calculations (Rounding in Multi-step Calculations), concept:rounding_when_the_digit_is_5 (Rounding when the Digit is 5)
Requires concepts: concept:significant_figures (Significant Figures)
title: Rounding off the Uncertain Digits

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:1
Section: Rules for Determining the Uncertainty in the Results of Arithmetic Calculations
Summary: This section outlines the rules for determining uncertainty and significant figures in the results of arithmetic calculations. It covers how relative errors propagate in products, the rules for rounding off results in multiplication, division, and subtraction, the dependence of relative error on numerical magnitude, and the guidelines for retaining intermediate digits in multi-step calculations.
Key terms: Uncertainty propagation, Significant figures, Relative error, Rounding off, Arithmetic operations, Intermediate results
Teaches concepts: concept:dependence_of_relative_error_on_numerical_magnitude (Dependence of Relative Error on Numerical Magnitude), concept:rule_for_intermediate_results_in_multi_step_computations (Rule for Intermediate Results in Multi-step Computations), concept:significant_figures_in_multiplication_and_division (Significant Figures in Multiplication and Division), concept:significant_figures_in_subtraction (Significant Figures in Subtraction), concept:uncertainty_propagation_in_products (Uncertainty Propagation in Products)
Requires concepts: concept:absolute_uncertainty (Absolute Uncertainty), concept:relative_error (Relative Error), concept:significant_figures (Significant Figures)
title: Rules for Determining the Uncertainty in the Results of Arithmetic Calculations

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:1
Section: Dimensions of Physical Quantities
Summary: This section introduces the concept of dimensions of physical quantities, defining them as the powers to which base quantities are raised to represent a derived quantity. It explains how mechanical quantities like volume and force are expressed in terms of mass, length, and time, and highlights that dimensions represent the qualitative nature of a physical quantity rather than its magnitude.
Key terms: dimensions, base quantities, derived units, mechanical quantities, qualitative nature of dimensions
Teaches concepts: concept:dimensions_of_a_physical_quantity (Dimensions of a Physical Quantity), concept:dimensions_of_mechanical_quantities (Dimensions of Mechanical Quantities), concept:qualitative_nature_of_dimensions (Qualitative Nature of Dimensions)
Requires concepts: concept:base_quantities_and_units (Base Quantities and Units), concept:derived_quantities (Derived Quantities)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:1
Section: Dimensional Formulae and Dimensional Equations
Summary: This section introduces the concepts of dimensional formulae and dimensional equations. It explains how physical quantities are represented in terms of base quantities (such as mass, length, and time) using specific mathematical expressions and equations, providing examples like volume, speed, acceleration, force, and mass density.
Key terms: dimensional formula, dimensional equation, base quantities, physical quantity, dimensions
Teaches concepts: concept:dimensional_equation (Dimensional Equation), concept:dimensional_formula (Dimensional Formula)
Requires concepts: concept:base_quantities_and_units (Base Quantities and Units), concept:dimensions_of_physical_quantities (Dimensions of Physical Quantities)
title: Dimensional Formulae and Dimensional Equations

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:1
Section: Dimensional Analysis and its Applications
Summary: This section introduces dimensional analysis as a tool for describing physical behavior, checking the dimensional consistency (homogeneity) of mathematical equations, and deducing relationships between physical quantities. It highlights the fundamental rule that only physical quantities with identical dimensions can be added or subtracted.
Key terms: Dimensional analysis, Dimensional consistency, Homogeneity, Physical quantities, Dimensions
Teaches concepts: concept:dimensional_analysis (Dimensional Analysis), concept:principle_of_dimensional_homogeneity (Principle of Dimensional Homogeneity)
Requires concepts: concept:dimensions_of_physical_quantities (Dimensions of Physical Quantities), concept:physical_quantities (Physical Quantities)
title: Dimensional Analysis and its Applications

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:1
Section: Checking the Dimensional Consistency of Equations
Summary: This section teaches how to check the dimensional consistency of physical equations using the Principle of Homogeneity of Dimensions. It explains that physical quantities can only be added or subtracted if they have the same dimensions, and demonstrates how to test equations for dimensional correctness. It also highlights the limitations of dimensional analysis, specifically that a dimensionally correct equation is not necessarily physically correct due to dimensionless constants.
Key terms: Principle of Homogeneity of Dimensions, Dimensional Consistency, Dimensionless Constants, Dimensional Analysis, Dimensionless Functions
Teaches concepts: concept:dimensional_consistency (Dimensional Consistency), concept:limitations_of_dimensional_consistency (Limitations of Dimensional Consistency), concept:principle_of_homogeneity_of_dimensions (Principle of Homogeneity of Dimensions)
Requires concepts: concept:dimensions_of_physical_quantities (Dimensions of Physical Quantities)
title: Checking the Dimensional Consistency of Equations

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:1
Section: Deducing Relation among the Physical Quantities
Summary: This section explains how to deduce relationships among physical quantities using dimensional analysis and the principle of homogeneity. It provides a step-by-step derivation of the time period of a simple pendulum as a worked example and discusses the limitations of this method, such as its inability to determine dimensionless constants.
Key terms: dimensional analysis, principle of homogeneity, physical quantities, simple pendulum, dimensionless constant, exponents
Teaches concepts: concept:deducing_relations_among_physical_quantities (Deducing Relations among Physical Quantities), concept:limitations_of_dimensional_analysis (Limitations of Dimensional Analysis)
Requires concepts: concept:dimensions_of_physical_quantities (Dimensions of Physical Quantities), concept:principle_of_homogeneity_of_dimensions (Principle of Homogeneity of Dimensions)
title: Deducing Relation among the Physical Quantities

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:2
Section: INTRODUCTION
Summary: This section introduces the study of motion, defining kinematics as the description of motion without considering its causes. It introduces rectilinear motion as motion along a straight line and explains the point object approximation used to simplify motion analysis.
Key terms: motion, rectilinear motion, point object approximation, kinematics
Teaches concepts: concept:kinematics (Kinematics), concept:point_object_approximation (Point Object Approximation), concept:rectilinear_motion (Rectilinear Motion)
Requires concepts:
title: INTRODUCTION

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:2
Section: INSTANTANEOUS VELOCITY AND SPEED
Summary: This section defines instantaneous velocity as the limit of average velocity as the time interval approaches zero, expressing it mathematically as the derivative of position with respect to time. It also explains how to determine velocity graphically using the slope of a tangent line and defines instantaneous speed as the magnitude of instantaneous velocity.
Key terms: Instantaneous velocity, Instantaneous speed, Differential coefficient, Position-time graph, Slope of tangent
Teaches concepts: concept:calculus_representation_of_velocity (Calculus Representation of Velocity), concept:graphical_determination_of_velocity (Graphical Determination of Velocity), concept:instantaneous_speed (Instantaneous Speed), concept:instantaneous_velocity (Instantaneous Velocity)
Requires concepts: concept:average_velocity (Average Velocity), concept:differential_calculus (Differential Calculus), concept:position_time_graph (Position-Time Graph)
title: INSTANTANEOUS VELOCITY AND SPEED

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:2
Section: ACCELERATION
Summary: This section defines acceleration as the rate of change of velocity with time, distinguishing between average and instantaneous acceleration. It explains how to interpret acceleration graphically using position-time and velocity-time graphs, and demonstrates that the area under a velocity-time graph represents displacement.
Key terms: acceleration, average acceleration, instantaneous acceleration, velocity-time graph, displacement, constant acceleration
Teaches concepts: concept:acceleration (Acceleration), concept:average_acceleration (Average Acceleration), concept:constant_acceleration (Constant Acceleration), concept:displacement_from_velocity_time_graph (Displacement from Velocity-Time Graph), concept:graphical_interpretation_of_acceleration (Graphical Interpretation of Acceleration), concept:instantaneous_acceleration (Instantaneous Acceleration)
Requires concepts: concept:derivative (Derivative), concept:slope_of_a_line (Slope of a Line), concept:velocity (Velocity)
title: ACCELERATION

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:2
Section: KINEMATIC EQUATIONS FOR UNIFORMLY ACCELERATED MOTION
Summary: This section derives and summarizes the three fundamental kinematic equations for rectilinear motion under constant acceleration using graphical, algebraic, and calculus methods. It then applies these equations to analyze physical phenomena such as free fall, Galileo's law of odd numbers, vehicle stopping distance, and human reaction time.
Key terms: kinematic equations, uniform acceleration, free fall, Galileo's law of odd numbers, stopping distance, reaction time
Teaches concepts: concept:calculus_derivation_of_kinematic_equations (Calculus Derivation of Kinematic Equations), concept:free_fall (Free Fall), concept:galileos_law_of_odd_numbers (Galileo's Law of Odd Numbers), concept:kinematic_equations_for_uniformly_accelerated_motion (Kinematic Equations for Uniformly Accelerated Motion), concept:reaction_time (Reaction Time), concept:stopping_distance_of_vehicles (Stopping Distance of Vehicles)
Requires concepts: concept:integral_calculus (Integral Calculus), concept:uniform_acceleration (Uniform Acceleration), concept:velocity_time_graph (Velocity-Time Graph)
title: KINEMATIC EQUATIONS FOR UNIFORMLY ACCELERATED MOTION

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:3
Section: INTRODUCTION
Summary: This section introduces the necessity of vectors for describing motion in two and three dimensions, contrasting it with one-dimensional motion where direction can be managed using simple signs. It outlines how vector operations are essential for defining velocity and acceleration in a plane.
Key terms: vectors, one-dimensional motion, multi-dimensional motion, motion in a plane
Teaches concepts: concept:need_for_vectors_in_multi_dimensional_motion (Need for Vectors in Multi-dimensional Motion)
Requires concepts: concept:kinematic_quantities (Kinematic Quantities), concept:one_dimensional_motion (One-dimensional Motion)
title: INTRODUCTION

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:3
Section: UNIFORM CIRCULAR MOTION
Summary: This section introduces uniform circular motion, defining it as motion along a circular path at a constant speed. It derives the direction and magnitude of centripetal acceleration and establishes the relationships between linear speed, angular speed, time period, and frequency.
Key terms: uniform circular motion, centripetal acceleration, angular displacement, angular speed, time period, frequency
Teaches concepts: concept:angular_displacement_and_angular_speed (Angular Displacement and Angular Speed), concept:centripetal_acceleration (Centripetal Acceleration), concept:relation_between_linear_speed_and_angular_speed (Relation between Linear Speed and Angular Speed), concept:time_period_and_frequency_in_circular_motion (Time Period and Frequency in Circular Motion), concept:uniform_circular_motion (Uniform Circular Motion)
Requires concepts: concept:instantaneous_acceleration (Instantaneous Acceleration), concept:position_and_velocity_vectors (Position and Velocity Vectors), concept:vector_subtraction (Vector Subtraction)
title: UNIFORM CIRCULAR MOTION

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:3
Section: SCALARS AND VECTORS
Summary: This section introduces scalar and vector quantities, highlighting their definitions, notations, and algebraic rules. It explains position and displacement vectors in a plane and defines the conditions for the equality of vectors, distinguishing between free and localized vectors.
Key terms: Scalar quantity, Vector quantity, Position vector, Displacement vector, Equality of vectors, Free vectors, Localised vectors
Teaches concepts: concept:displacement_vector (Displacement Vector), concept:equality_of_vectors (Equality of Vectors), concept:free_and_localised_vectors (Free and Localised Vectors), concept:position_vector (Position Vector), concept:scalar_quantity (Scalar Quantity), concept:vector_quantity (Vector Quantity)
Requires concepts: concept:coordinate_system_origin (Coordinate System Origin), concept:physical_quantity (Physical Quantity)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:3
Section: MULTIPLICATION OF VECTORS BY REAL NUMBERS
Summary: This section explains how multiplying a vector by a real number scales its magnitude and potentially alters its direction depending on the sign of the multiplier. It also describes how multiplying a vector by a dimensional scalar affects the physical dimensions of the resulting vector.
Key terms: vector multiplication, scalar multiplier, vector magnitude, vector direction, physical dimensions
Teaches concepts: concept:dimensional_multiplication_of_vectors (Dimensional Multiplication of Vectors), concept:vector_scaling_by_a_negative_real_number (Vector Scaling by a Negative Real Number), concept:vector_scaling_by_a_positive_real_number (Vector Scaling by a Positive Real Number)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:3
Section: ADDITION AND SUBTRACTION OF VECTORS — GRAPHICAL METHOD
Summary: This section explains the graphical methods for adding and subtracting vectors, specifically focusing on the triangle and parallelogram laws. It also defines the properties of vector addition, introduces the null vector, and demonstrates practical applications using resultant velocity.
Key terms: triangle law of vector addition, head-to-tail method, commutative law, associative law, null vector, subtraction of vectors, parallelogram method
Teaches concepts: concept:commutative_and_associative_properties_of_vector_addition (Commutative and Associative Properties of Vector Addition), concept:null_vector (Null Vector), concept:parallelogram_method_of_vector_addition (Parallelogram Method of Vector Addition), concept:subtraction_of_vectors (Subtraction of Vectors), concept:triangle_law_of_vector_addition (Triangle Law of Vector Addition)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:3
Section: RESOLUTION OF VECTORS
Summary: This section explains the resolution of vectors into component vectors, focusing on rectangular components in two and three dimensions. It introduces unit vectors along coordinate axes and derives formulas for calculating a vector's magnitude and direction from its components.
Key terms: Vector resolution, Unit vector, Rectangular components, Magnitude of a vector, Position vector
Teaches concepts: concept:rectangular_components_of_a_vector (Rectangular Components of a Vector), concept:three_dimensional_vector_resolution (Three-Dimensional Vector Resolution), concept:unit_vector (Unit Vector), concept:vector_magnitude_and_direction_from_components (Vector Magnitude and Direction from Components), concept:vector_resolution (Vector Resolution)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:3
Section: VECTOR ADDITION – ANALYTICAL METHOD
Summary: This section introduces the analytical method of vector addition by algebraically combining components in two and three dimensions. It also derives the Law of Cosines and Law of Sines to determine the magnitude and direction of a resultant vector, demonstrating their application to real-world velocity problems.
Key terms: analytical method, vector components, Law of Cosines, Law of Sines, resultant vector, unit vectors
Teaches concepts: concept:analytical_method_of_vector_addition (Analytical Method of Vector Addition), concept:law_of_cosines_for_vectors (Law of Cosines for Vectors), concept:law_of_sines_for_vectors (Law of Sines for Vectors), concept:resultant_velocity (Resultant Velocity), concept:vector_addition_in_three_dimensions (Vector Addition in Three Dimensions)
Requires concepts: concept:parallelogram_method_of_vector_addition (Parallelogram Method of Vector Addition), concept:trigonometric_functions (Trigonometric Functions), concept:vector_components (Vector Components)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:3
Section: MOTION IN A PLANE
Summary: This section extends the kinematic concepts of position, displacement, velocity, and acceleration to two-dimensional motion in a plane. It defines these quantities in vector component form and explains how to determine their magnitudes, directions, and graphical representations.
Key terms: position vector, displacement vector, average velocity, instantaneous velocity, average acceleration, instantaneous acceleration, tangential direction
Teaches concepts: concept:acceleration_in_2d (Acceleration in 2D), concept:direction_of_velocity_in_2d (Direction of Velocity in 2D), concept:position_vector_and_displacement_in_2d (Position Vector and Displacement in 2D), concept:velocity_in_2d (Velocity in 2D)
Requires concepts: concept:derivative_calculus (Derivative (Calculus)), concept:vector_components_and_unit_vectors (Vector Components and Unit Vectors), concept:vector_subtraction (Vector Subtraction)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:3
Section: MOTION IN A PLANE WITH CONSTANT ACCELERATION
Summary: This section derives the vector equations of motion for an object moving in a plane with constant acceleration. It establishes that two-dimensional motion can be resolved into two independent, simultaneous one-dimensional motions along perpendicular axes.
Key terms: constant acceleration, velocity-time relation, position-time relation, independence of perpendicular motions
Teaches concepts: concept:independence_of_perpendicular_motions (Independence of Perpendicular Motions), concept:position_time_relation_in_a_plane (Position-Time Relation in a Plane), concept:velocity_time_relation_in_a_plane (Velocity-Time Relation in a Plane)
Requires concepts: concept:constant_acceleration_in_one_dimension (Constant Acceleration in One Dimension), concept:vector_components (Vector Components)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:3
Section: PROJECTILE MOTION
Summary: This section introduces projectile motion as the combination of independent horizontal motion with constant velocity and vertical motion with constant acceleration due to gravity. It derives the parabolic trajectory equation and formulas for key parameters including time of flight, maximum height, and horizontal range.
Key terms: projectile motion, trajectory, parabola, time of flight, maximum height, horizontal range
Teaches concepts: concept:horizontal_range_of_a_projectile (Horizontal Range of a Projectile), concept:maximum_height_of_a_projectile (Maximum Height of a Projectile), concept:projectile_motion (Projectile Motion), concept:time_of_flight (Time of Flight), concept:trajectory_of_a_projectile (Trajectory of a Projectile)
Requires concepts: concept:acceleration_due_to_gravity (Acceleration due to Gravity), concept:equations_of_motion (Equations of Motion), concept:vector_resolution (Vector Resolution)
title: PROJECTILE MOTION

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:4
Section: INTRODUCTION
Summary: This section introduces dynamics by discussing what governs the motion of bodies. It defines force as an external agency required to change a body's state of motion and distinguishes between contact and non-contact forces.
Key terms: dynamics, force, external agency, contact force, non-contact force
Teaches concepts: concept:contact_force (Contact Force), concept:dynamics (Dynamics), concept:force (Force), concept:non_contact_force (Non-contact Force)
Requires concepts: concept:kinematics (Kinematics)
title: INTRODUCTION

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:4
Section: CIRCULAR MOTION
Summary: This section analyzes the dynamics of circular motion, focusing on the centripetal force required to keep an object moving in a circular path. It derives the maximum safe speeds for vehicles negotiating turns on both level and banked roads, highlighting how banking reduces reliance on friction.
Key terms: centripetal force, static friction, banked road, optimum speed, maximum safe speed
Teaches concepts: concept:banking_of_roads (Banking of Roads), concept:centripetal_force (Centripetal Force), concept:motion_of_a_car_on_a_level_road (Motion of a Car on a Level Road), concept:optimum_speed (Optimum Speed)
Requires concepts: concept:centripetal_acceleration (Centripetal Acceleration), concept:newtons_second_law_of_motion (Newton's Second Law of Motion), concept:static_friction (Static Friction)
title: CIRCULAR MOTION

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:4
Section: SOLVING PROBLEMS IN MECHANICS
Summary: This section outlines a systematic methodology for solving mechanics problems by defining a system and its environment. It introduces the construction and application of free-body diagrams (FBDs) and demonstrates how to apply Newton's laws of motion to multi-body systems.
Key terms: free-body diagram, system, environment, action-reaction pairs, normal force, Newton's laws of motion
Teaches concepts: concept:action_reaction_pairs (Action-Reaction Pairs), concept:free_body_diagram_fbd (Free-Body Diagram (FBD)), concept:system_and_environment (System and Environment)
Requires concepts: concept:force (Force), concept:newtons_laws_of_motion (Newton's Laws of Motion)
title: SOLVING PROBLEMS IN MECHANICS

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:4
Section: POINTS TO PONDER
Summary: This section provides critical conceptual clarifications on Newtonian mechanics, emphasizing the relationship between force, acceleration, and velocity, the instantaneous nature of force, the true meaning of centripetal force, the self-adjusting nature of static friction, and the simultaneity of action-reaction pairs.
Key terms: force, acceleration, velocity, instantaneous force, centripetal force, static friction, action-reaction, equilibrium
Teaches concepts: concept:instantaneous_nature_of_force (Instantaneous Nature of Force), concept:physical_nature_of_centripetal_force (Physical Nature of Centripetal Force), concept:relationship_between_force_and_acceleration (Relationship between Force and Acceleration), concept:self_adjusting_nature_of_static_friction (Self-Adjusting Nature of Static Friction), concept:simultaneity_of_action_and_reaction (Simultaneity of Action and Reaction)
Requires concepts: concept:centripetal_acceleration (Centripetal Acceleration), concept:newtons_second_law_of_motion (Newton's Second Law of Motion), concept:newtons_third_law_of_motion (Newton's Third Law of Motion)
title: POINTS TO PONDER

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:4
Section: ARISTOTLE’S FALLACY
Summary: This section examines Aristotle's fallacy, which incorrectly asserted that an external force is always required to keep a body in motion. It explains that this view is flawed because it fails to account for opposing forces like friction and viscosity present in the natural world.
Key terms: Aristotelian law of motion, Aristotle's fallacy, external force, friction, uniform motion
Teaches concepts: concept:aristotelian_law_of_motion (Aristotelian Law of Motion), concept:aristotles_fallacy (Aristotle's Fallacy)
Requires concepts: concept:force (Force), concept:friction (Friction)
title: ARISTOTLE’S FALLACY

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:4
Section: THE LAW OF INERTIA
Summary: This section explores Galileo's experiments with inclined and double inclined planes, which challenged the Aristotelian view of motion. These observations led to the definition of inertia as a body's natural resistance to changing its state of rest or uniform motion.
Key terms: Inertia, Inclined Plane, Constant Velocity, Frictionless Plane, Uniform Motion
Teaches concepts: concept:galileos_double_inclined_plane_experiment (Galileo's Double Inclined Plane Experiment), concept:galileos_inclined_plane_observations (Galileo's Inclined Plane Observations), concept:inertia (Inertia)
Requires concepts: concept:acceleration (Acceleration), concept:force (Force), concept:velocity (Velocity)
title: THE LAW OF INERTIA

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:4
Section: NEWTON’S FIRST LAW OF MOTION
Summary: This section introduces Newton's First Law of Motion, which states that an object remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by a net external force. It explains the relationship between zero net external force and zero acceleration, explores the concept of inertia through everyday examples, and demonstrates how to infer forces from an object's state of motion.
Key terms: Newton's First Law of Motion, Inertia, Net External Force, Acceleration, Uniform Motion, Normal Force
Teaches concepts: concept:force_inference_from_motion_state (Force Inference from Motion State), concept:inertia (Inertia), concept:net_external_force (Net External Force), concept:newtons_first_law_of_motion (Newton's First Law of Motion)
Requires concepts: concept:acceleration (Acceleration), concept:force (Force), concept:uniform_motion (Uniform Motion)
title: NEWTON’S FIRST LAW OF MOTION

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:4
Section: NEWTON’S SECOND LAW OF MOTION
Summary: This section introduces Newton's second law of motion, defining linear momentum and establishing the quantitative relationship between net external force and acceleration. It explores the vector nature of force, its application to systems of particles, and introduces the concept of impulse.
Key terms: momentum, Newton's second law, newton, impulse, impulsive force
Teaches concepts: concept:impulse (Impulse), concept:linear_momentum (Linear Momentum), concept:newton_unit_of_force (Newton (Unit of Force)), concept:newtons_second_law_of_motion (Newton's Second Law of Motion), concept:vector_components_of_force (Vector Components of Force)
Requires concepts: concept:acceleration (Acceleration), concept:newtons_first_law_of_motion (Newton's First Law of Motion), concept:vector_resolution (Vector Resolution)
title: NEWTON’S SECOND LAW OF MOTION

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:4
Section: NEWTON’S THIRD LAW OF MOTION
Summary: This section introduces Newton's third law of motion, which states that forces always occur in equal and opposite pairs representing mutual interactions between bodies. It clarifies that action and reaction forces act simultaneously on different bodies, and explains how internal forces cancel out within a system.
Key terms: Newton's third law of motion, action and reaction, mutual interaction, internal forces, simultaneity of forces
Teaches concepts: concept:action_reaction_pair_targets (Action-Reaction Pair Targets), concept:internal_forces_of_a_system (Internal Forces of a System), concept:newtons_third_law_of_motion (Newton's Third Law of Motion), concept:simultaneity_of_action_and_reaction (Simultaneity of Action and Reaction)
Requires concepts: concept:force (Force), concept:impulse (Impulse), concept:newtons_second_law_of_motion (Newton's Second Law of Motion)
title: NEWTON’S THIRD LAW OF MOTION

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:4
Section: CONSERVATION OF MOMENTUM
Summary: This section defines and derives the law of conservation of momentum for an isolated system of interacting particles. Using a two-body collision as an application, the law is proven using Newton's second and third laws of motion.
Key terms: conservation of momentum, isolated system, collision, impulse
Teaches concepts: concept:law_of_conservation_of_momentum (Law of Conservation of Momentum)
Requires concepts: concept:impulse (Impulse), concept:momentum (Momentum), concept:newtons_second_law_of_motion (Newton's Second Law of Motion), concept:newtons_third_law_of_motion (Newton's Third Law of Motion)
title: CONSERVATION OF MOMENTUM

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:4
Section: EQUILIBRIUM OF A PARTICLE
Summary: This section defines the equilibrium of a particle as the state where the net external force acting on it is zero. It details the conditions for equilibrium under two, three, and general n concurrent forces, and demonstrates how to solve equilibrium problems using component resolution and free-body diagrams.
Key terms: Equilibrium, Translational equilibrium, Concurrent forces, Free-body diagram, Component resolution
Teaches concepts: concept:component_method_for_equilibrium (Component Method for Equilibrium), concept:equilibrium_of_a_particle (Equilibrium of a Particle), concept:equilibrium_under_concurrent_forces (Equilibrium under Concurrent Forces), concept:translational_equilibrium (Translational Equilibrium)
Requires concepts: concept:free_body_diagram (Free-Body Diagram), concept:newtons_first_law_of_motion (Newton's First Law of Motion), concept:vector_addition (Vector Addition)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:4
Section: COMMON FORCES IN MECHANICS
Summary: This section introduces the common forces encountered in mechanics, distinguishing between non-contact forces like gravity and contact forces such as normal reaction, friction, tension, and spring forces. It explains the mathematical formulation of spring force and tension, and concludes by explaining that macroscopic contact forces fundamentally arise from microscopic electromagnetic interactions.
Key terms: gravitational force, contact forces, normal reaction, friction, spring force, tension, force constant
Teaches concepts: concept:contact_forces (Contact Forces), concept:gravitational_force (Gravitational Force), concept:microscopic_origin_of_contact_forces (Microscopic Origin of Contact Forces), concept:spring_force (Spring Force), concept:tension (Tension)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_charge (Electric Charge), concept:force (Force)
title: COMMON FORCES IN MECHANICS

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:4
Section: Friction
Summary: This section introduces friction as the parallel component of contact force that opposes relative motion between surfaces. It defines and analyzes static, kinetic, and rolling friction, deriving their empirical laws and coefficients. The section also explores practical applications, including motion on inclined planes, accelerating frames, and methods to reduce or utilize friction.
Key terms: Friction, Static Friction, Kinetic Friction, Rolling Friction, Coefficient of Static Friction, Coefficient of Kinetic Friction, Angle of Repose, Contact Force
Teaches concepts: concept:angle_of_repose (Angle of Repose), concept:friction_as_a_contact_force_component (Friction as a Contact Force Component), concept:kinetic_friction (Kinetic Friction), concept:rolling_friction (Rolling Friction), concept:static_friction (Static Friction)
Requires concepts: concept:newtons_laws_of_motion (Newton's Laws of Motion), concept:normal_force (Normal Force), concept:resolution_of_forces (Resolution of Forces)
title: Friction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:5
Section: Introduction
Summary: This introductory section contrasts the everyday usage of the terms 'work', 'energy', and 'power' with their precise, rigorous definitions in physics. It establishes the foundation for studying these quantities, noting the mathematical prerequisite of the scalar product of vectors.
Key terms: work, energy, power, scalar product
Teaches concepts: concept:energy_physics_definition (Energy (Physics Definition)), concept:power_physics_definition (Power (Physics Definition)), concept:work_physics_definition (Work (Physics Definition))
Requires concepts: concept:vector (Vector)
