subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:5
Section: The Scalar Product
Summary: This section introduces the scalar (or dot) product of two vectors, defining it both algebraically and geometrically. It covers key algebraic properties such as commutativity and distributivity, derives the product in component form, and demonstrates how to calculate the angle and projection between vectors.
Key terms: scalar product, dot product, projection of a vector, commutative law, distributive law, unit vectors, component form
Teaches concepts: concept:geometric_projection_of_a_vector (Geometric Projection of a Vector), concept:orthogonality_of_vectors (Orthogonality of Vectors), concept:properties_of_scalar_product (Properties of Scalar Product), concept:scalar_product (Scalar Product), concept:scalar_product_in_component_form (Scalar Product in Component Form)
Requires concepts: concept:trigonometric_functions (Trigonometric Functions), concept:vector_components (Vector Components), concept:vector_magnitude (Vector Magnitude)
title: The Scalar Product

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:5
Section: POWER
Summary: This section defines power as the rate at which work is done or energy is transferred, distinguishing between average and instantaneous power. It derives the relationship between power, force, and velocity, and introduces standard units of power and energy such as the watt, horsepower, and kilowatt-hour.
Key terms: Power, Average Power, Instantaneous Power, Watt, Horsepower, Kilowatt-hour
Teaches concepts: concept:average_power (Average Power), concept:instantaneous_power (Instantaneous Power), concept:kilowatt_hour (Kilowatt-hour), concept:power (Power), concept:units_of_power (Units of Power)
Requires concepts: concept:force (Force), concept:velocity (Velocity), concept:work (Work)
title: POWER

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:5
Section: COLLISIONS
Summary: This section introduces the physics of collisions, analyzing how conservation laws of momentum and kinetic energy apply to elastic and inelastic interactions. It derives mathematical formulations for both one-dimensional and two-dimensional collisions, and extends the concept to non-contact scattering processes.
Key terms: Elastic collision, Inelastic collision, Completely inelastic collision, One-dimensional collision, Two-dimensional collision, Scattering
Teaches concepts: concept:completely_inelastic_collision (Completely Inelastic Collision), concept:elastic_collision (Elastic Collision), concept:inelastic_collision (Inelastic Collision), concept:one_dimensional_elastic_collision (One-Dimensional Elastic Collision), concept:scattering (Scattering), concept:two_dimensional_collision (Two-Dimensional Collision)
Requires concepts: concept:conservation_of_linear_momentum (Conservation of Linear Momentum), concept:kinetic_energy (Kinetic Energy), concept:vector_resolution (Vector Resolution)
title: COLLISIONS

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:5
Section: Notions of Work and Kinetic Energy: The Work-Energy Theorem
Summary: This section introduces the concepts of kinetic energy and work, deriving the Work-Energy Theorem for constant forces in both one and three dimensions. It also demonstrates the application of this theorem to physical systems involving gravitational and resistive forces.
Key terms: Kinetic Energy, Work, Work-Energy Theorem, Constant Force, Displacement, Resistive Force
Teaches concepts: concept:kinetic_energy (Kinetic Energy), concept:work (Work), concept:work_energy_theorem (Work-Energy Theorem)
Requires concepts: concept:kinematic_equations (Kinematic Equations), concept:newtons_second_law_of_motion (Newton's Second Law of Motion), concept:vector_dot_product (Vector Dot Product)
title: Notions of Work and Kinetic Energy: The Work-Energy Theorem

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:5
Section: Work
Summary: This section defines the physical concept of work done by a constant force as the scalar product of force and displacement. It details the conditions for positive, negative, and zero work, and establishes the dimensions and various units of work.
Key terms: Work, Constant Force, Scalar Product, Displacement, Joule, Positive Work, Negative Work, Zero Work
Teaches concepts: concept:positive_and_negative_work (Positive and Negative Work), concept:units_and_dimensions_of_work (Units and Dimensions of Work), concept:work_done_by_a_constant_force (Work Done by a Constant Force), concept:zero_work_conditions (Zero Work Conditions)
Requires concepts: concept:displacement (Displacement), concept:force (Force), concept:newtons_third_law_of_motion (Newton's Third Law of Motion), concept:scalar_product (Scalar Product)
title: Work

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:5
Section: KINETIC ENERGY
Summary: This section defines kinetic energy as a scalar quantity representing the work an object can perform by virtue of its motion, mathematically expressed as K = 1/2 m v^2. It provides typical kinetic energy values for various objects and demonstrates how to calculate changes in speed resulting from a reduction in kinetic energy through a practical ballistics example.
Key terms: Kinetic Energy, Scalar quantity, Mass, Speed, Work
Teaches concepts: concept:kinetic_energy (Kinetic Energy)
Requires concepts: concept:mass (Mass), concept:scalar_quantity (Scalar Quantity), concept:velocity (Velocity), concept:work (Work)
title: KINETIC ENERGY

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:5
Section: WORK DONE BY A VARIABLE FORCE
Summary: This section explains how to calculate the work done by a variable force using graphical analysis and integration. It represents work as the area under a force-displacement curve and compares the scalar work-energy theorem with the vector form of Newton's second law.
Key terms: variable force, definite integral, force-displacement graph, work-energy theorem, retarding force
Teaches concepts: concept:comparison_of_newtons_second_law_and_work_energy_theorem (Comparison of Newton's Second Law and Work-Energy Theorem), concept:graphical_method_for_work (Graphical Method for Work), concept:integral_representation_of_work (Integral Representation of Work), concept:work_done_by_a_variable_force (Work Done by a Variable Force)
Requires concepts: concept:definite_integral (Definite Integral), concept:newtons_second_law (Newton's Second Law), concept:work_energy_theorem (Work-Energy Theorem)
title: WORK DONE BY A VARIABLE FORCE

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:5
Section: THE CONCEPT OF POTENTIAL ENERGY
Summary: This section introduces potential energy as the energy stored in a body due to its position or configuration, focusing on gravitational potential energy. It establishes the mathematical relationship between conservative forces and potential energy, defining conservative forces as those where work done is path-independent.
Key terms: potential energy, gravitational potential energy, conservative force, work done, kinetic energy
Teaches concepts: concept:conservative_force (Conservative Force), concept:force_potential_energy_relation (Force-Potential Energy Relation), concept:gravitational_potential_energy (Gravitational Potential Energy), concept:potential_energy (Potential Energy)
Requires concepts: concept:derivative (Derivative), concept:kinetic_energy (Kinetic Energy), concept:work (Work)
title: THE CONCEPT OF POTENTIAL ENERGY

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:5
Section: THE CONSERVATION OF MECHANICAL ENERGY
Summary: This section introduces the principle of conservation of total mechanical energy, demonstrating that the sum of kinetic and potential energy remains constant under conservative forces. It defines conservative and non-conservative forces, and applies these principles to analyze a freely falling body and a bob undergoing vertical circular motion.
Key terms: mechanical energy, conservative force, non-conservative force, conservation of mechanical energy, vertical circular motion
Teaches concepts: concept:conservative_force (Conservative Force), concept:non_conservative_force (Non-conservative Force), concept:principle_of_conservation_of_mechanical_energy (Principle of Conservation of Mechanical Energy), concept:total_mechanical_energy (Total Mechanical Energy), concept:vertical_circular_motion (Vertical Circular Motion)
Requires concepts: concept:kinetic_energy (Kinetic Energy), concept:potential_energy (Potential Energy), concept:work_energy_theorem (Work-Energy Theorem)
title: THE CONSERVATION OF MECHANICAL ENERGY

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:5
Section: THE POTENTIAL ENERGY OF A SPRING
Summary: This section introduces Hooke's law and derives the work done by a spring force to define the potential energy of a spring. It demonstrates the conservation of mechanical energy in spring-mass systems and extends the work-energy theorem to include non-conservative forces like friction.
Key terms: Hooke's law, spring constant, spring force, potential energy of a spring, conservative force, non-conservative force
Teaches concepts: concept:conservation_of_mechanical_energy_in_spring_systems (Conservation of Mechanical Energy in Spring Systems), concept:hookes_law (Hooke's Law), concept:potential_energy_of_a_spring (Potential Energy of a Spring), concept:spring_constant (Spring Constant), concept:work_energy_theorem_with_non_conservative_forces (Work-Energy Theorem with Non-conservative Forces)
Requires concepts: concept:conservative_force (Conservative Force), concept:integration (Integration), concept:kinetic_energy (Kinetic Energy), concept:work_energy_theorem (Work-Energy Theorem)
title: THE POTENTIAL ENERGY OF A SPRING

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section introduces the transition from point mass mechanics to the study of extended systems, specifically defining rigid bodies. It categorizes rigid body motion into pure translation, pure rotation about a fixed or non-fixed axis, and combined motion such as rolling.
Key terms: extended body, rigid body, translational motion, rotational motion, axis of rotation, precession, rolling motion
Teaches concepts: concept:extended_body (Extended Body), concept:precession (Precession), concept:pure_rotational_motion_about_a_fixed_axis (Pure Rotational Motion about a Fixed Axis), concept:pure_translational_motion (Pure Translational Motion), concept:rigid_body (Rigid Body), concept:rolling_motion (Rolling Motion)
Requires concepts: concept:point_mass (Point Mass), concept:velocity (Velocity)
title: Introduction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: KINEMATICS OF ROTATIONAL MOTION ABOUT A FIXED AXIS
Summary: This section establishes a direct analogy between translational and rotational kinematics about a fixed axis. It introduces rotational kinematic variables and derives the kinematic equations for uniform angular acceleration.
Key terms: angular displacement, angular velocity, angular acceleration, rotational kinematics, uniform angular acceleration
Teaches concepts: concept:analogy_between_rotational_and_translational_kinematics (Analogy between Rotational and Translational Kinematics), concept:angular_displacement (Angular Displacement), concept:kinematic_equations_for_rotational_motion (Kinematic Equations for Rotational Motion), concept:uniform_angular_acceleration (Uniform Angular Acceleration)
Requires concepts: concept:integration (Integration), concept:linear_kinematic_equations (Linear Kinematic Equations)
title: KINEMATICS OF ROTATIONAL MOTION ABOUT A FIXED AXIS

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: DYNAMICS OF ROTATIONAL MOTION ABOUT A FIXED AXIS
Summary: This section establishes the dynamics of rotational motion about a fixed axis by drawing analogies to linear motion. It derives the expressions for work done by a torque, rotational power, and the rotational analogue of Newton's second law.
Key terms: Torque, Moment of inertia, Angular displacement, Angular acceleration, Rotational work, Rotational power
Teaches concepts: concept:newtons_second_law_for_rotational_motion (Newton's Second Law for Rotational Motion), concept:rotational_analogues (Rotational Analogues), concept:rotational_power (Rotational Power), concept:work_done_by_a_torque (Work Done by a Torque)
Requires concepts: concept:angular_velocity_and_acceleration (Angular Velocity and Acceleration), concept:moment_of_inertia (Moment of Inertia), concept:newtons_second_law_of_motion (Newton's Second Law of Motion), concept:torque (Torque)
title: DYNAMICS OF ROTATIONAL MOTION ABOUT A FIXED AXIS

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: ANGULAR MOMENTUM IN CASE OF ROTATION ABOUT A FIXED AXIS
Summary: This section analyzes the angular momentum of a rigid body rotating about a fixed axis, decomposing it into components parallel and perpendicular to the axis. It derives the relation between the axial angular momentum, moment of inertia, and angular velocity, establishing the rotational analogue of Newton's second law.
Key terms: Angular Momentum, Fixed Axis of Rotation, Moment of Inertia, Torque, Rotational Analogue of Newton's Second Law
Teaches concepts: concept:angular_momentum_of_a_rotating_rigid_body (Angular Momentum of a Rotating Rigid Body), concept:axial_component_of_angular_momentum (Axial Component of Angular Momentum), concept:perpendicular_component_of_angular_momentum (Perpendicular Component of Angular Momentum), concept:rotational_analogue_of_newtons_second_law (Rotational Analogue of Newton's Second Law)
Requires concepts: concept:angular_momentum (Angular Momentum), concept:moment_of_inertia (Moment of Inertia), concept:torque (Torque)
title: ANGULAR MOMENTUM IN CASE OF ROTATION ABOUT A FIXED AXIS

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: Centre of Mass
Summary: This section introduces the concept of the centre of mass as the average position of a system's mass. It derives mathematical formulations for discrete particle systems in one, two, and three dimensions, and extends these to continuous rigid bodies using integration and symmetry principles.
Key terms: centre of mass, mass-weighted mean, position vector, rigid body, continuous mass distribution, centroid, reflection symmetry, lamina
Teaches concepts: concept:centre_of_mass_of_a_lamina (Centre of Mass of a Lamina), concept:centre_of_mass_of_a_rigid_body (Centre of Mass of a Rigid Body), concept:centre_of_mass_of_a_two_particle_system (Centre of Mass of a Two-Particle System), concept:centre_of_mass_of_an_n_particle_system (Centre of Mass of an n-Particle System), concept:symmetry_principle_for_centre_of_mass (Symmetry Principle for Centre of Mass)
Requires concepts: concept:centroid (Centroid), concept:integral_calculus (Integral Calculus), concept:position_vector (Position Vector)
title: Centre of Mass

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: Motion of Centre of Mass
Summary: This section derives and explains the motion of the centre of mass for a system of particles. It establishes that the velocity and acceleration of the centre of mass depend on individual particle motions, and shows that the centre of mass moves under the influence of external forces alone, as if the entire mass were concentrated at that point.
Key terms: centre of mass, velocity of centre of mass, acceleration of centre of mass, external forces, internal forces, Newton's second law for a system of particles
Teaches concepts: concept:acceleration_of_the_centre_of_mass (Acceleration of the Centre of Mass), concept:internal_and_external_forces_in_a_system (Internal and External Forces in a System), concept:newtons_second_law_for_a_system_of_particles (Newton's Second Law for a System of Particles), concept:physical_significance_of_centre_of_mass_motion (Physical Significance of Centre of Mass Motion), concept:velocity_of_the_centre_of_mass (Velocity of the Centre of Mass)
Requires concepts: concept:centre_of_mass (Centre of Mass), concept:newtons_second_law_of_motion (Newton's Second Law of Motion), concept:newtons_third_law_of_motion (Newton's Third Law of Motion)
title: Motion of Centre of Mass

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: Linear Momentum of a System of Particles
Summary: This section defines the total linear momentum of a system of particles and relates it to the velocity of its centre of mass. It extends Newton's second law to show that the rate of change of total momentum equals the net external force, leading to the law of conservation of linear momentum when external forces are zero.
Key terms: total linear momentum, centre of mass velocity, Newton's second law, conservation of linear momentum, external force, centre of mass frame
Teaches concepts: concept:centre_of_mass_frame (Centre of Mass Frame), concept:law_of_conservation_of_linear_momentum_for_a_system (Law of Conservation of Linear Momentum for a System), concept:newtons_second_law_for_a_system_of_particles (Newton's Second Law for a System of Particles), concept:total_linear_momentum_of_a_system_of_particles (Total Linear Momentum of a System of Particles)
Requires concepts: concept:centre_of_mass_velocity (Centre of Mass Velocity), concept:linear_momentum_of_a_single_particle (Linear Momentum of a Single Particle), concept:newtons_second_law_of_motion (Newton's Second Law of Motion)
title: Linear Momentum of a System of Particles

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: Vector Product of Two Vectors
Summary: This section defines the vector product (or cross product) of two vectors, detailing its magnitude, direction, and key algebraic properties such as non-commutativity and distributivity. It explains the vector product of orthogonal unit vectors and demonstrates how to compute the cross product using a determinant representation in component form.
Key terms: Vector Product, Cross Product, Right-handed screw rule, Non-commutativity, Unit Vectors, Determinant Representation
Teaches concepts: concept:determinant_representation_of_vector_product (Determinant Representation of Vector Product), concept:non_commutativity_of_vector_product (Non-Commutativity of Vector Product), concept:right_handed_screw_rule (Right-Handed Screw Rule), concept:vector_product (Vector Product), concept:vector_product_of_unit_vectors (Vector Product of Unit Vectors)
Requires concepts: concept:determinant (Determinant), concept:vector (Vector)
title: Vector Product of Two Vectors

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: Angular Velocity and its Relation with Linear Velocity
Summary: This section defines angular velocity and angular acceleration for a rotating rigid body, establishing both scalar and vector relationships with linear velocity. It demonstrates that all particles in a rigid body share a common angular velocity and expresses the linear velocity vector as the cross product of angular velocity and the position vector.
Key terms: angular velocity, angular acceleration, rigid body rotation, right-handed screw rule, vector product, linear velocity
Teaches concepts: concept:angular_acceleration (Angular Acceleration), concept:angular_velocity (Angular Velocity), concept:angular_velocity_of_a_rigid_body (Angular Velocity of a Rigid Body), concept:angular_velocity_vector (Angular Velocity Vector), concept:relation_between_linear_and_angular_velocity_vectors (Relation between Linear and Angular Velocity Vectors)
Requires concepts: concept:circular_motion (Circular Motion), concept:rigid_body (Rigid Body), concept:vector_product (Vector Product)
title: Angular Velocity and its Relation with Linear Velocity

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: Torque and Angular Momentum
Summary: This section introduces torque and angular momentum as the rotational analogues of force and linear momentum, defining them mathematically using vector products. It derives the fundamental dynamical relationships between torque and angular momentum for both single particles and systems of particles, culminating in the law of conservation of angular momentum.
Key terms: Torque, Moment of Force, Angular Momentum, Vector Product, Conservation of Angular Momentum, Precession
Teaches concepts: concept:angular_momentum_of_a_particle (Angular Momentum of a Particle), concept:angular_momentum_of_a_system_of_particles (Angular Momentum of a System of Particles), concept:conservation_of_angular_momentum (Conservation of Angular Momentum), concept:relation_between_torque_and_angular_momentum (Relation between Torque and Angular Momentum), concept:torque_moment_of_force (Torque (Moment of Force))
Requires concepts: concept:linear_momentum (Linear Momentum), concept:newtons_second_law_of_motion (Newton's Second Law of Motion), concept:newtons_third_law_of_motion (Newton's Third Law of Motion), concept:vector_product (Vector Product)
title: Torque and Angular Momentum

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: Equilibrium of a Rigid Body
Summary: This section defines the conditions for the mechanical equilibrium of a rigid body, which requires both translational and rotational equilibrium. It explores the scalar equations of equilibrium, contrasts rigid body equilibrium with particle equilibrium, and introduces the concepts of partial equilibrium and couples.
Key terms: Mechanical Equilibrium, Translational Equilibrium, Rotational Equilibrium, Coplanar Forces, Partial Equilibrium, Couple, Moment of a Couple
Teaches concepts: concept:couple (Couple), concept:mechanical_equilibrium_of_a_rigid_body (Mechanical Equilibrium of a Rigid Body), concept:moment_of_a_couple (Moment of a Couple), concept:partial_equilibrium (Partial Equilibrium), concept:rotational_equilibrium (Rotational Equilibrium), concept:translational_equilibrium (Translational Equilibrium)
Requires concepts: concept:rigid_body (Rigid Body), concept:torque (Torque), concept:vector_addition (Vector Addition)
title: Equilibrium of a Rigid Body

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: Principle of moments
Summary: This section introduces the lever as a simple machine and explains its equilibrium conditions. It defines the principle of moments and mechanical advantage, demonstrating how a lever can be used to lift heavy loads with minimal effort.
Key terms: lever, fulcrum, load arm, effort arm, principle of moments, mechanical advantage
Teaches concepts: concept:fulcrum (Fulcrum), concept:lever (Lever), concept:load_arm_and_effort_arm (Load Arm and Effort Arm), concept:mechanical_advantage (Mechanical Advantage), concept:principle_of_moments (Principle of Moments)
Requires concepts: concept:rotational_equilibrium (Rotational Equilibrium), concept:torque (Torque), concept:translational_equilibrium (Translational Equilibrium)
title: Principle of moments

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: Centre of gravity
Summary: This section defines the centre of gravity (CG) as the point where the entire weight of a body acts and where the net gravitational torque is zero. It explores the relationship between the centre of gravity and the centre of mass, explaining that they coincide in a uniform gravitational field. Additionally, it details experimental methods to locate the CG and applies equilibrium conditions to solve mechanical problems.
Key terms: centre of gravity, centre of mass, gravitational torque, mechanical equilibrium, suspension method, translational equilibrium, rotational equilibrium
Teaches concepts: concept:centre_of_gravity (Centre of Gravity), concept:coincidence_of_centre_of_gravity_and_centre_of_mass (Coincidence of Centre of Gravity and Centre of Mass), concept:equilibrium_of_supported_rigid_bodies (Equilibrium of Supported Rigid Bodies), concept:experimental_determination_of_centre_of_gravity (Experimental Determination of Centre of Gravity)
Requires concepts: concept:centre_of_mass (Centre of Mass), concept:torque (Torque), concept:translational_and_rotational_equilibrium (Translational and Rotational Equilibrium)
title: Centre of gravity

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:6
Section: MOMENT OF INERTIA
Summary: This section introduces the moment of inertia as the rotational analogue of mass, characterizing a rigid body's resistance to rotational acceleration. It derives the rotational kinetic energy of a rigid body and defines the radius of gyration, while providing formulas for the moments of inertia of common geometric shapes.
Key terms: Moment of Inertia, Rotational Kinetic Energy, Radius of Gyration, Flywheel, Rotational Inertia
Teaches concepts: concept:flywheel (Flywheel), concept:moment_of_inertia (Moment of Inertia), concept:moment_of_inertia_of_regular_bodies (Moment of Inertia of Regular Bodies), concept:radius_of_gyration (Radius of Gyration), concept:rotational_kinetic_energy (Rotational Kinetic Energy)
Requires concepts: concept:angular_velocity (Angular Velocity), concept:kinetic_energy (Kinetic Energy), concept:rigid_body (Rigid Body)
title: MOMENT OF INERTIA

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:7
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section introduces the historical development of gravity and planetary motion theories. It covers Galileo's experiments on constant gravitational acceleration, the transition from the geocentric to the heliocentric model, and the observational work of Tycho Brahe and Johannes Kepler.
Key terms: acceleration due to gravity, geocentric model, heliocentric model, Kepler's laws, planetary motion
Teaches concepts: concept:galileos_observation_of_gravity (Galileo's Observation of Gravity), concept:geocentric_model (Geocentric Model), concept:heliocentric_model (Heliocentric Model), concept:keplers_laws_of_planetary_motion (Kepler's Laws of Planetary Motion)
Requires concepts: concept:acceleration (Acceleration), concept:circular_motion (Circular Motion)
title: Introduction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:7
Section: Energy of an Orbiting Satellite
Summary: This section derives and explains the kinetic, potential, and total mechanical energy of a satellite in a circular orbit. It introduces the concept of a bound state characterized by negative total energy and demonstrates how to calculate energy changes during orbit transfers.
Key terms: kinetic energy, potential energy, total mechanical energy, bound state, orbit transfer
Teaches concepts: concept:bound_state_of_a_satellite (Bound State of a Satellite), concept:gravitational_potential_energy_of_an_orbiting_satellite (Gravitational Potential Energy of an Orbiting Satellite), concept:kinetic_energy_of_an_orbiting_satellite (Kinetic Energy of an Orbiting Satellite), concept:satellite_orbit_transfer_energy (Satellite Orbit Transfer Energy), concept:total_mechanical_energy_of_an_orbiting_satellite (Total Mechanical Energy of an Orbiting Satellite)
Requires concepts: concept:circular_orbit_of_a_satellite (Circular Orbit of a Satellite), concept:gravitational_potential_energy (Gravitational Potential Energy), concept:kinetic_energy (Kinetic Energy)
title: Energy of an Orbiting Satellite

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:7
Section: Exercises
Summary: This section provides an illustrative diagram for exercises concerning the direction of gravitational intensity inside a hemispherical shell. It depicts points at the center and along the axis of symmetry to help analyze gravitational field directions.
Key terms: gravitational intensity, hemispherical shell, axis of symmetry
Teaches concepts: concept:gravitational_intensity_of_a_hemispherical_shell (gravitational intensity of a hemispherical shell)
Requires concepts: concept:gravitational_field_of_a_spherical_shell (gravitational field of a spherical shell), concept:gravitational_intensity (gravitational intensity)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:7
Section: Kepler's Laws
Summary: This section introduces Kepler's three laws of planetary motion: the Law of Orbits, the Law of Areas, and the Law of Periods. It explains the geometry of elliptical orbits and derives the constancy of areal velocity from the conservation of angular momentum under central forces.
Key terms: Kepler's laws, Law of Orbits, Law of Areas, Law of Periods, ellipse, semi-major axis, perihelion, aphelion, areal velocity, central force
Teaches concepts: concept:areal_velocity (Areal Velocity), concept:law_of_areas (Law of Areas), concept:law_of_orbits (Law of Orbits), concept:law_of_periods (Law of Periods)
Requires concepts: concept:central_force (Central Force), concept:conservation_of_angular_momentum (Conservation of Angular Momentum), concept:elliptical_geometry (Elliptical Geometry)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:7
Section: Universal Law of Gravitation
Summary: This section introduces Newton's Universal Law of Gravitation, detailing its mathematical formulation, vector representation, and the universal gravitational constant. It also explains the principle of superposition for calculating net gravitational forces from multiple point masses and presents the shell theorems for uniform spherical shells.
Key terms: Universal Law of Gravitation, Universal Gravitational Constant, Principle of Superposition, Shell Theorems, Centripetal Acceleration
Teaches concepts: concept:principle_of_superposition_for_gravitational_forces (Principle of Superposition for Gravitational Forces), concept:shell_theorems_of_gravitation (Shell Theorems of Gravitation), concept:universal_gravitational_constant (Universal Gravitational Constant), concept:universal_law_of_gravitation (Universal Law of Gravitation)
Requires concepts: concept:centripetal_acceleration (Centripetal Acceleration), concept:newtons_third_law_of_motion (Newton's Third Law of Motion), concept:vector_addition (Vector Addition)
title: Universal Law of Gravitation

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:7
Section: The Gravitational Constant
Summary: This section describes the experimental determination of the universal gravitational constant G by Henry Cavendish using a torsion balance. It explains the balance of gravitational and restoring torques in the experiment and provides the currently accepted value of G.
Key terms: Universal gravitational constant, Cavendish's experiment, Torsion balance, Gravitational torque, Restoring torque
Teaches concepts: concept:cavendishs_experiment (Cavendish's Experiment), concept:universal_gravitational_constant (Universal Gravitational Constant)
Requires concepts: concept:newtons_law_of_universal_gravitation (Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation), concept:torque_and_rotational_equilibrium (Torque and Rotational Equilibrium)
title: The Gravitational Constant

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:7
Section: Acceleration Due to Gravity of the Earth
Summary: This section explains how to calculate the gravitational force exerted by the Earth on objects located both outside and inside its volume using the shell theorem. It derives the formula for the acceleration due to gravity (g) at the Earth's surface and demonstrates how this value can be used to estimate the Earth's mass.
Key terms: Shell theorem, Acceleration due to gravity, Gravitational constant, Cavendish's experiment, Uniform density
Teaches concepts: concept:acceleration_due_to_gravity (Acceleration due to Gravity), concept:estimation_of_earths_mass (Estimation of Earth's Mass), concept:gravitational_force_inside_the_earth (Gravitational Force Inside the Earth), concept:shell_theorem_for_gravitation (Shell Theorem for Gravitation)
Requires concepts: concept:density (Density), concept:newtons_law_of_universal_gravitation (Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation), concept:newtons_second_law_of_motion (Newton's Second Law of Motion)
title: Acceleration Due to Gravity of the Earth

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:7
Section: Acceleration Due to Gravity Below and Above the Surface of Earth
Summary: This section derives and analyzes the variation of acceleration due to gravity both above and below the Earth's surface. It demonstrates that gravity is at its maximum on the surface and decreases with both altitude and depth.
Key terms: acceleration due to gravity, altitude variation, depth variation, binomial approximation, shell theorem
Teaches concepts: concept:acceleration_due_to_gravity_at_a_depth (Acceleration due to Gravity at a Depth), concept:acceleration_due_to_gravity_at_a_height (Acceleration due to Gravity at a Height), concept:approximation_of_gravity_for_small_heights (Approximation of Gravity for Small Heights), concept:variation_of_gravity_with_position (Variation of Gravity with Position)
Requires concepts: concept:binomial_approximation (Binomial Approximation), concept:newtons_law_of_universal_gravitation (Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation), concept:shell_theorem (Shell Theorem)
title: Acceleration Due to Gravity Below and Above the Surface of Earth

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:7
Section: Gravitational Potential Energy
Summary: This section introduces gravitational potential energy, deriving its expressions both near the Earth's surface and for variable gravitational forces at arbitrary distances. It also defines gravitational potential and explains how to calculate the total potential energy of a multi-particle system using the superposition principle.
Key terms: Gravitational Potential Energy, Conservative Force, Gravitational Potential, Superposition Principle, Reference at Infinity
Teaches concepts: concept:gravitational_potential (Gravitational Potential), concept:gravitational_potential_energy (Gravitational Potential Energy), concept:gravitational_potential_energy_for_variable_force (Gravitational Potential Energy for Variable Force), concept:gravitational_potential_energy_near_earths_surface (Gravitational Potential Energy near Earth's Surface), concept:gravitational_potential_energy_of_a_system_of_particles (Gravitational Potential Energy of a System of Particles)
Requires concepts: concept:conservative_force (Conservative Force), concept:newtons_law_of_universal_gravitation (Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation), concept:work_done_by_a_force (Work Done by a Force)
title: Gravitational Potential Energy

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:7
Section: Escape Speed
Summary: This section defines and derives the escape speed of an object from a celestial body using the principle of conservation of energy. It calculates the escape speed for Earth and the Moon, discusses its implications for planetary atmospheres, and analyzes projectile motion between two spheres using the concept of a neutral point.
Key terms: escape speed, escape velocity, conservation of energy, gravitational potential energy, neutral point, thermal velocity
Teaches concepts: concept:atmospheric_escape (Atmospheric Escape), concept:escape_speed (Escape Speed), concept:escape_speed_formula (Escape Speed Formula), concept:neutral_point (Neutral Point)
Requires concepts: concept:acceleration_due_to_gravity (Acceleration due to Gravity), concept:gravitational_potential_energy (Gravitational Potential Energy), concept:law_of_conservation_of_energy (Law of Conservation of Energy)
title: Escape Speed

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:7
Section: Earth Satellites
Summary: This section introduces Earth satellites, explaining their motion in circular or elliptical orbits governed by Kepler's laws. It derives the mathematical expressions for a satellite's orbital speed and time period, with special focus on satellites orbiting close to the Earth's surface.
Key terms: Earth satellites, orbital speed, time period, Kepler's laws, centripetal force, gravitational force
Teaches concepts: concept:earth_satellites (Earth Satellites), concept:keplers_law_of_periods_for_satellites (Kepler's Law of Periods for Satellites), concept:orbital_speed_of_a_satellite (Orbital Speed of a Satellite), concept:satellites_close_to_earth (Satellites Close to Earth), concept:time_period_of_a_satellite (Time Period of a Satellite)
Requires concepts: concept:centripetal_force (Centripetal Force), concept:keplers_laws_of_planetary_motion (Kepler's Laws of Planetary Motion), concept:newtons_law_of_universal_gravitation (Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation)
title: Earth Satellites

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:8
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section introduces the concepts of elasticity and plasticity, distinguishing between materials that regain their shape after deformation and those that deform permanently. It also highlights the engineering applications of understanding the elastic behavior of materials.
Key terms: elasticity, plasticity, elastic deformation, rigid body
Teaches concepts: concept:elastic_deformation (Elastic Deformation), concept:elasticity (Elasticity), concept:plasticity (Plasticity), concept:rigid_body (Rigid Body)
Requires concepts: concept:force (Force)
title: Introduction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:8
Section: Stress and Strain
Summary: This section defines stress as the internal restoring force per unit area and strain as the fractional change in dimension. It details three distinct types of stress and strain: longitudinal, shearing, and hydraulic, depending on the direction and nature of the applied deforming forces.
Key terms: stress, strain, longitudinal stress, shearing stress, hydraulic stress, tensile stress, compressive stress, volume strain
Teaches concepts: concept:hydraulic_stress_and_volume_strain (Hydraulic Stress and Volume Strain), concept:longitudinal_stress_and_strain (Longitudinal Stress and Strain), concept:shearing_stress_and_strain (Shearing Stress and Strain), concept:stress (Stress)
Requires concepts: concept:force (Force), concept:pressure (Pressure), concept:trigonometric_functions (Trigonometric Functions)
title: Stress and Strain

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:8
Section: Hooke's Law
Summary: This section introduces Hooke's law, which states that stress and strain are directly proportional to each other for small deformations. It also defines the modulus of elasticity as the constant of proportionality in this relationship.
Key terms: Hooke's law, stress, strain, modulus of elasticity, proportionality constant
Teaches concepts: concept:hookes_law (Hooke's Law), concept:modulus_of_elasticity (Modulus of Elasticity)
Requires concepts: concept:strain (Strain), concept:stress (Stress)
title: Hooke's Law

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:8
Section: Stress-Strain Curve
Summary: This section describes the experimental determination of the relationship between stress and strain using a tensile test. It analyzes the typical stress-strain curve of a metal, identifying key regions such as the proportional limit, yield point, ultimate tensile strength, and fracture point. It also classifies materials as ductile, brittle, or elastomers based on their stress-strain behavior.
Key terms: Stress-strain curve, Proportional limit, Yield strength, Ultimate tensile strength, Ductile, Brittle, Elastomers
Teaches concepts: concept:ductile_and_brittle_materials (Ductile and Brittle Materials), concept:elastomers (Elastomers), concept:proportional_limit (Proportional Limit), concept:stress_strain_curve (Stress-Strain Curve), concept:ultimate_tensile_strength (Ultimate Tensile Strength), concept:yield_point (Yield Point)
Requires concepts: concept:hookes_law (Hooke's Law), concept:strain (Strain), concept:stress (Stress)
title: Stress-Strain Curve

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:8
Section: ELASTIC MODULI
Summary: This section introduces the moduli of elasticity, which represent the constant ratio of stress to strain within a material's elastic limit. It defines and derives Young's modulus, shear modulus, bulk modulus, compressibility, Poisson's ratio, and the elastic potential energy stored in a stretched wire.
Key terms: Modulus of elasticity, Young's modulus, Shear modulus, Bulk modulus, Compressibility, Poisson's ratio, Elastic potential energy
Teaches concepts: concept:bulk_modulus_and_compressibility (Bulk Modulus and Compressibility), concept:elastic_potential_energy_in_a_stretched_wire (Elastic Potential Energy in a Stretched Wire), concept:modulus_of_elasticity (Modulus of Elasticity), concept:poissons_ratio (Poisson's Ratio), concept:shear_modulus (Shear Modulus), concept:youngs_modulus (Young's Modulus)
Requires concepts: concept:elastic_limit (Elastic Limit), concept:strain (Strain), concept:stress (Stress)
title: ELASTIC MODULI

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:8
Section: APPLICATIONS OF ELASTIC BEHAVIOUR OF MATERIALS
Summary: This section explores the practical applications of the elastic behavior of materials in structural engineering and geology. It covers the design of crane ropes, the bending and buckling of beams, the structural design of pillars, and the theoretical calculation of the maximum height of mountains on Earth.
Key terms: Yield strength, Young's modulus, Sag, Buckling, I-shaped beam, Elastic limit, Shearing stress
Teaches concepts: concept:bending_of_beams (Bending of Beams), concept:buckling (Buckling), concept:crane_rope_design (Crane Rope Design), concept:i_shaped_beams (I-shaped Beams), concept:maximum_height_of_a_mountain (Maximum Height of a Mountain)
Requires concepts: concept:shearing_stress (Shearing Stress), concept:yield_strength (Yield Strength), concept:youngs_modulus (Young's Modulus)
title: APPLICATIONS OF ELASTIC BEHAVIOUR OF MATERIALS

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section introduces fluids as substances that can flow, encompassing both liquids and gases. It compares the physical properties of solids, liquids, and gases, focusing on shape, volume, compressibility, and resistance to shear stress.
Key terms: Fluids, Liquids, Gases, Compressibility, Shear stress
Teaches concepts: concept:compressibility_of_fluids (Compressibility of Fluids), concept:fluids (Fluids), concept:shear_stress_in_fluids (Shear Stress in Fluids)
Requires concepts: concept:shear_stress (Shear Stress), concept:states_of_matter (States of Matter)
title: Introduction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: Pressure
Summary: This section introduces the concept of pressure as the normal force exerted per unit area, explaining its scalar nature, SI units, and behavior in static fluids. It also defines fluid density and relative density, illustrating these concepts with physical examples and data.
Key terms: Pressure, Pascal, Normal Force, Density, Relative Density
Teaches concepts: concept:density (Density), concept:normal_force_in_fluids (Normal Force in Fluids), concept:pascal (Pascal), concept:pressure (Pressure), concept:relative_density (Relative Density)
Requires concepts: concept:force (Force), concept:mass_and_volume (Mass and Volume), concept:newtons_third_law_of_motion (Newton's Third Law of Motion)
title: Pressure

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: Pascal's Law
Summary: This section introduces Pascal's Law, which states that pressure in a fluid at rest is equal at all points of the same height. It provides a geometric proof using a prismatic fluid element in static equilibrium, demonstrating that pressure is isotropic and a scalar quantity.
Key terms: Pascal's Law, fluid pressure, static equilibrium, prismatic element, scalar quantity
Teaches concepts: concept:isotropic_nature_of_fluid_pressure (Isotropic Nature of Fluid Pressure), concept:pascals_law (Pascal's Law)
Requires concepts: concept:fluid_pressure (Fluid Pressure), concept:static_equilibrium (Static Equilibrium)
title: Pascal's Law

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: Variation of Pressure with Depth
Summary: This section derives the linear relationship between fluid pressure and depth, showing that pressure increases with depth due to the weight of the fluid. It defines absolute and gauge pressure and explains the hydrostatic paradox, where pressure depends only on depth and not on the shape of the container.
Key terms: gauge pressure, absolute pressure, hydrostatic paradox, fluid density, atmospheric pressure
Teaches concepts: concept:absolute_pressure (Absolute Pressure), concept:gauge_pressure (Gauge Pressure), concept:hydrostatic_paradox (Hydrostatic Paradox), concept:pressure_depth_relationship (Pressure-Depth Relationship)
Requires concepts: concept:atmospheric_pressure (Atmospheric Pressure), concept:fluid_density (Fluid Density), concept:hydrostatic_equilibrium (Hydrostatic Equilibrium)
title: Variation of Pressure with Depth

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: Atmospheric Pressure and Gauge Pressure
Summary: This section explains the measurement of atmospheric pressure using Torricelli's mercury barometer and introduces various pressure units like torr and bar. It also describes the open-tube manometer for measuring gauge pressure, which is the difference between absolute and atmospheric pressure.
Key terms: Atmospheric Pressure, Mercury Barometer, Torr, Bar, Open Tube Manometer, Gauge Pressure
Teaches concepts: concept:atmospheric_pressure (Atmospheric Pressure), concept:gauge_pressure (Gauge Pressure), concept:mercury_barometer (Mercury Barometer), concept:open_tube_manometer (Open Tube Manometer), concept:units_of_pressure (Units of Pressure)
Requires concepts: concept:density (Density), concept:hydrostatic_pressure (Hydrostatic Pressure)
title: Atmospheric Pressure and Gauge Pressure

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: Hydraulic Machines
Summary: This section introduces hydraulic machines as practical applications of Pascal's law for pressure transmission in fluids. It explains the mechanics of hydraulic lifts and hydraulic brakes, deriving how force multiplication is achieved through differences in piston cross-sectional areas.
Key terms: Pascal's law, hydraulic lift, mechanical advantage, hydraulic brakes, incompressible fluid
Teaches concepts: concept:hydraulic_brakes (Hydraulic Brakes), concept:hydraulic_lift (Hydraulic Lift), concept:mechanical_advantage_of_hydraulic_systems (Mechanical Advantage of Hydraulic Systems), concept:pascals_law_for_pressure_transmission (Pascal's Law for Pressure Transmission), concept:volume_conservation_in_hydraulics (Volume Conservation in Hydraulics)
Requires concepts: concept:fluid_pressure (Fluid Pressure), concept:incompressible_fluid (Incompressible Fluid)
title: Hydraulic Machines

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: Streamline Flow
Summary: This section introduces fluid dynamics, distinguishing between steady (streamline) flow and turbulent flow. It defines streamlines and derives the equation of continuity, which expresses the conservation of mass for incompressible fluid flow.
Key terms: Fluid dynamics, Steady flow, Streamline, Equation of continuity, Volume flux, Turbulent flow, Critical speed
Teaches concepts: concept:equation_of_continuity (Equation of Continuity), concept:steady_flow (Steady Flow), concept:streamline (Streamline), concept:turbulent_flow (Turbulent Flow), concept:volume_flux (Volume Flux)
Requires concepts: concept:conservation_of_mass (Conservation of Mass), concept:fluid_density (Fluid Density)
title: Streamline Flow

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: Bernoulli's Principle
Summary: This section introduces Bernoulli's principle, which represents the conservation of energy for steady, incompressible, and non-viscous fluid flow. It derives Bernoulli's equation relating fluid pressure, kinetic energy density, and potential energy density, and outlines its physical limitations.
Key terms: Bernoulli's principle, Bernoulli's equation, conservation of energy, incompressible fluid, streamline, viscosity
Teaches concepts: concept:bernoullis_equation (Bernoulli's Equation), concept:bernoullis_principle (Bernoulli's Principle), concept:limitations_of_bernoullis_equation (Limitations of Bernoulli's Equation)
Requires concepts: concept:fluid_pressure (Fluid Pressure), concept:work_energy_theorem (Work-Energy Theorem)
title: Bernoulli's Principle

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: Speed of Efflux: Torricelli's Law
Summary: This section defines efflux as fluid outflow and derives the formula for the speed of efflux from a tank using Bernoulli's equation and the equation of continuity. It presents Torricelli's law, which states that the speed of efflux from an open tank equals the speed of a body falling freely through the same height.
Key terms: Efflux, Torricelli's law, Speed of efflux, Bernoulli's equation, Equation of continuity
Teaches concepts: concept:efflux (Efflux), concept:speed_of_efflux (Speed of Efflux), concept:torricellis_law (Torricelli's Law)
Requires concepts: concept:bernoullis_equation (Bernoulli's Equation), concept:equation_of_continuity (Equation of Continuity), concept:equations_of_motion_for_free_fall (Equations of Motion for Free Fall)
title: Speed of Efflux: Torricelli's Law

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: Dynamic Lift
Summary: This section explains dynamic lift, which is the force exerted on an object due to its motion through a fluid. It uses Bernoulli's principle to analyze the pressure differences that generate lift on spinning balls (the Magnus effect) and aircraft wings (aerofoils).
Key terms: Dynamic lift, Magnus effect, Aerofoil, Bernoulli's principle, Streamlines, Pressure difference
Teaches concepts: concept:aerofoil (Aerofoil), concept:dynamic_lift (Dynamic Lift), concept:magnus_effect (Magnus Effect)
Requires concepts: concept:bernoullis_principle (Bernoulli's Principle), concept:streamlines (Streamlines)
title: Dynamic Lift

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: Viscosity
Summary: This section introduces viscosity as the internal friction of fluids and explains laminar flow using the concept of strain rate. It defines the coefficient of viscosity, details its temperature dependence, and provides a practical method for its measurement.
Key terms: viscosity, laminar flow, strain rate, coefficient of viscosity, poiseuille
Teaches concepts: concept:coefficient_of_viscosity (Coefficient of Viscosity), concept:laminar_flow (Laminar Flow), concept:strain_rate (Strain Rate), concept:temperature_dependence_of_viscosity (Temperature Dependence of Viscosity), concept:viscosity (Viscosity)
Requires concepts: concept:shear_strain (Shear Strain), concept:shear_stress (Shear Stress)
title: Viscosity

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: Stokes' Law
Summary: This section introduces Stokes' law, which defines the viscous drag force acting on a spherical body moving through a fluid. It derives the expression for terminal velocity when gravitational, buoyant, and viscous forces reach equilibrium, and demonstrates its application.
Key terms: Stokes' law, viscous drag, terminal velocity, viscosity, buoyant force
Teaches concepts: concept:stokes_law (Stokes' Law), concept:terminal_velocity (Terminal Velocity)
Requires concepts: concept:buoyant_force (Buoyant Force), concept:newtons_laws_of_motion (Newton's Laws of Motion), concept:viscosity (Viscosity)
title: Stokes' Law

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: SURFACE TENSION
Summary: This section introduces surface tension and surface energy as molecular phenomena arising from unbalanced attractive forces on liquid surface molecules. It derives the relationship between surface tension, contact angle, and interfacial equilibrium, and explains physical consequences such as excess pressure in drops/bubbles and capillary action.
Key terms: surface tension, surface energy, angle of contact, excess pressure, capillary rise, capillary depression
Teaches concepts: concept:angle_of_contact (Angle of Contact), concept:capillary_action (Capillary Action), concept:excess_pressure (Excess Pressure), concept:surface_energy (Surface Energy), concept:surface_tension (Surface Tension)
Requires concepts: concept:hydrostatic_pressure (Hydrostatic Pressure), concept:work_and_mechanical_energy (Work and Mechanical Energy)
title: SURFACE TENSION

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 11
Chapter: ncert:physics:11:9
Section: Exercises
Summary: This section contains exercises and illustrations applying key fluid mechanics principles. It focuses on the application of the equation of continuity, surface tension, and pressure differences in drops and bubbles using horizontal pipes and wire frames.
Key terms: Equation of continuity, Surface tension, Pressure difference, Manometer, Liquid film
Teaches concepts: concept:equation_of_continuity_application (Equation of Continuity Application), concept:pressure_difference_in_drops_and_bubbles (Pressure Difference in Drops and Bubbles), concept:surface_tension_application (Surface Tension Application)
Requires concepts: concept:bernoullis_principle (Bernoulli's Principle), concept:equation_of_continuity (Equation of Continuity), concept:excess_pressure_in_drops_and_bubbles (Excess Pressure in Drops and Bubbles), concept:surface_tension (Surface Tension)
title: Exercises

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:10
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section outlines the historical evolution of the theories of light, starting from the corpuscular model proposed by Descartes and Newton to Huygens' wave theory, which was validated by Foucault's and Young's experiments. It also defines geometrical optics as a limiting case of wave optics for small wavelengths and introduces Maxwell's electromagnetic theory of light.
Key terms: corpuscular model, wave theory of light, geometrical optics, electromagnetic waves, interference
Teaches concepts: concept:corpuscular_model_of_light (Corpuscular Model of Light), concept:electromagnetic_theory_of_light (Electromagnetic Theory of Light), concept:geometrical_optics (Geometrical Optics), concept:wave_theory_of_light (Wave Theory of Light)
Requires concepts: concept:refraction_of_light (Refraction of Light), concept:snells_law (Snell's Law)
title: Introduction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:10
Section: Huygens Principle
Summary: This section introduces the concept of a wavefront as a surface of constant phase and classifies wavefronts into spherical and plane types. It explains Huygens' principle, which uses secondary wavelets to geometrically construct the future position of a wavefront, and addresses the physical absence of the backwave.
Key terms: Wavefront, Spherical wave, Plane wave, Huygens principle, Secondary wavelets, Backwave
Teaches concepts: concept:backwave_shortcoming (Backwave Shortcoming), concept:huygens_principle (Huygens' Principle), concept:plane_wavefront (Plane Wavefront), concept:secondary_wavelets (Secondary Wavelets), concept:spherical_wavefront (Spherical Wavefront), concept:wavefront (Wavefront)
Requires concepts: concept:phase_of_a_wave (Phase of a Wave), concept:wave_propagation (Wave Propagation)
title: Huygens Principle

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:10
Section: Refraction and Reflection of Plane Waves using Huygens Principle
Summary: This section derives the laws of reflection and refraction, including Snell's law, using Huygens' principle of wave propagation. It also explains how wavefronts are modified by optical elements like lenses, mirrors, and prisms, and establishes the constancy of wave frequency across media.
Key terms: Huygens' principle, wavefront, refraction, reflection, Snell's law, refractive index, critical angle, principle of equal time
Teaches concepts: concept:constancy_of_wave_frequency_in_refraction (Constancy of Wave Frequency in Refraction), concept:principle_of_equal_time (Principle of Equal Time), concept:reflection_of_plane_waves_using_huygens_principle (Reflection of Plane Waves using Huygens' Principle), concept:refraction_of_plane_waves_using_huygens_principle (Refraction of Plane Waves using Huygens' Principle), concept:wavefronts_through_optical_elements (Wavefronts through Optical Elements)
Requires concepts: concept:huygens_principle (Huygens' Principle), concept:refractive_index (Refractive Index), concept:wavefront (Wavefront)
title: Refraction and Reflection of Plane Waves using Huygens Principle

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:10
Section: Coherent and Incoherent Addition of Waves
Summary: This section explains the superposition principle and its application to the coherent and incoherent addition of waves. It derives the conditions for constructive and destructive interference based on path and phase differences, and discusses the resulting intensity distributions.
Key terms: Superposition principle, Coherent sources, Constructive interference, Destructive interference, Path difference, Phase difference, Incoherent sources
Teaches concepts: concept:coherent_sources (Coherent Sources), concept:constructive_interference (Constructive Interference), concept:destructive_interference (Destructive Interference), concept:incoherent_addition_of_waves (Incoherent Addition of Waves), concept:resultant_intensity_of_interfering_waves (Resultant Intensity of Interfering Waves), concept:superposition_principle (Superposition Principle)
Requires concepts: concept:path_difference (Path Difference), concept:phase_and_phase_difference (Phase and Phase Difference), concept:wave_displacement (Wave Displacement)
title: Coherent and Incoherent Addition of Waves

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:10
Section: Interference of Light Waves and Young’s Experiment
Summary: This section explains the challenge of obtaining coherent light sources and introduces Young's double-slit technique to overcome it. It derives the mathematical conditions for constructive and destructive interference, which produce alternating bright and dark fringes on a screen.
Key terms: Coherent sources, Incoherent sources, Young's double-slit experiment, Path difference, Bright fringes, Dark fringes, Interference fringes
Teaches concepts: concept:bright_fringes (Bright Fringes), concept:coherent_sources (Coherent Sources), concept:dark_fringes (Dark Fringes), concept:interference_fringes (Interference Fringes), concept:youngs_double_slit_experiment (Young's Double Slit Experiment)
Requires concepts: concept:path_difference (Path Difference), concept:wave_interference (Wave Interference)
title: Interference of Light Waves and Young’s Experiment

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:10
Section: Diffraction
Summary: This section introduces the phenomenon of wave diffraction, focusing on the single-slit diffraction pattern. It derives the conditions for diffraction minima and maxima, compares diffraction with interference, and explains how energy conservation is maintained through light redistribution.
Key terms: Diffraction, Single slit, Central maximum, Secondary maxima, Minima, Energy redistribution
Teaches concepts: concept:diffraction (Diffraction), concept:diffraction_minima_and_maxima_conditions (Diffraction Minima and Maxima Conditions), concept:energy_conservation_in_wave_optics (Energy Conservation in Wave Optics), concept:interference_vs_diffraction (Interference vs. Diffraction), concept:single_slit_diffraction (Single Slit Diffraction)
Requires concepts: concept:interference_of_light (Interference of Light), concept:monochromatic_light (Monochromatic Light), concept:wavefront (Wavefront)
title: Diffraction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:10
Section: Polarisation
Summary: This section introduces the concept of polarisation in transverse waves, focusing on light. It explains the distinction between unpolarised and plane polarised waves, details the function of polaroids, and derives Malus' Law to describe the intensity of transmitted light.
Key terms: transverse wave, linearly polarised wave, unpolarised wave, polaroid, pass-axis, Malus' law, crossed polaroids
Teaches concepts: concept:linearly_polarised_wave (Linearly Polarised Wave), concept:malus_law (Malus' Law), concept:pass_axis (Pass-axis), concept:polaroid (Polaroid), concept:unpolarised_wave (Unpolarised Wave)
Requires concepts: concept:electromagnetic_wave (Electromagnetic Wave), concept:transverse_wave (Transverse Wave)
title: Polarisation

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:11
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section introduces the historical discoveries of the late 19th and early 20th centuries that shaped modern physics, focusing on cathode rays, the discovery of the electron, and the determination of its specific charge and quantized elementary charge.
Key terms: cathode rays, specific charge, electron, charge quantization, Millikan's oil-drop experiment
Teaches concepts: concept:cathode_rays (Cathode Rays), concept:charge_quantization (Charge Quantization), concept:electron (Electron), concept:specific_charge_of_electron (Specific Charge of Electron)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_and_magnetic_fields (Electric and Magnetic Fields), concept:electric_charge (Electric Charge)
title: Introduction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:11
Section: Electron Emission
Summary: This section introduces the concept of electron emission from metal surfaces, explaining the surface barrier that prevents spontaneous escape. It defines the work function and the electron volt as key physical quantities, and outlines three primary methods of emission: thermionic, field, and photoelectric emission.
Key terms: surface barrier, work function, electron volt, thermionic emission, field emission, photoelectric emission
Teaches concepts: concept:electron_volt (Electron Volt), concept:field_emission (Field Emission), concept:photoelectric_emission (Photoelectric Emission), concept:surface_barrier (Surface Barrier), concept:thermionic_emission (Thermionic Emission), concept:work_function (Work Function)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_potential (Electric Potential), concept:free_electrons_in_metals (Free Electrons in Metals)
title: Electron Emission

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:11
Section: Photoelectric Effect
Summary: This section introduces the photoelectric effect through the historical observations of Hertz, Hallwachs, and Lenard. It explains how light incident on photosensitive metal surfaces causes the emission of electrons, a phenomenon that only occurs above a characteristic threshold frequency.
Key terms: Photoelectric effect, Photoelectrons, Threshold frequency, Photosensitive substances, Hertz's observations, Hallwachs' and Lenard's observations
Teaches concepts: concept:photoelectric_effect (Photoelectric Effect), concept:photoelectrons (Photoelectrons), concept:photosensitive_substances (Photosensitive Substances), concept:threshold_frequency (Threshold Frequency)
Requires concepts: concept:electromagnetic_spectrum (Electromagnetic Spectrum), concept:electron (Electron)
title: Photoelectric Effect

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:11
Section: Experimental Study of Photoelectric Effect
Summary: This section describes the experimental arrangement used to study the photoelectric effect and details how photocurrent and stopping potential depend on the intensity, frequency, and potential of incident light. Key findings include the linear relationship between intensity and photocurrent, the existence of a threshold frequency, and the instantaneous nature of electron emission.
Key terms: photoelectric effect, photocurrent, saturation current, stopping potential, threshold frequency, maximum kinetic energy, instantaneous emission
Teaches concepts: concept:instantaneous_photoelectric_emission (Instantaneous Photoelectric Emission), concept:maximum_kinetic_energy_of_photoelectrons (Maximum Kinetic Energy of Photoelectrons), concept:photoelectric_experimental_setup (Photoelectric Experimental Setup), concept:saturation_current (Saturation Current), concept:stopping_potential (Stopping Potential), concept:threshold_frequency (Threshold Frequency)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_potential_and_kinetic_energy (Electric Potential and Kinetic Energy), concept:photoelectric_effect (Photoelectric Effect), concept:wave_properties_of_light (Wave Properties of Light)
title: Experimental Study of Photoelectric Effect

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:11
Section: Photoelectric Effect and Wave Theory of Light
Summary: This section contrasts the wave theory of light with the experimental observations of the photoelectric effect. It explains the predictions made by the wave picture of light and details why it fails to account for key photoelectric phenomena, such as the threshold frequency and instantaneous electron emission.
Key terms: Wave theory of light, Photoelectric effect, Continuous energy absorption, Threshold frequency, Instantaneous emission
Teaches concepts: concept:failure_of_wave_theory_of_light (Failure of Wave Theory of Light), concept:wave_picture_of_light (Wave Picture of Light)
Requires concepts: concept:electromagnetic_waves (Electromagnetic Waves), concept:photoelectric_effect (Photoelectric Effect), concept:work_function (Work Function)
title: Photoelectric Effect and Wave Theory of Light

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:11
Section: Einstein’s Photoelectric Equation: Energy Quantum of Radiation
Summary: This section introduces Einstein's quantum picture of radiation, where electromagnetic energy is treated as discrete quanta called photons. It presents Einstein's photoelectric equation, which successfully explains the linear relationship between stopping potential and frequency, the existence of a threshold frequency, and the instantaneous nature of electron emission, all of which were experimentally verified by Millikan.
Key terms: quantum of energy, photon, Einstein's photoelectric equation, threshold frequency, stopping potential, Planck's constant, Millikan's experiments
Teaches concepts: concept:einsteins_photoelectric_equation (Einstein's Photoelectric Equation), concept:einsteins_quantum_picture_of_radiation (Einstein's Quantum Picture of Radiation), concept:linear_relation_between_stopping_potential_and_frequency (Linear Relation between Stopping Potential and Frequency), concept:millikans_experimental_verification (Millikan's Experimental Verification), concept:threshold_frequency_in_photoelectric_emission (Threshold Frequency in Photoelectric Emission)
Requires concepts: concept:photoelectric_effect (Photoelectric Effect), concept:stopping_potential (Stopping Potential), concept:work_function (Work Function)
title: Einstein’s Photoelectric Equation: Energy Quantum of Radiation

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:11
Section: Particle Nature of Light: The Photon
Summary: This section introduces the particle nature of light by defining the photon as a discrete quantum of energy and momentum. It details the fundamental properties of photons, such as their speed, neutrality, and conservation in collisions, and provides practical calculations for photon energy, emission rate, and photoelectric parameters.
Key terms: photon, photon energy, photon momentum, Compton scattering, Einstein's photoelectric equation, photon emission rate
Teaches concepts: concept:einsteins_photoelectric_equation (Einstein's Photoelectric Equation), concept:photon (Photon), concept:photon_emission_rate (Photon Emission Rate), concept:photon_energy (Photon Energy), concept:photon_momentum (Photon Momentum)
Requires concepts: concept:photoelectric_effect (Photoelectric Effect), concept:stopping_potential (Stopping Potential), concept:work_function (Work Function)
title: Particle Nature of Light: The Photon

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:11
Section: Wave Nature of Matter
Summary: This section introduces the wave nature of matter, starting with the dual nature of radiation and de Broglie's hypothesis that moving particles exhibit wave-like properties. It defines the de Broglie relation and explains why wave properties are observable in sub-atomic particles but negligible in macroscopic objects.
Key terms: dual nature, de Broglie hypothesis, de Broglie relation, de Broglie wavelength, Planck's constant, momentum
Teaches concepts: concept:de_broglie_hypothesis (de Broglie Hypothesis), concept:de_broglie_relation (de Broglie Relation), concept:de_broglie_wavelength_of_a_photon (de Broglie Wavelength of a Photon), concept:dual_nature_of_radiation (Dual Nature of Radiation), concept:wave_nature_of_macroscopic_vs_sub_atomic_objects (Wave Nature of Macroscopic vs Sub-atomic Objects)
Requires concepts: concept:momentum (Momentum), concept:plancks_constant (Planck's Constant)

Subject: physics

Section: Alpha-particle trajectory

Section: Electron orbits
Summary: This section analyzes the dynamics of electron orbits in a hydrogen atom using the Rutherford nuclear model. It derives the relationship between orbital radius and velocity, and calculates the kinetic, potential, and total energy of the orbiting electron, demonstrating that its negative total energy signifies a bound state.
Key terms: Rutherford nuclear model, electrostatic force, centripetal force, orbital radius, kinetic energy, potential energy, total energy, bound state

Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:12
Section: Atomic Spectra
Summary: This section introduces atomic spectra, detailing how excited atomic gases emit specific wavelengths to form emission line spectra. It also explains absorption spectra, which occur when white light passing through a gas produces dark lines at those same characteristic wavelengths.

text:

Section: Bohr Model of the Hydrogen Atom
Summary: This section introduces the Bohr model of the hydrogen atom, addressing the stability and spectral limitations of Rutherford's classical model. It details Bohr's three postulates regarding stationary states, angular momentum quantization, and radiative transitions, deriving the radius and energy levels of the electron's orbits.
Key terms: Rutherford's model, Bohr's postulates, stationary states, quantization of angular momentum, Bohr radius, energy levels, electron volt
Teaches concepts: concept:bohr_orbit_radius_and_energy_levels (Bohr Orbit Radius and Energy Levels), concept:bohrs_first_postulate (Bohr's First Postulate), concept:bohrs_second_postulate (Bohr's Second Postulate), concept:bohrs_third_postulate (Bohr's Third Postulate), concept:limitations_of_rutherfords_model (Limitations of Rutherford's Model)

text:

Grade: 12

Section: Energy levels
Summary: This section defines the energy levels of the hydrogen atom, focusing on the ground state, excited states, and ionization energy. It explains how electron transitions between these states result in energy absorption or photon emission, and introduces the energy level diagram.

Section: The Line Spectra of the Hydrogen Atom
Summary: This section explains the origin of emission and absorption line spectra of the hydrogen atom using Bohr's third postulate. It describes how transitions between discrete energy levels result in the emission or absorption of photons of specific frequencies.

Summary: This section explains how Louis de Broglie's wave-particle duality provides a theoretical basis for Bohr's second postulate of angular momentum quantization. text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12

Section: Size of the Nucleus
Summary: This section explains how the size of an atomic nucleus is determined using Rutherford's scattering experiments and introduces the empirical formula for nuclear radius. It demonstrates that nuclear density is constant across all nuclei and is extremely high compared to ordinary matter.

Section: Nuclear Force
Summary: This section introduces the nuclear force, which binds protons and neutrons within the atomic nucleus by overcoming electrostatic repulsion. It details key properties of this force, including its short-range nature, charge independence, and the potential energy relationship between nucleons as a function of distance.
Key terms: nuclear force, Coulomb repulsion, short-range force, saturation of forces, potential energy minimum, charge independence
