Key terms: electric dipole, dipole moment, axial field, equatorial field, point dipole, superposition principle
Teaches concepts: concept:dipole_field_distance_dependence (Dipole Field Distance Dependence), concept:electric_dipole_field_on_the_axis (Electric Dipole Field on the Axis), concept:electric_dipole_field_on_the_equatorial_plane (Electric Dipole Field on the Equatorial Plane), concept:electric_dipole_moment (Electric Dipole Moment), concept:point_dipole (Point Dipole)
Requires concepts: concept:coulombs_law (Coulomb's Law), concept:electric_dipole (Electric Dipole), concept:superposition_principle (Superposition Principle)
title: The field of an electric dipole

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Physical significance of dipoles
Summary: This section explains the physical significance of electric dipoles by distinguishing between polar and non-polar molecules based on their charge centers. It defines the center of charge and provides a practical example calculating the electric field of a dipole at axial and equatorial points.
Key terms: polar molecules, non-polar molecules, induced dipole moment, center of charge, electric dipole moment
Teaches concepts: concept:center_of_charge (Center of Charge), concept:induced_dipole_moment (Induced Dipole Moment), concept:non_polar_molecules (Non-polar Molecules), concept:polar_molecules (Polar Molecules)
Requires concepts: concept:center_of_mass (Center of Mass), concept:electric_dipole (Electric Dipole), concept:electric_field (Electric Field)
title: Physical significance of dipoles

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Dipole in a Uniform External Field
Summary: This section analyzes the behavior of an electric dipole when placed in both uniform and non-uniform external electric fields. In a uniform field, a dipole experiences zero net force but a net torque that tends to align it with the field. In a non-uniform field, the dipole experiences a net force, which explains phenomena such as the attraction of neutral paper by a charged comb.
Key terms: electric dipole, torque, uniform electric field, non-uniform electric field, polarization, dipole moment
Teaches concepts: concept:dipole_in_a_non_uniform_electric_field (Dipole in a Non-uniform Electric Field), concept:net_force_on_a_dipole_in_a_uniform_field (Net Force on a Dipole in a Uniform Field), concept:polarization_of_neutral_objects (Polarization of Neutral Objects), concept:torque_on_an_electric_dipole (Torque on an Electric Dipole)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_dipole (Electric Dipole), concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:torque_and_couple (Torque and Couple), concept:vector_cross_product (Vector Cross Product)
title: Dipole in a Uniform External Field

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Continuous Charge Distribution
Summary: This section introduces continuous charge distributions as a macroscopic approximation that simplifies calculations for large systems. It defines linear, surface, and volume charge densities, and derives the total electric field produced by such distributions using integration.
Key terms: continuous charge distribution, surface charge density, linear charge density, volume charge density, electric field, superposition principle
Teaches concepts: concept:continuous_charge_distribution (Continuous Charge Distribution), concept:electric_field_of_a_continuous_charge_distribution (Electric Field of a Continuous Charge Distribution), concept:linear_charge_density (Linear Charge Density), concept:surface_charge_density (Surface Charge Density), concept:volume_charge_density (Volume Charge Density)
Requires concepts: concept:coulombs_law (Coulomb's Law), concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:superposition_principle (Superposition Principle)
title: Continuous Charge Distribution

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Gauss's Law
Summary: This section introduces Gauss's law, which relates the net electric flux through a closed surface to the total enclosed charge. It provides a derivation for a point charge, defines the concept of a Gaussian surface, and discusses how symmetry simplifies electrostatic field calculations.
Key terms: Gauss's law, Gaussian surface, electric flux, enclosed charge, permittivity of free space, symmetry
Teaches concepts: concept:derivation_of_gausss_law_for_a_point_charge (Derivation of Gauss's Law for a Point Charge), concept:gaussian_surface (Gaussian Surface), concept:gausss_law (Gauss's Law), concept:symmetric_field_calculations (Symmetric Field Calculations)
Requires concepts: concept:coulombs_law (Coulomb's Law), concept:electric_flux (Electric Flux)
title: Gauss's Law

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Applications of Gauss's Law
Summary: This section explores the practical applications of Gauss's law to calculate the electric field due to symmetric charge distributions. It specifically derives the electric fields for an infinitely long straight wire, an infinite plane sheet, and a thin spherical shell.
Key terms: Gauss's law, Gaussian surface, linear charge density, surface charge density, spherical shell
Teaches concepts: concept:electric_field_due_to_a_uniformly_charged_infinite_plane_sheet (Electric field due to a uniformly charged infinite plane sheet), concept:electric_field_due_to_a_uniformly_charged_thin_spherical_shell (Electric field due to a uniformly charged thin spherical shell), concept:electric_field_due_to_an_infinitely_long_straight_uniformly_charged_wire (Electric field due to an infinitely long straight uniformly charged wire)
Requires concepts: concept:charge_density (Charge Density), concept:electric_flux (Electric Flux), concept:gausss_law (Gauss's Law)
title: Applications of Gauss's Law

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Field due to an infinitely long straight uniformly charged wire
Summary: This section derives the electric field of an infinitely long, uniformly charged straight wire using Gauss's law. By constructing a cylindrical Gaussian surface, the electric field is shown to be radial and inversely proportional to the distance from the wire.
Key terms: linear charge density, cylindrical Gaussian surface, Gauss's law, radial electric field, symmetry
Teaches concepts: concept:cylindrical_gaussian_surface (Cylindrical Gaussian surface), concept:electric_field_of_an_infinitely_long_charged_wire (Electric field of an infinitely long charged wire), concept:linear_charge_density (Linear Charge Density)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:electric_flux (Electric Flux), concept:gausss_law (Gauss's Law)
title: Field due to an infinitely long straight uniformly charged wire

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Field due to a uniformly charged infinite plane sheet
Summary: This section derives the electric field due to a uniformly charged infinite plane sheet using Gauss's law. It shows that the electric field is independent of the distance from the sheet and calculates the net flux through a rectangular parallelepiped Gaussian surface.
Key terms: Gaussian surface, electric flux, Gauss's law, infinite plane sheet, electric field
Teaches concepts: concept:electric_field_of_an_infinite_plane_sheet (Electric field of an infinite plane sheet), concept:gaussian_surface_for_a_plane_sheet (Gaussian surface for a plane sheet)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:electric_flux (Electric Flux), concept:gausss_law (Gauss's Law)
title: Field due to a uniformly charged infinite plane sheet

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Field due to a uniformly charged thin spherical shell
Summary: This section derives the electric field due to a uniformly charged thin spherical shell both inside and outside the shell using Gauss's law. It demonstrates that the field is zero inside the shell and behaves as a point charge field outside, and applies these principles to calculate the field of a simplified atomic model.
Key terms: spherical shell, Gaussian surface, spherical symmetry, surface charge density, Gauss's law
Teaches concepts: concept:electric_field_inside_a_charged_spherical_shell (Electric field inside a charged spherical shell), concept:electric_field_of_a_uniform_spherical_charge_distribution (Electric field of a uniform spherical charge distribution), concept:electric_field_outside_a_charged_spherical_shell (Electric field outside a charged spherical shell), concept:spherical_gaussian_surface (Spherical Gaussian Surface)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_flux (Electric Flux), concept:gausss_law (Gauss's Law), concept:surface_charge_density (Surface Charge Density)
title: Field due to a uniformly charged thin spherical shell

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Exercises Illustrations
Summary: This section provides visual illustrations for chapter exercises, depicting the deflection of charged particles in a uniform electric field and a point charge configuration above a square surface. These diagrams help students analyze particle trajectories and calculate electric flux using symmetry.
Key terms: charged particle deflection, uniform electric field, electric flux, symmetry, point charge
Teaches concepts: concept:deflection_of_charged_particles_in_a_uniform_electric_field (Deflection of charged particles in a uniform electric field), concept:electric_flux_calculation_using_symmetry (Electric flux calculation using symmetry)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:electric_flux (Electric Flux), concept:gausss_law (Gauss's Law)
title: Exercises Illustrations

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Electric Charge
Summary: This section introduces the fundamental concept of electric charge, detailing its historical discovery and experimental observations of attraction and repulsion. It explains the classification of charges into positive and negative, the operation of a gold-leaf electroscope, and the microscopic origin of charging via electron transfer.
Key terms: electric charge, polarity of charge, gold-leaf electroscope, electron transfer, electrification
Teaches concepts: concept:electric_charge (Electric Charge), concept:gold_leaf_electroscope (Gold-leaf Electroscope), concept:microscopic_origin_of_charging (Microscopic Origin of Charging), concept:polarity_of_charge (Polarity of Charge)
Requires concepts: concept:atom (Atom), concept:electron (Electron)
title: Electric Charge

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Conductors and Insulators
Summary: This section classifies materials into conductors, insulators, and semiconductors based on their electrical conductivity. It explains how electric charge distributes across the surface of conductors but remains localized on insulators, and introduces the electroscope as a charge detection device.
Key terms: Conductors, Insulators, Semiconductors, Charge distribution, Electroscope
Teaches concepts: concept:charge_distribution_on_conductors (Charge Distribution on Conductors), concept:charge_localization_on_insulators (Charge Localization on Insulators), concept:conductors (Conductors), concept:electroscope (Electroscope), concept:insulators (Insulators), concept:semiconductors (Semiconductors)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_charge (Electric Charge)
title: Conductors and Insulators

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Basic Properties of Electric Charge
Summary: This section introduces the fundamental properties of electric charge, focusing on its additivity, conservation, and quantisation. It defines point charges, establishes the coulomb as the SI unit of charge, and explains the difference between macroscopic and microscopic quantisation.
Key terms: point charges, additivity of charge, conservation of charge, quantisation of charge, coulomb, elementary charge
Teaches concepts: concept:additivity_of_electric_charge (Additivity of Electric Charge), concept:conservation_of_electric_charge (Conservation of Electric Charge), concept:coulomb (Coulomb), concept:point_charges (Point Charges), concept:quantisation_of_electric_charge (Quantisation of Electric Charge)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_charge (Electric Charge), concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:mole_concept (Mole Concept)
title: Basic Properties of Electric Charge

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Coulomb's Law
Summary: This section introduces Coulomb's law, which quantifies the electrostatic force between two point charges as being proportional to the product of their charges and inversely proportional to the square of their separation. It defines the SI unit of charge (the coulomb), introduces the permittivity of free space, and expresses the law in vector form to show consistency with Newton's third law.
Key terms: Coulomb's law, point charges, torsion balance, coulomb, permittivity of free space, vector notation
Teaches concepts: concept:coulomb_unit_of_charge (Coulomb (Unit of Charge)), concept:coulombs_law (Coulomb's Law), concept:permittivity_of_free_space (Permittivity of Free Space), concept:point_charge (Point Charge), concept:vector_form_of_coulombs_law (Vector Form of Coulomb's Law)
Requires concepts: concept:newtons_law_of_universal_gravitation (Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation), concept:newtons_third_law_of_motion (Newton's Third Law of Motion), concept:vector_algebra (Vector Algebra)
title: Coulomb's Law

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Forces between Multiple Charges
Summary: This section introduces the principle of superposition to calculate the net electrostatic force acting on a charge due to multiple other charges. It explains how the total force is the vector sum of individual pairwise Coulomb forces, generalizing the formulation to a system of n charges and demonstrating applications with symmetric triangular charge configurations.
Key terms: Principle of Superposition, Coulomb's Law, Vector Sum, Parallelogram Law, Electrostatic Force, Multiple Charges
Teaches concepts: concept:principle_of_superposition_in_electrostatics (Principle of Superposition in Electrostatics), concept:superposition_of_forces_for_n_charges (Superposition of Forces for n Charges), concept:superposition_of_forces_for_three_charges (Superposition of Forces for Three Charges)
Requires concepts: concept:coulombs_law (Coulomb's Law), concept:vector_addition (Vector Addition)
title: Forces between Multiple Charges

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Electric Field
Summary: This section introduces the concept of an electric field to explain action-at-a-distance between charges. It defines the electric field mathematically, relates it to electrostatic force, distinguishes between source and test charges, and details its physical properties.
Key terms: Electric Field, Source Charge, Test Charge, Electrostatic Force, Spherical Symmetry
Teaches concepts: concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:electrostatic_force_in_electric_field (Electrostatic Force in Electric Field), concept:properties_of_electric_field (Properties of Electric Field), concept:source_charge_and_test_charge (Source Charge and Test Charge)
Requires concepts: concept:coulombs_law (Coulomb's Law), concept:vector_fields (Vector Fields)
title: Electric Field

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Electric field due to a system of charges
Summary: This section explains how to determine the total electric field at a point due to a system of multiple point charges. It introduces the superposition principle for electric fields, showing that the total field is the vector sum of the individual fields.
Key terms: Superposition Principle, Electric Field, Vector Sum, System of Charges, Coulomb's Law
Teaches concepts: concept:electric_field_of_a_system_of_charges (Electric Field of a System of Charges), concept:superposition_principle_for_electric_fields (Superposition Principle for Electric Fields)
Requires concepts: concept:coulombs_law (Coulomb's Law), concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:vector_addition (Vector Addition)
title: Electric field due to a system of charges

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Physical significance of electric field
Summary: This section explains the physical significance of the electric field, highlighting its utility in electrostatics and its essential role as an independent physical entity in time-dependent electromagnetic phenomena. It also demonstrates practical applications through the motion of charged particles in uniform fields and the calculation of net electric fields using the superposition principle.
Key terms: electric field, electromagnetic waves, time delay, superposition principle, uniform electric field, vector field
Teaches concepts: concept:electric_field_as_a_vector_field (Electric Field as a Vector Field), concept:electromagnetic_field_in_time_dependent_phenomena (Electromagnetic Field in Time-Dependent Phenomena), concept:motion_of_charged_particles_in_a_uniform_electric_field (Motion of Charged Particles in a Uniform Electric Field), concept:superposition_principle_for_electric_fields (Superposition Principle for Electric Fields)
Requires concepts: concept:coulombs_law (Coulomb's Law), concept:newtons_laws_of_motion (Newton's Laws of Motion), concept:vector_addition (Vector Addition)
title: Physical significance of electric field

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Electric Field Lines
Summary: This section introduces electric field lines as a visual tool to represent the direction and magnitude of electric fields. It defines field lines, explains how their density relates to field strength using solid angles, and details their fundamental physical properties.
Key terms: Electric field lines, Field line density, Solid angle, Inverse square law, Electric dipole, Michael Faraday
Teaches concepts: concept:electric_field_line_density (Electric Field Line Density), concept:electric_field_lines (Electric Field Lines), concept:properties_of_electric_field_lines (Properties of Electric Field Lines), concept:solid_angle_in_inverse_square_law (Solid Angle in Inverse Square Law)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_dipole (Electric Dipole), concept:electric_field_vector (Electric Field Vector), concept:inverse_square_law (Inverse Square Law)
title: Electric Field Lines

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:1
Section: Electric Flux
Summary: This section introduces the concept of electric flux as a measure of the number of electric field lines passing through a surface. It defines the area vector, establishes the mathematical formulation of electric flux, and explains how to calculate total flux through a surface.
Key terms: electric flux, area vector, outward normal, electric field lines, flux integration
Teaches concepts: concept:area_vector (Area Vector), concept:electric_flux (Electric Flux), concept:total_electric_flux (Total Electric Flux)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:vector_dot_product (Vector Dot Product)
title: Electric Flux

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section introduces electrostatic potential energy by drawing an analogy with gravitational potential energy and conservative forces. It defines the work done in moving a charge within an electrostatic field and establishes the concepts of electrostatic potential energy difference and potential energy at a point.
Key terms: conservative force, electrostatic potential energy, potential energy difference, work done, path independence
Teaches concepts: concept:conservative_force (Conservative Force), concept:electrostatic_potential_energy_at_a_point (Electrostatic Potential Energy at a Point), concept:electrostatic_potential_energy_difference (Electrostatic Potential Energy Difference), concept:work_done_in_an_electrostatic_field (Work Done in an Electrostatic Field)
Requires concepts: concept:coulombs_law (Coulomb's Law), concept:gravitational_potential_energy (Gravitational Potential Energy), concept:work_energy_theorem (Work-Energy Theorem)
title: Introduction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Dielectrics and Polarisation
Summary: This section explains the behavior of dielectrics in an external electric field, contrasting them with conductors. It defines polar and non-polar molecules, introduces the concept of polarization and electric susceptibility, and describes how induced surface charges reduce the net electric field inside a dielectric.
Key terms: dielectric, polar molecule, non-polar molecule, polarisation, electric susceptibility, induced surface charge, bound charges
Teaches concepts: concept:dielectric_material (Dielectric Material), concept:dielectric_polarisation (Dielectric Polarisation), concept:electric_susceptibility (Electric Susceptibility), concept:induced_surface_charge (Induced Surface Charge), concept:polar_and_non_polar_molecules (Polar and Non-polar Molecules)
Requires concepts: concept:conductors_in_electrostatics (Conductors in Electrostatics), concept:electric_dipole_moment (Electric Dipole Moment), concept:electric_field (Electric Field)
title: Dielectrics and Polarisation

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Capacitors and Capacitance
Summary: This section introduces the capacitor as a system of two conductors separated by an insulator, used to store charge. It defines capacitance as the ratio of charge to potential difference, introduces the farad as its SI unit, and explains how dielectric strength limits a capacitor's maximum charge storage.
Key terms: Capacitor, Capacitance, Farad, Dielectric strength, Electric breakdown
Teaches concepts: concept:capacitance (Capacitance), concept:capacitor (Capacitor), concept:dielectric_strength (Dielectric Strength), concept:farad (Farad)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:electric_potential_difference (Electric Potential Difference)
title: Capacitors and Capacitance

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: The Parallel Plate Capacitor
Summary: This section introduces the parallel plate capacitor, deriving its internal and external electric fields. It establishes the formula for its capacitance based on plate geometry and discusses edge effects like the fringing of the field.
Key terms: Parallel plate capacitor, Capacitance, Electric field, Fringing of the field, Surface charge density
Teaches concepts: concept:capacitance_of_a_parallel_plate_capacitor (Capacitance of a Parallel Plate Capacitor), concept:electric_field_of_a_parallel_plate_capacitor (Electric Field of a Parallel Plate Capacitor), concept:fringing_of_the_field (Fringing of the Field), concept:parallel_plate_capacitor (Parallel Plate Capacitor)
Requires concepts: concept:capacitance (Capacitance), concept:electric_field_of_an_infinite_plane_sheet (Electric field of an infinite plane sheet), concept:relation_between_electric_field_and_potential (Relation between Electric Field and Potential)
title: The Parallel Plate Capacitor

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Effect of Dielectric on Capacitance
Summary: This section explains how inserting a dielectric material between the plates of a capacitor increases its capacitance. It derives the relationship between dielectric polarization, the dielectric constant, and the resulting electric field and potential difference. The concept is further illustrated through the calculation of capacitance when a dielectric slab of partial thickness is inserted.
Key terms: Capacitance, Dielectric Constant, Dielectric Polarization, Permittivity, Parallel Plate Capacitor
Teaches concepts: concept:capacitance_with_dielectric (Capacitance with Dielectric), concept:capacitance_with_vacuum (Capacitance with Vacuum), concept:dielectric_constant (Dielectric Constant), concept:dielectric_polarization_in_capacitors (Dielectric Polarization in Capacitors), concept:permittivity_of_a_medium (Permittivity of a Medium)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_polarization (Electric Polarization), concept:parallel_plate_capacitor (Parallel Plate Capacitor), concept:relation_between_electric_field_and_potential (Relation between Electric Field and Potential)
title: Effect of Dielectric on Capacitance

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Combination of Capacitors
Summary: This section explains how multiple capacitors can be combined in series and parallel configurations to achieve a desired effective capacitance. It derives the mathematical formulas for the equivalent capacitance of both arrangements and demonstrates their application in analyzing complex capacitor networks.
Key terms: effective capacitance, series combination, parallel combination, equivalent capacitance, charge distribution, potential drop
Teaches concepts: concept:effective_capacitance (Effective Capacitance), concept:parallel_combination_of_capacitors (Parallel Combination of Capacitors), concept:series_combination_of_capacitors (Series Combination of Capacitors)
Requires concepts: concept:capacitance (Capacitance), concept:electric_charge (Electric Charge), concept:electric_potential_difference (Electric Potential Difference)
title: Combination of Capacitors

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Energy Stored in a Capacitor
Summary: This section explains how energy is stored in a capacitor during the charging process and derives the formulas for electrostatic potential energy and energy density in the electric field. It also analyzes the redistribution of charge and the resulting energy loss when a charged capacitor is connected to an uncharged one.
Key terms: Electrostatic potential energy, Capacitor charging, Energy density, Electric field energy, Charge redistribution, Energy dissipation
Teaches concepts: concept:charge_redistribution_in_capacitors (Charge Redistribution in Capacitors), concept:electrostatic_potential_energy_of_a_capacitor (Electrostatic Potential Energy of a Capacitor), concept:energy_density_of_the_electric_field (Energy Density of the Electric Field), concept:energy_loss_in_capacitor_sharing (Energy Loss in Capacitor Sharing)
Requires concepts: concept:capacitance (Capacitance), concept:electric_field_of_a_parallel_plate_capacitor (Electric Field of a Parallel Plate Capacitor), concept:electric_potential_and_work_done (Electric Potential and Work Done)
title: Energy Stored in a Capacitor

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Electrostatic Potential
Summary: This section defines electrostatic potential and potential difference. It explains how potential is defined as work done per unit test charge, making it independent of the test charge's magnitude.
Key terms: electrostatic potential, potential difference, test charge, work done
Teaches concepts: concept:electrostatic_potential_at_a_point (Electrostatic Potential at a Point), concept:electrostatic_potential_difference (Electrostatic Potential Difference)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_charge (Electric Charge), concept:electrostatic_potential_energy (Electrostatic Potential Energy), concept:work_done_by_a_force (Work Done by a Force)
title: Electrostatic Potential

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Potential due to a Point Charge
Summary: This section derives the electrostatic potential due to a point charge by calculating the work done in bringing a unit positive test charge from infinity. It establishes the formula $V(r) = Q / (4\pi\epsilon_0 r)$, discusses the sign of the potential, and compares its $1/r$ variation with the $1/r^2$ variation of the electric field.
Key terms: electrostatic potential, point charge, work done, radial path, potential at infinity, electric field variation
Teaches concepts: concept:comparison_of_potential_and_field_variation (Comparison of Potential and Field Variation), concept:electrostatic_potential_of_a_point_charge (Electrostatic Potential of a Point Charge), concept:path_independence_of_electrostatic_work (Path Independence of Electrostatic Work), concept:potential_at_infinity (Potential at Infinity)
Requires concepts: concept:coulombs_law (Coulomb's Law), concept:definition_of_electrostatic_potential (Definition of Electrostatic Potential), concept:electric_field_of_a_point_charge (Electric Field of a Point Charge)
title: Potential due to a Point Charge

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Potential due to an Electric Dipole
Summary: This section derives and analyzes the electrostatic potential due to an electric dipole. It demonstrates that the potential depends on both distance and angle, falling off as $1/r^2$ at large distances, and contrasts this behavior with that of a single point charge.
Key terms: electric dipole, dipole moment, electrostatic potential, superposition principle, axial potential, equatorial plane, point dipole
Teaches concepts: concept:dipole_axis_potential (Dipole Axis Potential), concept:electric_dipole_potential (Electric Dipole Potential), concept:equatorial_plane_potential_of_a_dipole (Equatorial Plane Potential of a Dipole), concept:point_dipole (Point Dipole)
Requires concepts: concept:binomial_approximation (Binomial Approximation), concept:electric_dipole (Electric Dipole), concept:electrostatic_potential_of_a_point_charge (Electrostatic Potential of a Point Charge), concept:superposition_principle (Superposition Principle)
title: Potential due to an Electric Dipole

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Potential due to a System of Charges
Summary: This section explains how to calculate the total electrostatic potential due to multiple charges using the superposition principle, as well as for continuous charge distributions and uniformly charged spherical shells. It also provides practical examples for finding points of zero potential and analyzing the signs of potential differences, work, and energy changes.
Key terms: superposition principle, electrostatic potential, continuous charge distribution, spherical shell, potential difference, work done
Teaches concepts: concept:electrostatic_potential_difference_and_work_done (Electrostatic Potential Difference and Work Done), concept:net_zero_potential_points (Net Zero Potential Points), concept:potential_due_to_continuous_charge_distributions (Potential due to Continuous Charge Distributions), concept:potential_of_a_uniformly_charged_spherical_shell (Potential of a Uniformly Charged Spherical Shell), concept:superposition_principle_for_electrostatic_potential (Superposition Principle for Electrostatic Potential)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_field_of_a_spherical_shell (Electric Field of a Spherical Shell), concept:electrostatic_potential_of_a_point_charge (Electrostatic Potential of a Point Charge), concept:work_and_electrostatic_potential_energy (Work and Electrostatic Potential Energy)
title: Potential due to a System of Charges

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Equipotential Surfaces
Summary: This section defines equipotential surfaces and describes their geometry for point charges, uniform fields, and other charge configurations. It proves that the electric field is always perpendicular to these surfaces and derives the mathematical relation between electric field magnitude and potential gradient.
Key terms: equipotential surface, electric field, electric potential, potential gradient, concentric spheres
Teaches concepts: concept:equipotential_surface (Equipotential Surface), concept:equipotential_surfaces_in_a_uniform_electric_field (Equipotential Surfaces in a Uniform Electric Field), concept:equipotential_surfaces_of_a_point_charge (Equipotential Surfaces of a Point Charge), concept:normalcy_of_electric_field_to_equipotential_surfaces (Normalcy of Electric Field to Equipotential Surfaces), concept:relation_between_electric_field_and_potential (Relation between Electric Field and Potential)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:electric_potential (Electric Potential), concept:work_done_in_an_electric_field (Work Done in an Electric Field)

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Potential Energy of a System of Charges
Summary: This section defines and derives the electrostatic potential energy of a system of point charges as the work required to assemble them from infinity. It covers systems of two, three, and multiple charges, explaining how the sign of the potential energy depends on the nature of the charges.
Key terms: electrostatic potential energy, conservative force, work done, system of charges, like charges, unlike charges
Teaches concepts: concept:electrostatic_potential_energy_of_a_system_of_charges (Electrostatic Potential Energy of a System of Charges), concept:potential_energy_of_a_system_of_three_charges (Potential Energy of a System of Three Charges), concept:potential_energy_of_two_charges (Potential Energy of Two Charges), concept:sign_and_nature_of_electrostatic_potential_energy (Sign and Nature of Electrostatic Potential Energy)
Requires concepts: concept:conservative_force (Conservative Force), concept:coulombs_law (Coulomb's Law), concept:electrostatic_potential (Electrostatic Potential)
title: Potential Energy of a System of Charges

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Potential Energy in an External Field
Summary: This section explains how to determine the potential energy of charges and electric dipoles when placed in an external electric field. It covers the potential energy of a single charge, a system of two charges including their mutual interaction, and the torque-induced potential energy of a dipole.
Key terms: potential energy, external electric field, electrostatic potential, electric dipole, dipole moment, torque
Teaches concepts: concept:potential_energy_of_a_single_charge_in_an_external_field (Potential Energy of a Single Charge in an External Field), concept:potential_energy_of_a_system_of_two_charges_in_an_external_field (Potential Energy of a System of Two Charges in an External Field), concept:potential_energy_of_an_electric_dipole_in_an_external_field (Potential Energy of an Electric Dipole in an External Field)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_dipole (Electric Dipole), concept:electric_potential (Electric Potential), concept:electrostatic_potential_energy_of_a_system_of_charges (Electrostatic Potential Energy of a System of Charges)
title: Potential Energy in an External Field

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:2
Section: Electrostatics of Conductors
Summary: This section details the electrostatic behavior of conductors, outlining key properties such as zero internal electric field, normal surface electric field, and constant potential. It also explains the derivation of the surface electric field and the phenomenon of electrostatic shielding in cavities.
Key terms: conductors, charge carriers, electrostatic shielding, surface charge density, Gaussian surface
Teaches concepts: concept:charge_carriers_in_conductors (Charge Carriers in Conductors), concept:electrostatic_properties_of_conductors (Electrostatic Properties of Conductors), concept:electrostatic_shielding (Electrostatic Shielding), concept:equipotential_nature_of_conductors (Equipotential Nature of Conductors), concept:surface_electric_field_of_a_conductor (Surface Electric Field of a Conductor)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:electrostatic_potential (Electrostatic Potential), concept:gausss_law (Gauss's Law)
title: Electrostatics of Conductors

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section introduces the concept of electric current, defined as charges in motion. It contrasts transient currents like lightning with the steady currents that power everyday devices.
Key terms: electric current, charges in motion, steady current, electrostatics
Teaches concepts: concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:steady_electric_current (Steady Electric Current)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_charge (Electric Charge), concept:electrostatics (Electrostatics)
title: Introduction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Cells, EMF, Internal Resistance
Summary: This section introduces the electrolytic cell as a device to maintain steady current, defining its electromotive force (emf) and internal resistance. It derives the relationship between terminal potential difference, emf, and internal resistance, and calculates the current in a simple circuit.
Key terms: electrolytic cell, electromotive force, internal resistance, terminal potential difference, short circuit
Teaches concepts: concept:electrolytic_cell (Electrolytic Cell), concept:electromotive_force (Electromotive Force), concept:internal_resistance (Internal Resistance), concept:maximum_current_from_a_cell (Maximum Current from a Cell), concept:terminal_potential_difference (Terminal Potential Difference)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:electric_potential_difference (Electric Potential Difference), concept:ohms_law (Ohm's Law)
title: Cells, EMF, Internal Resistance

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Cells in Series and in Parallel
Summary: This section explains how to combine multiple electrochemical cells in series and parallel configurations. It derives the formulas for the equivalent electromotive force (emf) and equivalent internal resistance for both types of combinations.
Key terms: cells in series, cells in parallel, equivalent emf, equivalent internal resistance
Teaches concepts: concept:cells_in_parallel (Cells in Parallel), concept:cells_in_series (Cells in Series)
Requires concepts: concept:electromotive_force (Electromotive Force), concept:internal_resistance (Internal Resistance), concept:kirchhoffs_junction_rule (Kirchhoff's Junction Rule)
title: Cells in Series and in Parallel

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Kirchhoff's Rules
Summary: This section demonstrates the practical application of Kirchhoff's rules to solve complex electrical networks. It covers the simplification of symmetric resistor networks, such as a cube, and details the systematic multi-loop analysis of general circuits using simultaneous equations.
Key terms: Kirchhoff's first rule, Kirchhoff's second rule, Network symmetry, Equivalent resistance, Multi-loop network, Branch currents
Teaches concepts: concept:junction_rule_application (Junction Rule Application), concept:loop_rule_application (Loop Rule Application), concept:multi_loop_network_analysis (Multi-loop Network Analysis), concept:network_symmetry_analysis (Network Symmetry Analysis)
Requires concepts: concept:kirchhoffs_rules (Kirchhoff's Rules), concept:ohms_law (Ohm's Law), concept:series_and_parallel_resistor_combinations (Series and Parallel Resistor Combinations)
title: Kirchhoff's Rules

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Wheatstone Bridge
Summary: This section introduces the Wheatstone bridge, a circuit consisting of four resistors used to determine an unknown resistance. It derives the balanced condition where no current flows through the galvanometer and demonstrates how to analyze both balanced and unbalanced bridge circuits using Kirchhoff's rules.
Key terms: Wheatstone bridge, battery arm, galvanometer arm, balanced condition, null deflection, unknown resistance, meter bridge
Teaches concepts: concept:balanced_wheatstone_bridge_condition (Balanced Wheatstone Bridge Condition), concept:determination_of_unknown_resistance (Determination of Unknown Resistance), concept:unbalanced_wheatstone_bridge_analysis (Unbalanced Wheatstone Bridge Analysis), concept:wheatstone_bridge_structure (Wheatstone Bridge Structure)
Requires concepts: concept:galvanometer (Galvanometer), concept:kirchhoffs_rules (Kirchhoff's Rules), concept:ohms_law_and_electrical_resistance (Ohm's Law and Electrical Resistance)
title: Wheatstone Bridge

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Electric Current
Summary: This section defines electric current as the rate of flow of electric charge across a given area. It introduces the mathematical formulations for both steady current and time-varying instantaneous current, and establishes the ampere as the SI unit of current.
Key terms: electric current, steady current, instantaneous current, ampere, net charge
Teaches concepts: concept:ampere (Ampere), concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:instantaneous_current (Instantaneous Current)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_charge (Electric Charge), concept:limit_calculus (Limit (Calculus))
title: Electric Current

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Electric Currents in Conductors
Summary: This section explains the mechanism of electric currents in conductors, focusing on the nature of charge carriers in different materials. It describes how thermal motion results in zero net current in the absence of an electric field, and how an external electric field drives a current, which requires a battery or cell to remain steady.
Key terms: charge carriers, thermal motion, transient current, steady current, conductors
Teaches concepts: concept:charge_carriers (Charge Carriers), concept:steady_current_maintenance (Steady Current Maintenance), concept:thermal_motion_in_conductors (Thermal Motion in Conductors), concept:transient_current (Transient Current)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_charge (Electric Charge), concept:electric_field (Electric Field)
title: Electric Currents in Conductors

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Ohm's Law
Summary: This section introduces Ohm's law, which states that the electric current flowing through a conductor is directly proportional to the potential difference across its ends. It defines electrical resistance, derives its dependence on the conductor's physical dimensions to introduce resistivity, and establishes the vector form of Ohm's law using current density and conductivity.
Key terms: Ohm's law, resistance, resistivity, current density, conductivity
Teaches concepts: concept:current_density (Current Density), concept:electrical_conductivity (Electrical Conductivity), concept:electrical_resistance (Electrical Resistance), concept:ohms_law (Ohm's Law), concept:resistivity (Resistivity), concept:vector_form_of_ohms_law (Vector Form of Ohm's Law)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:potential_difference (Potential Difference)
title: Ohm's Law

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Drift of Electrons and the Origin of Resistivity
Summary: This section explains the microscopic mechanism of electrical conduction in metals, introducing the concepts of drift velocity and relaxation time. It derives the mathematical relationship between electric current and drift velocity, leading to a microscopic derivation of Ohm's law and electrical conductivity.
Key terms: drift velocity, relaxation time, current density, conductivity, electron collision
Teaches concepts: concept:drift_velocity (Drift Velocity), concept:microscopic_electrical_conductivity (Microscopic Electrical Conductivity), concept:microscopic_ohms_law (Microscopic Ohm's Law), concept:relation_between_current_and_drift_velocity (Relation between Current and Drift Velocity), concept:relaxation_time (Relaxation Time)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:ohms_law (Ohm's Law)
title: Drift of Electrons and the Origin of Resistivity

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Mobility
Summary: This section defines mobility as the magnitude of drift velocity per unit electric field and derives its relation to relaxation time. It also specifies the SI units of mobility and identifies the charge carriers in different materials.
Key terms: Mobility, Drift velocity, Electric field, Relaxation time, Charge carriers
Teaches concepts: concept:mobility (Mobility), concept:mobility_in_terms_of_relaxation_time (Mobility in Terms of Relaxation Time), concept:si_unit_of_mobility (SI Unit of Mobility)
Requires concepts: concept:drift_velocity (Drift Velocity), concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:relaxation_time (Relaxation Time)
title: Mobility

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Limitations of Ohm's Law
Summary: This section examines the limitations of Ohm's law, detailing how the linear relationship between voltage and current fails in various materials and devices. It highlights deviations from linearity at high currents, directional dependence of current in diodes, and non-unique V-I relationships including negative resistance in materials like Gallium Arsenide.
Key terms: Ohm's law limitations, non-linear V-I characteristics, directional dependence, negative resistance, Gallium Arsenide
Teaches concepts: concept:deviations_from_linearity (Deviations from Linearity), concept:directional_dependence_of_current (Directional Dependence of Current), concept:negative_resistance (Negative Resistance), concept:non_unique_v_i_relationship (Non-unique V-I Relationship)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:ohms_law (Ohm's Law), concept:voltage (Voltage)
title: Limitations of Ohm's Law

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Resistivity of Various Materials
Summary: This section classifies materials into conductors, insulators, and semiconductors based on their electrical resistivity. It compares their characteristic resistivity ranges and highlights how semiconductor resistivity decreases with temperature and impurity addition.
Key terms: Electrical resistivity, Conductors, Insulators, Semiconductors, Temperature dependence
Teaches concepts: concept:conductors (Conductors), concept:insulators (Insulators), concept:semiconductors (Semiconductors)
Requires concepts: concept:electrical_resistivity (Electrical Resistivity)
title: Resistivity of Various Materials

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Temperature Dependence of Resistivity
Summary: This section examines how the electrical resistivity of different materials, including metals, alloys, and semiconductors, varies with temperature. It introduces the temperature coefficient of resistivity and explains these variations microscopically using electron relaxation time and carrier density.
Key terms: Resistivity, Temperature coefficient of resistivity, Alloys, Semiconductors, Relaxation time, Number density
Teaches concepts: concept:microscopic_origin_of_resistivity_temperature_dependence (Microscopic Origin of Resistivity Temperature Dependence), concept:temperature_coefficient_of_resistivity (Temperature Coefficient of Resistivity), concept:temperature_dependence_of_resistivity_in_alloys (Temperature Dependence of Resistivity in Alloys), concept:temperature_dependence_of_resistivity_in_metals (Temperature Dependence of Resistivity in Metals), concept:temperature_dependence_of_resistivity_in_semiconductors (Temperature Dependence of Resistivity in Semiconductors)
Requires concepts: concept:electrical_resistivity (Electrical Resistivity), concept:ohms_law_and_electrical_resistance (Ohm's Law and Electrical Resistance), concept:relaxation_time_and_free_electron_model (Relaxation Time and Free Electron Model)
title: Temperature Dependence of Resistivity

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:3
Section: Electrical Energy, Power
Summary: This section explains the potential energy changes of charges moving through a conductor and how collisions lead to energy dissipation as heat, known as Joule heating. It defines electric power and ohmic loss, and demonstrates how transmitting power at high voltages minimizes energy waste in transmission lines.
Key terms: Potential Energy, Joule Heating, Electric Power, Ohmic Loss, Power Transmission
Teaches concepts: concept:electric_power_dissipation (Electric Power Dissipation), concept:joule_heating (Joule Heating), concept:potential_energy_of_charges_in_a_conductor (Potential Energy of Charges in a Conductor), concept:power_transmission_loss (Power Transmission Loss)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:electric_potential (Electric Potential), concept:ohms_law (Ohm's Law)
title: Electrical Energy, Power

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:4
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section introduces the connection between electricity and magnetism through Oersted's discovery that electric currents produce magnetic fields. It also establishes the standard dot and cross notation conventions for representing three-dimensional field directions on a two-dimensional plane.
Key terms: Oersted's discovery, magnetic field, electric current, concentric circles, dot and cross notation, electromagnetism
Teaches concepts: concept:dot_and_cross_notation (Dot and Cross Notation), concept:magnetic_field_of_a_straight_current_carrying_wire (Magnetic Field of a Straight Current-Carrying Wire), concept:oersteds_discovery (Oersted's Discovery)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:magnetic_field (Magnetic Field)
title: Introduction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:4
Section: The Moving Coil Galvanometer
Summary: This section introduces the Moving Coil Galvanometer (MCG), explaining its construction, radial magnetic field, and torque balance principle. It details how the MCG is converted into an ammeter using a parallel shunt resistance or into a voltmeter using a series resistance, and defines current and voltage sensitivities.
Key terms: Moving Coil Galvanometer, radial magnetic field, torsional constant, shunt resistance, current sensitivity, voltage sensitivity
Teaches concepts: concept:conversion_of_galvanometer_to_ammeter (Conversion of Galvanometer to Ammeter), concept:conversion_of_galvanometer_to_voltmeter (Conversion of Galvanometer to Voltmeter), concept:current_sensitivity (Current Sensitivity), concept:moving_coil_galvanometer (Moving Coil Galvanometer), concept:torque_balance_in_mcg (Torque Balance in MCG), concept:voltage_sensitivity (Voltage Sensitivity)
Requires concepts: concept:magnetic_torque_on_a_current_loop (Magnetic Torque on a Current Loop), concept:ohms_law (Ohm's Law), concept:series_and_parallel_resistors (Series and Parallel Resistors)
title: The Moving Coil Galvanometer

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:4
Section: Magnetic Force
Summary: This section introduces the concept of magnetic fields produced by moving charges and defines the Lorentz force acting on a charge in the presence of electric and magnetic fields. It details the features of magnetic force, defines its SI unit (tesla), and derives the magnetic force acting on a current-carrying conductor.
Key terms: magnetic field, Lorentz force, magnetic force, tesla, right-hand rule, current-carrying conductor
Teaches concepts: concept:lorentz_force (Lorentz Force), concept:magnetic_field (Magnetic Field), concept:magnetic_force_on_a_current_carrying_conductor (Magnetic Force on a Current-Carrying Conductor), concept:magnetic_force_on_a_moving_charge (Magnetic Force on a Moving Charge), concept:tesla (Tesla)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:electric_field (Electric Field), concept:vector_cross_product (Vector Cross Product)
title: Magnetic Force

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:4
Section: Motion in a Magnetic Field
Summary: This section analyzes the motion of a charged particle in a uniform magnetic field. It explains why the magnetic force does no work, derives the radius and frequency of circular motion when velocity is perpendicular to the field, and describes helical motion when there is a parallel velocity component.
Key terms: magnetic force, centripetal force, circular motion, helical motion, pitch, cyclotron frequency
Teaches concepts: concept:circular_motion_in_a_magnetic_field (Circular Motion in a Magnetic Field), concept:cyclotron_frequency (Cyclotron Frequency), concept:helical_motion (Helical Motion), concept:pitch_of_helix (Pitch of Helix), concept:work_done_by_magnetic_force (Work Done by Magnetic Force)
Requires concepts: concept:centripetal_force (Centripetal Force), concept:magnetic_force_on_a_charge (Magnetic Force on a Charge), concept:work_and_kinetic_energy (Work and Kinetic Energy)
title: Motion in a Magnetic Field

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:4
Section: Magnetic Field due to a Current Element, Biot-Savart Law
Summary: This section introduces the Biot-Savart law, which mathematically describes the magnetic field generated by an infinitesimal current element. It defines the permeability of free space, compares magnetic fields with electrostatic fields, and establishes the fundamental relationship between electromagnetic constants and the speed of light.
Key terms: Biot-Savart law, current element, permeability of free space, Right Hand Screw rule, Coulomb's law, speed of light
Teaches concepts: concept:biot_savart_law (Biot-Savart Law), concept:comparison_of_biot_savart_law_and_coulombs_law (Comparison of Biot-Savart Law and Coulomb's Law), concept:permeability_of_free_space (Permeability of Free Space), concept:relation_between_electromagnetic_constants_and_speed_of_light (Relation between Electromagnetic Constants and Speed of Light), concept:right_hand_screw_rule (Right Hand Screw Rule)
Requires concepts: concept:coulombs_law (Coulomb's Law), concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:vector_cross_product (Vector Cross Product)
title: Magnetic Field due to a Current Element, Biot-Savart Law

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:4
Section: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop
Summary: This section derives the magnetic field along the axis of a circular current-carrying loop using the Biot-Savart law. It also explores special cases, including the magnetic field at the center of the loop, the field of a multi-turn coil, and the right-hand thumb rule for determining field direction.
Key terms: circular current loop, axial magnetic field, Biot-Savart law, right-hand thumb rule, multi-turn coil, semi-circular arc
Teaches concepts: concept:magnetic_field_at_the_center_of_a_circular_loop (Magnetic Field at the Center of a Circular Loop), concept:magnetic_field_of_a_multi_turn_coil (Magnetic Field of a Multi-turn Coil), concept:magnetic_field_of_a_semi_circular_arc (Magnetic Field of a Semi-circular Arc), concept:magnetic_field_on_the_axis_of_a_circular_loop (Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Loop), concept:right_hand_thumb_rule_for_current_loops (Right-Hand Thumb Rule for Current Loops)
Requires concepts: concept:biot_savart_law (Biot-Savart Law), concept:vector_integration (Vector Integration)
title: Magnetic Field on the Axis of a Circular Current Loop

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:4
Section: Ampere’s Circuital Law
Summary: This section introduces Ampere's Circuital Law as an alternative formulation of the Biot-Savart law for calculating magnetic fields in highly symmetric systems. It defines the mathematical statement of the law, the concept of an Amperian loop, and applies it to derive the magnetic fields of infinite straight wires and thick wires.
Key terms: Ampere's Circuital Law, Amperian loop, line integral, right-hand rule, cylindrical symmetry, steady current
Teaches concepts: concept:amperes_circuital_law (Ampere's Circuital Law), concept:amperian_loop (Amperian Loop), concept:magnetic_field_of_a_thick_wire (Magnetic Field of a Thick Wire), concept:magnetic_field_of_an_infinite_straight_wire (Magnetic Field of an Infinite Straight Wire), concept:right_hand_rule_for_straight_wires (Right-Hand Rule for Straight Wires)
Requires concepts: concept:biot_savart_law (Biot-Savart Law), concept:gausss_law (Gauss's Law), concept:magnetic_field (Magnetic Field)
title: Ampere’s Circuital Law

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:4
Section: The Solenoid
Summary: This section defines a solenoid as a helical coil of wire and describes its magnetic field pattern. It derives the formula for the uniform magnetic field inside a very long solenoid using Ampere's circuital law and demonstrates its calculation.
Key terms: Solenoid, Helix, Amperian loop, Ampere's circuital law, Uniform magnetic field
Teaches concepts: concept:long_solenoid_approximation (Long Solenoid Approximation), concept:magnetic_field_of_a_finite_solenoid (Magnetic Field of a Finite Solenoid), concept:magnetic_field_of_an_ideal_solenoid (Magnetic Field of an Ideal Solenoid), concept:solenoid (Solenoid)
Requires concepts: concept:amperes_circuital_law (Ampere's Circuital Law), concept:magnetic_field_of_a_circular_loop (Magnetic Field of a Circular Loop), concept:right_hand_rule (Right-hand Rule)
title: The Solenoid

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:4
Section: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere
Summary: This section derives the magnetic force between two parallel current-carrying conductors, showing that parallel currents attract and antiparallel currents repel. It uses this force to define the SI base unit of current, the ampere, and subsequently defines the coulomb in terms of the ampere.
Key terms: parallel currents, magnetic force, ampere, coulomb, attraction, repulsion, current balance
Teaches concepts: concept:attraction_and_repulsion_of_currents (Attraction and Repulsion of Currents), concept:definition_of_the_ampere (Definition of the Ampere), concept:definition_of_the_coulomb (Definition of the Coulomb), concept:magnetic_force_between_parallel_conductors (Magnetic Force between Parallel Conductors)
Requires concepts: concept:biot_savart_law (Biot-Savart Law), concept:lorentz_force_formula (Lorentz Force Formula), concept:newtons_third_law_of_motion (Newton's Third Law of Motion)
title: Force Between Two Parallel Currents, The Ampere

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:4
Section: Torque on Current Loop, Magnetic Dipole
Summary: This section derives the torque on a current-carrying loop in a uniform magnetic field and defines the magnetic dipole moment. It establishes a mathematical analogy between magnetic and electric dipoles, analyzing their equilibrium states and noting the non-existence of magnetic monopoles.
Key terms: torque, rectangular current loop, magnetic dipole moment, stable equilibrium, electric dipole analogy, magnetic monopole
Teaches concepts: concept:circular_current_loop_as_a_magnetic_dipole (Circular Current Loop as a Magnetic Dipole), concept:equilibrium_of_a_magnetic_dipole (Equilibrium of a Magnetic Dipole), concept:magnetic_dipole_moment (Magnetic Dipole Moment), concept:magnetic_monopoles (Magnetic Monopoles), concept:torque_on_a_current_loop (Torque on a Current Loop)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_dipole (Electric Dipole), concept:magnetic_field_of_a_circular_loop (Magnetic Field of a Circular Loop), concept:torque (Torque)
title: Torque on Current Loop, Magnetic Dipole

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:5
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section introduces the history and universal nature of magnetism, highlighting its connection to moving charges. It also outlines the fundamental properties of magnets, such as pole alignment, attraction and repulsion, and the non-existence of isolated magnetic monopoles.
Key terms: magnetism, bar magnet, magnetic poles, magnetic monopoles, Earth's magnetic field
Teaches concepts: concept:fundamental_properties_of_magnets (Fundamental Properties of Magnets), concept:magnetism (Magnetism), concept:non_existence_of_magnetic_monopoles (Non-existence of Magnetic Monopoles)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:magnetic_field (Magnetic Field)
title: Introduction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:5
Section: The Bar Magnet
Summary: This section explores the physical properties of a bar magnet, visualizing its magnetic field lines and establishing its equivalence to a current-carrying solenoid. It also analyzes the behavior of a magnetic dipole in a uniform magnetic field, including torque and potential energy, and highlights the mathematical analogy between electrostatic and magnetic dipoles.
Key terms: bar magnet, magnetic field lines, solenoid, magnetic dipole, magnetic moment, torque, magnetic potential energy, electrostatic analog
Teaches concepts: concept:bar_magnet_as_an_equivalent_solenoid (Bar Magnet as an Equivalent Solenoid), concept:electrostatic_magnetic_dipole_analogy (Electrostatic-Magnetic Dipole Analogy), concept:magnetic_dipole_in_a_uniform_magnetic_field (Magnetic Dipole in a Uniform Magnetic Field), concept:magnetic_field_lines (Magnetic Field Lines), concept:magnetic_potential_energy (Magnetic Potential Energy)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_dipole (Electric Dipole), concept:solenoid (Solenoid), concept:torque (Torque)
title: The Bar Magnet

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:5
Section: Magnetism and Gauss's Law
Summary: This section introduces Gauss's law for magnetism, which states that the net magnetic flux through any closed surface is always zero. This law mathematically reflects the physical absence of isolated magnetic monopoles, meaning magnetic field lines must always form continuous, closed loops.
Key terms: Gauss's law for magnetism, magnetic flux, magnetic monopoles, closed loops, electrostatic flux
Teaches concepts: concept:absence_of_magnetic_monopoles (Absence of Magnetic Monopoles), concept:gausss_law_for_magnetism (Gauss's Law for Magnetism), concept:hypothetical_monopole_modification_of_gausss_law (Hypothetical Monopole Modification of Gauss's Law), concept:magnetic_flux (Magnetic Flux), concept:properties_of_magnetic_field_lines (Properties of Magnetic Field Lines)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_charge (Electric Charge), concept:gausss_law_for_electrostatics (Gauss's Law for Electrostatics), concept:magnetic_field (Magnetic Field)
title: Magnetism and Gauss's Law

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:5
Section: Magnetisation and Magnetic Intensity
Summary: This section introduces the concepts of magnetisation and magnetic intensity to describe how materials respond to external magnetic fields. It defines key parameters including magnetic susceptibility, relative magnetic permeability, and total magnetic permeability, demonstrating their application in a solenoid with a material core.
Key terms: Magnetisation, Magnetic Intensity, Magnetic Susceptibility, Relative Magnetic Permeability, Magnetic Permeability
Teaches concepts: concept:magnetic_intensity (Magnetic Intensity), concept:magnetic_permeability (Magnetic Permeability), concept:magnetic_susceptibility (Magnetic Susceptibility), concept:magnetisation (Magnetisation), concept:relative_magnetic_permeability (Relative Magnetic Permeability)
Requires concepts: concept:magnetic_field_of_a_solenoid (Magnetic Field of a Solenoid), concept:magnetic_moment (Magnetic Moment), concept:permeability_of_vacuum (Permeability of Vacuum)
title: Magnetisation and Magnetic Intensity

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:5
Section: Magnetic Properties of Materials
Summary: This section classifies magnetic materials into diamagnetic, paramagnetic, and ferromagnetic categories based on their magnetic susceptibility and permeability. It defines the quantitative ranges of these properties for each material class.
Key terms: diamagnetic, paramagnetic, ferromagnetic, magnetic susceptibility, relative permeability
Teaches concepts: concept:diamagnetic_materials (Diamagnetic Materials), concept:ferromagnetic_materials (Ferromagnetic Materials), concept:paramagnetic_materials (Paramagnetic Materials)
Requires concepts: concept:magnetic_susceptibility (Magnetic Susceptibility), concept:relative_permeability (Relative Permeability)
title: Magnetic Properties of Materials

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:5
Section: Diamagnetism
Summary: This section defines diamagnetism, explaining how diamagnetic substances are repelled by external magnetic fields due to induced atomic magnetic moments opposing the field. It details the underlying mechanism of electron orbital motion, lists common examples, and introduces superconductors and the Meissner effect as cases of perfect diamagnetism.
Key terms: Diamagnetism, Orbital magnetic moment, Meissner effect, Superconductors, Magnetic susceptibility, Relative permeability
Teaches concepts: concept:diamagnetism (Diamagnetism), concept:meissner_effect (Meissner Effect), concept:orbital_magnetic_moment (Orbital Magnetic Moment), concept:superconductors (Superconductors)
Requires concepts: concept:lenzs_law (Lenz's Law), concept:magnetic_susceptibility (Magnetic Susceptibility), concept:relative_permeability (Relative Permeability)
title: Diamagnetism

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:5
Section: Paramagnetism
Summary: This section defines paramagnetic substances as materials that become weakly magnetised in an external magnetic field and are weakly attracted to magnets. It explains their atomic origin due to permanent atomic magnetic dipoles and describes how their magnetisation depends on temperature and external field strength, eventually reaching saturation.
Key terms: Paramagnetic substances, permanent magnetic dipole moment, thermal motion, magnetic susceptibility, relative permeability, magnetic saturation
Teaches concepts: concept:atomic_origin_of_paramagnetism (Atomic Origin of Paramagnetism), concept:magnetic_saturation_in_paramagnets (Magnetic Saturation in Paramagnets), concept:paramagnetic_substances (Paramagnetic Substances), concept:temperature_dependence_of_paramagnetism (Temperature Dependence of Paramagnetism)
Requires concepts: concept:magnetic_dipole_moment (Magnetic Dipole Moment), concept:magnetic_susceptibility (Magnetic Susceptibility), concept:relative_permeability (Relative Permeability)
title: Paramagnetism

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:5
Section: Ferromagnetism
Summary: This section introduces ferromagnetism, explaining how ferromagnetic substances get strongly magnetised in external fields. It details the domain theory of ferromagnetism, the distinction between hard and soft magnetic materials, and the temperature dependence of these properties.
Key terms: Ferromagnetism, Ferromagnetic substances, Domains, Hard magnetic materials, Soft magnetic materials, Relative magnetic permeability
Teaches concepts: concept:domain_theory_of_ferromagnetism (Domain Theory of Ferromagnetism), concept:ferromagnetic_substances (Ferromagnetic Substances), concept:hard_magnetic_materials (Hard Magnetic Materials), concept:soft_magnetic_materials (Soft Magnetic Materials), concept:temperature_dependence_of_ferromagnetism (Temperature Dependence of Ferromagnetism)
Requires concepts: concept:magnetic_dipole_moment (Magnetic Dipole Moment), concept:paramagnetism (Paramagnetism), concept:relative_magnetic_permeability (Relative Magnetic Permeability)
title: Ferromagnetism

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:6
Section: Exercises
Summary: This section provides practical exercises applying Lenz's law and Faraday's law to determine the direction of induced currents. It features visual scenarios involving moving magnets, changing loop shapes, and varying currents to reinforce the core principles of electromagnetic induction.
Key terms: Lenz's law, Faraday's law, induced current, magnetic flux, electromagnetic induction
Teaches concepts: concept:application_of_lenzs_law (Application of Lenz's Law), concept:electromagnetic_induction_in_loops_and_solenoids (Electromagnetic Induction in Loops and Solenoids)
Requires concepts: concept:faradays_law_of_electromagnetic_induction (Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction), concept:lenzs_law (Lenz's Law), concept:magnetic_flux (Magnetic Flux)
title: Exercises

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:6
Section: Introduction
Summary: This section introduces the historical context and definition of electromagnetic induction, highlighting how changing magnetic fields generate electric currents. It traces the connection between electricity and magnetism from Oersted's discoveries to the pioneering experiments of Faraday and Henry.
Key terms: electromagnetic induction, magnetic field, electric current, Faraday, Oersted
Teaches concepts: concept:electromagnetic_induction (Electromagnetic Induction), concept:electromagnetism_connection (Electromagnetism Connection)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:magnetic_field (Magnetic Field)
title: Introduction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:6
Section: The Experiments of Faraday and Henry
Summary: This section describes the foundational experiments of Michael Faraday and Joseph Henry that led to the discovery of electromagnetic induction. It demonstrates that electric currents are induced in a coil either through relative motion with a magnet or current-carrying coil, or by varying the current in a stationary neighboring coil.
Key terms: electromagnetic induction, galvanometer, relative motion, magnetic coupling, tapping key
Teaches concepts: concept:electromagnetic_induction_via_relative_motion (Electromagnetic Induction via Relative Motion), concept:induction_by_changing_current (Induction by Changing Current), concept:magnetic_coupling_enhancement (Magnetic Coupling Enhancement)
Requires concepts: concept:electric_current (Electric Current), concept:galvanometer (Galvanometer), concept:magnetic_field (Magnetic Field)
title: The Experiments of Faraday and Henry

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:6
Section: Magnetic Flux
Summary: This section introduces the concept of magnetic flux, defining it mathematically for both uniform and non-uniform magnetic fields. It also establishes magnetic flux as a scalar quantity and defines its SI unit, the weber.
Key terms: Magnetic flux, Area vector, Weber, Scalar quantity, Uniform magnetic field, Non-uniform magnetic field
Teaches concepts: concept:magnetic_flux (Magnetic Flux), concept:magnetic_flux_in_non_uniform_fields (Magnetic Flux in Non-uniform Fields), concept:weber (Weber)
Requires concepts: concept:area_vector (Area Vector), concept:electric_flux (Electric Flux), concept:magnetic_field (Magnetic Field)
title: Magnetic Flux

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:6
Section: Faraday's Law of Induction
Summary: This section introduces Faraday's Law of Induction, which states that a change in magnetic flux through a circuit induces an electromotive force (emf) proportional to its rate of change. It provides the mathematical formulation for single and multi-turn coils, identifies the variables influencing flux change, and demonstrates practical calculations of induced emf and current.
Key terms: Faraday's law of induction, induced emf, magnetic flux, multi-turn coil, rate of change of flux, electromagnetic induction
Teaches concepts: concept:emf_in_multi_turn_coils (EMF in Multi-turn Coils), concept:faradays_law_of_electromagnetic_induction (Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction), concept:induced_electromotive_force (Induced Electromotive Force), concept:variables_influencing_magnetic_flux (Variables Influencing Magnetic Flux)
Requires concepts: concept:magnetic_flux (Magnetic Flux), concept:ohms_law (Ohm's Law)
title: Faraday's Law of Induction

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:6
Section: Lenz's Law and Conservation of Energy
Summary: Lenz's law determines the polarity of an induced electromotive force (emf), showing that it always opposes the change in magnetic flux that created it. This opposition ensures that electromagnetic induction is fully consistent with the law of conservation of energy, converting mechanical work into electrical energy.
Key terms: Lenz's law, induced emf, magnetic flux, conservation of energy, Joule heating, induced current
Teaches concepts: concept:conservation_of_energy_in_induction (Conservation of Energy in Induction), concept:direction_of_induced_current (Direction of Induced Current), concept:lenzs_law (Lenz's Law)
Requires concepts: concept:conservation_of_energy (Conservation of Energy), concept:faradays_law_of_induction (Faraday's Law of Induction), concept:magnetic_flux (Magnetic Flux)
title: Lenz's Law and Conservation of Energy

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:6
Section: Motional Electromotive Force
Summary: This section introduces motional electromotive force (emf) and derives its expression using both Faraday's law of induction and the Lorentz force on free charge carriers. It also discusses how time-varying magnetic fields generate electric fields and applies these concepts to rotating conductors.
Key terms: Motional electromotive force, Lorentz force, Faraday's law, Induced electric field, Rotating conductor, Magnetic flux
Teaches concepts: concept:emf_in_a_rotating_conductor (EMF in a Rotating Conductor), concept:induced_electric_fields_from_time_varying_magnetic_fields (Induced Electric Fields from Time-Varying Magnetic Fields), concept:lorentz_force_explanation_of_motional_emf (Lorentz Force Explanation of Motional EMF), concept:motional_electromotive_force (Motional Electromotive Force)
Requires concepts: concept:faradays_law_of_induction (Faraday's Law of Induction), concept:lorentz_force (Lorentz Force), concept:magnetic_flux (Magnetic Flux)
title: Motional Electromotive Force

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:6
Section: Inductance
Summary: This section introduces the concept of inductance as the constant of proportionality between magnetic flux linkage and current. It explores mutual inductance between coupled coils, including the reciprocity theorem, and self-inductance of a single coil, deriving expressions for the induced back emf, stored magnetic energy, and magnetic energy density.
Key terms: Inductance, Flux linkage, Mutual inductance, Self-inductance, Reciprocity theorem, Back emf, Magnetic energy density, Henry
Teaches concepts: concept:inductance (Inductance), concept:magnetic_energy_density (Magnetic Energy Density), concept:magnetic_potential_energy (Magnetic Potential Energy), concept:mutual_inductance (Mutual Inductance), concept:reciprocity_theorem (Reciprocity Theorem), concept:self_inductance (Self-Inductance)
Requires concepts: concept:faradays_law_of_electromagnetic_induction (Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction), concept:magnetic_field_of_a_solenoid (Magnetic Field of a Solenoid), concept:magnetic_flux (Magnetic Flux)
title: Inductance

subject: physics
text:
Subject: physics
Grade: 12
Chapter: ncert:physics:12:6
Section: AC Generator
Summary: This section explains the principles, components, and operation of an AC generator, which utilizes electromagnetic induction to produce alternating current. It derives the formula for the sinusoidally varying induced electromotive force (emf) in a rotating coil and describes its commercial applications.
Key terms: AC generator, armature, slip rings, carbon brushes, peak emf, alternating current, electromagnetic induction, sinusoidal variation
Teaches concepts: concept:ac_generator (AC Generator), concept:armature_slip_rings_and_brushes (Armature, Slip Rings, and Brushes), concept:induced_emf_in_a_rotating_coil (Induced EMF in a Rotating Coil), concept:peak_emf (Peak EMF), concept:sinusoidal_variation_of_emf (Sinusoidal Variation of EMF)
Requires concepts: concept:angular_velocity (Angular Velocity), concept:electromagnetic_induction (Electromagnetic Induction), concept:faradays_law_of_induction (Faraday's Law of Induction), concept:magnetic_flux (Magnetic Flux)
title: AC Generator
